# Thread: Optimal health question regarding deep wound and sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drsongihan — 2024-07-10T19:51:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336924>

My primary goal in my journey is to help my teen with bipolar depression (catatonic depression, no will to live, anger).
I am myself healthy as far as I can see, but I have two questions related to my own health.

1) I tend to compromise sleep because I am so eager wanting to be awake to learn and do and use my time. I have no problem sleeping whenever I decide to sleep and in almost any environment. I only have rarely insomnia, probably when I drink 2 coffee in the evening. I do know if I sleep too little, my muscles hurt, but recently I figured out that I can eliminate that when I take a cold bath (thanks to the Kruse wisdom) or sunbathe.

Still, I assume that this is fundamentally a bad thing since I might be shortening my lifespan? How do I balance the need to sleep enough vs use my time awake to do things (learn, research, read, talk to friends, help). How little sleep is enough? How do I know I sleep enough?

2) The only other place where I notice that my health is not optimal is that when I have a wound (it could be due to bug bite or anything) it tends to make a deep crater, become dark and generate an ugly scab that is really hard to ignore. It's mostly on my legs and arms, i.e. the areas that are exposed to sun. Doesthis mean anything? Is a deeper, crater-like, wound a foreboding of something or is there a mechanism that can explain this phenomenon?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-07-10T20:40:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336927>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> How do I balance the need to sleep enough vs use my time awake to do things (learn, research, read, talk to friends, help)

Use only the solar cycle (not a clock) to determine bedtime and wake time. Go to sleep when you are tired and get up when you are not.

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> How little sleep is enough?

Depends on your personal context (your environment, your redox potential, your age, etc.)

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> How do I know I sleep enough?

How do you feel when you wake in the morning?

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=336924)
>
> The only other place where I notice that my health is not optimal is that when I have a wound

Wound healing is circadian. If wound healing is off, sounds to me like your rhythm is off

And if something is biting you and leaving a crater, I would go get that checked.

### caroline — 2024-07-10T22:42:14+0300

### drsongihan — 2024-07-11T05:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-336943>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=336927)
>
> Use only the solar cycle (not a clock) to determine bedtime and wake time. Go to sleep when you are tired and get up when you are not.
>

>

> How do you feel when you wake in the morning?
>
>
> Wound healing is circadian. If wound healing is off, sounds to me like your rhythm is off
>
> And if something is biting you and leaving a crater, I would go get that checked.

I agree! The solar cycle as the alarm is a good compass, and I will keep trying. However, at 40 to 50 latitude, the summer nights are short and the winter nights are long, so it just does not mesh with practical life so easily unless somebody does not need any centralized money to make their life work. For everyone at 40 latitude and up moving to lower latitude is not a practical solution for planet earth either? It only works in this circle because we all are still the minority, and in my circle I would be the "crazy" one, which plays in our favor that moving is an option. Anyway,
as soon as I turn on artificial light (despite using blue blocking glasses) in the evening we are disrupting the solar cycle as our compass. I guess we will have to try our best, but the logical consequence is that we should sleep longer in winter and shorter in summer if we live in high latitude?

How I "feel" has two aspects. It's more complicated because we are complicated mammals with a Ferrari engine up there, as Jack would say.

One is the rudimentary physical wellness. The other is the eagerness and motivation of wanting to learn, understand, discover, change the world .... and is also as much human nature as tending to our rudimentary physical wellness, of course as long as we are healthy at our core. If we are sick, the latter anyway goes out the window. I guess we have to always watch and balance, and in my case, I abused the rudimentary physical wellness because I have no ailment but it could easily catch up with me in the future, so better watch out. One thing I noticed is that being out in the sun longer does seem to give me more energy (initially it made me more tired).

Re wound, all my wound tend to be deeper, not just when a bug bites. They do heal not too slow, but in ways that tend to make a big scab, and then leave a scar or at the minimum a dark spot. The doctor will tell me "don't scratch" but I know that this is not the issue. Rather, it's how my body react to wounds. Do you have an article that shows how wound healing is connected to the Circadian rythm?

Whatever skin is more exposed must see sunlight!

I haven't seen much discussion on this, but you should try quitting caffeine entirely. Very powerful and addictive chemical that, at least if you are as sensitive to it as I am, is going to wreck your ability to align your circadian clock with the light and dark cycles. Lots of high-functioning people have the same problem with wanting to use their time at the expense of sleep, but I found that once I gave up caffeine I was much more willing to go to bed early and found it easier to wake up naturally in the morning.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-07-13T05:21:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-337037>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337013)
>
> Caffeine is one thing I have failed to give up. I have drank coffee with heavy cream religiously basically every morning for as long as I can remember.

I still enjoy a decaf (with heavy cream as well) from time to time.

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=337013)
>
> My energy levels and sleep seem to be okay from what I can tell. Unfortunately, I don't have any baseline data on myself though. Just kinda go off of relative feelings.
>
> But, I do often wonder if I could be even more optimal without the coffee ritual. I highly suspect that I could be, but I fail every time I attempt to abstain from it. Most I remember going is 2 days without it in recent memory. It always pulls me back in. Caffeine is certainly a hell of a drug

It's definitely more addictive than it's given credit for. When I quit drinking coffee daily it took me a couple of months to completely rid myself of the withdrawal symptoms. The first couple days I would fall asleep before 9pm, whereas normally I would be up past midnight. Then headaches, irritability, cravings, etc.

Apparently there is a genetic component to caffeine metabolism, with both fast and slow variants. I am definitely in the "slow" bucket, so it used to mess up my sleep even with a single cup in the morning. Might be worth an experiment to go without it at some point, but if you're a fast metabolizer, I suspect a morning cup has much less of a negative effect on your internal clock.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-13T19:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-health-question-regarding-deep-wound-and-sleep.29129/post-337083>

What I have noticed over the years reading/watching “experts” is there are truth seekers and then there are those pushing a position - especially when it comes to health, food and faith. I look for the truth seekers.

In the physical world, an important point when looking for info on how something will affect YOU - be it caffeine, alcohol, palio, carnivore, nnEMF (from “DE” to RF) remember . . . It depends (on YOU). Yes there are general principles that will apply to most - but specifically for YOU . . . It depends. So many variable's both in you and your environment.

I have done caffeine and alcohol for a season (I like the flavors) and gone without for a season, I’ve been a vegetarian (2 years), I’ve eaten carnivore - I don’t notice any difference. Fasting (3 days, 10 days, now mostly intermittent) - works for me. Light, water, magnetism - works for me. Prayer/meditation - works for me. I have also noticed that the most ignorant people on any given subject tend to be those who are an “expert” in another.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T17:16:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-intro-to-dr-jack.29131/post-338099>

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

This is why you must embrace the suck the life.
Life has a tension between comfort and discomfort. Discomfort affords us the perspective to ask better questions about our current assumptions. In my opinion, discomfort emerges from our minds when our choices are suppressing our true identity.
A great way to poison a human is to remove any hardship or struggle from his life. One of the most essential things for any leader is never letting anyone else define who you are and what you choose. Being unconventional does not mean the idea of a mito-hack cannot work. Unconventional means the prescription has not made it into conventional textbooks. This is what quantum biology is and the recent melanopsin data show us; you better pay deep attention to Black Swan mitochondriacs as your environment is made unfavorable by the trends of modern human networking.
Darwin gave us the false meme that life is about the survival of the fittest and that competition is critical to success. It is not. Why do I say this? It is about the survival of the wisest. Unless you know the epigenetic code, which is our software program that runs the genes, which are the hardware in cells, the gene of DNA has no fundamental meaning. That has made Darwin and Watson Crick's perspective meaningless for us and for medicine. This paradigm of "genomic belief" has been utterly toxic for success with illness.

Letting go of toxic environments you put yourself in can be one of the hardest things to do in life, but it is one of the most wise things one can do to change their life. Life is much less a competitive struggle for survival than a triumph of cooperation and creativity. Build your career and network around this concept and you'll win. The most important thing in understanding life's cipher is hearing what isn't being said aloud. It is analogous to what we've found in genetics. Ignoring the genes and paying attention to the junk is where the rainbow really lies. Nature never gives up her secrets easily. We must learn to ignore the noise genes create in professional science and focus on the junk DNA.

The same is true with talk. Words we use are not as critical in human communication as we believe. Just as it is the silent part of the genome that runs our DNA, in language, it is the things not said in words, but present between words that are critical. They hold the key to successful understanding in life. The art of reading between the lines is the quest of the wise mitochondriac. Always pay attention to the pauses, the nonverbal chatter, and the movement that occurs between words to understand what is really going on.

Learning is like the fuel that moves the electrons of your body toward a destination of success. Learning requires an ear that listens and an intellect that is aware and has the patience to endure mistakes. Errors are the growing pains of wisdom. The invariable mark of wisdom in you is when you see the possible in the improbable and the miraculous in the common. Wisdom is not built by time and does not comes not from age, but from education, learning, and thinking.

The irony of modern centralized science is this: Physicists/astronomers believe that if they want to understand the beginning and end of the universe they should aim understand the life cycle of the stars to unlock the mystery of the evolution of the universe. They believe if they can work out how stars change and evolve over time they believe they could begin to understand how the universes changes over time. The irony? Stars emit light and it is the light that changes the universe. None of the scientist in biology have ever thought once about how light causes the evolution of cells in living beings. Amazing contraction to me. The lesson to be learned and tattoo'd into your brain?

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

If you get it wrong,

### CHANGES IN LIGHT PREDICT EPIGENETIC PROGRAMMING and that explains where your problems and diseases come from. It is not your genome that define you.​

# Thread: Hormones and thermodynamics.  Anything losing energy gets larger.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormones-and-thermodynamics-anything-losing-energy-gets-larger.29135/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-11T16:58:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormones-and-thermodynamics-anything-losing-energy-gets-larger.29135/post-336957>

Hormones and thermodynamics. Anything losing energy gets larger.

The Skoptzy (or Skoptsy, meaning the castrated), also called the White Doves, were a Christian sect whose male members, to attain their ideal of sanctity, subjected themselves to castration. Their origin in the 18th century, their spread through a large part of Russia and into Romania and Bessarabia.

Tandler and Grosz obtained an x-ray of the skull in a 20-yr-old Skoptzy man, who had been castrated at age 10 yr and observed that the sella turcica was grossly enlarged. The longer the destruction of the gonads was present the larger the pituitary became. We see the same effect in people with hormone destuction who have pituitary tumors. All pituitary tumors are associated with energy loss and fossa enlargement given enough time has elapsed. Koch obtained x-rays of the skull in 10 Skoptzy men and reported that the pituitary glands were normal in size in 3, enlarged in 4, and“ particularly” enlarged in 3. In the latter group, there was also erosion of the dorsum sellae of the pituitary.

There is a deep decentralized lesson here for decentralized clinicians.

### [Long-Term Consequences of Castration in Men: Lessons from the Skoptzy and the Eunuchs of the Chinese and Ottoman Courts](https://academic.oup.com/jcem/article/84/12/4324/2864451)

Castration of men and males of other species was almost certainly the first experiment in endocrinology (if not in zoology), and the literature on the subj

 academic.oup.com

# Thread: WHY THE USA HAS TO REJECT BASEL 3 AND START OVER :  CHEVRON IMPLICATIONS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337105>

The banking cartel is looking to to push everyone into feudalism with their Basel 3 regulations and nothing in our Constitution requires us to follow it. Unfortunately the regulators of the currency at the Fed do have a say because of how Chevron Doctrine worked prior to its reversal.

We need to take away banking regulators power and give them back to Congress so the SCOTUS can be the ultimate arbiter of what is legal.

The capital regulation plan, also known as the Basel III Endgame proposal, will require banks to maintain nearly [30% more capital](https://fsforum.com/news/required-gsib-capital-increase-would-far-exceed-government-estimates), resulting in increased borrowing costs for lower- and middle-income Americans. If you listened Federal regulators (Powell) have not properly considered how this proposal will impact Americans across the country, especially those in underserved communities. This means those people will need Bitcoin more than one can imagine.
Even a few sats will matter of the Fed implements Basel 3.

Basel 3 will reduce bank lending and this will help private credit issuers. Those are the people who prop up the banking cartel.

Faced with additional capital requirements, banks have two broad choices — raise additional capital to maintain existing footprint or shrink current footprint. Evidence from the initial Basel III implementation reveals that banks will choose the latter. One of the consequences will be a further shift of lending to unregulated firms that are free from oversight and capital requirements, increasing the risk of financial instability. Basel 3 tells us that we are getting closer to financial calamity and Bitcoin world.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337106>

Private credit funds are [pools](https://www.bairdwealth.com/globalassets/pdfs/help/private-credit-funds-disclosure.pdf) of actively managed capital that invest primarily in loans to private companies — a type of nonbank financial intermediation, or NBFI also called shadow banking. So, while investors could start a bank with the goal of lending to businesses, they can also choose to avoid the expansive bank regulatory framework by, instead, pooling their funds and hiring what in banking would be called loan officers. This regulation-light approach is allowed because lenders to these funds, unlike depositors, give up their right to withdraw money early and (Scout’s honor!) they will not seek government assistance if the loans go bad. This is a horrible idea.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337109>

There is nothing new about nonbanks offering banking-type products. Also not new is that one can typically point to bank regulation as a key driver for such innovation. For example, money market mutual funds were a [market response](https://www.sec.gov/comments/4-619/4619-34.pdf) to the caps on interest payments by banks on deposits (former Regulation Q). They are now a $5.7 trillion market [prone to runs](https://academic.oup.com/rfs/article-abstract/34/11/5402/6289999?redirectedFrom=fulltext) and [requiring assistance](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/mmlf.htm) from the Federal Reserve during stressed market conditions to stem financial contagion.

The U.S. banking system now provides [just 33 percent of total credit](https://www.bis.org/statistics/totcredit.htm) to the U.S. non-financial sector today. The lower this number goes the less Bitcoin you need the higher it goes the more you'll need.

If Basel 3 goes in as law that is a key signal.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337110>

I learned about this the hardware during hurricane Katrina in 2005. There is capital requirements that do exist for [insurance companies](https://fedfin.com/karen-petrou-the-apples-to-martians-comparison-of-pe-capital-to-that-demanded-of-banks/), but they do not define capital as narrowly or [rigorously](https://content.naic.org/industry/ucaa/chart-foreign-statutory-capital-surplus) as banking regulators do and this makes them more risky and subject to loss. This is why I pulled all my money from annuities that they offered in the early 2000s during the .com scam.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337111>

Hedge funds and the markets-related activities of insurance companies are completely the wild west and many people have their capital tied up in these vehicles. They should be considered toxic if Basel 3 is adopted.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337114>

Corporations have relationships with banks for a slew of credit and other products and services. So why would a bank let a customer take this business elsewhere?

Simple answer: once the bank factors in the cost of figuring out the additional capital required for these credit transactions, the cost of that additional capital, the costs imposed from the back and forth with bank supervisors examining the bank’s transactions, and then any additional capital and other constraints if the bank is subject to [stress tests](https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/stress-tests-capital-planning.htm), then the price at which the bank would be able to provide the credit will be easily undercut by the private credit funds.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337115>

Private credit also locks up investor funds. Investors in private credit funds typically cannot withdraw their money on demand. This means if you participate in this pool you are taking massive risks in a Basel 3 world. There are long lock-in periods and even more complex structures. If you invest in these pools you can read about [zombie funds](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2023-09-24/private-equity-zombie-firms-leave-pension-funds-with-hard-choices#xj4y7vzkg) for these credit funds.

Some nonbank lenders can [impose withdrawal limits during stressed market condition](https://www.wsj.com/articles/investors-yank-money-from-commercial-property-funds-pressuring-real-estate-values-11670293325)s — if a bank were to do it, it potentially could be treated as a failed bank. In theory, this reduces the risk of a failure in a private credit fund creating contagion and wider financial instability.

Unfortunately, this view misses the point that when [systemic risk comes calling](https://www.berkshirehathaway.com/letters/2016ltr.pdf#page=5), lock-in periods will not matter. Basel 3 creates systemic risks in the entire sector.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337116>

Burdensome regulations drive U.S. banks out of the business of providing credit to communities. This vacuum will be filled by poorly capitalized private credit funds (and other nonbank lenders) that will provide credit when times are good but likely disappear when times are tough. You need to avoid these areas if Basel 3 comes in.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T22:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337117>

Everyone saw how counterparty risk took down many of the banks who did business with crypto businesses 2020-2023. The same risks will happen to non banking credit providers. In this way, Basel 3 is the mechanism for land & asset confiscation for the banking cartel. You need to be aware of this and how the Fed talks about this going forward.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337118>

From a historical perspective, it raises huge concerns in me that the next financial crisis, when it inevitably occurs, will require massive money printing and bailouts of unregulated, leveraged firms with systemically large economic footprints. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337119>

>
> From a free market perspective, the effects of the B3E proposals might suggest to smooth brained thinking that robust capitalism that will be boosted by the B3E proposals. Do not be fooled.

This is great for owners of Bitcoin but will be disasterous for anyone who owns fiat assets.

Fundamentally I agree. With the same swipe of a pen that can bring in B3E or alter any law, so can legally banning competing assets or currencies. Leviathan governments and those in power will not go down easily.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T23:08:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337120>

The GFC of 2008 was largely the result of imprudent bank lending & borrowing in real estate, & the legislation and regulation that followed largely focused only on the banks and, specifically, the amount of capital they hold. This created the void that non banking credit providers now fill. They have few capital controls so their risk profile for you is off the charts. Communities will be wiped free if they are financed this way.

My concern is retirement and pension funds some of you may have cash in. Pension funds are increasingly using hedge funds to boost their yields for the public and this makes them very risky. Moreover, insurance companies are increasingly owned by private equity looking for additional sources of funds. All of us pay the price when large, heavily leveraged firms go belly up — which is why we should think twice about creating a regulatory framework that drives business away from the brightly lit world of highly regulated banks and into the shadows of private credit. **_Personally, I want Basel 3 because I think it leads to fiat explosion faster._**

But for some of you living on a fiat standard and owning assets in the USA you may not like this.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-13T23:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-the-usa-has-to-reject-basel-3-and-start-over-chevron-implications.29150/post-337121>

What is being sold to the Fed by Basel? That Basel 3 is needed because the financial risk of the GFC is still uncovered in banks.

Basel III is **an internationally agreed set of measures developed by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision in response to the financial crisis of 2007-09**. It is not a binding agreement for Congress unless the Fed mandates it. Congress should never do this and in my opinion it is the biggest reason why the recent Chevron decision is BIG.

Basel is trying to sell this to Powell as a needed measure to strengthen the regulation, supervision and risk management of banks. Putin has cut his ties with the Rothschild banking cartel in the last 5 years. The BRICS seem to be headed this way as well.

The smart move is for the USA to back fiat with Bitcoin and tell Basel 3 to fuck off, but given the DNC and Biden reliance on Senator Warren I seriously doubt this happens.

Basel 3 is composed of three parts, or pillars.
Pillar 1 addresses capital and liquidity adequacy and provides minimum requirements.
Pillar 2 outlines supervisory monitoring and review standards.
Pillar 3 promotes market discipline through prescribed public disclosures.

As of July 2024 it is over 1000 pages. I have read most of it.

### drsongihan — 2024-07-15T00:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-thinking-and-political-affiliation.29157/post-337171>

I am used to my world order being upside down and confusing wherever I look and go these days. Just today, I learned that pasteurization of milk has nothing to do with killing some germ in milk, but germs that can come along from cow's behind, and if done right, that raw milk is much better to digest. The idea that raw milk is easier to digest than pasteurized milk is opposite of what we learned, like it seems with everything.

Another part that I cannot reconcile is decentralized thinking and political affiliation and the idea who is "kin". I listened to Russel Brand's Brandemic Comedy special and it was not only hilarious, but very much truth spilling. He talked about, with humor, how crazy hypocritical it is that the whole mainstream media was talking about and showing Wuhan's wet market, riling up xenophobia and running the narrative that the crazy raw bat-eating Chinese have caused the coronavirus gone viral, but kept a secret that Wuhan had a gain-of-function program on bat's coronavirus, studying how to make the virus more deadly, but even when such news was trickling out that the narrative turned into "evil Chinese government running such dangerous program", but not a word about the U.S. role (Fauci etc). I thought Russel Brand's show was brilliant and brought home the message of truth, but in a humanistic and kind way, while being hilarious.

Yet, his channel is full of pro-Trump b.s. I mean, what has Trump anything to do with decentralized thinking? Sure, he is an out-of-the-box thinking individual, but driven by greed, self absorbed narcissism, unkind, fuels hatred, making ordinary people feel "he gets them" while also emptying their pockets. He is as far from decentralized thinking, curiosity driven and humanistic vision as it gets. But why is somebody like Russell Brand at the same time pro-Trump?

I guess there is no such thing as political affiliation for me anymore. Each person, each situation and each message has to be vetted one by one, separately, by my own judgement whether it's truth-seeking or not. Sigh. This is a lot of work, but worth the journey.

> ### [Thread by @DrJackKruse on Thread Reader App](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1913605005883384240.html)
>
> @DrJackKruse: Why will prion disease disease be a collateral effect if you do not develop the turbocancer first? @Kevin\_McKernan TIME TO LEARN SOME BIOPHYSICS FOLKS BECAUSE YOUR BIOCHEMISTRY EXPERTS ARE IGNORANT. Th...…
>
>  threadreaderapp.com

Fascinating. I knew about misfolding caused by broken inflammatory response. I knew that cysteines are potent anti oxidant. But the concept that the cysteines are there to protect proteins from the potentially harmful effect of UV excitation of aromatic amino acids is not a connection I made.

One of the most prominent protein that misfolds and forms tangles in the broodest category of neurodegenerative diseases is tau. Tau tangle deposits are diagnostic of Alzheimer's, CTE, progress supra nuclear palsy and even Down syndrom. There is no a cure nor diagnosis from the medical community. A lot of sensational non-sense, yes, a lot of scandals, yes, but no diagnosis. Literally, definitive diagnosis comes post mortem.

It was found in the past decade that tau tangles spread by a prion-like mechanism. In other words, not only the prion protein, but also tau tangles act like a prion, spreading from cell to cell.

The native protein tau has two natural cysteines, at site 291 and 322. These disulfide bonds are never formed, neither in healthy tau when associated with microtubules, nor in pathological tau when they stack into amyloid fibrils that have prion like competency. I was always wondering why the hell would tau have two native cysteines if it does not use it? Well, it looks like it is a protection switch that I need to look into.

Amazing.

### John Patrick — 2024-07-15T21:09:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337198>

Hey this is Michael Moreau, and I attended the Jack Kruse Q & A regarding financial issues about the globe, on July 14th, yesterday, 2024. I am writing this thread to reach out to anyone who might be able to help me in my desire to move to El Salvador. To anyone who is presently living in El Salvador or is enroute in the next few weeks, I would like to schedule a visit to the country to have a look see at the environment, the people, and the possible land opportunities. My finances are such that I do need to find a small place to hang my hat on to rest, car rental, and where to stay economically. Health wise, I need to be near the salt water to ground and walk about, and don't mind driving into the hills to sleep at night. It would be great to connect with someone who is already in El Salvador and has made the transition or making the transition from the U.S. to the tropics. My email is ([healthyassurance@gmail.com](mailto:healthyassurance@gmail.com)), and from there I can provide a phone number to call when possible. I thank you kindly for your input regarding this S.O.S, message in a bottle. Michael Moreau

### drsongihan — 2024-07-16T16:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337217>

Hi Michael, I recommend that you contact Maria Theresa (MT) who rents out a room in Atami where you can walk every day to the beach. It's 60 a day for the room. MT also connected me to someone who rented me a car for 20 a day. I have that car now, but her friend Oscar might have other cars. You need WhatsApp for contacting people and Waze as your main Map.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-16T16:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337218>

### John Patrick — 2024-07-16T16:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337219>

Dr. Songihan, thank you for your reply ever so kindly. How might I contact Maria Theresa, (MT), does she have an email address, or do I call her on a phone. I will look up Atami. You mention (need), WhatsApp for contacting people, so I should get that app and Maria will be listed in that app in some way, shape, or fashion. I am very new to the app tech world, as you can tell. Now, what about Waze. Is that another sort of app on the phone, or something else I need to learn about. So, it seems a phone is critical to getting about anywhere these days. I presume you are in El Salvador now, and what do folks do about food if they rent a room. Hot plate set up, kitchenette, or just eat whatever at a grocery store or farmers market? Water sources might be bottle from the store or tap in the rented room? Thank you for your information. It gives me a point to put on the map.

### John Patrick — 2024-07-16T17:27:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337220>

Thanks to you Jack the Administrator, I can look at other options a bit more but have to budget a trip as best I can. Also, the car I was considering bringing to El Salvador when I make the big more, is a 2002 Honda CRV I bought for 5 grand in 2018, so Kelley Blue Book is about 1,200, and the tax bringing it in country someone wrote in chat is like 60%. That is rather insignificant from my point of reference, if they allow that age of car into the country. I had my mechanic do a complete on it, so it is roadworthy. When I go the call 4 years ago that my brother was not going to live more than two weeks, from a doctor in Blake Hospital, I drove cross country from Washington to Florida, in it and slept in it at truck stops, so I am used to that form of making ends meet. and must get to the tropics to see if this challenge with the cramping feet is, a returning of what some call Post Polio Sequalae. Walking is becoming really difficult. Your mention of SV-40 in the Ruben-Kruse-Kennedy photo (Assembly 2023) video - got my attention big time. Having been paralyzed with so-called Polio at age 5, in 1955, I spent months recuperating in Port Jefferson Hospital, then was released barely, on a wing and a prayer. Only then to be inoculated with 5 subsequent Salk Vaccines, and one Sabin Sugar Cube. I thought natural immunity was good enough as nature intended. If so why would the lab coat boys need to sextuple down. Doing the best I can with this script from genocide.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-07-16T19:33:47+0300

### drsongihan — 2024-07-16T22:35:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337225>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337219)
>
> Dr. Songihan, thank you for your reply ever so kindly. How might I contact Maria Theresa, (MT), does she have an email address, or do I call her on a phone. I will look up Atami. You mention (need), WhatsApp for contacting people, so I should get that app and Maria will be listed in that app in some way, shape, or fashion. I am very new to the app tech world, as you can tell. Now, what about Waze. Is that another sort of app on the phone, or something else I need to learn about. So, it seems a phone is critical to getting about anywhere these days. I presume you are in El Salvador now, and what do folks do about food if they rent a room. Hot plate set up, kitchenette, or just eat whatever at a grocery store or farmers market? Water sources might be bottle from the store or tap in the rented room? Thank you for your information. It gives me a point to put on the map.

I think I can post here
Maria Theresa: +503 7682 7715
You can message her per WhatsApp. She has a room with air conditioning. It’s 5 min walking to a beach with magnetic sand. They have shared kitchen and shared washer. The kitchen has everything so you don’t need to buy anything, but its community one. You can buy grocery with your car and they also have a once a week organic food delivery that you can sign up for. If you stay with Maria Theresa she has clean water available in bottles and in big water dispensers. She offers eggs gratis but the rest you would buy and put into their fridge.
It’s 20 min walk to 5 min walk to the Bitcoin Farmers market on Sundays 9-12!
Oscar told me he has another car for 20 a day. You need to use WhatsApp.
Oscar: +503 7118 5291
Think of WhatsApp like texting and Waze like Google map but works better.

Meaning, if I buy a car, where do I go, a big city, a local garage off the beaten path, I have no idea what the landscape or terrain looks like yet, so I am thinking that everywhere a person goes, there are old houses, chickens running around, a gas station every 10 miles away, small grocery stores, or is it like modern city life?

### pfedorow — 2024-07-17T18:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337261>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337248)
>
> Hello pfedorow: when you mention better to sell in U.S. where I live now, and "Buy here", meaning El Salvador, can you describe your experience in "buying" a car (if you have) in El Sal, how old the cars are, what condition, average price, just a few pointers. Meaning, if I buy a car, where do I go, a big city, a local garage off the beaten path, I have no idea what the landscape or terrain looks like yet, so I am thinking that everywhere a person goes, there are old houses, chickens running around, a gas station every 10 miles away, small grocery stores, or is it like modern city life?

I don't own one yet here, but I believe many members got Jeremy Franco to help out. Also, there is a company called [Bitcoin cars](https://www.instagram.com/bitcoincars_elsalvador) that helped a few people i know get a car.

There are lots of accident recovery vehicles here, so it is great to get a mechanic and a report on the vehicle before you purchase it.

It is pretty modern, but there are lots of time warps from the 1970s here. It is growing fast and changing even faster. There are lots of little tiendas to buy small things, but you probably want to do grocery runs to get what you need.

Anthonys post reminds me: bring an Ethernet cable and whatever connector from Ethernet to your computer and phone. Even 10 years ago we did not have WiFi everywhere but now it’s an exception to not have it. MT ‘s place has WiFi turned off but you can easily connect via hardwire to the internet.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-07-18T02:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337281>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337198)
>
> Hey this is Michael Moreau, and I attended the Jack Kruse Q & A regarding financial issues about the globe, on July 14th, yesterday, 2024. I am writing this thread to reach out to anyone who might be able to help me in my desire to move to El Salvador. To anyone who is presently living in El Salvador or is enroute in the next few weeks, I would like to schedule a visit to the country to have a look see at the environment, the people, and the possible land opportunities. My finances are such that I do need to find a small place to hang my hat on to rest, car rental, and where to stay economically. Health wise, I need to be near the salt water to ground and walk about, and don't mind driving into the hills to sleep at night. It would be great to connect with someone who is already in El Salvador and has made the transition or making the transition from the U.S. to the tropics. My email is ([healthyassurance@gmail.com](mailto:healthyassurance@gmail.com)), and from there I can provide a phone number to call when possible. I thank you kindly for your input regarding this S.O.S, message in a bottle. Michael Moreau

MIchael the advice from the others is great, take it and run with it.

My advice is not to worry and jump in and figure it out when you get here, there is plenty of help and DO NOT be afraid.

If i were in your shoes i would start at Maria Theresas because this is a scouting trip and majority of the community are in and around Atami where she is, so you have many people to help out, answer questions and point you in the right direction.

Use MT's as a base and drive around wherever you want to look at from there. Organise day trips and have fun.

She could organise a cheap rental from what i see. I leaned on Jeremy Franco and would always recommend him, i use his mechanic and they sourced me a car, until then i rented from the mechanic Jeremias. Lovely guy.

Remember when you get here that this is a developing nation so its not what you might be used to.
Infrastructure is improving all the time. The major highways are now amazing and still being extended. Lots of potholes in side streets but you just dodge them!

Your comment 'I have no idea what the landscape or terrain looks like yet, so I am thinking that everywhere a person goes, there are old houses, chickens running around, a gas station every 10 miles away, small grocery stores, or is it like modern city life?'

Expect old run down houses with lots of new development happening so a mix. Its very poor and the houses are very basic for many.

City more modern, lots of shopping malls if thats what you require and they will be familiar. Lots of little stores to buy produce daily, bigger shopping trips into the city to stock up.

Yes to chickens running around, as well as horses, cows, pigs, ducks etc in my area, i am not sure as much in Atami but you will definitely see them! I love it still and don't think i would get sick of it except the stray dogs drive me demented but in time i am sure that will change too.

Lots of houses with razor wire - remnants from the years of civil war and gang violence.

Gas stations popping up everywhere as are more local shopping spots.

Wonderful and very helpful local Salvadorans, beautiful people with very little, very kind, it's been a very humbling experience and a privilege to be living amongst them.

You will make friends no matter where you chose to live, locals are wonderful and you have a car so can drive to meet community.

I live 1.5 hours away and can go in sometimes 3 times a week to the city or La Libertad and its not a problem, i love where i live but its not for everyone, point being find a place that you love, that suits your needs, that fits into your budget and then build your community. It will happen quicker and easier than you are thinking.

Keep asking questions and everyone will help, just book your trip and the rest will fall into place.

Good luck, you are going to be fine, you have already made the hardest decision which is to relocate, the rest will happen as it needs to.

Laudy, thank you for your enthusiastic support and directive information as it is easy to get used to your descriptions of practical life in the country. When I went to Playa Del Carmen (Yucatan), two and a half years ago to check it out for my health, I stayed at a youth Hostel. Unbeknownst to me, the place turned into a disco-inferno, and they proceeded to party till 4 or 5 in the am. So, after the warm golden sunset dimed at 8 or so pm, just as I was falling asleep, the rocking of the house began. Needless to say, I had to deal with that for two weeks and really got exhausted. I will not repeat that option. I am actually looking toward the reality of seeing the little critters you mentioned as landscape wildlife that mother nature created for us to enjoy. Alltami and MTs seems to be a good choice to get some perspective and "venture" out on day hops. However, your area is also a consideration. Here's why, your area might just have a small chunk of land, and I mean small chunk, that my son and I can build a small "tiny house" on like my friend here in the states builds for the Veterans. I am looking into the future a bit I know, but a mitochondriac does that when the idea presents itself. As an outsider, I understand that flying into El Salvador, a person books a flight from the U.S, to San Salvador, then must get to your side of the country by either of three ways. Rent a car... take a long bus ride... or get a driver like Uber to drive to El Salvador. How much does that driver to "El Sal" usually cost, and If I am going through the city proper of San Salvador, why not arrange to stop at the place where they issue a NIT card, in case I wish to purchase a car, property, or a business. When I was in the Yucatan, and spoke very little Spanish, it was easy to get "taken" for various things like even renting a car, then at the markets. It appears that you are describing people who are more "at ease" with gringos from other nations and rather friendly on many levels. So, is language an issue? Jack on the call mentioned that app for translating to Spanish which makes life very easy. I will take your advice and "chill" with the overthinking and just "do". Making the arrangements, booking the ticket, getting the place to stay, are first and foremost to launch my program. What area of El Sal is considered your neighborhood, so I can look and see how far it is from the Altima beach scene. Thank you, Michael.

Jack on the call mentioned that app for translating to Spanish which makes life very easy. I will take your advice and "chill" with the overthinking and just "do". Making the arrangements, booking the ticket, getting the place to stay, are first and foremost to launch my program. What area of El Sal is considered your neighborhood, so I can look and see how far it is from the Altima beach scene. Thank you, Michael.

I second Laudy. I would say you cannot answer these questions from afar. You just need to jump. And then see for yourself.

There are cheap and expensive options to get residency but all of that takes a bit of time, and there is a procedure that requires background check and a lawyer. All very simple, but nothing you can do from far. 10 days in a country I have never thought about coming, I feel like I got really strong connections thanks to the Krusers.

But it’s a ground game and contact sport. You cannot theorize any of that from afar. Book your flight and come to stay with MT. Text her you want to stay say a week or a month. In fact I recommend a month. It’s 60 or 85 a day or 850 or 1000 a month. So you can do the math that the per month option is cheaper. In fact for a start it does not get much cheaper since it also comes with walking distance to the beach, fully equipped kitchen and a strong community. You don’t need to worry about night life or WiFi. After 7 pm it sleepy time since people are up with the sunrise around 5.30 am.

1\. Book a flight.
2\. Contact MT.
3\. Ask her to pick you up. It’s $50 and it does not get much cheaper. Or contact Oscar and have him meet you at the airport with the rental. If you do, arrive during daytime. It’s not easy to drive into Atami residential in the dark the first time. There is ok street lighting.
4\. Bring Cash and bitcoin
5\. Bring Ethernet, bring a phone with Waze and WhatsApp and a plan that allows you to use your US phone for free in ES. ATT or Telekom.
6\. If there is “stuff” you like, such as freshly ground coffee, cotton clothes or leather flip flop, whatever is important to you for daily life you have to bring it. I brought a coffee grinder, coffee press, high quality mountain salt, a wine opener, a sharp knife and an oyster knife. You can tell where my priorities lie.  there is no Amazon or Target here, so you cannot just pop into a shop and buy conveniences.

Visiting Costa Del Sol, buying a land … none of that works from afar.

So as Jack would say, buy the damn ticket and show us you did, and make sure it’s like in a few days. No need to wait much longer.

### John Patrick — 2024-07-20T22:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337398>

I am thanking everyone for providing information to me in this Forum, thank you... I am planning to get to El Salvador and stay for at least 4 weeks or in days, 28. I can only be out of the USA due to Social Security Disability regulations for under 30 days, otherwise they review my details and can cut off the tap. So, this whole move to thing is really touchy, and I will have to come see, then plan, then make the shift, and become a part of a new life. My timeline is to consider a move first to situate my brother in August, then visit El Sal in August or September, then make the move when I can see a small place, find a small rental, or something to live in. A small slice of land I can build a "tiny house" on to relocated to. But I am thinking too far out-front. Long range, get my son involved as a journeyman carpenter in the union, to come and build a few of these small units according to specs in El Sal, then be the person who rents them to others, offering EMF and Wi-Fi free dwellings, 6-inch construction for ease of heat and AC. My friend has already built 20 of these "tiny houses" for Veterans in the U.S. and is considering many more with the help of the state. Right now, it's a dream in the making, but now I know why I had to help him get a bunch of Veterans assembled from different churches to offer to help him clear the land. Had no real idea why I had to do it, but it is clear as can be. all these years later.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-07-21T05:04:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337407>

> [John Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=337292)
>
> Laudy, thank you for your enthusiastic support and directive information as it is easy to get used to your descriptions of practical life in the country. When I went to Playa Del Carmen (Yucatan), two and a half years ago to check it out for my health, I stayed at a youth Hostel. Unbeknownst to me, the place turned into a disco-inferno, and they proceeded to party till 4 or 5 in the am. So, after the warm golden sunset dimed at 8 or so pm, just as I was falling asleep, the rocking of the house began. Needless to say, I had to deal with that for two weeks and really got exhausted. I will not repeat that option. I am actually looking toward the reality of seeing the little critters you mentioned as landscape wildlife that mother nature created for us to enjoy. Alltami and MTs seems to be a good choice to get some perspective and "venture" out on day hops. However, your area is also a consideration. Here's why, your area might just have a small chunk of land, and I mean small chunk, that my son and I can build a small "tiny house" on like my friend here in the states builds for the Veterans. I am looking into the future a bit I know, but a mitochondriac does that when the idea presents itself. As an outsider, I understand that flying into El Salvador, a person books a flight from the U.S, to San Salvador, then must get to your side of the country by either of three ways. Rent a car... take a long bus ride... or get a driver like Uber to drive to El Salvador. How much does that driver to "El Sal" usually cost, and If I am going through the city proper of San Salvador, why not arrange to stop at the place where they issue a NIT card, in case I wish to purchase a car, property, or a business. When I was in the Yucatan, and spoke very little Spanish, it was easy to get "taken" for various things like even renting a car, then at the markets. It appears that you are describing people who are more "at ease" with gringos from other nations and rather friendly on many levels. So, is language an issue? Jack on the call mentioned that app for translating to Spanish which makes life very easy. I will take your advice and "chill" with the overthinking and just "do". Making the arrangements, booking the ticket, getting the place to stay, are first and foremost to launch my program. What area of El Sal is considered your neighborhood, so I can look and see how far it is from the Altima beach scene. Thank you, Michael.

Hi Michael,

I totally understand your PDC experience, it is a phenomenon also experienced in parts of El Salvador and throughout Central America from what I have been told. You will find out when you stay in places to explore them and hopefully rule that out.

I totally understand your questions and had many myself.

Regarding the language, when living here you start to learn a little bit more by trying to communicate with locals and i use 'google translate' as my translator to help which is very useful, however, a lot of people here will try to communicate, even if its sign language, and ususally someone steps in that has a few words of english and you tend to sort it out.

Also download 'Waze' for driving, it will serve you well.

I organised and relied on Jeremy Franco to pick us up/drop us off.

My advice is maybe that is something you could do.

Speak with Jeremy before you get here and organise having his service for the day. Get a decent flight time into the country, get Jeremy to pick you up from the airport and take you to a government office to get your NIT immediately then he could drop you off at your accomodation at MT's.

It will save you time and a lot of the stress of doing it all yourself which i wouldn't advise until you start to figure things out.

My area is called Costa del Sol in La Paz region. I am 25 minutes from the airport and 1.5 hours from Atami and 1 - 1.5 hours from San Salvador depending on where i am going. I dont have a problem with the driving and find it easy to do.

There is a lot of area between Costa del Sol and Atami, as Jack said to you look at some places where the locals live without a large group of expats or tourists and it should be far more affordable.

Also there are areas a short drive from the beach suburbs that could be a compromise as well where you drive to the beach of a morning, i am sure you will find the spot that works for you.

All the best!

### Gene Kalmens — 2024-07-21T07:07:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-michael-moreau.29160/post-337408>

There is an additional info resource - a TG (telegram) group Bitcoiners in El Salvador. Telegram is another app popular as a group communication tool. The invite link is [https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador](https://t.me/bitcoinersinelsalvador).

There are almost 2,000 members who discuss ALL practical aspects of life in and relocation to El Salvador. You will find businesses who offer useful services. For example, you can get a used car from a guy with a handle @BTCRonny. Francesco with a handle @moneydelics is the admin of this group.

Last year, I was able to experience first hand how kind and helpful the local bitcoiners are to the visitors and expats from the so called 1st world countries.

Meaning, suppose a spider or snake bites a person. Do they just go to the emergency or urgent care like in the U.S. with their Medicare card and Medi Gap to fill in the cracks. Do they pay cash or bitcoin for services rendered. Are visitors (foreigner's), gringo's, allowed to go to emergency or urgent care. I am not wishful, but I am an outdoors person and I see that there are 5 poisonous snakes to be watchful for while visiting the lands of El Salvador. While on expedition in the jungles of Ecuador back in 1990, we had to be leery and weary of centipedes, spiders of all types, and some snakes as well. If you're an indoor person well it is not that much of an issue. Your thoughts?

MT introduced me to a wise El Salvadorian men, Joaquin, who was a godsend to me. A wise and decentralized human who knows what the right thing to do is because he has his feet on the ground, literally, he has a beautiful outdoor oasis he built and follows his intuition and his nose as his guide. I was going to learn Spanish from him but I instead just learned life lessons from him.

Joaquin told me that the health care in the state run hospital is free. There is a small clinic in El Tunco for “snake bites” that is for free and the larger hospital is near Playa San Diego, right around the roundabout between Attami and going up towards San Salvador. That clinic is for free and you will be cared for. If you go to a private practice what you pay out of pocket is less than what would be your deductible in the US.

According to Joaquin the healthcare system is good there and I believe him!

# Thread: WHY DID WE DELETE VITAMIN C GENES AND WHAT DOES UV GUT HEALTH HAVE TO DO WITH IT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-we-delete-vitamin-c-genes-and-what-does-uv-gut-health-have-to-do-with-it.29163/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-16T23:17:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-we-delete-vitamin-c-genes-and-what-does-uv-gut-health-have-to-do-with-it.29163/post-337227>

The next big question in DMs and emails I got from this pod was how did we delete Vitamin C genes and how was it related to UV light and hair loss?

ANSWER: How do you get past the nuclear membrane once you electromagnetically make Vitamin D in the skin? Sunlight energy is also used to sulfate cholesterol FIRST to get the Vitamin D train rolling in the skin to the blood to get to nDNA. When it is sulfated, it becomes water soluble in blood. When it isn't sulfated, your liver responds to protect the nucleus in cells, and it upregulates LDL production to act like a sponge to protect the AMO physics inside of cells. So, when your centralized dermatologist tells you to stay out of the sun and take the secosteroid Vitamin D from a plant or animal, it is also packaged in seed oil because it is normally a fat-soluble compound. Few realize that the simple act of taking D3 pills and living indoors behind glass walls is raising your LDL cholesterol constantly. BigHarma knew it, which is why BigHarma taught every doctor since they found the evidence in the 1950s military documents that they could profit massively by training doctors ignorant of light's effects to put their patients on statins. They knew to blame it on biochemistry gone awry and baited the PhDs with the papers on the mevalonate pathway Q cycle. The mevalonate pathway is a sequence of cellular reactions leading to farnesyl pyrophosphate, the common substrate for the synthesis of cholesterol, dolichol, dolichyl phosphate, and CoQ10, and for protein prenylation (a post-translational modification necessary for the targeting and function of many proteins). When you review the history of the evidence, you know they are playing you like a fiddle. You are their mouse, and they are the cat. Few see the game plan and less of you get it. Cholesterol and Vitamin D3 are nearly identical in chemical structure. Most people need to learn this. Sunlight naturally sulfates these things (pic below). The only difference in both bio-molecules is a single, double bond in the second ring of the cholesterol backbone. Remember, light is BURIED at the electronic level in all things, including cholesterol and Vitamin D. Your pills are sold to you soaked in seed oils because it is a fat-soluble vitamin. This process sulfates everything at our surfaces and just below the surface, and this makes many things water soluble and lowers charge density. When you block sulfation, you create a need for more drugs because you lower the tissues in energy, and this exacerbates the timing mismatch in mtDNA. This fuels more mitochondrial mutations and creates disease phenotypes, which fuels their centralized business models. Melanin, Vitamin D, cholesterol, heparin, and nitric oxide all respond to the UV part of the spectrum when it is in sunlight. Few people know that the UV and blue-green parts of the spectrum work in unison to very locally vasodilate blood vessels in the skin. I showed people the melanopsin 2014 paper 1000 times, which showed that blue light is capable of dilating blood vessels, but not one person asked me about the AMO physics of the interaction. This area interested me as a neurosurgeon because I was looking for a mechanism to explain the queer things that happen in brain bleeds called subarachnoid hemorrhages. We see them in trauma and in aneurysm ruptures. I have always felt that a lack of light operating in unison is behind most of the cerebrovascular pathologies that neurosurgeons treat. They are all symptoms of low redox power in the brain. Nitric oxide (NO) is a well-known “gasotransmitter” in the literature because a Nobel Prize was given for its discovery in 1992. It is a gas that acts focally and locally when it is released. It does not have global effects. This tells you something about TIMING, too. It acts RAPIDLY, not CHRONICALLY. Vitamin D actions act CHRONICALLY. This tells you that NO signaling is likely behind many light-induced chronic diseases. Electrified membranes absorb gas and turn it into many other biomolecules. This, in turn, induces high blood pressure by causing endothelial dysfunction. Mitochondria are intimately involved in importing nitrogen into tissues to create the substrates that eventually become NO when sunlight is present. Nitrogen substrates are not created by direct eNOS synthesis. Nature provided a clue to me as to why humans got rid of Vitamin C genetically for glutathione in this AMO physics dance. Here is more evidence of magnetochemistry in humans being used. When glutathione and nitric oxide are powered by terrestrial sunlight, this allows humans to produce S-nitroso glutathione (GSNO). DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE 101 is why we lost our hair and why we no longer have Vitamin C genes.

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-landlord-requiring-wifi-stays-on-for-security-system.29178/post-337301>

Hola! 4 days ago I moved to El Salvador

The landlord has security cameras around the property.

I’ve been turning WiFi off at night which supposedly disrupts the security system

He’s asking / sort of requiring - the WiFi stay on at night - which is not going to happen

He’s suggesting EMF blankets

The security camera box is in a small room off the master. Im putting to use my new measuring tools and there is not a lot of radiation coming from the box to reach my sleeping area. But doubtful a kill switch to the room would work as most likely it’s on the same cicuit

My suggestion is to hard wire camera system to Ethernet

Curious if anyone else had has same experience and or insights on how to approach this situation!

Gracias

IF the WiFi needs to be on, put a shield between it and your bedroom.

# Thread: ANIMAL DATA CAN NEVER BE APPLIED TO HUMANS: ALL NUTRITION DATA FLAWED
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/animal-data-can-never-be-applied-to-humans-all-nutrition-data-flawed.29182/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-19T14:52:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/animal-data-can-never-be-applied-to-humans-all-nutrition-data-flawed.29182/post-337326>

**_ANIMAL DATA CAN NEVER BE APPLIED TO HUMANS: THIS IS WHY ALL NUTRITION DATA FLAWED_**

Centralized science has no proper model. They use lab animals and they are useless to predict what will happen in us.

Why do centralized scientist need to scrap most of their beliefs around food and diet? The key reason is that human only trials will avoid the controversial issues concerning "experimental scaling". That is, can results of animal experiments be extrapolated to humans with accuracy? **Extrapolation is not possible at this time.
**
As an example, researcher Dr. Mary Ellen O'Connor from the Dept. of Psychology, University of Tulsa in the 1960s observed that the drug thalidomide was proven clinically safe using laboratory rats, although the effect on humans was tragic.
As another example, physician Sally Faith Dorfman from the Center for Disease Control, Atlanta noted that aspirin is lethal to laboratory rats. So, extrapolation can fail either way. The reason is simple: How we use magnetochemistry to control the flow of energy is radically different than any other living creature on Earth.

# Thread: Mysterious labs despite healthy lifestyle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mysterious-labs-despite-healthy-lifestyle.29184/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sunman — 2024-07-19T17:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mysterious-labs-despite-healthy-lifestyle.29184/post-337333>

Hi everyone.
My name is Edi and I live in Delaware.
I lead a lifestyle as closely as possible to what Jack prescribes in his blogs as I've read them all twice. My health improved tremendously and stayed that way for about 9 years. I am a pilot, I used to eat popcorn daily on my flying trips, after the workday is over but also eat a lot of canned sardines and canned oysters as this is the only things available on short layovers. I drink Fiji water during my work day and RO filtered water with a drop of Flannagans Crystal Energy in it on overnights that I bring from home. I usually fast through dinner time until after flying is over. My only morning intake is a smoothie made from Maca root and coconut oil plus Mercola multivitamins and krill oil. About a year ago I started using ghee instead of coconut oil but had constant diarrhea since doing this but still persisted for six months and finally changed back to coconut oil last month. At night my smoothies consist of the Seven Treasure mushroom powder and msm + magnesium glycinate in the smoothie plus a Ancient earth minerals and vitamins k2+d3 and Cod liver oil. I watch the sunrise daily whenever possible comes out to about half the time and sunset as well. I spend 1-3 hours out in strong UV sunlight daily in warm months. I do occasional cold therapy. I have the EMR-TEK red light saunas in my home and use them in winter time. I sleep on a Magnetico sleep pad and under a emf reducing bed canopy while on grounded bedsheets. no wifi in my house.
About two years ago on my way home from a foreign country I started having pains in my leg thigh muscle but didn't think much of it. about a year later I had big pains in my stomach muscles on the right side (right side of the "six pack") and the pain seemed to migrate over time to the shoulders and then left side of stomach. This was in the aftermath of me using chlorine dioxide for a few months. the pains lasted for a few months and it was worst at night. Then it subsided for a few months and last winter my right knee swelled up above the joint and I got a bakers cycst thereafter with water accumulation. this persisted for about six months and now its migrating again first to my right arm joint then left arm join and left knee. Currently my right arm join seems fine but left arm joint and both knees are swollen up and hurt at night. My blood test shows all normal except: Antinuclear Ab by Multiplex is POSITIVE, Sedimentation Rate-Westergren is 29 (very high, 0-15 scale) and C-Reactive Protein, Quant is 36 (very high, 0-10 scale). see attached.
Last year I started eating tomatoes again after being off them for 9 years but have stopped now again. I ate a lot of tomatoes throughout last years summer and fall but have stopped doing so since then.
I have a lot of mosquitoes in my backyard and think that I might have contracted Lymes disease somehow but unsure. If it is Lyme then it may be late stage Lyme in which case what may be my treatment options.
Not sure if a 30 minute consult with Jack would help here as well. Trying to post in the ASK JACK forum but it wont allow me due to insufficient privileges despite being a Silver Member.
I would really appreciate any insight into why my body is undergoing this disease and what the possible solutions may be. See my labs results attached hereto.
God bless.
Edi.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-19T20:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/july-19th-2024-cyberattack.29185/post-337338>

I don't think you guys fully grasp how big this is.

Around a billion computers are bricked worldwide, mostly corporate ones. This isn't just an online service going down for a few hours.

Every affected computer needs to be rebooted in fail mode and have a driver manually removed.

Most corporate computers given to employees don't let users do this themselves. Even if they could, imagine every single double-digit IQ wagie trying to handle a moderately complex task when many don't even know what a file is anymore.

I can't stress enough the scale of this happening.

My son was stranded in ATL Airport coming to El Salvador during this event.

### Michalis — 2024-07-19T22:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/july-19th-2024-cyberattack.29185/post-337351>

Not sure if it was by omission or bad planning or whatever. I am not 100% sure if it was a cyber attack / supply chain attack. I think we will find out soon so this is work in progress. I am well aware about the WEF cyberattack warnings etc etc. Right now I am going to keep an open mind about it.

To Jack's point It is big because you can see the scale of the fuckup if it is used maliciously. Who knows, they might have been testing the waters. As I said I keep an open mind. What happened was benign as far as I am concerned because it can be easily recovered by a tech support guy that gets their hand on the device and can follow 4-5 steps to remove the file.

On anothet note you can do things that can shut down everything. You can literally brick systems and wont be recovered unless they are rebuilt.

Generally I see a lot of incompetence in the field. A lot of cockups happening everywhere and all the time. There are a lot of people who are just not doing their job properly. They don't care. I see this every day and it makes my life harder at work. It also spikes my blood pressure which is not only a blue light issue. Literally people's attitudes making my blood boil. The world would have been a better place if people did their jobs better.

So just a heads up from an SME. Those enterprises that have Intel AMT enabled, (vpro) can remotely connect to the bricked systems and fix them. You can literally mount a USB stick remotely and rebuild the system if you got this enabled. You can even go to the BIOS or windows recovery remotely and over the internet. Intel AMT acts like a server ILO but for end user devices and can be configured with or without user consent depending the deployment scenario. So the back door has been always there. But is an enterprise grade one you can actually use and can be useful at times. I do push for using it as it can save the clients a lot of money and if configured properly it is generally secure. But they don't usually listen
There is another side to the coin too. It has also been used in the past to hijack systems. So just a heads up, this is what I am worried about a lot.
