### drsongihan — 2024-08-02T06:52:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-337978>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=337924)
>
> LESSONS FROM A DECENTRALIZED DONKEY: Embrace the suck in life. It is not about survival of fittest, it is about survival of the wisest.
>
>
>
> One day, a farmer’s donkey fell into a well. The poor animal cried loudly for hours while the farmer tried to figure out a way to get him out. Finally, the farmer decided that the donkey was old and the well was dry and needed to be covered anyway; it wasn’t worth pulling the donkey out of the well.
>
>
>
> He invited all his neighbors to help him. They each grabbed a shovel and began to throw dirt into the well. The donkey realized what was happening and cried out loudly. Then, to everyone’s surprise, he quieted down after a few shovelfuls of dirt.
>
>
>
> The farmer finally looked down into the well and was amazed at what he saw. With each shovelful of dirt, the donkey was doing something incredible: he was shaking off the dirt and stepping on top of it. Very soon, everyone was astonished as the donkey reached the mouth of the well, climbed over the edge, and trotted out.
>
>
>
> Life is going to throw dirt at you, all kinds of dirt. The trick to getting out of the hole is to shake it off and use it to step up. Each of our problems is a step up. We can get out of the deepest holes if we don’t give up.
>
> Use the dirt thrown at you to get ahead!
>
>
>
> Remember the 5 rules to be happy:
>
> 1\. Free your heart from hate.
>
> 2\. Free your mind from distractions.
>
> 3\. Simplify your life.
>
> 4\. Give more and expect less.
>
> 5\. Love more and shake off the dirt, because in this life, you have to be a decentralized solution, not the problem!

Love it Jack. I am embracing exactly that, and love the 5 principles. I don’t know the answer but I have a clarity of mind and feel good about it. My solution may be a slightly different version but your principles that you laid out are key to my approach, and I know I will get there. The mantra that I orient myself by now is love, clarity and decentralized thinking.

PS. Matt. Really appreciate your input. There is some thinking behind my strategy. In my current job I can cruise along, I can simplify life, I can do whatever I want to do, I can teach my students, I can optimize my money and I have flexibility to focus on my son. Next year I can take a sabbatical. I just don’t have sabbatical now. Finding a new job comes with huge overhead and to get the sort of salary and freedom I have in a new job requires way more centralized bs than I am willing to go through. I am unfireable and that is the sort of luxury that is rare to replicate. For long term health I need to optimize money too, but I am convinced that I will be rolling with the punches as they come. I am making pretty dramatic changes in my life right now, but I do that happily, am not panicking and have clarity that I never had until recently thanks to this community and Jack. I am not worried because I know what the direction I should go and what to look for to know whether I am directionally right. It cannot be that the entire world population has to move South to do well. But I do agree we all should be moving towards less populated areas. I also agree with you that lower latitude is ket and my new plan is to request sabbatical (I will get it) and move south for extended periods of time like 1 year. But to request that so early (I am in my new job only 1 year) I need to justify it. If I can find something in El Salvador or Antigua Guatemala, perfect, and if not I have a green oasis in Bangalore inside the IISc campus, at 12the latitude where I can stay 1 year next year. Knowing what is important, having the correct frame of thinking, the directionally correct theory and a community to ask for input is huge even if I don’t know the exact way out of the water well. I remember listening to the psychiatrist and therapists and being so confused and thinking these people seem to not even know where is up and where is down! Now I know where is up and where is down.

### EWO — 2024-08-03T09:50:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338028>

A job can be a golden handcuff.

### EWO — 2024-08-03T10:00:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338029>

Some context; I have 14 y/o twins, 2 dogs, solo parent, homeschooling since 2020, a move to the Gulf Coast was part of our journey. Have been peripherally around here (Jack's work) since apx 2014. I've given up a lot (including income) to devote myself to raising and schooling my kids.

You seem quite blessed with material resources, but you need fewer than you think to materialize what matters.

### drsongihan — 2024-08-03T18:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338044>

> [EWO said:](/goto/post?id=338029)
>
> Some context; I have 14 y/o twins, 2 dogs, solo parent, homeschooling since 2020, a move to the Gulf Coast was part of our journey. Have been peripherally around here (Jack's work) since apx 2014. I've given up a lot (including income) to devote myself to raising and schooling my kids.
>
> You seem quite blessed with material resources, but you need fewer than you think to materialize what matters.

Wisdom and happiness can be found anywhere and in many different forms. Your imagination is your boundary and your world. To me decentralization means you are seeking truth, you are following the light and you work towards being a free thinking and free acting human being that is ready to renovate itself every day. How to get there takes many paths. Any attempt to make that look one way is formulaic and rigid. We need all sorts of people to make life on earth possible. There are jobs, there is passion and there is calling. And for me, my job is a calling to teach and seek truth. If the time comes where it becomes limiting I will change it and anybody should. But if I am so fortunate to have the opportunity to make a difference I will follow that, and the only arbiter of that is the one doing it. I am agreeing with the general idea that we have to orient ourselves towards the sun and nature. I also agree with you that giving up is a very important part of one's journey. What everyone decides to give up and how that looks like is individually different. What is absolutely critical is that you follow your own compass while making sure you are clear eyed and that you constantly question and check your compass. Luckily, the sun can be seen from almost every corner of the world  !

I recently learned about Jainism. Its one of the older form of religion that preceded Budhism. There is a minority in India (still a few Million) that practices Jainism. The Jain protocol looks surprisingly similar to Jack Kruse's Leptin protocol. Get up 30 min before sunrise, be out and worship the sun, be out, eat before sunset, only fire after sunset, don't snack and eat etc Their diet protocol is very different, but remember that this is in India at 15th Latitude with plenty of sun. Still, they eat plenty coconut oil and ghee, but no meat.

The Jain Gurus work around butt naked. Not even a single piece of clothes on them. It is a decades of process to become a Jain Guru. The female version wears white cotton clothes, and the male wears nothing. The whole journey of Jainism is to give up everything, one by one, family, house, possession and finally clothes. So by that standard, any comfort that any of us seek is still superfluous.

### drsongihan — 2024-08-15T17:23:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338448>

Ok here is an update. The past 3 weeks have been very busy. Trying to establish new lifestyle, selling house, emf safing house and organizing a big conference. But here are a few observations from the past few weeks.

Conference at the Rocky Mountains and yet we sat all day in blue light. I tried to open all doors to the coffee break space and was told that I opened the emergency exits. What the hell. I ate lunch on the patio of the dining hall in the glazing sun. Interestingly, few enthusiastically joined. When I came back I was sun-hungry for 2 days and needed to rehab with sun and cold shower. Interestingly I never before even felt such imbalance.

I now got a little camping chair and table that I set up in the courtyard between two buildings. Luckily it’s a nice sunny and green space. Now holding office hours outside and forced my group to a lunch/sun break. One kid who used to refuse to come out now told me she no longer wants to go inside.

Bought body voltmeter and emf meter brands recommended by Anthony’s webpage. I am not sure how to read the tri emf meters yet (need a consult) but I am shocked to the core by the result of the body voltmeter. Almost every single electric device plugged into the wall within 2 meter from me increases body voltage. If everything is plugged out I am at 20-80 mV. If I am outside on grass 10-20 mV. But near my toaster it shoots to 1.5 V and any plugged in light adds easily 0.5 V. But here is the most shocking part. I turned off WiFi and 5G in my ATT fiberoptics gateway device, I.e. turned into an old fashioned modem. Btw it’s not do easy - took me a while to figure out. Now, regardless of the RF effect, my body voltage with just the gateway plugged in went from 20 to maybe 40 mV. That’s fine because I can put that modem further away from us in distance.

But what shocked me was that when I plugged in the Cat8 Ethernet cables I had my body voltage shot up to 4 Volts!!! I tried that experiment 10 times. This is a freaking hazard. Then I got some old Ethernet cables I had and they were fine! So beware that Ethernet connection = good is not correct. I am sure the RF is fine but my body voltage went crazy. Anybody knows how to search for shielded Ethernet cables?

My son: getting there, slowly. Taking WiFi away and turning off data was a huge fight. Humongous. But I did. The Ethernet cable is the only way now to get to internet in our house but I now have to first find shielded Ethernet cables. Also no more charging of phone in bedrooms. Including for me. Charging station is in kitchen that anyway is a bit EMF loaded because of the different devices.

PS1. All lamps add to body voltage. That’s a dilemma with all the IR lamps. Need to get at least 1 EMRTek lamp. I did find that battery powered things are fine. So I have an analogue alarm to take away any excuse needing the phone anywhere near the bed.

PS2. The neutrals in my house are all good except for one outlet that I now taped up. I suppose the ceiling wiring for the overhead lamps are not so bad if my body voltage is 20-30 mV?

### rtd — 2024-08-15T18:13:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338452>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=338448)
>
> Anybody knows how to search for shielded Ethernet cables?

The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) defines industry standards. This includes Ethernet and various cable shielding standards (IEEE Std 1143-YEAR). The more testing, reliability (e.g. aerospace applications) cost more, pay for what you get. My guess is you are looking patch cables.

Here is a low cost Cat8 shielded from Amazon:

[Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/Ethernet-Shielded-Outdoor-Weatherproof-Connector/dp/B07QLD7Y7B?th=1)

Ideally the cable has a product sheet to verify the features. Cat 8 is most likely overkill. You want at least 1000BaseT shielded twisted pair cable.

Here is a link to LCOM Cat 6 double shielded cables. If you look at the product specs, it is 10/100 basedT Ethernet (slower speed). If you call L-Com, see if the have double shielded 1000BaseT Cat6

Alternatively, a good IT or technician person can build custom ethernet cables.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-15T19:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338456>

Cat5 has an all plastic plug/connector Cat6A & Cat8 (forget Cat 7) are shielded & have metal plugs so you can ground your network, removing the electric field.

DrSongihan - When you have time give me a call and I can help with the BVK 972-849-9500 PST

### Mat.I — 2024-08-16T03:52:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338475>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=338448)
>
> I turned off WiFi and 5G in my ATT fiberoptics gateway device,

Make sure you check with your RF meter that WIFI is truly disabled (if you haven't already done this). Check this near the router as in some cases, this will only turn off the network broadcast, however, still be emitting RF from the adaptor in the router.

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=338448)
>
> Anybody knows how to search for shielded Ethernet cables?

Shielded must also be grounded a certain way with the use of (full metal jacks). Maybe a easy way would be handle the grounding at a hub rack/switch/splitter. I'm sure Anthony can help out with this.

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=338448)
>
> All lamps add to body voltage

You can lower this with earthing the lamps.

As for building biology standards - refer to the attached.

All the best.
Mat

I purchased gold shielded ethernet cables, but the body voltage shoots up from 0.5V to 5 V. I bought 4 different gold shielded Cat8 ethernet cables. These products do not what they say they do. I now did purchase specifically EMF-free Ethernet cable where each lead is wrapped by grounding gold, and I am happy to report back. But so far, it looks like the Cable industry is not being checked.
Interestingly, my older, slower, plastic Ethernet cables are fine. They are not shielded, yet the body voltage goes up just a bit like any device does. Not great, but better than the so called shielded ethernet doing the opposite.

What I now ordered, and I will see whether they work are: [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0BTV5ZZ41/ref=ppx\_yo\_dt\_b\_asin\_image\_o00\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0BTV5ZZ41/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_image_o00_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-16T21:11:48+0300

> What I was shocked by was that the gold shielded CAT8 ethernet cables increased the body voltage well beyond anything I have seen in my house, even worse than my un-grounded toaster.

That is because your network is ungrounded. If the network is grounded your BV will go down when you touch the ethernet wire.

### Saichi — 2024-08-16T21:58:13+0300

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-16T22:33:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338519>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=338518)
>
> What filters emi from the router to computer?

If by “EMI” you are referring to “DE” then a “DE” whole house filter would reduce EMI/”DE” on the whole system. Reduce not eliminate. Also if the ethernet network is properly grounded it virtually eliminates the electric field and therefore the “DE” (which is part of the electric field). Touching the ethernet cable will change this.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-18T06:58:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338574>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=338502)
>
> All of my outlets are perfectly grounded except for one that I now taped up. But still, even devices with "grounding" plug will increase your body voltage by at least 200 mV. What I was shocked by was that the gold shielded CAT8 ethernet cables increased the body voltage well beyond anything I have seen in my house, even worse than my un-grounded toaster.
> Will call [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/)

Ungrounded = "antenna" / "conductor" with nowhere for the EF to go. It must be grounded otherwise it will create more issues. Hence why BV goes up.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-18T07:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338575>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=338518)
>
> What filters emi from the router to computer?

Depending what frequencies you are dealing with, and most of this can be dealt with via appropriate "clean" low impedance grounding. Good home networks should be shielded and grounded all cables should use full metal jacks note, with RJ45's you will have some leakage from the breach in the 360 degree "faraday" to the points of contact. Network switches / patch panels should also be shielded and grounded. There is something I have been researching in this field that commonly gets over looked with the method of grounding that I wish to experiment with and test first prior to discussing it. In my opinion, grounds & bonds from LV - Power & ELV - Comms should be kept separate. In AUS we have a cabling code that also addresses this segregation requirement.

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T21:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338590>

Thanks for all the wonderful discussion! After a phone call with Anthony that was very insightful, and then further poking and experimenting with my body voltage meter and electric field meters, I think I figured it out.

My house has very good ground. That's not the problem. When I have everything unplugged body voltage is between 50 and 300 mV. When I turn off breaker, it is 30-50 mV. I suspect if I turn on the kill switch and turn off EVERYTHING in my house, my body voltage will be 0 mV.

My environment's low frequency EMF is also very good. When I stand on my patio or grass, then my body EMF is basically 0 mV.

However, as some of you were suspecting, the Gateway modem itself is not grounded -- dang ATT. But there are solutions. I will ground my modem through ethernet to my house ground. Thanks Anthony for this idea. There are several ways to do this. One approach I will test is to get a Surge protector, ground it to my house ground, and that should suck up the floating EMF. These devices are meant to protect our devices from lightning. But we can use the same principle to ground out everything and minimize nnEMF.

What is still true is that you cannot trust any of the "shielded" ethernet cables since they seem to carry much more EMF than any other electric cable I have tested so far. In other words, if your device is not grounded, then Ethernet cables can be REALLY HAZARDOUS. I measured a body EMF of 13 V consistently. That means I can totally be a battery to drive a light bulb! I should do a Science experiment. Have an ungrounded Modem, and then touch a lead to an LED with a wet finger and the other lead to the ground and see whether I can lit it! Anyhow 13 V dropped consistently and repeatedly to below 0.6 V when I disconnect the Ethernet at the modem, and then when I go out to my yard and back, the level is back down around 0.2 V. Plug in the Ethernet again, my body voltage shoots up to 11-13 Volt. Not even my un-grounded toaster did that.

While I wait for my surge protector device or ethernet to ground cable, I learned of an intermediate trick from Anthony. When I plug in my MacBook computer to the outlet using the GROUNDED power cord, WITH the ethernet cable on where my body voltage was at 11-13 V, this value dropped to 0.6 V just by using my MacBook with the power cable plugged in, but only if its the grounded cable and from my understanding, only reliable with a Mac.

The morale of the story for me is that I have now thoroughly experienced that we have to be experimentalist and measure, NOT imagine or guess. What I found was not what I would have guessed. I also learned that the body voltage meter is an extremely good measure because it actually measures what your body or skin is picking up.

Today, I also had the pleasure of meeting up with another member from my neighborhood, Gene, and had a wonderful convo! Based on his experience, I will try out the Trendnet that feeds the internet through the outlets. Since the Chicago outlets are so well grounded, I would trust internet coming through my outlets, but only after I measure my body voltage and electric field. I was starting to get really annoyed by the Ethernet cable salad in my house, especially with an ungrounded modem!

Thank you community! This is the best group. Depending on what your environment is, most of us have experienced being called "crazy" by our loved ones and friends. This journey is one, at least for me and I think for most of us, where we have to choose: embrace the inconvenient truth. But after all, it's not that inconvenient, and it's a lot of fun to learn new things. Geez. I am telling you. A body voltage of 11 Volt!! We are being electrified without knowing it.

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T21:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-338591>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=338511)
>
> Ethernet grounding adapter works but ethernet cables still transmit loads of emi from the router.

I will report back on it. My suspicion is that it might now. I thought so too, but seeing how my body voltage dropped by just grounding my computer through the outlet makes me think that if I ground my modern, the shielded ethernet cable should be fine.

I think what it is is that the shielded, and hence metal plated, ethernet cables are worse offenders if the modem is not grounded. My old plastic ethernet cables were less bad. Once my modem is grounded, my guess is that the floating EMF will go away, but I will let you know.

Also, I will now rather connect my modem to a surge protector and then from there to the outlet via a grounded plug, and then use the trendnet to get internet through shorter, shielded, ethernet cables.

This journey is one, at least for me and I think for most of us, where we have to choose: embrace the inconvenient truth. But after all, it's not that inconvenient, and it's a lot of fun to learn new things. Geez. I am telling you. A body voltage of 11 Volt!! We are being electrified without knowing it.

Important thing to understand is where the fields / interference are originating from. Example you may solve an issue via grounding paths further down stream from the issue e.g. if you are getting leakage / EMI / return path issues from AC/DC power supplies (say the one powering an router you could still address this down stream e.g. through multiple ethernet cable bonds to grounds or straight at the router via the DC jack 0v and bond to ground. You would just check polarity to make sure the DC jack is wired the common way (e.g. + inner pin & 0V outer). I only know this from my own experimentation and having applied this out in the field for other electronic applications. What I'm saying is understand where your issues are coming from. E.g. you may even have issues with coax cables (TV or cable internet) causing EF & MAG issues, if you just isolate your power, yes great for sleeping, but not great for day to day exposure. Just note BV is measuring pressure differential between you and the earth only (not current flow or contact currents) e.g. local effect of EF charge on skin but obviously dose not measure current flow. E.g. wet skin = low impedance, dry skin higher impedance & certain frequencies will breakdown the impedance of the skin irrespective of conditions. This does not tell us how many electrons are getting into the body via the skin. I have seen people do this via electrode pads on skin or just grabbing the leads to measure in the uA current range with a Fluke 287 or 289. Don't think you are in the clear just because you have measured BV, no doubt it will have impacts due to pressure differentials on your body, however the so called "safe" levels may not be subject to the actual current associated with it, hence the reason not to ground yourself inside with any power on. Some examples of this.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Hence why the ultimate solution may to eliminate this all together as best a possible via whole house DC

This youtube video puts this in context in terms of micro amps of what a ungrounded vs grounded laptop note the 0.7 uA measurement (likely from background) -

### drsongihan — 2024-09-08T08:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-339394>

Thanks to the wonderful recommendation of this Forum members, I finished reading the book "The Invisible Rainbow" by Arthur Firstenberg. What an amazing book that is so well researched, an amazing historical account, comprehensive and enlightening. He does not focus directly on sunlight, but considering sunlight as part of the electromagnetic spectrum, this book does an amazing job in explaining the well documented, and largely forgotten (by centralized science and practitioner), history of the dramatic consequences of nnEMF on human health. From neurological conditions, diabetes, heart conditions, and the Influenza virus, all being an "electrical" illness. I highly recommend this book, I sent this to a few friends, and this answers in principle the question I posed above about the impact of nnEMF better than any one publication could possibly could. This book was published in 2020 so it would be missing the aftermath of Covid, but Arthur Firstenberg already makes a compelling connection between major steps in electrification to the occurrence of Influenza, the Spanish flu, the Hong Kong flue etc, so I am sure he will be looking into this direction. One difference, however, is that the Covid flu was likely lab-generated, so perhaps different from MERS etc? So whether a major electrification event was needed, or whether the electrification is perhaps the explosion of LED lights and 3G/4G/5G network or the installation and expansion of smart meters is to be seen.

My son's trajectory is positive, but progress is slow.

My observation is that:
1) Introducing as much sunlight as I can decreased his anxiety attacks. The fact that he even had developed such condition was really strange because before Covid, he never had anxiety attacks.
2) Keeping to a strict (but modified) Circadian schedule and complete blackout at night eliminated mood swings. At this point, he would be no longer diagnosed as bipolar, not because he does not have it, but because the DSM criteria is looking for non-sensical patterns that not everyone has. I know for myself he still has the condition. Anyhow, with this rhythm and only taking lithium, his sleep issues are gone.

However, no mood swings does not mean = good mood or positive or good energy. His mood, energy and attitude is improving only very slowly, but it is improving.

3) Eliminating Wifi, cleaning my house of nnEMF and eliminating narrow-blue light altogether is improving sleep and mood, but as I said the change is very slow. The EMF polution that we still have is cellular data, and once I read the "Invisible Rainbow" it's clear that there are certain nnEMF solution that envelops the entire earth and we cannot avoid them anyway.

One major problem I see is low dopamine, low energy, negative attitude and also low immunity. It is interesting that his sleep issues stabilized quickly, making me think his melatonin production is okay. I did notice that opening window and turning on red-light early morning impacts his sleep. But still, melatonin seems okay.

In Arthur Firstenberg's book, the early discovery of the condition neurasthenia caught my attention, and how to this day, this condition is accepted and diagnosed as an EMF illness in Russia, Asia and the Eastern block, but not in the Western part of the world. Sigmund Freud is largely responsible by renaming much of neurasthenia as "anxiety neurosis" and hence denying environmental factors as cause, but rather "a weak mind" b.s. Somehow in that context, Firstenberg talks about a porphyrin disease that used to be correctly diagnosed, then for decades under diagnosed and is slowly becoming recognized again that affects 5-10% of people. It originates from a broken cytochrome C or cytochrome oxidase malfunction that the ends up generating the empty porphyrins and then the heme filled porphyrins etc at wrong timings or out of sync or something like that. If somebody has this condition, one ends up having a condition in which we accumulate more heavy metals AND Zinc. I did not quite understand why this is, but something to do with the liver is working too hard to compensate for the dysfunctional cytochrome's in our mitochondria. People with this condition can have Zinc and metal toxicity, contrary to common belief that everyone tends to be Zinc deficient. Problem is that blood serum Zinc level is not a good indicator because the toxic Zinc still achieves homeostasis in the blood, but accumulates in the brain somewhere. I then looked up metal detox agents, and almost every review says something about much improved energy and mood, and it often is caused by mold toxicity. I am wondering whether anybody knows something about this, and how to figure out whether this is one more facet in my son's health.

I am trying to help him build up melanin and vitamin D and all the good stuff from UV. Since daytime sunlight is not enough here at the 43 Latitude, I am additionally using a UVA and UVB in combination with IR/far-IR lamps during late morning or day, but my observation is that this takes a long time.

The thing is that his vitamin D level is not as terrible as I would have imagined (40 before summer started, but I have to test him now after all we tried), he is tan, his eye sight is excellent and no myopia, and he apparently generates enough melatonin.

And yet, something is zapping his energy and immunity in serious ways, while anxiety and mood swings recovered much faster with my remedies that I outlined. It's probably because the sun therapy requires more intense sun, better sun and longer durations, but I am also wondering whether he has Zinc/metal toxicity?

The question is what is the condition under which such metal toxicity is built up? In his case, it cannot be just exposure. But rather, something about his system is broken. He got the Covid vaccine and a booster in 2020/21. Meanwhile, our house in CA had huge radioactivity with radon level of 40. Sounds like a horrible combination to me, but I am looking for specific mechanisms so I can research it and see how I can reverse what happened then.

What is the factor X that is blocking him the most from re-gaining energy, immunity and dopamine/motivation?
\- More sunlight + lower latitude (I am sure of it. Working on spending time south this Winter)
\- Minimize EMF (I did it largely. He does look at the computer, with ethernet cable, no Wifi, grounded, no body current etc, but he still does)
\- Factor X: my feeling is that there is something more that is impaired.
\- I did notice that somehow fresh berries have a very positive effect on him. Weird, but the effect was very strong. I was just a bit puzzled because I did operate under the premise that light has to come first, but maybe he is just very deficient in something.

On the first point, there is a lot of compelling evidence that the Covid-19 virus escaped from the Wuhan virology lab (sponsored by US). These viruses do not easily jump from bat to human by natural mutation, and the death numbers from Covid-19 was definitely not the pattern of a common cold. Saying that Covid-19 was like a common cold endangers the entire community of decentralized thinkers as being coo coo and not credible. You have to be precise when making claims.

I have never heard Jack saying that you can chelate the "jab". How would that work? What would you be chelating? You can chelate metals with porphyrin derivatives. What are you talking about?

On the last one, also disagree. In biology, there is no "absolute". Our body has thresholds. When Anthony suggests less than 1V during day and less than 0.2 V during night, these are thresholds recommended by building biologists. Most computers on a battery and the Mac with a grounded charging cable measures less than 0.2 V. In fact, if you have good ground in your house (which I do) then you can in principle ground yourself by touching the Mac. I have measured it, and I go by experiments.

On the question whether even more minuscule amounts of electricity and other environmental factors could affect our biology? Of course. But at some level, this becomes unknowable and results in people living in fear. By that standard, Firstenberg makes a good case that certain electric effects can be sensed around the entire globe. These are not effects that we can shield ourselves from, as long as we are still using internet and relying on technology.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-08T23:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-339414>

So as not to set people up for “failure“, disappointment and worry we give people the Body Voltage (BV) target from the Building Biology Institute (BBI) of less that 1,000mV during the day and less than 100mV at night - “slight concern”. The BBI “no concern” is less than 10mV at night, something in this modern world I very rarely see. I would rather people feel good about 50mV than worry about 20mV.

Working with chronic EHS people, who are far more willing to make the necessary life style changes and spend more money, lower readings are possible.

My latest enlightenment story strangely came from the book "Invisible Rainbow": I am thinking my son has metal toxicity, in addition to the other burdens. The Covid-19 vaccine, nnEMF modulating the BBB, potentially heavy metal in the vaccines and other stuff, and my CA house being basically a mini-LINAC with ground radon level of 40 picoCurie per whatever unit (the toxicity level being at 4) my thinking is that there is no way that he does NOT have metal / radical / toxin poisoning when his BBB was leaky, when his immune system is impacted, got the jab multiple times AND exposed to sky-high radioactivity.

I started with a clinoptilolite zeolite + bentonite detox for him and I (I always apply the same protocol on myself) a few days ago, and it looks as if there is a positive effect already. After reading and researching, I feel like all of us should do a clinoptilolite zeolite detox. Will report back.

The positive effect must be because EDTA, or better the much gentler clinoptilolite zeolite "clay", chelates metals (lead, mercury, etc) that many of us have in our fatty tissues and, in those with a leaky blood brain barrier, in our brains. Chelating metals will have a positive effect on folks with leaky brain, which is aggravated by inflammation, nnEMF etc, and those with a jab injury.

However, that does not mean EDTA is chelating "the jab". If DNA fragments from the jab get incorporated into your DNA, there is nothing EDTA can do. At that point, you can hope for really healthy mitochondria that will turn over vigorously or just hope you are lucky. The really dangerous vaccine injury is caused by the residual DNA fragments that cannot be chelated away.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-16T16:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-339627>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=339622)
>
> I also love learning from your journal.
>
> How is the zeolite on the stomach? An MD 1y ago recommended me zeolite for EHS withouth recommending a specific one - some poor quality zeolites contain toxins I read. He also recommended me melatonin. Stopped that. he explained nothing and was all small talk.
>
> As I suspect stomach ulcers - I wonder if zeolite will be too hard. I am trying to find an MD who does an H. Pylori blood/stool/breath test. Struggling with the MD's gatekeeper secretary :s
> Also I am nt sure of metal poisoning but I have some amalham fillings in 2 teeth and one hurts sometimes but all dentists say there's nothing. May have inhaled mercury from broken thermometer and bulbs and who knows what's falling from the air (many chem/contrails).
>
> Would you/anyone recommend any test to check metal toxicity - prior to trying zeolite? The German podcaster BIO360 also recommends zeolite.
>
> Good healing for you and your son. xxx

Of course I have to be careful and say that I am only sharing my journal and what I learned.

And I have to also repeat the mantra "redox before detox". Because otherwise whatever led to the toxin accumulation will just continue.

Having said that I can say that my extensive research seems to suggest that zeolites is the gentlest way to detox and there is nearly no contraindication as long as you use a clean source of Clinoptilolite. EDTA is a stronger, more indiscriminate chelator that one should not use for daily maintenance and without a doctor's supervision. Chlorella, cilantro etc alone dislodged the metal without a mechanism of capturing and removing them. Active charcoal is also more indiscriminate and does not have the same therapeutic effect

In contrast, zeolite is a cage mineral that contains sodium or potassium and will gently exchange out other positively charged species that are "free" and larger. Excess Zn2+, heavy metal anyway and some radical species ... It cleans positively charged "free" molecules and ions that we are not supposed to have in excess in free form by capturing them in exchange of its own sodium and cation, while these clay particles are excreted out of the body. Zelites are said to clean at the cellular level, but I am not sure how that would work since the particulates are not necessarily nanometer scale, even though the cages are in the Angstrom scale. I can also confirm that it is gentle on the stomach, by reports and my own experience. Clay have been used by ancient civilization and cultures to have healing power, and sick people ate specific clay. It is thought to be a specific zeolite that did the magic.

This is the one my son and I are trying: [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0D1V4BFQS/ref=ppx\_yo\_dt\_b\_asin\_image\_o08\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0D1V4BFQS/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_image_o08_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
(Notice that this one has a few other ingredients including chlorella, but since zeolite can capture what is dislodged, in combination this combo should work well.)
This is one that are used for kids and should be gentle, and easily doable: [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00I2LYBKE/ref=ppx\_yo\_dt\_b\_asin\_image\_o08\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00I2LYBKE/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_image_o08_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
(This one only has mainly zeolite for detox, and has additional vitamin B12 etc)

P.S. For full disclosure, my son and I have stopped taking supplements except for DHA, and now this one for a trial.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-21T02:42:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-339776>

I just have to express my love and gratitude to this forum. I only "discovered" Jack in June this year, but I think I was ready because one podcast immediately induced a tsunami in me and nothing is left standing in terms of how I think about the world, life, health and science.

My son is coming home, he is waking up to be himself again, he is smiling and talking, he is expressing his thoughts and functioning again. I am very aware that its end of September only, and the season change will be felt around mid to end of October. I am paying attention. We are living a tight "redox" protocol, and most recently added a "detox" protocol. It's as if something (e.g. metals) was clogging his brain, and that is now slowly melting away, while his redox allows him to regenerate and build something up. I am aware that I am extremely lucky that we found a bottleneck so quickly, but this will certainly become our lifestyle going forward. 5 months ago he was on 4+ medication and miserable and a shell of a person. Today, I removed all meds except for lithium. I still have to weather another winter, and observe how good his bioelectricity and molecular clocks are before I can remove or reduce lithium, but lithium is not a medicine that generates dependencies or reprograms your receptors. 5 months ago, he was catatonic and explosive/angry, had mood swings and was unable to be among people. 5 months ago, he had missed 1 year of school. Now, he goes to school every day on his bike, and gets the morning and evening sun, and has no mood swings. He started to chat with me for extended times -- unthinkable before. He was even sharing with me his political view, and I was glad to see that he was putting his own thoughts together, and it happened to be opposite to what our family's original political leanings were, and more in line with my current thinking. I never said anything about politics to him. He is becoming a thinking human!! He is doing his school work, he leaves the phone and computer faithfully in the kitchen ... well, I give him not much choice since there is only two ethernet connections in my house now. But he is buying in.

Seeing him smile, talk and opine and see him woken up is priceless. I usually don't share photos, but let me do that today. One from the sunrise walk from this morning and one from my son doing his homework in the red lit room yesterday evening.

I have no illusion that my journey is done. There is more work to do, the journey continues, many bumps will be on the way and we will likely face new and other crisis. But I am confident that I can figure things out by alining with nature and by continuing to learn how Mother Nature works.

Thanks forum, and thanks Jack Kruse.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-21T04:33:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-339783>

>
> Always looking at raising your natural detox pathways before doing some supplemental detox. Rule #1 here is redox before detox.

Agreed completely. My mantra too. But for accuracy sake, zeolite is a specify type of natural clay that was discovered even by ancient civilization to be healing when they eat specific kind of “dirt”. And unlike supplements like melatonin or other natural products, it does not alter the biochemical homeostasis, and it is only effective if something was “broken” in you so that your brain soaked up a lot of metal that it’s not supposed to. That’s just hard to clean up fast naturally. My friend who has tinnitus thinks it’s helping her too. But, I need to conclude my response by saying let’s not forget that this is my n=1 journey. I am sharing for inspiration, not to advocate for a specific approach, and certainly redox before detox.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-21T18:08:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-339798>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=339795)
>
> My understanding of the detox process is that you can actually make your situation worse with out the natural pathways up and still living in a shitty environment . "that's just hard to clean up naturally" sounds like a snake oil pitch of someone who doesn't know better.

Peter, I know your response comes from your experience and good thoughts and intentions to help and make the world a better place, but I want to remind you that each of us are n=1. Wisdom and truth still lies in each of us, each of us are on a learning journey and nobody, including Jack, is above anybody's individual wisdom and truth. My n=1 journey is true for me, and that's that. I made a big point emphasizing that I am not advocating for a specific approach, though I personally think metal toxicity that many of us accumulate is a blind spot of this forum. My miraculous experience is what it is, and I am hoping to inspire others to continue on their journey however long it takes. Mine took 4 years, and still counting. I cannot unsee or inexperience what I saw and experience, but I will make sure to honestly report throughout the winter.

About shitty places. A shitty place for one person might not be a shitty place for another person. Everyone's health is different, everyone's haplotype, epigenetic is different and local EMF cleanliness is vastly different. I too thought I have to move to closer to the equator, and I was planning on it, but I could not make that happen. So we mitigated at home, and the suburb of Chicago has some benefits that I did not think of before. The number of sun days are actually wonderful, the air is clean, the ground is well done, grass everywhere to ground and the lake is right there for everyone to walk or drive to and jump into the water. The winter will come, and that will be our challenge, but I am bio-hacking with light when natural light is not enough, and flying to Mexico to refuel and will embrace the cold and outdoors in the winter. If Chicago is a shitty place that is impossible to stay healthy, then the entire Northern Europe should migrate down and away since Chicago is a whole 10 latitude lower than Northern Europe, and has more sun days than e.g. England, Germany, Netherland and Denmark combined. If everyone moved towards the equator we would be destroying the livelihood of those who are living near the equator by coming with more money, means and what not. I am convinced, and I agree, that those of us who are very sick, or too sick, or exceptionally sensitive like the canary in the coal mine should move to near the equator to get healthy. It's easier and faster and necessary. Put your oxygen mask on first, for sure. If I had been able to do that, I might not have needed the detox strategy, but where I live, I needed to accelerate the recovery and it worked. To be clear, I agree, many detox approaches are dangerous, perhaps you are referring to it in general terms. They dislodge the metal and then there is no mechanism to clean up. The body had tucked it away in a safe place, and then the detox just uproots that without removing it. But this is where zeolite is a very special kind because it scoops it up gently and excretes. I think many people--not everyone--can find wisdom and health in many places. Not in the middle of a city, not on the 10th floor of an Apt complex and not near a huge transformer of a power line, not near a cell phone tower, and not where too many people live in stone's throw from you, but in many places. There is one universal truth, but none of us know what that looks like since the universe is vast. We have not even touched on healing frequencies, plasma and other forces that play a role. There is so much more and there is a lot of learning to do. But I am convinced that nature gave us a myriad of tools to deal with danger and illness, even the manmade ones (see the concluding remarks of the Invisible Rainbow) and we just have to keep listening, revising our view and keep renovating. I will keep you posted.

### drsongihan — 2024-09-21T18:20:22+0300

Agreed. I just see that the Equinox is tomorrow--yikes. I read QE 47 & 48 twice, but I think I have to read it again now that I have gained a bit more understanding. It was very dense, so each time I read I get 40% out of it. For some reason, those with SAD have a specific date when they crash, and those dates range from Oct 1 to Oct 30. I am very alert, and will keep you all posted if and when we survive October, or if things dip how bad they dip. One thing I am insisting on is that my son takes his bike with thick tires to school every day, rain or shine, and the only exception will be when the roads are icy. Side note: it's astounding how narrow minded some of the folks in the Chicago northern suburbs (its a well-off snobby area) are. B/c my son is one of very few kids who drive a relatively long way on the street to school (he does not go to the neighborhood school, but a private one further away where we can insist on our own rules to some extent) there is no day that does not pass that a pedestrian, a driver or a cyclist yells at him "drive on the walkway!" or "drive on the street!", depending on who yells. Some people are so dopamine-deficient that a kid on the bike angers them. Geez. Anyway, it's a good 20 min bike ride, so the hope is that the sun rays together with the cold temperature will be a big boost.

>
> Wonderful news- lovely to read the positive responses mentally and physically in your son. Curious to hear if you ever did any tests to look at his metal and/or mineral metabolism prior to starting to binders? No pressure to share- just interested in these things.

Thank you Chloe. I purposefully did not test for the following reason. Only acute metal toxicity and only of specific heavy metals show up in tests. If positive, it's certainly bad and I would have done the detox. But the body knows how to protect itself, so it accumulates metals in sneaky fatty places to protect you. Of course in the long run, these pockets still make you sick but they don't show up in tests. Also, metal does not just mean mercury or cadmium. There are myriads of other metals, and even Zn2+ in free excess can be toxic due to something else that's wrong. We are just not supposed to have free cations or cationic radicals freely in our brain tissue, and the idea is that if those accumulated over time, we can help by detoxing. If someone has a perpetual imbalance, no detox in the world can help since that imbalance needs to be addressed first. Also, my son is doing so much better but I want him to focus on "living" and not bother him with more blood tests of which he has done so many. He has gone through so many different treatments that I am trying to minimize any more testing and lab visits. We are so done with going anywhere near the hospital more than necessary  ! I know you can relate. Anyhow, I just knew that a negative test does not mean anything, and therefore I went with the "trial and error" approach, knowing that the choice of material is least intrusive. If somebody wants to be absolutely sure, I would not even take the one I took that still had multiple factors in them, but simply take clean cliniptilolite zeolite, nothing else. Disclaimer: n=1 journey!

### drsongihan — 2024-09-30T16:53:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340200>

I listened to several podcasts of Jordan Peterson and guests. While generally I liked his clarity, and the fact that he is speaking his mind and seeking truth irrespective of repercussions, I realized that he really is missing on one important truth, especially as he attempts to broach the topic of god and evolution, spirituality and biology. He argues that they are connected--and I agree--but he misses out on starting from the ground truth of nature to begin with. He reminds me of a smart men with a lot of potential, but who is stumbling along in the dark without "light" to get to the goal. He will never get there. He will never help anybody for real, unless he gets the other part right. I emailed him with the following content. Maybe his ears will perk up or maybe it will just land in his dumpster. We will see. Really, without at least 1 teacher in the Peterson Academy, there is no point of this attempt to re-do education. If anybody has his personal email, let me know. He has a big reach, and therefore getting his attention would matter.

"
Dear Dr. Peterson

I am usually a fan of yours, and I like your clarity and intellect. This is important to emphasize so that you know where I come from. I am more in agreement with you than not. I am even in agreement on the topic of politics that I would have never thought that I could be possibly agreeable. The same way you pivoted towards the lesser of the "evil" so to speak, I did the same, shockingly. However, there was one argument I heard you and Bill Maher discussed in a recent podcast on affirmative action. Generally, I agree that affirmative action is a flawed approach. So, let's not talk about HOW we get to somewhere, but let's walk about the WHAT and the WHY.

Clinician and Medical Practice: The issue is that you do not understand enough biology to know that clinical care is and has to be VERY different for men, women, white-skinned person and dark-skinned person simply because of the dramatic differences in biology. This has nothing to do with opinion. The healthy standard for vitamin D level for a white and dark person is different, different for men and women and the perceived differences in pain tolerance in minorities can cost lives. There are a long list of biological reasons why clinical care has to be adjusted to the different race and gender, and that this is not a minor issue. This well-known concept, however, will never get traction unless doctors represent the community. A black doctor is more likely to be sensitive to black peoples need, and a woman is more likely to be educated on women health. The current clinical standard is more optimized for white men, and that is not a matter of opinion. The reason why it is important to have a representation in ethnicity and skin color and gender among doctors that roughly represent the population is simply prosperity and health for all. It affects everyone. Having a healthier black population is important not only for the black community but also for the white population because at the end resources are shared, for better or for worse. In an ideal world where the medical system is NOT broken, it should not matter what the color of the skin of the doctor is. But in the current "Middle Ages" we are in, it matters.

Finally, when it comes to many other topics that you discuss, while I agree with the clarity of arguments and I truly enjoyed the podcast with Bret Weinstein and Heather Haying from former Evergreen, there is generally something missing in your conceptual understanding, and that is nature and biology. I agree with Bret. At the end, there is one truth. The truth converges, and whether you call that god, spirit or the universe is not that important at our current level of understanding. Once science advances far enough, it turns out phenomena that were previously considered "magic" can be explained by science or spirituality, but that does not make it is any less magical and spiritual. For example, feeling spiritually connected, the idea of telepathy, the experience of something bigger than yourself ... is all real, scientific and spiritual at once... so once we get to that point, god and science is all the same, and life is divine.

However, neither you nor I and nor anybody will get to that complete enlightenment in our lifetime. However, on our way there, until the two are united, we need both, the compass of spirituality and the compass of nature/biology because currently we do not understand how they are the same. If not, you will wobble through life, not understanding why you and your loved ones get inexplicably sick or why you cannot rescue and help your loved ones as effectively as you wish through their mental health challenges.

The compass you are missing is the compass of nature/biology/universe. You are not considering the divine and magical nature of Circadian Biology, the importance of embracing the whole spectrum of photons of the sun and the ground that earth provides. The best person to follow to quickly get up to speed on that is Dr. Jack Kruse. He is much more "extreme" so to speak in his clarity and at times more belligerent sounding than you, and he would have gotten canceled well before you, but he found his own smart way to have a big influence, including on RFK, Trump and I hope also Musk. Nobody understands the connection between spirituality and nature/biology better than Jack Kruse. I have a 14-year-old son that the doctors diagnosed with severe bipolar depression, while as you know BPD is thought to be not curable and treated with heavy-duty medication because lithium is often not enough. I have diligently gone through Centralized Psychiatry and Psychology and Therapy for 3.5 years, and 6 months ago decided to fire them all because I concluded Psychiatry and Psychology is broken and failed us completely. Why? Because they are stuck in the middle ages. It's no different than the doctors of the middle ages bleeding people or poking holes into the head and pretending to know something about how to heal the body, if in reality they had no idea or not enough. Today's Psychiatrists and Psychologists don't know anything, or not enough to be relevant. Understanding one receptor at a time is not understanding the brain. But understanding negative thought patterns and how to undo them is not any closer to healing, and not enough to heal a broken brain. I still find your teaching very enlightening, but most professionals in your realm are not anywhere close to be clear as you are, nor are they curious. Anyhow, I decided to take my son's health into my hand. With a heavy-duty diagnosis, apparently not curable, on 4 psychiatric medications and completely out of control in his behavior, not going to school, AND with a significant risk for suicide, heart disease to neurodegeneration, what do I have to loose? 4 months later, I got my son back. It was actually simple. I strictly followed Dr. Jack Kruse's protocol. I quit all medicine except for lithium because lithium makes sense. We followed the strict circadian biology rhythm, paid huge attention to sunlight and how/when we eat. My son is back to his "normal" self. No psychoanalysis and therapy in the world, none of the treatment for depression that you are outlining would have helped him in his acute state because initially it was a much more heavily a "biological" problem. The problem is that when the brain is inflamed, behavior will be modified, but the treatment first has to treat the inflammation, not the behavior. Once the person is healthier, I 100% believe in the various therapeutic modality you are discussing, but unless you embrace the power of the sun you will never treat depression in any meaningful way. The key point is HOW to treat brain inflammation correctly has nothing to do with clinical practice and is NOT contradicting spirituality. The only way to treat brain inflammation is to follow the divinity of nature and sun. We woke up with the sunrise. I opened the window for him to see the morning sunrays. I maximized sun exposure. We ate during the day and outside. We grounded. We paid attention to non-processed food, and I made his room pitch dark at night the way our ancestors slept. I removed Wifi and all electrical devices that were inducing 60 Hz current in our bodies. I detoxed him with zeolites from clay, and we have windows and doors open as much as we can, I brought nature into our house and ourselves into nature as much as we can, and I force him to take a long bike ride to school and back (he now quite enjoys it), rain or shine. That's it. At that level, spirituality and nature converges, but because your and everyone's understanding is incomplete, you cannot apply spirituality randomly by a gut feeling UNLESS you are truly connected to the universe and know the truth because your intuition will guide you the right way. Till then, you have to understand and follow Nature's guide IF you really want to help someone and get tangible results, not just feel better about yourself. You actually have to follow the ONE truth about how nature works, the same way that there is one spirit or good or universe. The first step has to be to worship the sun and to respect circadian biology and nature. After that comes therapy, meditation, mindfulness, nutrition and exercise, not other way around.

What has this anything to do with the topic of clinical practice and representation that I started with? It has in that the way you and Bill Maher talked about it, I immediately knew that you do not understand the divinity of the human body and health. Well, in an ideal world once doctors understand how to heal, it does not matter what the skin color or gender of the doctor is. But till then, there is a greater chance to get health to more people, which ultimately benefits everyone, if the doctors at least come from the community and represent the community. Not only politics, but medical care HAS to be local. On the other hand, Dr Kruse will say that the entire Clinical education and practice has to be blown apart. I agree. Except there is one thing that is not replaceable. Students willing to go through the rigor of medical school are needed, just through a different kind of curriculum. We cannot make stuff up. That's the danger of quacks and functional medicine if people who know a sliver of truth sell it as a whole truth, and if people who have not gone through the rigor of training think they know everything or enough. A rigorous education is needed to have a chance at getting to the truth in a comprehensive way, and I believe this is why you are building the Peterson academy. It's just that I highly highly highly recommend to add a teacher on Circadian biology that Dr. Jack Kruse recommends.

I hope I am making sense. MANY guests you had on your podcasts are connected to Dr. Jack Kruse, and the most important one is RFK Junior. Once you see the divinity of life through the lens of mother nature, your teaching will be much more effective and your argument will become much more complete.

Best wishes,

**Songi Han**
"

### drsongihan — 2024-10-01T06:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340213>

A few notes for my journal. Things are stable but I am acutely aware that the natural light is dimming while our work and school schedule won’t be adjusted in the Winter. So I am watching my son.

I am increasingly augmenting with biohacking. Now sunrise is at 6.45 and will eventually be 7.30 in deep winter. I turn on infrared + far infrared panel and open window at 6 am no matter the season since school start time is not changing. Breakfast at 7 am. Windows are all open, but now I add Infrared + UV panel on in breakfast area at 7.30 am. We already had squirrel and other creatures visit us indoors or the wind knocking down indoor things by leaving all windows and doors open, but oh well. I am wasting a lot of energy with the light panels, but then saving a lot of energy by not heating and nearly no light after 7 pm. We stop eating at 6 pm at the latest, but try to stop at 5 pm if possible. Eating as much fat and proteins and winter food as possible, my son drinking deuterium depleted water and still take detox zeolites. He bikes to school at 8 am, school start is 8.30, so even in the deep winter the bicycle path in morning and evening is when sun is out. Hopefully that sun exposure plus cold will help him. I am no longer ever telling him to wear any jackets. Funny how life has changed. Now I am relieved when he seems to bike in the cold. Not sure what else I can do. The new biohack I added is the UV light at 7.30 am for about 30 min (always simultaneously with infrared and far infrared) and again at 3.45 pm - 4.30 pm after school. After 4.30, only red light panel on, while all windows and doors are open.

My son is doing well but I need to wait through Nov. Stable, content, functional and better energy … but in my opinion his dopamine level is still low, and still lower agency than I want to see in a healthy child. I am not wishing for a miracle, I am infinitely grateful for where we landed so quickly already, but rather I am jotting down notes and journaling for myself. I am observing that in my sons case seeing an increase in his dopamine level to increase his “agency” is probably the aspect that will take the longest. My guess is that at the minimum a full long summer in a good environment or maybe many more years. It feels like transforming a zombie monster into a human. I now have a functional, but subdued, kid, and my job is to make sure his wings can grow.

My kid did not have the luxury of growing up like I did, virtually without technology (not even TV) and mostly outdoors until age 26 because I did not know better to provide a better environment for him. It will take a while to rebuild his solar panels to be robust.

### drsongihan — 2024-10-01T13:23:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340216>

PS: He goes to a private school now. First one in our family, but it’s necessary so that we have flexibility, so I can take him out of school and a lot less rules to follow and a lot less compliance requests. The public school in this area in Illinois is the worse I have seen, despite being rated the best in the country by criteria such as test scores. They are worse than in CA, because they are all about being in lockstep, following rules, compliance and all indoors all day. In Southern California, maybe because there is not much money or whatever, school is more chaotic in a good way, kids from preschool to high school are virtually outdoors all day while it’s sunny all year long. The classrooms are makeshift kind of 1 story building barracks, all doors open because it’s too hot, there are not enough seats in the cafeteria so kids eat lunch in the yard sitting on the ground, and the gym building is so lousy that kids do PE outdoors and the amphitheater not big enough so all performances are also fully or half outdoors. The entire school district where we used to live, starting this fall, banned cell phone and Apple Watch from school or kids have to park the phone in a “cell phone hotel” during school hours. Soccer was always outdoors and even orchestra practice because there is not enough high quality indoor spaces. I realize that’s why my kids were totally fine until Covid. We were lucky. Even if there is stuff or radar in the air in CA, the sun is the sun and strong, and pre-Covid we used little technology. And upon lockdown, my kids who were virtually outdoor all day, suddenly were indoor all day + screentime exploded from near 0 to 8 hours a day due to school zoom + ultra high radon level + other things. It’s so obvious now why things went south so fast.

Now daily life feels like I am walking through a strange apocalyptic-esque landscape. My research group is the only ones sitting outside for lunch; not enough but it’s something. Even that I had to force them out. I bought for each desk NIRA lights from Scott Zimmerman that has a spectrum for 300 to 3000 nm. At least indoor that’s the best that is out there. But the rest of the building is so sadly blue lit it’s crazy. I am wearing my blue blocking glasses indoors, asked my LED overhead to be switched off, have a red light panel in my office and asked for hardwired internet. Whenever I leave my office it feels like going through a toxic fume of bright LED light, and kids sit there all day and even eat their lunch. I sometimes want to tell the random kids eating their sad lunch to fucking go outside to eat their lunch. But no that won’t do anything. I rather focus on my family, my friends and my students who trust me and listen, slowly but surely. It’s just so hard to even operate once you “see”. Even if it’s just the blue light, none of the other shenanigans going on, even that is literally hard to unsee. My focus is to stay healthy and to help my kid to full recovery. I feel like we are living through “war”, except nobody seems to realize. My focus is to stay alive, keep my family and group healthy, and be smart to do my work that I am passionate about to have an impact in the long run while not getting canceled.

### drsongihan — 2024-10-02T00:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340260>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=340222)
>
> Science is a tough place not to get cancelled these days.
>

I am prepping for a big 2-part Seminar at U Wash in Seattle (it’s the Home University of Pollack). I submitted an abstract about the stuff we do in terms of measuring water structuring at the nanometer scale with our new spectroscopy tools. I added a single sentence at the end of my abstract tgar we have now tools at place to examine the controversy on EZ water that persists in the science community and bridge the two communities (the conventional water researchers and the ones who are looking for relevant answers). I got a long 2 page tirade of an email from a faculty there who pretty much told my host “how dare she drops the terminology EZ water if it’s well known that it’s debunked”. Gheezus. I am not even famous yet. It’s just a seminar. It’s a sentence that says we now have the tools at hand to examine this exciting question. And somebody already flips out. I wonder what’s wrong with them? Are they scared? Are they dumb? What wound did I poke? Well my answer: I am doubling down by reading a lot more and prepping for the last 3 minutes in which I will explain what exactly is the claim and how it can be tested rigorously. I am not concluding since I have not done the experiment, but I will explicitly state how this hypothesis will be tested. It will drive that person crazy who argues it’s “all just electro osmosis” ignoring the fact that the EZ zone is seen irrespective of charge and ion concentration.

With my new clarity I am also seeing better. Brand is better than Peterson but also missing the main point. After hearing another Peterson podcast I realize his understanding is very limited, he is making stuff up and is like a person looking under the lamppost just because it’s there.

Sadly there are not enough people that still impress me and teach me something every time. So far Jack, RFK. I like Brett Weinstein and I am still searching. I will soon start reading Mae Wan Ho books. We will see.

If not there is no difference between a snake oil salesman or centralized doctors who prescribe statin.

Well, if not, here is the whole blueprint laid out for many sleeping folks to see clearly via your glass eyes sprayed with Windex. Managed care really is part of the bioweapons program being used against "We The People." For those who do not know the Maxwell family controls PEER journals. This plan is all linked to the internet and to USA/Israeli intelligence agencies. Via Big Tech it links to CMS/HHS to the captured bureacracies in the FDA/CDC/NIH and Federal branches of governments. Ingenious, nafarious, and deadly. It appears you can taper a Ponzi scheme after all.

I spent 4 hours listening to your Danny Jones podcast. Spectacular. I agree.

My question was not that I doubt this, but rather how many and who is in active “know” and control and who is being controlled? Maybe we will never know. Certainly Gates, Fauci, the Rockefellers, general Gross etc

About the ponze scheme: completely agree. But the extent of corruption and control is the worse in clinical research.

I will lay out some differences:

Huberman and anybody who is faculty in the medical school do not have a set salary but have to earn 9 or 12 months salary from grant money or other funding. Right there they are enslaved.

In the natural sciences we have a hard salary and real tenure. Grant is needed but extra and pulling a grant will not ruin us.

Clinical faculty do not have prerogative to their lab space. In the natural sciences, I have my space that I control and I neither pay rent nor utility cost. We have to teach but there is considerable freedom in the scope. I am not teaching philosophy or political science, so nobody cares except for those in the major. Because big Pharma and big tech don’t care either, even the scope of the curriculum can be altered if we want to. The only reason why teaching freshmen is a pain in the butt is because of the medical students who need to take chemistry; their curriculum or requirement is so formulaic and rigid that we are forced to teach exactly what the medical student candidates need for their Mcat. But once they are out, typically after 2 quarters, or even if not in the upper division classes the rigidity of course content control dramatically loosens.

Btw Becker could be canceled so fast because he was a clinical researcher whose lab and salary were contingent upon having grants. Look at all the scientists getting truly canceled. The brilliant and brave clinical researchers are hit the hardest because big Pharma cares.

Clinical directors don’t read their papers I guess, but we painstakingly write our own papers from scratch, mull over 10 times with the student and publish like 1 paper per grad student per 2-3 years. So if you have a group of 20, then it’s 5-10 papers a year. The peer review system is broken, we have the oracle problem for sure, but there are many ways to publish, including the arxiv system and open journal concept.

There is still corruption, power play and coward, low agency, behavior, but the system is currently set up such that if there is a will there is a way. It’s like in your old days when the doctors worked for the patients and they had the say, not the clinic. This probably will change in the long run but the big powers are so busy waging war that they we are still flying under the radar.

### Chlo_e — 2024-10-02T12:55:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340301>

Songhi have you listened to joint interviews with Scott Zimmerman and Robert Fosbury? I know you mentioned purchasing some of Scott's NIRA lamps so you may have already come across this material. Although they seem quite 'mainstream' they are pushing their ideas and research in a good direction I think and they are big advocates of reconnecting with nature and copying nature.

Andrew Marino has some interviews but they are hard to find and not readily available on spotify etc, there are some links on his website to specific interviews he's done.

Sasha Latypova and Mike Yeadon are interesting for pharma-related content. Mike Yeadon has been cancelled to hell and high water.

Would the works of John Stuart Reid be of interest? Where sound meets water? His own views and experiences relating to water-structuring and protein synthesis might be worth a listen as it relates to your own line of work.

There is a 4 hour lecture from 2006 someone else posted a while back by Dr Bill Deagle, I think it can only be found on Rumble- his content cannot be found on YT or the usual podcast channels. I can't say it all made perfect sense and it's an intense listen but a lot of it ties in with present goings-on. He starts by talking about 'bird flu' as a warning for a future 'pandemic' and does a lengthy piece about bioweapons.

Always recommend the Salt Path trilogy for a macro-level case study of nature and neuro-REgeneration.

P.S Peterson may not be a completely lost cause. His wife Tammy recently put out a YT interview with someone who was paralysed after receiving a mandated covid jab so I think slowly this stuff is entering his awareness. Probably he does not feel as confident speaking out about such matters and likes to stay in his lane/personal Overton Window. If you listen to more obscure interviews with him where he talks about his previous benzo addiction and how essentially all plant foods were irritating his nervous system- he is likely very light/EMF-sensitive and damaged and doesn't realise, but has found carnivore alleviates some of the symptoms. His daughter is the same, but they don't seem to have connected enough dots. They are hyoer-focused on carnivore or mold or psychosocial issues only. Just my 2 cents

### eve001 — 2024-10-03T20:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340375>

> [markthesunbather said:](/goto/post?id=340367)
>
> I've gotten to where I don't have a lot of use for intellectual, academia philosopher/religious types after having really meditated on what all I've learned here. I mean, I \*want\* to like some of these guys, but there's something off about the kind of person that knows all the arguments for metaphysical light like the back of their hand.....but can't seem to figure out how a not-so-metaphysical lightbulb is fucking them up.
>
> Just hard to take these people seriously now. Maybe that's a shame, you tell me, I don't know.

Hi, the hard science people here who are not familiar with the philosophy of science may want to check out these:

Paul Feyerabend "Against Method"
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Against\_Method](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Against_Method) (1975)
Thomas S. Kuhn "The Structure of Scientific Revolutions" (1962) [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The\_Structure\_of\_Scientific\_Revolutions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Structure_of_Scientific_Revolutions)

\= Here one can find observations and analysis on scientific method, on how scientific paradigms rise and fall, and on truth and its limits.

Philosophy can be a great discipline, just like medicine, when practiced with skill and freedom.
