### Jas0n Sanchez — 2024-10-06T22:53:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340521>

> [Gene Kalmens said:](/goto/post?id=337829)
>
> [@drsongihan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31459/) I also live in a Chicago suburb and regularly swim in Lake Michigan, with the campus where you work in direct view. April-May and October-November are great months to get cold in the lake.
> My personal circumstances are entirely different, but here is what I do in my house:
> 1\. Router wifi is disabled. A wifi access point exists and can be turned on if necessary, using an easy outlet switch. Rarely on, but always off for the night.
> 2\. Wired internet is implemented using Trendnet power adaptors. This way, you can even get wired internet on smart phones and tablets anywhere in your house.
> 3\. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) 's all house emf filter installed, with a kill switch for the bedroom. In Illinois, electrical conduits are enforced by the code, so very little EMF gets emitted from the walls as long as nothing is plugged in.
> 4\. Amber and red incandescent bulbs are used throughout the house.
> 5\. I have two EMR-TEK devices (they are flicker-free unlike from other manufacturers), an IR heat lamp from Home Depot, and a Spertis Vitamiin D light. I hardly use them in summer. I use these lights based on Jack's recommendation during a consult.
> 6\. I use IRIS software on my computer and an eye care flicker-free monitor with a blue blocking function. When I work remotely, I have a setup to work outside May-September, with minimal clothing. Always a standing desk.
> 7\. In the office, I have a red bulb and a yellow daylight bulb plugged in next to my computer, modified screen RGB color settings, and bue-blocking glasses at all times. I go outside regularly and take a full hour during the lunch break to take a dip in the lake or walk on lake front exposed to sun.
>
> These are my 2 cents. If you would like to meet up somewhere near the beach, it will be an easy task for me.
>
> Gene

Gene, these are very helpful tips! TrendNet suggestion blew my mind! Hardwired internet is what I’m trying to install. Very thankful. Jason

### drsongihan — 2024-10-14T20:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340992>

All right, my response to being yelled at by somebody who I believe is one of the smartest people of the current time and I highly respect for his courage (of course that's Jack) against the conspiracy against humanity is to go right back at and inquire to the why and what did I miss.
[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) I scheduled a consult with you at the earliest time slot I could identify to ask more questions. Looking forward to it.

Meanwhile, let me clarify that my son cannot go to lower latitude at the moment because his dad is blocking it. By law, dad can veto a movement of his son. His dad is so centralized that I cannot even convince him that blue light needs to be blocked. I am not trying to convince him, but I have to navigate the landscape in order to raise my son, to not run into problems with the law, while also earning money and following my own passion. We will spend 3 weeks in December in Mexico and I have planned a fake sabbatical this coming year at the 13th latitude where I can work remotely for 6 months without loosing my job. For someone who is not informed of my situation (not Jack, but one of the attendant) to casually comment on how folks cannot be inconvenienced and be so sure of themselves speaks more about their own narrow-minded thinking than mine. Folks who do not know what they don't know and stay humble and kind in the face of the vastness of the universe are more likely to get their punch on their mouth than not. I care about this forum that has given me so much wisdom, so much generosity and kindness and a real life saving input, but I have no tolerance for negativity or know-it-betters. Keep in your n=1 lane. There is more that we don't know than we know. That is true for everyone, and in light of that, orienting ourselves towards Nature is the wisest thing to do. Having said that, let's not forget that everyone in this forum is living a first world convenient life (defined as running water, clean water supply, means to eat healthy and have freedom of movement). Let's not fool ourselves with some sort of virtue signaling who is giving up more. What matters is that we learn how to get our n=1 health, mind and body back, and contribute to keeping the human species free thinking beings.

I give Jack a pass because he does know better than anyone, and he is taking on the whole world and risking his life in doing so.

Interesting observation that I am sure all of you faced. Once you engage in decentralized thinking, the entire house of card falls, nothing is left standing. When this happens, suddenly the friend group also divides into those who reject the ideas you share before you even began the sentence, and those who get curious and inquire. The latter is a small group, but I am grateful that I have those. Time to make some new friends!

### Jas0n Sanchez — 2024-10-14T20:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-340997>

Songi,
Thank you for your journal and insights. I have a lot of respect for you and empathize with your challenges that you are having to overcome. I have learned a lot from your shared experiences and input from others who wish you and your son the best.

> All right, my response to being yelled at by somebody who I believe is one of the smartest people of the current time and I highly respect for his courage (of course that's Jack) against the conspiracy against humanity is to go right back at and inquire to the why and what did I miss.
> [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) I scheduled a consult with you at the earliest time slot I could identify to ask more questions. Looking forward to it.

> Meanwhile, let me clarify that my son cannot go to lower latitude at the moment because his dad is blocking it. By law, dad can veto a movement of his son. His dad is so centralized that I cannot even convince him that blue light needs to be blocked. I am not trying to convince him, but I have to navigate the landscape in order to raise my son, to not run into problems with the law, while also earning money and following my own passion. We will spend 3 weeks in December in Mexico and I have planned a fake sabbatical this coming year at the 13th latitude where I can work remotely for 6 months without loosing my job. For someone who is not informed of my situation (not Jack, but one of the attendant) to casually comment on how folks cannot be inconvenienced and be so sure of themselves speaks more about their own narrow-minded thinking than mine. Folks who do not know what they don't know and stay humble and kind in the face of the vastness of the universe are more likely to get their punch on their mouth than not. I care about this forum that has given me so much wisdom, so much generosity and kindness and a real life saving input, but I have no tolerance for negativity or know-it-betters. Keep in your n=1 lane. There is more that we don't know than we know. That is true for everyone, and in light of that, orienting ourselves towards Nature is the wisest thing to do. Having said that, let's not forget that everyone in this forum is living a first world convenient life (defined as running water, clean water supply, means to eat healthy and have freedom of movement). Let's not fool ourselves with some sort of virtue signaling who is giving up more. What matters is that we learn how to get our n=1 health, mind and body back, and contribute to keeping the human species free thinking beings.
>
> I give Jack a pass because he does know better than anyone, and he is taking on the whole world and risking his life in doing so.
>
> Interesting observation that I am sure all of you faced. Once you engage in decentralized thinking, the entire house of card falls, nothing is left standing. When this happens, suddenly the friend group also divides into those who reject the ideas you share before you even began the sentence, and those who get curious and inquire. The latter is a small group, but I am grateful that I have those. Time to make some new friends!

### drsongihan — 2024-10-18T16:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-341177>

I have a question to those who reside or resided in "shitty" areas, as this forum refers to. My question is about cold air exposure, not CT. So temperature now dropped steeply, and morning is in the low 40ies, while daytime is still sunny enough that it can get hot. Does it matter how long do I expose myself to cold? (We are D4 Haplotype -- very uncoupled! Mongolian genes, came down to East Asia via Siberia ) Like with the sun, is it the longer cold and outside the better, or is it more about the contrast to exposing myself to cold several times a day?

My son bikes 30 min to and back from school, and I am proud of him to not complaining since it's pretty cold and windy in the morning. I am trying to myself sit outside and work in the morning for a few hours, but my hands are frozen. I trained my body enough that I can walk around in a T-shirt, if I breathe properly, at 40 degree, but that'sdifferent from sitting at 40 degree and trying to work. I am just wondering how long should I torture myself or go to my proverbial "fire-pit" and just have windows open?

### drsongihan — 2024-10-18T21:01:08+0300

> 2) Derived from my readings here and on jackkruse.com, getting cold temp exposure with out grounding is different from being insulated riding a bike in cold. Being active is good. So is being grounded. The cold air minus altitude (assuming Chicago is low altitude) probably not beneficial because low UV light.
>
> My takeaway, shoot away.

I thought the effect of cold is to activate the brown adipose pathway to generate melanin from inside by generating UV light inside you. That's how ancestors living in Scandinavia and anywhere above 40th did well as long as they lived in their natural environment. Cold is supposed to offset the missing UV light by generating UV from inside you. I just don't know how cold and for how long. The longer at cold the better or is a contrast good?

For now, I am going outside at sunrise, barefoot and drink my coffee outside and try to sit outside as long as I can for 1-2 hours, and then I sit inside with windows open, and then try to go out as often as I can, including 2 hours outside in the early evening. Next, I want to figure out how to do my cooking outside, both in the morning and evening. Maybe I set up a camping situation in my backyard.

Cold is supposed to offset the missing UV light by generating UV from inside you. I just don't know how cold and for how long. The longer at cold the better or is a contrast good?
>
> For now, I am going outside at sunrise, barefoot and drink my coffee outside and try to sit outside as long as I can for 1-2 hours, and then I sit inside with windows open, and then try to go out as often as I can, including 2 hours outside in the early evening. Next, I want to figure out how to do my cooking outside, both in the morning and evening. Maybe I set up a camping situation in my backyard.

I think any level of cold exposure is beneficial, but my recollection from the blogs is that being immersed in cold water is something like 10x more effective at cooling your skin temperature than cold air, and metal is 10x more effective than water (which is why it can be dangerous if used unsupervised). Being outside is always better than being inside, and you'll probably see some minor benefits from cold air exposure, but if you wanted to minmax the effect in less time CT in water is much more effective.

On contrast, Jack wrote about the Fournier Effect with temperature contrast in the redox Rx blog, worth cehcking that out.

Show them the picture below 100 times until their warped brains get it. 380nm light is the metabolic switch for mTOR that WHOLLY DETERMINES anabolism and catabolism choices in EVERY cell, especially the defective ones, and it repairs the periodicity of the circadian clock. ### drsongihan — 2024-10-20T17:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-341294>

I just made a discovery yesterday about nnEMF that is illuminating. After Jack keeps yelling at me for trying to biohack instead of coming down to low latitude, I decided to double down on both routes. Re latitude, I will be in Mexico, Yucatan for 4-5 weeks this December, and figuring out how to be at 13th next year, especially winter. Re biohacking, I should re-name it as mitigating and biohacking, since it's a lot about eliminating. Just because it is wiser to come down to lower latitude does not mean we should stop mitigating and biohacking.

So, my son continues to do well, but I also know intuitively that his wellness is fragile in the sense that I think he is sensitive to negative input. I am observing him every step of the way and found that whenever he sits at his favorite spot on the sofa, he becomes slightly irritated and snappy. This changes quite drastically when he is the kitchen etc. and outside, he is happy, even singing, depending on where he is. I have windows open and the sofa area is well lit with sunlight through the open window, and I only have red or orange light from Hooga, or NIRA light for the kitchen--even that, I only use during day. For certain daytime hours, and I have plant growth lights in corners and a reptile lamp near his sofa. That's for context so you can imagine my environment. But back to his sensitivity, his mood tanks slowly on the sofa, and recovers in the kitchen. So I decided that I will measure nnEMF again with body voltage, rf, magnetic field etc. I had done that for the whole house, but decided to check his area again. Boom! Everywhere in the house during day time body voltage is between 50 mV to 100 mV, and climbs to 200 mV near areas where I have devices plugged in (e.g. kitchen) but near his sofa, right where his head is, it shot up to above 2-6 Volt! The reptile lamp that I used near him was adding only a little bit of body voltage, but I had accidentally in the extension cord his guitar amplifier plugged in that was causing all the body voltage. Turns out half of the problem was the extension cord that was somehow VERY faulty and was adding several volt even without anything, the amplifier added another few volt, and the Reptile lamp added 0.2V of body voltage. But the reptile lamp also adds flicker--both Jack and another member reminded me of this problem. So, I unplugged everything, and moved the EMR-TEK Krypton device that I had in the kitchen to his sofa to shine right from behind on the back of his head (I recently bought the smaller Krypton that has infrared at multiple wavelength + UVB + UVA for short times). I confirmed the EMR-TEK device did not add any body voltage. EMR-TEK is very expensive compared to other devices because its Flicker free (I got the newest one that has even less nnEMF than the earlier version).

I swear that I saw an immediate improvement within a single day. Now I have the Reptile lamp in the kitchen, but much further from where he sits. The reason I am keeping the Reptile lamp + black light is that it dramatically contributed to his recovery and general well being. So imagine a continuous recovery curve, but with small dips in mood when he sits near his sofa, even though the overall recovery is positive. There is little UV at 43 latitude in the winter, so I have to supplement UV unless I go down to lower latitude. Turns out, Jack was right again: EMR-TEK is the way to go to get UV through the Krypton device.

To summarize for those who don't want to go back to 20 pages of journal, here are the mitigations that we have done so far, and still learning.
1) Strict day-night cycle, strict bedtime routine. 9pm through 6.30am.
2) Pitchblack bedroom, not even a single photon, even taped up led spot on the battery pack that I use for a night light.
3) There is no light switch that he can accidentally use to have blue light, including bathroom and hallway, in the evening or generally indoors. The only blue light containing lights are lamps that I have to deliberately plug in, such as the plant growth light and the NIRA lights. I use the IRIS software. Phone has turned up the color to maximally red, minimally blue, and I often make use of Airplane mode or the Zap-app.
4) Cold bedroom temperature, and generally minimal heating in the house.
5) Maximize sunlight and outdoor time. In addition, windows open in every room. On rainy and windy days, I only open certain windows that still keeps us dry and does not knock things over, and on sunny days more windows. I spend on work days 4-5 hours outside and on weekend all day.
6) Removed Wifi. Everything is hardwired. I took Gene's advice and also got Trendnet, but even that I try to keep the internet to only 2 locations in the house near open windows, and have a hard wire going to the Patio. \*
7) Unplugged pretty much every device except for built-in kitchen appliances and recessed lamp--but they do not contribute much to body voltage. Lamps and coffee machines are plugged out and I plug it in when I use them. My son and I leave phone in kitchen, never take to bedroom.
8) Grounding barefoot
9) Eating window 7am through 5pm, eat meals on Patio, protein and fat rich, minimal carb, and all organic.
10) Obvious, but maybe worth mentioning. Absolutely no contact with fluoride. No fluoride containing toothpaste, no water from the tap, not even filtered, and no fluoride containing medicine (e.g. most antidepressants, such as Prozac, Zoloft, and many many other meds have fluorine). Better even, I just don't go to the doctor anymore, no medicine, no flu vaccine, no more vaccine in any shape or form.\*\*

\* I am not yet turning off breaker in bedroom at night, but that might be the next thing I do--but so far the nnEMF values are good in the house according to the devices I have. I have bad cell phone reception that I take as a positive. I use hardwire in the house and don't hold the phone in my hand or near my ear. I use airtube headphone if I need to use a headphone. No dirty electric, and no other nnEMF that are measurable.
\*\* For myself, I decided that I am not doing any of the centralized preventive b.s. No mammogram and never done colonoscopy. I still go to annual checkup mainly to see the blood test results. I still go annually to the OB, but since year after year the results show I am healthy, I am not sure I should skip that too. On the other hand, blood draw, urine test, pap smears are relatively non-invasive checks, so I might keep it.

So far, this is not biohacking, but rather mitigating since I am eliminating technology and EMF, not adding technology or measures.

Now to biohacking.

9) I use red-light for me in the evening and love it. I turn on infrared and far-infrared light in my son's room in the morning at 6.30am, whilst also opening the window. Btw, I plug in and out the whole extension cord because having these lamps turned off only but plugged in would disturb his sleep due to bogy voltage. I have infrared and far-infrared light constantly on in the two locations where we have hardwired internet after 6pm.
10) Added UV light for 7am-8am and 12pm-4pm through plant growth lamp in far corners, black light + reptile lamp in kitchen (above story) and Krypton from EMR-TEK panel on his head at the hang-out sofa.
11) Minimal supplements and meds. Only lithium and DHA. We eat iodine-rich food, and still take zeolites that were a game changer for us, but even that is eliminating. I am taking for myself only DHA.
12) I do CT, cold shower, and I am starting Lake swimming/dipping. My son says he is doing cold shower, but who knows.
13) My son drinks deuterium depleted water--expensive but worth it, or Alaskan mountain water that I buy.
14) I have a Magnetico pad for his bed.

I am listing all of this to be clear that I went to an extreme in mitigating and biohacking, after reading Jack's blog on these topics 3-5 times. Even so, I got lucky because my son is only 14 AND because he was very healthy pre-covid by all visible standards, he has a good tan, black hair, dark colored eyes, excellent body composition, 20-20 vision, highly sensitive taste and smell buttons, so he had still a relatively good foundation that got spectacularly screwed up by the environment.

And yet, I agree, going to lower latitude will be how I can get him whole for real.

### drsongihan — 2024-10-20T22:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-341302>

I have some reminiscing to do about my childhood. In light of the Danny Jones podcast and Jack's post, it's relevant and it does connect to my present journey.

I had so much resentment for my mother as a teen. She is a loner, she is principled, she is considered "crazy" and extreme, she was ruthless in doing what she believed in and she did nothing what society asked her of. My teenage years were challenging because I was not allowed to do anything "fashionable" if she did not believe it's the right thing. But in hindsight, I have to say she was ahead of her time. She was not right about everything, but she was more right than not. She is the reason why I spent every waking hour in my life outside both in Korea and in Germany.

I grew up in Germany age 2 to 11 and 20-26, in Korea age 0-2 and 11 - 19, and after age 26 came to Berkeley, USA and after that I stayed in California until 1 year ago when I moved to the Northern suburb of Chicago, near Lake Michigan.

In Korea, she was the only woman at that time, in the 60ies and 70ies, who would go hiking by herself and tan at the beach. Girls were supposed to be indoors, knitting and be quiet. Her brothers and her mother tried to keep her in the house and control her, but my mom had grandmother who always snug her out. Her grandmother lived till 97, she was a chain smoker, a drinker and a free spirit. She probably saw the "rebel" talent in my mom and protected her to do whatever she wanted to do. Her grandmother was still powerful, and neither her brothers nor mother could cross the wrath of her grandmother. When I was born, my mom had me outdoors all the time, despite her mother chastising her that this crazy women is ruining her child.

In Germany, my memory is that in elementary school, she would snatch me straight from school and go straight to the pool every single day in the summer religiously. In the winter, she would throw me out of the house after brief homework, no matter the weather and not let me in until dinner time--though that was normal at that time, and I had plenty company outside. Weekend early morning, she would pack car at 6am (dad was just following whatever mom said we are doing that day) and drive up to the mountains to hike. We were too poor to ski in a real resort, so we had this add-on skis for boots that we would carry up the mountains and ski down. She only bought organic and real food, in the eighties when it was fashionable to eat can and packaged food. I had never seen a can or spaghetti from a box until I was an adult. She did not believe in margarine when in the eighties everyone ate that. It was always real butter and food from scratch, and never ate out in restaurant or fast-food. Ice cream was a rare treat. Never had a store bought beverage, ever. Jam was homemade, even pizza dough and any and all cake on birthdays. Childhood is happy memory, pretty much living outside.

Back in Korea, things were less ideal because the crazy education system keeps kids in school for 10 hours. But the windows were not tight, we had in the first years coal oven in the middle of the classroom, recess was of course outside, there is no such thing as an indoor gym whatsoever at that time, no phone and no technology, and obviously incandescent lights. Even so, I remember that I had to choose an outdoor sports that back then girls were not encouraged to do. So in the summer I did track & field and in the winter I did ice speed skating, but on a make-shift outdoor arena. Anything indoors that would cost money, I was not allowed to do. I was only allowed to go to school trips that were for free and outdoors, and none that cost money that typically was something indoors. My mom said its a waste of money. I was not allowed to have any fashionable clothes--you can imagine how mad I was as a teen. She would hammer into my brain how vanity will poison me and drilled into me to not give in unless I want to become a dumb, brainless, idiot (her exact words). I have to explain how radical that was in the eighties and nineties when the whole purpose of girls education in Korea is for the girl can marry into a "good family". It was important that the girls looked "pretty". In my final high school year, girls were propped up by their mothers for all sorts of beauty correction and craziness, especially those from richer families. The poorer families had no time to worry about b.s. like that. It was kind of funny because I was a whole 5 skin tones darker than anybody in my class in a country where the beauty standard is to be as fair as possible--no wonder birth rate in South Korea is at 0.7 or something crazy like that--and my nick name was "the blacky" (ggam-dung-ee in Korean). I was the only girl among the richer families with a deep tan and toned muscle. The only "sin" of mine was to read books in the middle of the night, underneath my blanket, with a flash lamp on. That explains why I am the only one in my family who developed myopia. Nobody in my entire extended family wears glasses, uncles and aunts included, cousins etc, but I am sure this will have changed for the younger generation.

There is something else that was unique about my mom. She was the source of what you would call conspiracy theory. I have no idea where she got that from, but early one she would tell us to not believe what we see and hear. She told me in my teen years when we came back to South Korea that the U.S. is propping up forever wars, that the US--Soviet Union conflict was fabricated for money, that South Korea is a puppet of the U.S., how the IMF financial crisis was fabricated by the powers etc. I think she is a bit wrong about China, North Korea, but the point is that she defied any mainstream narrative. I remember that my parents got phone calls from the school that they are worried about our family because both my brother and I wrote essays along the lines of communism is not effective, but not all evil, and that the conflict is fabricated by the U.S. to keep South Korea in check. She looked at us incredulously and said "I thought you were smarter than that to know to think what you think and not write the damn thing on paper and submit it to the machine". This was a time when you go to jail by saying anything pro-communism. My mom was funny on the phone, I still remember, she told the school officials that the kids must have been confused, that they shared the story of what the communist propaganda would say that my parents told us to watch out for. Of course, I don't believe in communism since it always turns into faschism, but the point is that my mother taught me that mainstream narrative is there to control people, not the truth, and that I need to look beyond that.

From there, it is a long way to today. She was so driven and only worried about that I would acquire the skills to survive in the world as in independent person, that there was no space and room for love and affection. That was tough for me. I knew that I am on my own, which also meant that there is no margin to fool around. Once I left the house, I got some support for College (remember, I went back to Germany for College where education is for free, $0 tuition) but then I was on my own since age 21, not just money but also for every decision. My mother being such a hard headed women, I did not want to ask for any advice since I knew what her advice would be or she would chastise me for not thinking straight. The thing is that she was sometimes right and sometimes wrong. So, I cut her out in all decision making and did my own thing--she also did not ask me to come home, unless I wanted to. I don't think I could have been trying out independence so early on and so cold turkey if it was not for having this kind of mother. I am not recommending this as a great parenting style, but this is part of my history.

Part of my mistake also was that I rejected many things what my mother was standing for. The problem is that her message was right, but she was so on "survival" mode that love came short. I did not trust her because she was often contradicting herself and would never admit her mistakes. If the hard message and love & trust could be combined, that would have been more effective. But so be it. The biggest mistake I made was to marry the wrong men, and that is of course because I was not in the right mindset. I knew it even back then but did not have the courage to call off the wedding. I wanted a family and kids, and wanted to settle. This is also where having a family who you love and trust becomes critical--if I had a sounding board, a mother I could trust to share and ask for wisdom, I could have called off the wedding. Or who knows.

My husband made my life joyless for 18 years, but at least I have two beautiful kid, and now I am separated, we have sold any common property we have, and I can start a new journey once my son is 18 or whenever I decide that I am ready to watch out for myself. Both kids struggled mightily during Covid, but they also gave me purpose and most importantly, they taught me something profound. Their struggle was the punch on my mouth as Jack would call it. I am thankful for the Covid crisis because the punch on my mouth came simultaneously and very visibly, like a switch was turned on and darkness came with great intensity. Without Covid, it would have taken me longer to realize what's wrong. My kids were "perfectly fine and healthy" so to speak (to the outside) and within a few months of the lockdown, something profoundly changed. I could help my daughter over her struggles much easier because she was pulling with me on the same string, she was trying hard, she was equally confused, but also committed to get better. My daughter is 18, studies Biochemistry somewhere in Southern California in the middle of nature and is a budding independent thinker. She calls me all the time. So, I am not sure what's better. She is certainly less resilient and hardened than I was who learned survival skills from my mom, she is definitely more spoiled than I ever was, but she trusts me and calls me. Maybe there is something good to that. She took herself off both Lithium and Vyvanse that she was on for her ADHD that was impacting her during high school, did not ask me but just declared that she is done, and told me she is studying outdoors and seems to be enjoying herself. She told me that "I can figure out how to feel good without the damn medicine". So I told her "right on, just maximize outdoor time and sleep in dark". Her room mates are not night agitators and one even uses a sunset lamp, so there is some awareness. I got my daughter a NIRA lamp for her desk and an amber reading lamp. Her boyfriend is an outdoor fanatic -- I told her that this is the best qualification I have heard. I don't ask about safe sex, drugs or alcohol, I only ask her to maximize outdoor time with her friends and boyfriend. The rest will settle itself.

You all know the story of my son, so no need to repeat. He is asking me about the Universe all the time, dark matter and atomic physics. I am just happy that he now thinks life is worth living and that he is putting effort into something. He was not long ago declared "hopeless" and somebody who will have to live with this severe chronic condition for the rest of his life for which there is no cure. I owe it to Jack and this community that I was able to reverse that, and is keeping me accountable to walk the final walk to fully heal him. I am trying and on it.

The point of this story is that there are layers of stories behind someone's story. I am lucky because of my mother and my childhood that was protected according to something very close to what Jack is teaching EVEN for the eighties and nineties that were less polluted than now. If somebody seems to recover slower or faster, or has a better or worse health foundation, there are generations of history to look back on. Obviously, I screwed up my environment already as a child and teenager by reading under the blanket with a flash lamp on for hours. My curiosity and education is both a blessing and a curse. I developed some sort of mild ADHD symptoms (mild because I can function fine, but my focus is not optimal) due to probably the reduction of POMC neurons and that carried over to both of my kids. We will see whether my daughter can renovate herself--she is young, she is aware, and has an outdoorsy boyfriend who seem like a good human. There is hope.

I have recently became aware of the story of North Korean defector Yeonmi Park. Hers is the only story worth listening to. I was puzzled why was she so vilified in the mainstream media and thought this has nothing to do with me. But I was wrong. Listen to her story in the Joe Rogan podcast from 3 months ago. It's riveting, and also very clear why the mainstream media and centralized forces tried to silence and cancel her. The only reason why she was not fully canceled is because the UN, Clintons, Bill Gates, Bezos, and main stream media underestimated her. She is small, initially appears like a little girl with a small voice and does not appear to be someone who can have influence. So, they thought just canceling her looked like would be enough, and no need to destroy her. The centralized powers are wrong. I will predict to you that she will become the most uncomfortable force that the centralized powers have to deal with unless they kill her, which might happen. Why do I say that? Because she has an unimaginable inner strength and she has ONE goal--to tell her story and to not shut up. Nothing can destroy her after what she went through and survived. What I describe as a "tough mother" and challenging childhood is laughable compared to what she went through. It was her dad who instilled that in her, and her mother who stood by her so that Yeonmi can live. Her dad was sent to prison camp when she was 11, came out as a physically broken men when she was 13, released probably because he was useless as a laborer and maybe to make an example to the village what happens to people who think for themselves, she escaped with her mom at age 13, was a sex slave for 2 years in China. She survived that, was able to smuggle her dad out, and buried him in the mountains of Mongolia by herself. He asked her to fight to tell her story and never give up to survive. She walked with a group of people across the Gobi desert by foot, at - 40 degree, to Mongolia and finally escaped to South Korea at age 15, and then came to the U.S., studied in Columbia and now lives in the U.S. She wrote two books, and you have to read the book "In order to live". She tells Joe Rogan and the host of Socrates in the City (also worth listening to) her story, how she survived, and that she only had the will to survive because her dad instilled in her to never give up, to always come right back when punched down, and survive to live so that she can tell her story to the world. Through her story, you can see the incredible corruption of Hollywood and the political elite. They will rather make movies and news stories about Boka Haram and ISIS, but they will not touch a story about the Millions of people who are living through the Holocaust for decades under the auspices of China and North Korea because the Chinese communist party is sponsoring every single Hollywood movie, without which Hollywood cannot survive. If I hear again anything about charity and women's right bullshit from Hollywood, while they actively boycott the stories of Northkorean defectors because that will cross China, I will vomit. The most profoundly amusing and disturbing story is when Yeonmi arrives at Columbia University and meets the Woke. She tells Joe Rogan that after she survives starvation, 2 years of sex slavery, death and destruction, and came finally to the promise land, to be free from the North Korean regime, and to then learn that she is being muzzled at Columbia University was more traumatic because, she says, there is nowhere to go from here. She had arrived in America, the promised land, and realize that she instead landed at this place where girls in Lululemon leggings that cost more than what her mother was sold for as a sex slave in China talk about "being oppressed", drink "green detox juice" for $15 that she had every day in North Korea in the rice fields before the leaves turn toxic, and is faced with DEI that explains consent and male supremacy. She tells Socrates in the city that she was deeply offended to learn that the Woke were re-defining rape, racism and oppression, and even what the definition of a women is, that these privileged people dared to use these terms for their bullshit, and then completely shut her up and cancel her, in fact tell her that she is "brainwashed", and will try to actively bury her story. Her awakening also came during Covid. She told the host that her goal was to tell her story of her people, but the only network that would give her a forum to tell her story without censorship and without precondition and welcomed her with open arms were the so called Right Wing media and Fox News. She says that she is not saying Fox News is perfect, but they let me tell my story, they did not muzzle me and they aired it. Btw, there is nothing right wing about Joe Rogan. The fact that he is even called "right wing" and "conservative" boggles my mind. Anyway, I am weaving back \-)) to the main point. Everything is connected, global and personal history is important and there is a reason why things are the way they are if we can go back in time long enough. Yeonmi Park came the furthest and may change the world, at least she has a shot at shaking up the elite's fake narrative, because her dad instilled in her to think for herself and to fight to survive, to live to tell her story, while her mom was a warrior to stand by her side. I highly recommend that you listen to both podcasts, Joe Rogan, and Socrates in the city. It will give you energy and determination, anger, clarity but also hope for the future. Her story is also the best story that illuminates what's wrong with the "Woke", why this is not just annoying but dangerous, and that after all America is STILL the land of hope because there is a chance that this country can turn around, but only if the November election goes to Trump. Trump is not perfect, but the only shot at a better future is with team Trump. I say that as a former staunch Democrat. It's gamer over if Kamala and the machine behind wins. I have finally come to the point where I can openly say that even in my own environment, to friends and colleagues. It's important because there is a lot of dangerous group think that needs to be disrupted.

### Shanti — 2024-10-21T19:59:19+0300

### doctorwu85 — 2024-10-21T22:08:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-341345>

> [Shanti said:](/goto/post?id=341339)
>
> Thank you for sharing Songihan. I didn’t find the Iris App in the App Store. Can you please tell me how to find it?

### [Protect your Eyes. Be Healthy. Achieve more | Iris](https://iristech.co)

Iris is Blue Light Filter and Screen Dimmer for Eye Protection which makes Monitors healthy for the eyes. With Iris, you can reduce the Blue Light emitted from your screen for better sleep, reduce the Brightness of your screen without PWM and avoid eye pain.

 iristech.co

It's a computer application. I don't think they have an iPhone app, but you can drastically change your red/blue light settings in the Accessibility menu.

### drsongihan — 2024-12-01T19:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-343131>

Here is an update from me. Long silence, ironically because my son is going through a robust recovery I then discovered all the "dead bodies" I did not take care of in my science job and needed to clean house + move to Yucatan Peninsula late Nov/Dec/early Jan. His dad and I are taking turns to get him through remote school while in Mexico.

A few things that I have learned for myself, small or big.

\-- El Salvador is significantly, dramatically better than Yucatan island for two reasons. The 5G situation is horrendous and the electrical wiring in the Airbnb homes are somehow so bad that my son told me he can literally "hear" electricity. Had to move twice. It's still worth it because of the sun, but everything is better in El Salvdor. The sun, the latitude, the magnetic beach, and the whole wifi/cell phone pollution. I could literally feel the magic standing on the El Zonte beach. No matter how popular, I have to say I feel magical on the El Zonte beach, so it's my personal favorite because my skin and gut say so.

\-- I have put every extra dollar into Bitcoin since getting introduced to it and still continuing to put any extra money every month into bitcoin. I am able to convert the largest fraction of my Fiat money to bitcoin in May 2025, delayed due to life's circumstances. Of course May 2025 will be worse than now, but I know it will still be worth it. Better late than never. After that, I have basically financial sovereignty.

\-- I will focus on what I am called for and what I am good at. Each of us are different and have different talents. After the U.S. election, times have changed. All stories that were considered "crazy conspiracy" are one by one manifesting, from the covid being a designer virus, to the mRNA covid "vaccine" crime against humanity, to the deep state, the globalist agenda. Everything. If you had told me that I would take Alex Jones seriously 6 months ago I would have called you a nut job, but here we are. Many of us did our parts in nudging the election outcome. In my small world, many think I am now a nut job but I also was able to get a few friends completely on board, with the 11 yo son of my friend asking to buy him blue blocking glasses. I did the body voltage measurements in their house, and the little boy's eyes open wide. One kid at a time. But that also means, we can leave the advocacy and truth teller's work to the best of the best out there who have a big microphone, who are effective and just closely watch them, and other than that focus on our own craft and passion, friends and family, whatever is our calling in our own way. Charity begins in our own homes and heart. The best charity is the charity that we are capable of delivering with our own hands and minds.

\-- I will go back focusing on my craft and becoming really good at it and known for it because that is what I am good at, trained to do and where my passion lies. I am extremely grateful to the intense 9 months of learning in which I dropped almost everything I am doing to unlearn and learn. Amazing. And I will continue to do so. But at the end of the day, each of us should play a role in this world in our own way doing what we meant to do, and that looks different for everyone. Learn life crazy, but then look for guidance in your own heart and thoughts. I learned for myself something very critical that may sound cruel and un-emphatic, but I truly understood this to be true for myself. Don't be a victim, it's a choice we make. Every failure and hardship is the most amazing teaching moment at the same time, and so many of us in this forum came closer to the truth by experiencing loss and failure. Do something with the newfound wisdom, and that "something" can be seemingly tiny or big to the outside world -- it does not matter. What is important is that we ourselves learn to feel good about ourselves, feel grateful and feel love. In that regard, I like what Sagdhuru says "Don't become an issue in your own life. Be the master of your own thoughts and emotion."

\-- I begun to embrace spirituality. There are bigger forces, connections and coherence in this world. I had always felt the bigger forces but still rejected spirituality. Now, I will embrace RFK Jr's approach--fake it till you make it. One day when our scientific understanding becomes advanced enough, science and spirituality may become the same. But meanwhile we don't know enough about biology, our earth and the planets to rationalize everything. You don't know what you don't know, and that applies to every single human, no matter how smart or enlightened they are. The best shot we have is if we stay true to ourselves, be brave and surrender to the "good" coherences that are healing and connecting. I believe that this is also the most important fault line for the globalist, transhumanist and eugenicists out there. They all have in common to be empty, cruel, and power hungry, but they do not have the super power of spirituality and love. It is my optimistic hope that we can eradicate this cancer of this world through the awakening of enough people to the beauty of this life and planet.

\-- My son is seemingly recovered, but that does not mean he is healed. I am focusing on light, water and magnetism and hope to give him as much of a head start as I can. His mind and body is better, but still clouded because I cannot openly talk to him about his healing journey. Only when I can say all the things above and he embraces it with an open mind, then I know he got it. I am confident now that he can live a functional life and that he will not be self destructive, and his life zest is eager enough to live. Whether he will live truly with eyes wide open, I cannot tell. I am hopeful but not confident.

\-- Finally, I recommend that you listen to the 2 hour podcast from PDB podcast of Anneka Lucas. The Patrick guy, the interviewer, is a douche bag, a light weight and not worth Anneka. Unfortunate for Anneka, PDB was the first podcast she went on. But because Patrick was such a douche, her wisdom came through even better. She was mind controlled by MK Ultra-esque program by David Rockefeller himself, was trafficked as a 9 year old to the most powerful elites by Rothchilds as a child sex slave for the purpose of blackmailing. That includes Pierre Trudeau who is Justin Trudeau's dad that Anneka describes as sadistic and sick. Many other world leaders are implicated. If we thought Jack is crazy, Anneka pretty much confirms everything that Jack told us. The Patrick idiot tried several times to corner Anneka into a blue/red narrative and democrats/republican divisive rhetoric, and she repeated in her own powerful way "No. You do not understand that the so called divisive issues are purposefully co-opted by the global eugenicists. Plant parenthood was co-opted by the globalists to weaponize abortion as "THE" feminist topic--this was by design. This is not a democrat vs republican issue, the issue is that there is a globalist network of evil people who are planning to divide and conquer. We need to fight them." Patrick did not get it, or perhaps he is a CIA asset--who knows. But if you look at what Anneka did with her life, you will see that she made it out with spirituality, love and clarity of mind. We have a lot to learn from someone like Anneka. In my opinion, her story is the biggest story, and I wish somebody can help her reach a bigger audience.

### UlliJoanna — 2024-12-07T15:16:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/songis-optimal-journal-with-14yo-son.29212/post-343488>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=341294)
>
> I just made a discovery yesterday about nnEMF that is illuminating. After Jack keeps yelling at me for trying to biohack instead of coming down to low latitude, I decided to double down on both routes. Re latitude, I will be in Mexico, Yucatan for 4-5 weeks this December, and figuring out how to be at 13th next year, especially winter. Re biohacking, I should re-name it as mitigating and biohacking, since it's a lot about eliminating. Just because it is wiser to come down to lower latitude does not mean we should stop mitigating and biohacking.
>
> So, my son continues to do well, but I also know intuitively that his wellness is fragile in the sense that I think he is sensitive to negative input. I am observing him every step of the way and found that whenever he sits at his favorite spot on the sofa, he becomes slightly irritated and snappy. This changes quite drastically when he is the kitchen etc. and outside, he is happy, even singing, depending on where he is. I have windows open and the sofa area is well lit with sunlight through the open window, and I only have red or orange light from Hooga, or NIRA light for the kitchen--even that, I only use during day. For certain daytime hours, and I have plant growth lights in corners and a reptile lamp near his sofa. That's for context so you can imagine my environment. But back to his sensitivity, his mood tanks slowly on the sofa, and recovers in the kitchen. So I decided that I will measure nnEMF again with body voltage, rf, magnetic field etc. I had done that for the whole house, but decided to check his area again. Boom! Everywhere in the house during day time body voltage is between 50 mV to 100 mV, and climbs to 200 mV near areas where I have devices plugged in (e.g. kitchen) but near his sofa, right where his head is, it shot up to above 2-6 Volt! The reptile lamp that I used near him was adding only a little bit of body voltage, but I had accidentally in the extension cord his guitar amplifier plugged in that was causing all the body voltage. Turns out half of the problem was the extension cord that was somehow VERY faulty and was adding several volt even without anything, the amplifier added another few volt, and the Reptile lamp added 0.2V of body voltage. But the reptile lamp also adds flicker--both Jack and another member reminded me of this problem. So, I unplugged everything, and moved the EMR-TEK Krypton device that I had in the kitchen to his sofa to shine right from behind on the back of his head (I recently bought the smaller Krypton that has infrared at multiple wavelength + UVB + UVA for short times). I confirmed the EMR-TEK device did not add any body voltage. EMR-TEK is very expensive compared to other devices because its Flicker free (I got the newest one that has even less nnEMF than the earlier version).
>
> I swear that I saw an immediate improvement within a single day. Now I have the Reptile lamp in the kitchen, but much further from where he sits. The reason I am keeping the Reptile lamp + black light is that it dramatically contributed to his recovery and general well being. So imagine a continuous recovery curve, but with small dips in mood when he sits near his sofa, even though the overall recovery is positive. There is little UV at 43 latitude in the winter, so I have to supplement UV unless I go down to lower latitude. Turns out, Jack was right again: EMR-TEK is the way to go to get UV through the Krypton device.
>
> To summarize for those who don't want to go back to 20 pages of journal, here are the mitigations that we have done so far, and still learning.
> 1) Strict day-night cycle, strict bedtime routine. 9pm through 6.30am.
> 2) Pitchblack bedroom, not even a single photon, even taped up led spot on the battery pack that I use for a night light.
> 3) There is no light switch that he can accidentally use to have blue light, including bathroom and hallway, in the evening or generally indoors. The only blue light containing lights are lamps that I have to deliberately plug in, such as the plant growth light and the NIRA lights. I use the IRIS software. Phone has turned up the color to maximally red, minimally blue, and I often make use of Airplane mode or the Zap-app.
> 4) Cold bedroom temperature, and generally minimal heating in the house.
> 5) Maximize sunlight and outdoor time. In addition, windows open in every room. On rainy and windy days, I only open certain windows that still keeps us dry and does not knock things over, and on sunny days more windows. I spend on work days 4-5 hours outside and on weekend all day.
> 6) Removed Wifi. Everything is hardwired. I took Gene's advice and also got Trendnet, but even that I try to keep the internet to only 2 locations in the house near open windows, and have a hard wire going to the Patio. \*
> 7) Unplugged pretty much every device except for built-in kitchen appliances and recessed lamp--but they do not contribute much to body voltage. Lamps and coffee machines are plugged out and I plug it in when I use them. My son and I leave phone in kitchen, never take to bedroom.
> 8) Grounding barefoot
> 9) Eating window 7am through 5pm, eat meals on Patio, protein and fat rich, minimal carb, and all organic.
> 10) Obvious, but maybe worth mentioning. Absolutely no contact with fluoride. No fluoride containing toothpaste, no water from the tap, not even filtered, and no fluoride containing medicine (e.g. most antidepressants, such as Prozac, Zoloft, and many many other meds have fluorine). Better even, I just don't go to the doctor anymore, no medicine, no flu vaccine, no more vaccine in any shape or form.\*\*
>
> \* I am not yet turning off breaker in bedroom at night, but that might be the next thing I do--but so far the nnEMF values are good in the house according to the devices I have. I have bad cell phone reception that I take as a positive. I use hardwire in the house and don't hold the phone in my hand or near my ear. I use airtube headphone if I need to use a headphone. No dirty electric, and no other nnEMF that are measurable.
> \*\* For myself, I decided that I am not doing any of the centralized preventive b.s. No mammogram and never done colonoscopy. I still go to annual checkup mainly to see the blood test results. I still go annually to the OB, but since year after year the results show I am healthy, I am not sure I should skip that too. On the other hand, blood draw, urine test, pap smears are relatively non-invasive checks, so I might keep it.
>
> So far, this is not biohacking, but rather mitigating since I am eliminating technology and EMF, not adding technology or measures.
>
> Now to biohacking.
>
> 9) I use red-light for me in the evening and love it. I turn on infrared and far-infrared light in my son's room in the morning at 6.30am, whilst also opening the window. Btw, I plug in and out the whole extension cord because having these lamps turned off only but plugged in would disturb his sleep due to bogy voltage. I have infrared and far-infrared light constantly on in the two locations where we have hardwired internet after 6pm.
> 10) Added UV light for 7am-8am and 12pm-4pm through plant growth lamp in far corners, black light + reptile lamp in kitchen (above story) and Krypton from EMR-TEK panel on his head at the hang-out sofa.
> 11) Minimal supplements and meds. Only lithium and DHA. We eat iodine-rich food, and still take zeolites that were a game changer for us, but even that is eliminating. I am taking for myself only DHA.
> 12) I do CT, cold shower, and I am starting Lake swimming/dipping. My son says he is doing cold shower, but who knows.
> 13) My son drinks deuterium depleted water--expensive but worth it, or Alaskan mountain water that I buy.
> 14) I have a Magnetico pad for his bed.
>
> I am listing all of this to be clear that I went to an extreme in mitigating and biohacking, after reading Jack's blog on these topics 3-5 times. Even so, I got lucky because my son is only 14 AND because he was very healthy pre-covid by all visible standards, he has a good tan, black hair, dark colored eyes, excellent body composition, 20-20 vision, highly sensitive taste and smell buttons, so he had still a relatively good foundation that got spectacularly screwed up by the environment.
>
> And yet, I agree, going to lower latitude will be how I can get him whole for real.

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

# Thread: THE QUICKEST WAY TO RUIN MITO REDOX: viral contamination thru gain of function research.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quickest-way-to-ruin-mito-redox-viral-contamination-thru-gain-of-function-research.29232/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-31T15:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quickest-way-to-ruin-mito-redox-viral-contamination-thru-gain-of-function-research.29232/post-337863>

For those who do not believe viruses are real you might want to see the modern day version or Drs/ Mary Sherman and Sarah Stewart: Judy Mikovits, PhD is that person and she has been made persona non grata for her discovery on viral contamination in vaccines in 2011.

She is a biochemist and molecular biologist with more than 33 years of experience. Internationally known, a veritable “rock star” of the scientific world, she served as the director of the lab of Antiviral Drug Mechanisms at the National Cancer Institute before directing the Cancer Biology program at EpiGenX Pharmaceuticals. She later developed the first neuroimmune institute.

Her early work focused on cancer and HIV, her latest on Chronic Fatigue Syndrome and autism. She has published more than 50 peer-reviewed articles.

In 2011, she made the discovery that destroyed her career. She found that at least 30% of our vaccines are contaminated with gamma-retroviruses. Not only is this contamination associated with autism and chronic fatigue syndrome, it is also associated with Parkinson’s, Lou Gehrig’s disease, and Alzheimer’s. The retroviruses contaminating vaccines originate from mice used for research. Viruses attack how mitochondria handle electrons.

The electron transport chain in the mitochondria of humans, and mammals as a whole, reverses the process of photosynthesis in chloroplasts. Electrons, hydrogen (H+), and oxygen (O2) make ATP and water (H2O), extremely simply put. Blue light (and nnEMF, both as some of the highest energy outputs) disrupts the complex enzymes of the ETC, as well as electron transfer capacity (electron leak), K+ signaling (crucial to mitochondrial water handling), ATP synthesis, thus disrupting H2O formation as end product of the ETC. The chromophores (light sensors) that make up the ETC shift from ground, to singlet, to triplet state, which transfers electrons to O2, forming superoxide (O2•-) and a photosensitizer radical cation. That triplet state chromophore transfers energy to O2 to form singlet O2. The leakage of electrons interacts with O2 to form more O2•-. That O2•- receives more electrons from this leakage to form other reactive oxygen species (ROS), such as hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), hydroxy radicals (·OH), and perhydroxyl radicals (HO2·). Electron leakage from the ETC is caused by the disruption and subsequent damage to complex/cytochrome enzymes.

"As described above, our previous study reported that the lens exists in a hypoxic environment which is important in maintaining lens transparency, and that oxygen levels are generally lower closer to the lens. Why is putting an excimer laser in the hands of a refractive eye surgeon like giving a serial killer a universal hotel room key?

Over 3.8 billion years, sunrise’s role in TCA/urea activation evolved with light, temperature and oxygen variations to optimize energy and repair, with thanatotranscriptomic genes adapting to these variables and these biological cycles. LASIK demoloshes this stiochiometry in nanoseconds.

Daytime TCA activity stores fat reserves, reducing sleep’s metabolic burden and enhancing repair (e.g., mitophagy).
Circadian Sync: Sunrise aligns melatonin suppression with TCA/urea cycle activation, ensuring deep sleep occurs at the end of a day that kills you slowly. It works this way so your colonies of mitochondria can recover every night to give you more time on Earth. If this fails you can die rapidly in ways you cannot fathom.

This blog tells you how the thanatotranscriptomic genes evolved and what their stiochiometry is. These genes were critical in evolving wakefulness from sleep. Enhancing AM light exposure (e.g., UV-mimicking therapies) amplifies TCA/urea benefits, complementing DDW production and ideal oxygen utilization to create precision in UPE output to extend life day after day. Temperature control is critically important to this thanatotranscriptomic method. This evidence is still seen in melatonin action today. It is maximally active in the brain after a 3-4 degree drop in temperature of the CSF. LASIK destroys this relationship.

### Katie P — 2025-07-23T03:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-sunglasses-glasses-contacts-lasik-cataract-surgery-destroy-redox.29237/post-356822>

I had PRK surgery in the military 17 years ago to correct my 20/400 vision so I could beome a pilot. I consider PRK the more painful cousin of LASIK, since you have to sit in a dark room for four days while the epithelium layer healed from being sanded down. Looking back, maybe that kind of forced sensory deprivation would’ve been slightly handy after a nuclear blast or maybe the opposite is true. I also didn't follow their instructions to wear sunglasses after that first week. I've always loathed sunglasses in part because I can’t stand anything that feels like an accessory masquerading as a necessity. Plus, let’s be honest, there’s a certain personality type that seems to gravitate toward sunglasses, either to avoid eye contact, or maybe they’re just running low on dopamine. Either way, I opted for squinting.

Lately, I’ve noticed the skin around my eyes never burns or tans, leaving permanent white rings like I’m wearing phantom sunglasses. I used to think it was a redox issue, but now I wonder if it’s just leftover damage from PRK.

Even if there is damage in my eye, I've felt better over the last 2 years of following Jack's advice then I had in the 10 years prior. I also haven't been sick in the last two years, only barely a throat tingle here and there, which is something I didn't think was possible after years of being sickly under any amount of additional stress. Having a year round tan and going to bed when the sun goes down makes a huge fucking difference.

So far, I've met with a few ophthalmologists and specifically asked about removing the cataract and leaving it alone without implanting an IOL This idea was a no go from all of them. However, a highly recommended ophthalmologists suggested the Bausch and Lomb EnVista Aspire IOL which apparently allows “some light"-- but I just got word. This particular IOL allows "minimal light."

With that said, what about the following:

—Forgo the surgery and hire a chauffeur until science catches up.

—Fly to Germany or Japan where one might find someone to implant an IOL like the Teleon IOL or better.

—Wait until ES has someone who’ll implant an IOL which absorbs the entire light spectrum.

—Move to ES where the stable light might reverse the cataract?

# Thread: Why do diabetics lose their superoxide pulse and why do their WBCs not work well?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/>

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-02T06:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/post-337976>

The family of NADPH-dependent oxidases is called Nox. What do NOX do? They create ROS in cells for a PURPOSE, and that purpose is not bad. The sole function of this enzyme family is the production of ROS specifically, superoxide (O2−) Centralized science perpetrated the lie that ROS was only associated only with detrimental effects on cellular structures. The first described family member was Nox2 & Nox2 is responsible for the respiratory burst in phagocytes that drive immune function. That certainly is not a bad thing. It is necesary for life and longevity.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-02T06:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-diabetics-lose-their-superoxide-pulse-and-why-do-their-wbcs-not-work-well.29248/post-337977>

All Nox enzyme family members share a membrane-bound catalytic core structure, a glycoprotein consisting of six trans-membrane α-helical domains (the actual gp91phox in Nox2), which contains two conserved HEME groups near the N-terminus. Why is the heme domain a big deal in infection risk in a nnEMF blue lit world? Blue light destroys all heme based photoreceptors. This means blue light destroys NOX and this decreases ROS and superoxide. This is why type 1 and 2 diabetics have no superoxide pulse from cytochrome 1. NO B12 and many other non visual photoreceptors get blown up by blue light and nnEMF. I hope some of you are beginning to see how all this stuff links to the light you ABUSE. This is where chronic disease occurs.

If you have dementia never wear a hat or sun glasses.

Transcranial Near Infrared light stimulation of the brain improves dementia and cognition.

Only 6 MINUTES twice a day was enough to stimulate significant improvement in memory and other symptoms

The patients exhibited less anxiety, improved mood, energy, and positive daily routine after ~14-21 days of treatment.

Infrared light enhanced the clearance of amyloid plaque through phagocytosis, lower cytokine levels, increased NAD+, and increased ATP

This is all very beneficial to brain function.

And of course... these are frequencies of light found in SUNLIGHT

This bodes well for the safety of the treatment, as we've been exposed to infrared light from the sun for billions of years

But it also means getting outside in the sun is an accessible free therapy that is highly neuroprotective.

# Thread: El Salvador Water - Reverse Osmosis or Other Options?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thejessallen — 2024-08-03T02:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338014>

Hola amigos who live in El Salvador. How do you get decent water here? Can I buy reverse osmosis water somewhere? (I'm in La Libertad)

Does anyone sell countertop machines?

Would the under the sink filtration system actually improve the water quality from the sink?

Thanks in advance for your time and insights

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-08-03T05:45:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-water-reverse-osmosis-or-other-options.29250/post-338021>

Hola Jess!

Water is difficult, i still dont have a solution i am totally happy with yet.

I would prefer to test my tap water to know exactly what is in it but it is very expensive to do so.

RO is the best you will find and can have an undersink unit installed but you will need to get a remineraliser or use sea salt to add back minerals.

You can see the units on display at Vidri and likely get advice there.

Haven't looked for a bench top here and don't imagine they have them, but I did bring an Acqua Tru with me from the US which is a bench top RO unit which became cumbersome and we couldnt keep up with filling the small tank so its not being used any more.

Currently using Perlitas in big bottles or Evian when i can find it at Pricesmart.

Good luck.

Agree. A counter top AquaTru is great for low volume, yet requires many filters and power. I do not like the handle either; it should be one solid brass handle IMO because hands have germs, and all the little cut out slots are pockets I find are not easy to clean. I can smell it after a few uses.

Berkey gravity filters have many different sized filtration systems. I own a Berkey system for emergency situations. A multi filter system and buying bottled water on sale maybe a good approach. I buy 5 gallon Poland Spring where I live.

Are there any local springs to fill up? I have done that, it can be a pain over time. I have a lot of respect for the saying, "Carry your own water".

# Thread: Pre-bipolar, chromosome quadruplication and light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4

### Emmanuela — 2024-08-04T15:38:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/post-338064>

Ok so I am using a friend’s situation to practice my understanding of the posts and webinars i have absorbed so far.

A good friend of mine and her youngest son both have quadruplication on chromosome 8p22.
She has some physical signs of the chromosome abnormality but no mental ones.
He went from being a relatively normal and high energy child until schools reopened during covid.
His mom homeschooled almost fully outdoors instead of doing the virtual learning.
But when he returned, the schools here had teachers in suits, masks, visors, the whole 9 yard and the kid snapped.
He has been recently diagnosed as pre-bipolar.
When unmedicated, he has “random” boughts of aggressive behavior to the point of being dangerous, pulling a knife on his dad, and the like, then 5 minutes later he is sweet like it never happened.
He cannot sense the need to pee or poo until last minute.
He is much calmer and less likely to have an episode when camping or outdoors in general. His urgency to use the washroom even diminishes when camping.
He struggles with math.
The mother is working on switching light, hardwiring the house, etc.

My thoughts:
How does the eye clock go awry to that extent “all of a sudden”?

From reading the Quantum Engineering 47 and listening to the june 13 webinar, my understanding is that he probably needs his mom to be very anal about the light environment, sunrise, and high dha seafood.
I was thinking the bathroom urgency has to do with the vagus nerve connection once i read the vagus nerve mito hacking blogs.

Probably the leptin reset would help the boy too although he isn’t chubby by any means, my understanding is his circadian rhythm is way off.

I am still trying to piece together the chromosome quadruplication to understand what that really means for him.

Also I keep thinking there had to be a physical trigger other than how the return to school would have affected him emotionally.

Ill go back to digging and post my continued thoughts.

What happens in your colony of mitochondria every AM writes a story in the arteries of your flesh. ### Emmanuela — 2024-08-07T16:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-bipolar-chromosome-quadruplication-and-light.29255/post-338154>

Wait, this makes sense. So the mother’s mother has severe depression and was abused by her bipolar husband. And i was just reading a couple papers on the connection between nitric oxide signaling problems and violent behavior in children.

Then, i am reading this: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7764698/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7764698/)

And it says the SLC7A2 is located on chromosome 8p22, that’s his and his mother’s quadruplications.
The paper mentions the role of cat2 in l arginine supply for NO production.
Could the quadruplication be reducing the NO activity in general?

This means he is extremely, extremely sensitive to anything that will further reduce nitric oxide.
Maybe the question shouldn’t be why did he start exhibiting the behavior all of a sudden but rather, the constant time outside delayed the onset of the behavior?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-16T20:55:56+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-338093>

CAN THE MOON MAKE YOU CRAZY?

Before the invention of artificial light at night, also called ALAN, you had to wait for a full moon to stay up late and do bad things.
In this study we see how sleep patterns are effected by the lunar cycle. The word lunatic comes from luna = moon.

[https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.06.01.128728v1](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.06.01.128728v1)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T16:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-338094>

studies of artificial light at night (ALAN), we find each person has a different threshold for amount of brightness/lux they can tolerate at night. This is what they also find when studying moon/lunar cycle. Some people handled it much worse than others. A long time ago...They called them LUNATICS. Or werewolves.... The problem is that today we have infinitely more lunatics because you don’t have to wait for a full moon, you can simulate a 50-100x more powerful super moon every night with high powered LED tech screens & ALAN.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-05T17:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-338096>

Wisdom from @DrJackKruse from CPC #4

\- this is hard to 'super simplify'- Genes encode proteins- most of our DNA does not code for proteins- it is NOT 'junk' as many smart people once proposed- epigenetics is the connection between our environment (at all scales) and how/which genes and not-junk areas are transcribed into proteins and give us ability to 'try out' new configurations that can help us survive/adapt to environmental changes.
As is clear from Jack's work and speaking- the common pathway between our numerous environmental sensors (skin, eyes, chromophores, taste buds, light sensors, temp sensors, diet etc) -- are our mitochondria - which convert those signals down into their quantum state of electrons and protons -- and are the primary driver of epigenetic adaptation/response to the environment.
to get from 'get morning sun, live circadian and cold adapted' to the next step in understanding Jack's connection between quantum physics and biology/biochemistry -- you have to spend the time to digest and understand the above.
He makes multiple evolutionary biology cases for this even at this early stage in his blogs. (which is remarkable since I am >90 in and and still very 'early' in the process).
"Because the K-T event used a sped up epigenetic signaling program to allow eutherian mammals to sense changes in their environment quickly and adapt, modern humans have the most adaptable and fastest ability to change epigenetic program on this planet today. We use junk DNA to first change DNA expression and with sustained pressure selected for the genomic change and expanded it in survivors. This is a great modern example of how a sped up epigenetics works in us. We need to think about the good and bad it can do. It is definitely a double edged sword for modern humans."
[](https://x.com/eric_novack/article/1820454876612370694/media/1820454556171706368)
[https://jackkruse.com/cpc4-evolutionary-friend-or-foe/](https://jackkruse.com/cpc4-evolutionary-friend-or-foe/)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-31T16:44:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-moon-has-a-lesson-about-alan.29260/post-339105>

All planets with life will eventually be found to have massive ambipolar or polarization fields associated with them. Why? It tells you the planet is loaded with electrons in the ionosphere that surrounds the rock below. This global electric platform is the key to understanding why all electric currents precede basic chemistry and biochemistry and why LIGHT > FOOD at all levels of understanding. The food guru narratives continue to implode as new data comes in from space. This ambipolar electric field on Earth is generated exclusively by the outward pressure of ionospheric electrons. Most of those electrons are coming from oxygen radicals.

The ambipolar field of Earth controls the structure of the polar ionosphere, where aurora manifest, boosting the scale height by 271% based on new data generated by NASA's suborbital Endurance rocket. NASA has inferred that this increases the supply of cold O+ ions to the magnetosphere by more than 3,800%! This is a big deal because it gives us a clue why this blog is deadly acccurate in its science. This now fully explains why other mechanisms such as wave–particle interactions can heat and further accelerate them to escape velocity. The electrostatic field of Earth is strong enough by itself to drive the polar wind by itself and is probably the origin of the cold H+ ion population that dominates much of the magnetosphere that I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture on H+ and D. As is clear from Jack's work and speaking- the common pathway between our numerous environmental sensors (skin, eyes, chromophores, taste buds, light sensors, temp sensors, diet etc) -- are our mitochondria - which convert those signals down into their quantum state of electrons and protons -- and are the primary driver of epigenetic adaptation/response to the environment.
