**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changes-in-light-predict-epigenetic-programming.29261/post-338098>

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

This is why you must embrace the suck the life.
Life has a tension between comfort and discomfort. Discomfort affords us the perspective to ask better questions about our current assumptions. In my opinion, discomfort emerges from our minds when our choices are suppressing our true identity.
A great way to poison a human is to remove any hardship or struggle from his life. One of the most essential things for any leader is never letting anyone else define who you are and what you choose. Being unconventional does not mean the idea of a mito-hack cannot work. Unconventional means the prescription has not made it into conventional textbooks. This is what quantum biology is and the recent melanopsin data show us; you better pay deep attention to Black Swan mitochondriacs as your environment is made unfavorable by the trends of modern human networking.
Darwin gave us the false meme that life is about the survival of the fittest and that competition is critical to success. It is not. Why do I say this? It is about the survival of the wisest. Unless you know the epigenetic code, which is our software program that runs the genes, which are the hardware in cells, the gene of DNA has no fundamental meaning. That has made Darwin and Watson Crick's perspective meaningless for us and for medicine. This paradigm of "genomic belief" has been utterly toxic for success with illness.

Letting go of toxic environments you put yourself in can be one of the hardest things to do in life, but it is one of the most wise things one can do to change their life. Life is much less a competitive struggle for survival than a triumph of cooperation and creativity. Build your career and network around this concept and you'll win. The most important thing in understanding life's cipher is hearing what isn't being said aloud. It is analogous to what we've found in genetics. Ignoring the genes and paying attention to the junk is where the rainbow really lies. Nature never gives up her secrets easily. We must learn to ignore the noise genes create in professional science and focus on the junk DNA.

The same is true with talk. Words we use are not as critical in human communication as we believe. Just as it is the silent part of the genome that runs our DNA, in language, it is the things not said in words, but present between words that are critical. They hold the key to successful understanding in life. The art of reading between the lines is the quest of the wise mitochondriac. Always pay attention to the pauses, the nonverbal chatter, and the movement that occurs between words to understand what is really going on.

Learning is like the fuel that moves the electrons of your body toward a destination of success. Learning requires an ear that listens and an intellect that is aware and has the patience to endure mistakes. Errors are the growing pains of wisdom. The invariable mark of wisdom in you is when you see the possible in the improbable and the miraculous in the common. Wisdom is not built by time and does not comes not from age, but from education, learning, and thinking.

The irony of modern centralized science is this: Physicists/astronomers believe that if they want to understand the beginning and end of the universe they should aim understand the life cycle of the stars to unlock the mystery of the evolution of the universe. They believe if they can work out how stars change and evolve over time they believe they could begin to understand how the universes changes over time. The irony? Stars emit light and it is the light that changes the universe. None of the scientist in biology have ever thought once about how light causes the evolution of cells in living beings. Amazing contraction to me. The lesson to be learned and tattoo'd into your brain?

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.

If you get it wrong,

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-24T17:02:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changes-in-light-predict-epigenetic-programming.29261/post-371963>

The study I'm referring to and pictured below, recently published in **[npj Aging](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41514-025-00244-x)** (2025) by researchers at **Emory University** and **Baylor College of Medicine**, found that psilocin (the metabolite of psilocybin) increased the replicative lifespan of human skin and lung cells by up to **57%**. In aged mice, monthly doses increased the 10-month survival rate from **50% to 80%**, while visibly reducing signs of aging like fur whitening. I'm not into mice studies but this needs to be studied in humans.

While the researchers focused on **SIRT1 expression**, telomere preservation, and reduced oxidative stress, my framework suggests a deeper, subatomic mechanism involving **deuterium depletion** and **melanin biology**.

**Psilocybin as a "Deuterium Filter"**
In my framework, psilocybin is a "power up" because it may solve the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)** problem at the mitochondrial level:

**Mitochondrial Efficiency:** The study noted that psilocybin improves mitochondrial respiratory capacity and ATP production efficiency. In my view, this is because psilocybin-treated cells are better at delivering **deuterium-depleted water** to the ATPase pumps, preventing the "mechanical friction" caused by heavy hydrogen.

**Melanin's Role in Mushrooms:** Many mushrooms use melanin to capture ionizing radiation and convert it into metabolic energy. If psilocybin synthesis is linked to these melanized pathways, the molecule itself might be "pre-depleted" of deuterium, acting as a high-quality **methyl and acetyl donor** that doesn't "stall" enzymes like CYP2C19.

**Solar/Light Mimicry:** Interestingly, the research shows that psilocybin induces gene expression changes in the visual cortex that **closely mirror light-induced changes**, even in total darkness. This suggests psilocybin might act as a "chemical surrogate" for the solar frequencies needed to maintain the **proton tunneling** efficiency of the NF-κB and SIRT1 switches.

**Why Longevity Researchers Missed This**
Mainstream geneticists attribute the 50% lifespan extension to "gene regulation" (SIRT1, p16, p21). However, if the **Inner Mitochondrial Membrane (IMM)** charge is restored by psilocybin’s impact on De-fragging **structured water**, the "genetic" changes are merely the downstream result of a restored **40-million-volt mitochondrial battery**.

Researchers at Emory University and Baylor College of Medicine have discovered that psilocin, the active metabolite of psilocybin, can dramatically delay cellular senescence, the process where cells stop dividing and begin to age. In preclinical trials, human skin cells treated with the compound lived 51% longer, while fetal lung cells saw a staggering 57% increase in lifespan compared to untreated samples. These findings suggest that the impact of psychedelics may extend far beyond the brain, potentially influencing the fundamental biological pathways that govern how our cells resist stress and decay. This sounds like a deuterium story I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture.

**A logical step for future study:** If psilocybin’s benefit is truly about deuterium depletion, we should see a **blue shift in UPEs** (increased coherence) and an increase in the **dielectric constant** of the cellular water following treatment, essentially reversing the "biological brownout."

**CITES**
1\. [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/)
2\. [https://blogs.bcm.edu/2025/07/22/from-the-labs-can-psychedelic-mushrooms-slow-down-aging/](https://blogs.bcm.edu/2025/07/22/from-the-labs-can-psychedelic-mushrooms-slow-down-aging/)
3\. [https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/13/6/1727](https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/13/6/1727)
4\. [https://www.sciencealert.com/psilocybin-extends-life-of-human-cells-by-50-in-wild-new-study](https://www.sciencealert.com/psilocybin-extends-life-of-human-cells-by-50-in-wild-new-study)

# Thread: Does anyone know much about the MPO cardiovascular marker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7

### Daniela Lawler — 2024-08-06T17:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/post-338118>

My hubby tested very high and when I looked into it discovered it is an enzyme released by the white blood cells in response to inflammation and damage to the arteries. does anyone know how much research they is behind this marker and have any info I can take away?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-07T15:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-know-much-about-the-mpo-cardiovascular-marker.29263/post-338143>

Myeloperoxidase (MPO) is an enzyme stored in azurophilic granules of polymorphonuclear neutrophils and macrophages and released into extracellular fluid in the setting of inflammatory process and is associated with increased ROS/RNS creation due to altered mitochondrial metabolism and altered biophoton release. This is due to environmental changes that are not light/dark controlled.

The excess release of endogenous light is not creating enough UV light endogenously and this is not causing translation of POMC to create alpha beta or gamma MSH = less melanin inside and melanin inside deals with excess ROS/RNS production. As a result of this cascade, myeloperoxidase rises and it has been correlated with CVD disease because excess MPO has been linked as marker of plaque instability in PAD disease and coronary heart disease.

The cascade has many other parts associated with it discussed on the forum. See here ----> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-research.29101/page-2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-research.29101/page-2)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-water-and-magnetism-a-dr-jack-original.29277/post-338213>

I gave a presentation recently, the final piece of getting certified as a functional medicine health coach

I introduced Dr Jack Kruse (and Dr Alexis Cowan) as my mitochondriac mentors

I said Dr Jack coined the “light water and magnetism” as a 3 legged stool for mitochondrial optimization

I just came across a guy called Dr Jalal Khan. He’s speaking like Dr Jack but mentioned jack just at the very end along with others.

He even used these slides- this is what made me wonder… maybe it wasn’t jack who framed the famous “light water and magnetism” phrase?

He used the clock analogy and even mentions bitcoin.

I’m so grateful for Dr Jack and feel everyone should know his name and work.

Jalal often says “what I think is” and often followed by something I heard jack say.

It was my first presentation in many years, so maybe giving kudos to others while presenting is not cool?

He quotes Dr Doug Wallace as the expert on mitochondria

Only at the very end did he have a credits slide and quickly mention Dr Jack Kruse

Is this professional? The way it’s done? He sounded so much like Jack. It made me wonder is this what Doug Wallace sounds like? Or did this guy straight copy jack and leave that part out.

maybe because it’s I’m new - I haven’t given a presentation in decades. It felt right to mentioned Jack and Alexis often…

When someone had a health question - I said joining Jacks group is best place to get answers and Dr Alexis has lots of free content that’s worth learning.

Kind of wild - was curious what you might think?

I bought the Super Sleep System. I can get a little bit of insomnia every now and then, but nothing major.

I am doing quite well, probably 80% and just want to get to 100%, 100% for me would be if I can lift weights again. If I take it easy on exercise and have low stress, I'm feeling energy and enthusiastic nearly every day. Just need to increase my capacity for more.

I wake up early and walk in the sun for an hour within an hour of the Sunrise. I do a cold plunge every morning. I have already had someone come to my house to help minimize nnEMF in it years ago. I turn off bluethooth on my phones during the day. I turn my WiFi router off and my phones off at night when I go to sleep. My computer has IRIS software on it. I wear blue light blocking glasses at night and keep all my lights off in my home as much as possible. Etc. Etc.

So my UV is great (people comment on how tan I am often), I'm outside a lot, I do cold plunges nearly every day, and I have minimized nnEMF. I don't think your hypothesis here is accurate for me and there is something else going on here.

Dirty Electricity “DE” is an artifact, harmonic of the 60Hz (50Hz) electric field - turn off the bedroom circuits - no electric field and therefore no “DE”, no magnetic field and no RF from those circuits. “DE” is less than 5% of the electric field, the “DE” is not the problem the electric field is. A barrier/shield is good to reduce the RF coming from outside, why use a barrier to reduce “DE” - turn off the circuit. If you have electric field in the room the Magnetico COULD be picking them up and “amplifying“ them, but I don’t know, I have never tested a Magnetico.

This is not my area but a thought is - could the magnetic field from the Magnetico, because of orientation, be in conflict with the natural magnetic field of your location?

Body Voltage Meter/Kit here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2024-08-15T03:27:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/terrible-insomnia-with-magnetico.29278/post-338423>

> [TaylorJamesTJ said:](/goto/post?id=338407)
>
> I would say the room I have slept in being lower EMF isn't the argument here because I was sleeping good before the Magnetico. I am only using the other room **_because_** of the Magnetico. Only after the Magnetico did the sleep become dramatically affected. That is the **only** factor here. I think people are forgetting my sleep was good before the Magnetico and the Magnetico was the only change and **_then_** sleep became consistently terrible. I have not had sleep so bad that I only slept 1.5 hours in a night before for many years and that happened the first night with the Magnetico.

I understand what you are outlining here, however, it does not solve what is actually creating the issue. I know via the process of elimination you could say well if I eliminate the Magnetico the issue goes away. Test it with all the power isolated. You are truly flying blind here if you cannot measure what is going on when introducing a new variable (there is no baseline here). An absolute minimum would be to check body voltage. Measuring takes any assumptions and/or qualitative opinions out of the equation.

> [TaylorJamesTJ said:](/goto/post?id=338407)
>
> When the EMF expert came to my home a few years ago he did those measurements. My room was pretty good and we used a barrier behind myy bed and below it to lower the EMF and "dirty electricity" even more for sleeping, and with his tools he was saying my exposure to dirty electricity or EMF's was excellent for my bed area.

DE is only one small component. Define pretty good? This varies from one Building Biologist to another. Just because you measured a few years ago, it does not mean you will have the same results if measured today. There are many factors that could be at play here.

> [TaylorJamesTJ said:](/goto/post?id=338407)
>
> I did not experience vertigo or nausea while using the Magnetico. I have not killed the electricity in that room to test it out. What tool do you prefer to use to measure body voltage on / off?

Body voltage can be measured with a very basic multi-meter providing you already know what you are doing and can make up some earth jumpers with extensions, plenty of youtube videos out there showing you how to do this OR you could purchase Anthony's kit which I just looked at - it appears to have all you need plus extras and would be very beginner friendly and looks to be good value for the amount.

Long term - I would look at researching the EMF space so you don't need to pay a BB to measure your place and get some decent semi-professional meters. They will last a very long time providing they're looked after. When introducing new things &/or modifying existing I always measure, e.g. even if something gives you the option to disable BT or WIFI it doesn't necessarily mean it will disable it (hence the importance to measure) do not assume.

All the best.
Mat

0,5-2 microwatt/m2 in this bedroom at bedlevel.

Celltowers at +- 2-3km.
Plenty of solar pannels in street. (DE?)

> Long term - I would look at researching the EMF space so you don't need to pay a BB to measure your place and get some decent semi-professional meters. They will last a very long time providing they're looked after. When introducing new things &/or modifying existing I always measure, e.g. even if something gives you the option to disable BT or WIFI it doesn't necessarily mean it will disable it (hence the importance to measure) do not assume.
>
> All the best.
> Mat

Thank you for the detailed response! Which decent semi-professional meter do you use / have?

# Thread: Corporate Income Tax in El Salvador.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/corporate-income-tax-in-el-salvador.29289/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Heerink — 2024-08-12T18:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/corporate-income-tax-in-el-salvador.29289/post-338313>

Dr. Jack,

With access to President Bukele, can you convince his government to lower the Corporate Income Tax in El Salvador?

The current corporate income tax (CIT) rate in El Salvador is 30%. There is a reduced CIT rate of 25% for companies that obtain taxable income equal to or less than 150,000 United States dollars (USD) in the fiscal year.
However this 30% rate is more than the 21% in the USA and in the top 10% of the world.

A lower CIT would benefit their economy because companies would invest and start/move their business to El Salvador (including mine)
Especially a low rate for online business would spur economic growth.

Something like this (several countries use this model):

-   For taxable profits up to $200,000, the rate should be ~15%.
-   For taxable profits exceeding $200,000, the rate should be ~20%.

Thanks,

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-12T21:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/corporate-income-tax-in-el-salvador.29289/post-338339>

Hire an attorney for this information

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.29301/post-338419>

Dr. Kruse,

I am in the very early stages of a huge project that I intend to start on in the very near future.

I am in the process of developing my own school. I am currently a public school teacher and I do not believe in this system at all. So I will be developing my own school with the help of professionals that I truly believe in. (You, of course, being one of them).

My goal is to develop a micro-campus that allows the students to be in the best possible learning environment, which means that we need to be in natural light. As much as I would like to just “have it outside”, there will be days in which being inside is the only option. Of course, we could opt for a lot of windows allowing for natural light to come in but we know that even the windows will block full spectrum lighting/allow in too much blue light.

Would you have any thoughts or recommendations on what an optimal school would look like if you were to build one out?

This school will be starting off very small but as we become more successful, I would like to build it out into a larger campus that has the heath and well-being of the students as a top priority.

Thank you for your time!

All the best,

\-Clancy Benton

### martin.v — 2024-08-15T16:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338446>

Hello Kruse community!

I'm not sure if where you reside happens similarly, but here we can't ask for more than **one** Vitamin D lab per year, otherwise you need to pay for each one of them (very expensive). Doesn't happen with any other normal value.

Funny right? I know what you may think, this looks twisted!

I was still wondering if the Vit D study was somehow more complex in the way the biochemist can take it, do some of you know if this is the case or is it just a regular lab that Big P doesn't want you to have?

This happens in Argentina, and happens with one of the best medical providers, which makes it even more suspicious.

Anyway, wondering if you had similar stories!
Have a nice one,
Martin

### NikkiYork — 2024-08-16T08:20:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338481>

They took Vitamine D off the annual check up panel.
In California you need to specifically request it and otherwise pay for it, but it’s not expensive with… lifeextension I think is the name of the company.

Just noticed: last year Vitamine D insufficiency was <20mg/mol this year they lowered it to <10.

I spoke with a doctor from the East coast, and he said they want to really not rely on any of that test anymore, it would not indicate anything. Oh well.

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T22:17:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338597>

As someone who used to think that conspiracies are silly: I think this is not just ignorance or accidental, but a much more cynical situation. Its the doctors and insurance lobby suppressing information on purse because nowadays vitamin D levels look bad across the board, and they are not willing to say that really we should be exposed to UV from sunlight ideally, or if not possible at least via a therapy lamp because that would mean the entire dermatology society would have to put their hands up and say "mea culpa".

My friend from Germany (mostly 50's latitude) reminded me that the situation looked very different in the 70ies and 80ies. In Germany, kids and adults received UV lamp therapy in Fall and Spring (like monthly). His dad was a family doctor and its a therapy that he routinely used. Such practice is gone. Also, getting into a tanning bed in the winter was a thing in Germany that everyone did. Now only some do it, but most are afraid of the UV. I myself remember being told (I grew up in Germany) by my parents to get as deep of a tan as possible in the summer so that I stay healthy in the Winter. I don't know what my mom knew, but she went religiously to the village's pool every day where I spent all hours between school end and dinner time in the sun, swimming, sunning and playing. I turned black in the summer, and of course nobody used sunscreen. Maybe some adults took tan oil but none of the kids used sunscreen. All of this changed. It's a new conspiracy spread by doctors in the 2000's, and I heard that there was apparently even a plan to explicitly suppress any research papers that emphasize the importance of vitamin D.

Another friend of mine who grew up in a village near Quebec told me that she grew up knowing that the sun is extremely precious. All kids in the village were sent 30 minutes before the school bus comes to the bus station and were told to face east and watch the sun rise. They were told that if they don't see the sun rise, they will get sick and crazy in the winter. She told me that the entire village did that, and she grew up doing that all her childhood. All kids were sitting at the bus station bench, cold and shivering, watching the sunrise, but it was normal since the entire village did that. During recess the kids were sent out no matter the temperature, explicitly because they needed to get UV exposure during the solar noon. And they walked back around 3.30pm from school when the sun was setting. These village Canadians knew something and worshipped the sun. But of course today, all the wisdom that was practiced 20 years ago is lost because there is no more village culture anyway, it's all city and because people got all brainwashed. Now, kids are watching the iPhone in the warm room or heated car, definitely not watching the sunrise outside 30 min before the school bus comes, and definitely not as a group event.

Isn't it crazy that the intuitive wisdom was there even in the 80ies, but got lost somewhere between the 90ies and now?

### martin.v — 2024-08-19T05:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-lab-question-argentina.29308/post-338618>

> [drsongihan said:](/goto/post?id=338597)
>
> As someone who used to think that conspiracies are silly: I think this is not just ignorance or accidental, but a much more cynical situation. Its the doctors and insurance lobby suppressing information on purse because nowadays vitamin D levels look bad across the board, and they are not willing to say that really we should be exposed to UV from sunlight ideally, or if not possible at least via a therapy lamp because that would mean the entire dermatology society would have to put their hands up and say "mea culpa".
>
> My friend from Germany (mostly 50's latitude) reminded me that the situation looked very different in the 70ies and 80ies. In Germany, kids and adults received UV lamp therapy in Fall and Spring (like monthly). His dad was a family doctor and its a therapy that he routinely used. Such practice is gone. Also, getting into a tanning bed in the winter was a thing in Germany that everyone did. Now only some do it, but most are afraid of the UV. I myself remember being told (I grew up in Germany) by my parents to get as deep of a tan as possible in the summer so that I stay healthy in the Winter. I don't know what my mom knew, but she went religiously to the village's pool every day where I spent all hours between school end and dinner time in the sun, swimming, sunning and playing. I turned black in the summer, and of course nobody used sunscreen. Maybe some adults took tan oil but none of the kids used sunscreen. All of this changed. It's a new conspiracy spread by doctors in the 2000's, and I heard that there was apparently even a plan to explicitly suppress any research papers that emphasize the importance of vitamin D.
>

All kids in the village were sent 30 minutes before the school bus comes to the bus station and were told to face east and watch the sun rise. They were told that if they don't see the sun rise, they will get sick and crazy in the winter. She told me that the entire village did that, and she grew up doing that all her childhood. All kids were sitting at the bus station bench, cold and shivering, watching the sunrise, but it was normal since the entire village did that. During recess the kids were sent out no matter the temperature, explicitly because they needed to get UV exposure during the solar noon. And they walked back around 3.30pm from school when the sun was setting. These village Canadians knew something and worshipped the sun. Wops! great podcast, in fact I can literally take with me the explanation for people when they try to get away with the Vit D supplements instead of getting into the sun:

"We know for a fact that Vit D is a fat soluble molecule, when sunlight hits your skin, it takes cholesterol, converts it to Vitadmin D, but it actually sulfates it too, in order to make it water soluble so it can get pass through to your arteries and get to the liver and kidneys where it's converted (to 25OH and 1.25OH right?). When you take a Vit D supplement, it comes in seed/olive oil, so it raises your triglycerides and LDL as a consequence."

Got it now

# Thread: When Rx’s don’t reverse your condition. Next steps?  YOU MISSED THE nnEMF story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dantothep — 2024-08-16T14:36:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-338490>

“This means all diseases that are tied to the loss of electrons are COMPLETELY REVERSIBLE.” - Jack Redox RX

I thought I would write as much detail as possible in the hope that this may trigger some ideas in this learned community. I firmly believe the quote from Jack above & I know I can reverse this rather than simply manage it.

I haven’t been here for a while. I started following the protocols in 2015, they really got me out of a nightmarish hole that I found myself in whilst living and working in London for 15 years.

In case anyone views this post as negative; it is in no way. Without finding Jacks work there is no doubt in my mind that I would either be very seriously ill, sectioned or dead had I tried to work through fixing my issues (severe amnesia, energy collapses, mental health issues) in the same environment that made me sick.

I moved to southern Spain in 2016 after Jack’s advice, and my life significantly improved as a result. I moved for the 300+ days of clear sky and sun per year, the access to the beaches, the nature, the change of pace of life. I currently live about 400m above sea level, in a very low EMF house (tested regularly) surrounded by a few neighbours and nature mostly. I have had the least financial and work stress in the past 2 years than ever before.

**Symptomatology**

This is a quagmire of cycles with no consistent pattern \[apart from the seasonal one - more on that later\]. I have periods of good health, characterised by high energy, high libido, clear focused thoughts and ideation, great memory recall, easy breaths strong connection to source through meditation etc. I know what it is like to feel VITAL. I still get some symptoms in this state, and these can come on very quickly and sometimes leave just as quickly.

My issue is a lack of consistency with energy, inflammation & mood. The severity depends on where my redox is at that point in time. This can shift hourly, daily, or weekly. I have had some years with very few flare cycles. Currently this has been the worst year since 2014 when I was living in London. The “shit” cycle started in grass pollen season (late April), and I have only had 1 or 2 weeks of normal cycle since then. Its very hard for me to see a high level pattern that I can use to come up with a treatment plan.

I am here as I feel I may have missed something in Jacks teachings or maybe someone can see something that I do not.

**_General symptoms:_**

A. Energy control. I can switch between being able to perform fairily athletic abilities (vaulting, climbing, martial arts high kicks), to being out of breath when walking up stairs. This switch can be intra-daily. Although I must caveat I have never had any stamina for med-high intensity aerobic activities since I was younger. However my ability to tolerate exercise has dimished massively in the past 10 years.

B. Solar Plexus pain, distention & Mood swings & Brain Fog. It feels like a tension, that when palpitated is most sore in the midway between my back and front. This pain affects my ability to breathe from the dan tien \[belly\] as it feels like energy is unable to flow up or down past this point, as if something is swollen and prevents my diaphragm from moving fully. When this hits above a 4/10 in severity it starts to affect mental processing & my mood quite severely. My thought process becomes like wading through mud and anger and frustration present themselves. At an 8/10 I will start exhibiting severe mental health issues. Sometimes this is accompanied by blaoting and gas. Stress is the trigger here

C. Pain / Inflammation / tightness of hips, legs and feet or thoracic spine and shoulders. I do have congenital scoliosis and had a severe head trauma at the age of 16 months (falling from 2nd story window onto my skull and fracturing it). Sometimes the leg / foot pain is crippling. General tightness in the front myofascial line i.e. when arching spine.

D. Continuously swollen glands. I cannot remember a time when they weren’t swollen.

E. Stool variance. Colour and consistency vary without pattern. More vegtable fibre generally improves consistency. Wiping is more yellow than brown (every time). Sometimes greasy.

F. Muscular weakness. Comes and goes very quickly.

G. Athletes foot / Tinea Scalp. I am barefoot most of the time, I would concur that when I wear shoes from more than a day it gets worse. I have thick hair so maybe the

H. Inability to gain weight.

I. Lack of consistently deep sleep. A lot of movement in the night. But I consistently dream most nights.

J. Libido very inconsistent and not tied to energy levels. E.g. Can have low energy and high libido and have large ejaculate size.

K. Loss of urine control when urinating (intermittent).

**_Symptoms when in a longer term shit cycle:_**

-   Severe loss of weight. From 62 to 55kg in a week or two. Max weight ever was 65KG. Height is 173Cm.
-   Suicidal tendencies.
-   Side of head pressure headaches.
-   Tinnitus
-   Swollen hands and face with reynauds (used to be only winter, but now any time).
-   Hands break out in blisters (which taste of iron), then are itchy and crack and bleed.
-   Extreme exhaustion, needing to sleep up 18 hours per day.
-   Amnesia
-   Dizziness
-   Short of breath (Hypoxic)
-   Tremors
-   Piles
-   Mild Jaundice (bilirubin levels always within ref).
-   Pins and Needles
-   Tremors
-   Heart palpitations
-   Jaw pain

**Background Context

_Home Environment:_**

0.0010 mw/m2 or lower \[RF\]
0.06 uT or lower \[Mag\]
3.75 \[Elec\]
400 PPM CO2
30 to 50 RH
0.006 or lower TVOC
9 PM2.5
11 PM10

**_AM Routine \[when working\]_**

-   Up at sunrise or just before.
-   Meditate 10-30 mins
-   Qi Gong / general movement / 40 min walk
-   Breakfast
-   Work outside on laptop \[I run a semi-automated UK business from Spain\]
-   Strength training if energy allows.

**_RX’s that I implement regularly (at least 80% of the time):_**

1.  Sungazing at sunrise and sunset.
2.  Sunbathing through the year, with 90% of clothing removed. No skin burn.
3.  Water delivered from the best local spring (approx 100km away).
4.  No Phones near body without being on airplane mode.
5.  Eating food seasonally and as locally as possible. Try and make the last meal of the day the smallest
6.  In winter regular Redlight sessions (x2 per week), sauna x 1 per week

Whilst all these protocols helped, I still had issues that would appear cyclically. So I continued to search, I’ve seen many, FM docs, natropaths, healers of varying types. Some worked for a while, but none of the treatments held for long. Things that have helped:

A. Regular meditation. Various techniques learned. From vipassana to dynamic meditation (living in every moment with more awareness).

B. Fighting Monkey (western style Qi Gong), has been very helpful in regaining child like mobility. [https://fightingmonkey.net/](https://fightingmonkey.net/)

C. Plant medicines & therapy (helped to heal past traumas and some unconscious behaviours)

D. Tuning forks.

**_Allopathic Tests (over the past 4 years) with some clinical significance._**

+Using SNPs to find that I have \[very mild\] Hemachromatosis. Initial ferritin was over 200, after 2 sessions (approx 400ml of blood letting)

It was back to around 50, and maintenance is needed once a year (sometimes longer). I am of the belief that environment trumps SNPS, so have put less focus on this. See attached for the profiles. Clearly challenged in serotonin and dopamine focused SNPS however.

+Very slowly declining pattern of insulin and peptide C and cyclically high Amylase and lipase. Although HB1AC is stabilised at 5.6 (pre pre-diabetic ref levels). Having never tested this at young age could I have always had low insulin? I did mange to get this down in winter in 2021 with Red Light Therapy 30cm/w2 for max 3 mins.

+MRI’s for Iron accumulation = clear.

+MRI’s liver, pancreas, and ducts for any sinister growth = clear

+Ultrasound of renal and gallbladder = clear

+Endoscopy & Colonoscopy = clear

+H Pylori = Clear

**_Past History_**

-   Jaundice at birth
-   Fractured skull at 18 months
-   Undiagnosed stomach ulcer from 12 to 23 years old. Fix was 3 days of antibiotics (such relief post treatment)
-   Asthma and Eczema as child.
-   Hernia (left inguinal)

**Triggers**

\+ Grass pollen season. In Spain it’s earlier than the UK. This always leaves me a state of exhaustion and puts me in the “shit” cycle.
\+ High levels of acute stress. Although I am MUCH better equipped to deal with this now.
\+ High food intake with little movement.
\+ Too much alcohol. TBH I am find alcohol less and less appealing as I age.

**Other Observations**

Vit D OH25 - is around 60-70 in summer and 30-40 in winter.

120 Min insulin curve was fine even with low insulin and pep C.

Butyeko course - I took on a Butyeko tutor and tried for 2 months, and improved my control pause. And then a shit cycle kicked in and my scores went back down and I lost the motivation to dedicate 1.5 hours a day to it. CP is around 20.

MDMA - After a dose I have noticed a decrease in the solar plexus pain, more focus and more stability in general, which has led to the belief that I may well be producing too much serotonin. However due to MDMA’s neurotoxicity this isn’t a long term solution. Other non selective agonists I have tried don’t have the same long lasting effect. Although I am yet to try micro-dosing of LSD25.

Cannot tolerate high fat meals especially. Lamb fat.

Frozen (3 week) raw organic calf liver smoothies. These always had a positive effect on me until this year.

Hair and nails grow very quickly compared to others in the family.

Methylene Blue makes me anxious and edgy.

Aspirin & Coffee - I know this isn’t a long term solution due to a-MSH down regulation, but it combined with microdosing of coffee (cannot handle more than half a cup of Arabica, due to it being highly stimulating for me) can give me some temporary relief.

Supplements - I use various minerals but not daily. I have tried most things with varying degrees of success, I don’t feel anything exogenous should be used for long periods.

Pharmacuticals - I haven't used them in years.

**Proposed next course of action**

A. Look into VDR Fok1 (+/+, GG) and test 1,25-hydroxy D. Could this be why as a rule I'm worse in summer?

B. High dose creatine. I have found a few anecdotal cases of people who struggled to put on weight, and had wild energy variance that have used creatine with some success.

C. Moving to the Spanish coast. Although a higher pop density, this would give me the ability to live at sea level and spend most of my time at or nr the beach.

D. Moving to low density equatorial zone to allow things to heal, before returning back to Spain. How long is enough? Where is best?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-20T22:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-338722>

What is the “Comet” do you mean “Cornet”?
The body voltage meter measures electric fiends that have been induced into the body which acts as an antenna and by giving them a path of least resistance to ground. It does NOT measure RF. Electric fields in the home (60Hz / 50Hz) radiate out 6ft - 8ft from the source. The S&SP2 measures RF. You can pick up, measure RF from miles away. Electric fields are basically steady whereas RF comes in waves, or a better analogy in bullets. This is where sample rates come in. I believe the TF2 is missing many/most of the bullets because with 4G/5G they are too fast for the sensor. I believe the TF2 would be great in El Salvador where, it is my understanding, there is only 3G. The electric field measurement on the TF2 and Cornet meters is a “spot” reading. Great for outside under power lines etc but not accurate enough for bedroom readings.

### rtd — 2024-08-20T22:19:23+0300

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=338679)
>
> How can one be sure that S&SP2 isn't giving false positives?
>
> Is there a way of a calibration testing?

Try finding a calibration lab? that could cost some $$. When I worked in automated testing, devices that took measurements- DMMs, frequency counters, etc- had a calibration schedule. Any test results captured outside the calibration date were considered null and void. There is an entire industry set around instrument calibration.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-31T16:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-339103>

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=338490)
>
> “This means all diseases that are tied to the loss of electrons are COMPLETELY REVERSIBLE.” - Jack Redox RX
>
> I thought I would write as much detail as possible in the hope that this may trigger some ideas in this learned community. I firmly believe the quote from Jack above & I know I can reverse this rather than simply manage it.
>
> I haven’t been here for a while. I started following the protocols in 2015, they really got me out of a nightmarish hole that I found myself in whilst living and working in London for 15 years.
>
> In case anyone views this post as negative; it is in no way. Without finding Jacks work there is no doubt in my mind that I would either be very seriously ill, sectioned or dead had I tried to work through fixing my issues (severe amnesia, energy collapses, mental health issues) in the same environment that made me sick.
>
> I moved to southern Spain in 2016 after Jack’s advice, and my life significantly improved as a result. I moved for the 300+ days of clear sky and sun per year, the access to the beaches, the nature, the change of pace of life. I currently live about 400m above sea level, in a very low EMF house (tested regularly) surrounded by a few neighbours and nature mostly. I have had the least financial and work stress in the past 2 years than ever before.
>
> **Symptomatology**
>
> This is a quagmire of cycles with no consistent pattern \[apart from the seasonal one - more on that later\]. I have periods of good health, characterised by high energy, high libido, clear focused thoughts and ideation, great memory recall, easy breaths strong connection to source through meditation etc. I know what it is like to feel VITAL. I still get some symptoms in this state, and these can come on very quickly and sometimes leave just as quickly.
>
> My issue is a lack of consistency with energy, inflammation & mood. The severity depends on where my redox is at that point in time. This can shift hourly, daily, or weekly. I have had some years with very few flare cycles. Currently this has been the worst year since 2014 when I was living in London. The “shit” cycle started in grass pollen season (late April), and I have only had 1 or 2 weeks of normal cycle since then. Its very hard for me to see a high level pattern that I can use to come up with a treatment plan.
>
> I am here as I feel I may have missed something in Jacks teachings or maybe someone can see something that I do not.
>
> **_General symptoms:_**
>
> A. Energy control. I can switch between being able to perform fairily athletic abilities (vaulting, climbing, martial arts high kicks), to being out of breath when walking up stairs. This switch can be intra-daily. Although I must caveat I have never had any stamina for med-high intensity aerobic activities since I was younger. However my ability to tolerate exercise has dimished massively in the past 10 years.
>
> B. Solar Plexus pain, distention & Mood swings & Brain Fog. It feels like a tension, that when palpitated is most sore in the midway between my back and front. This pain affects my ability to breathe from the dan tien \[belly\] as it feels like energy is unable to flow up or down past this point, as if something is swollen and prevents my diaphragm from moving fully. When this hits above a 4/10 in severity it starts to affect mental processing & my mood quite severely. My thought process becomes like wading through mud and anger and frustration present themselves. At an 8/10 I will start exhibiting severe mental health issues. Sometimes this is accompanied by blaoting and gas. Stress is the trigger here
>
> C. Pain / Inflammation / tightness of hips, legs and feet or thoracic spine and shoulders. I do have congenital scoliosis and had a severe head trauma at the age of 16 months (falling from 2nd story window onto my skull and fracturing it). Sometimes the leg / foot pain is crippling. General tightness in the front myofascial line i.e. when arching spine.
>
> D. Continuously swollen glands. I cannot remember a time when they weren’t swollen.
>
> E. Stool variance. Colour and consistency vary without pattern. More vegtable fibre generally improves consistency. Wiping is more yellow than brown (every time). Sometimes greasy.
>
> F. Muscular weakness. Comes and goes very quickly.
>
> G. Athletes foot / Tinea Scalp. I am barefoot most of the time, I would concur that when I wear shoes from more than a day it gets worse. I have thick hair so maybe the
>
> H. Inability to gain weight.
>
> I. Lack of consistently deep sleep. A lot of movement in the night. But I consistently dream most nights.
>
> J. Libido very inconsistent and not tied to energy levels. E.g. Can have low energy and high libido and have large ejaculate size.
>
> K. Loss of urine control when urinating (intermittent).
>
> **_Symptoms when in a longer term shit cycle:_**
>
>
> -   Severe loss of weight. From 62 to 55kg in a week or two. Max weight ever was 65KG. Height is 173Cm.
> -   Suicidal tendencies.
> -   Side of head pressure headaches.
> -   Tinnitus
> -   Swollen hands and face with reynauds (used to be only winter, but now any time).
> -   Hands break out in blisters (which taste of iron), then are itchy and crack and bleed.
> -   Extreme exhaustion, needing to sleep up 18 hours per day.
> -   Amnesia
> -   Dizziness
> -   Short of breath (Hypoxic)
> -   Tremors
> -   Piles
> -   Mild Jaundice (bilirubin levels always within ref).
> -   Pins and Needles
> -   Tremors
> -   Heart palpitations
> -   Jaw pain
>
> **Background Context
>
> _Home Environment:_**
>
> 0.0010 mw/m2 or lower \[RF\]
> 0.06 uT or lower \[Mag\]
> 3.75 \[Elec\]
> 400 PPM CO2
> 30 to 50 RH
> 0.006 or lower TVOC
> 9 PM2.5
> 11 PM10
>
> **_AM Routine \[when working\]_**
>
> -   Up at sunrise or just before.
> -   Meditate 10-30 mins
> -   Qi Gong / general movement / 40 min walk
> -   Breakfast
> -   Work outside on laptop \[I run a semi-automated UK business from Spain\]
> -   Strength training if energy allows.
>
> **_RX’s that I implement regularly (at least 80% of the time):_**
>
> 1.  Sungazing at sunrise and sunset.
> 2.  Sunbathing through the year, with 90% of clothing removed. No skin burn.
> 3.  Water delivered from the best local spring (approx 100km away).
> 4.  No Phones near body without being on airplane mode.
> 5.  Eating food seasonally and as locally as possible. Try and make the last meal of the day the smallest
> 6.  In winter regular Redlight sessions (x2 per week), sauna x 1 per week
>
>
> Whilst all these protocols helped, I still had issues that would appear cyclically. So I continued to search, I’ve seen many, FM docs, natropaths, healers of varying types. Some worked for a while, but none of the treatments held for long. Things that have helped:
>
> A. Regular meditation. Various techniques learned. From vipassana to dynamic meditation (living in every moment with more awareness).
>
> B. Fighting Monkey (western style Qi Gong), has been very helpful in regaining child like mobility. [https://fightingmonkey.net/](https://fightingmonkey.net/)
>
> C. Plant medicines & therapy (helped to heal past traumas and some unconscious behaviours)
>
> D. Tuning forks.
>
>
> **_Allopathic Tests (over the past 4 years) with some clinical significance._**
>
> +Using SNPs to find that I have \[very mild\] Hemachromatosis. Initial ferritin was over 200, after 2 sessions (approx 400ml of blood letting)
>
> It was back to around 50, and maintenance is needed once a year (sometimes longer). I am of the belief that environment trumps SNPS, so have put less focus on this. See attached for the profiles. Clearly challenged in serotonin and dopamine focused SNPS however.
>
> +Very slowly declining pattern of insulin and peptide C and cyclically high Amylase and lipase. Although HB1AC is stabilised at 5.6 (pre pre-diabetic ref levels). Having never tested this at young age could I have always had low insulin? I did mange to get this down in winter in 2021 with Red Light Therapy 30cm/w2 for max 3 mins.
>
> +MRI’s for Iron accumulation = clear.
>
> +MRI’s liver, pancreas, and ducts for any sinister growth = clear
>
> +Ultrasound of renal and gallbladder = clear
>
> +Endoscopy & Colonoscopy = clear
>
> +H Pylori = Clear
>
> **_Past History_**
>
> -   Jaundice at birth
> -   Fractured skull at 18 months
> -   Undiagnosed stomach ulcer from 12 to 23 years old. Fix was 3 days of antibiotics (such relief post treatment)
> -   Asthma and Eczema as child.
> -   Hernia (left inguinal)
>
> **Triggers**
>
> \+ Grass pollen season. In Spain it’s earlier than the UK. This always leaves me a state of exhaustion and puts me in the “shit” cycle.
> \+ High levels of acute stress. Although I am MUCH better equipped to deal with this now.
> \+ High food intake with little movement.
> \+ Too much alcohol. TBH I am find alcohol less and less appealing as I age.
>
> **Other Observations**
>
> Vit D OH25 - is around 60-70 in summer and 30-40 in winter.
>
> 120 Min insulin curve was fine even with low insulin and pep C.
>
> Butyeko course - I took on a Butyeko tutor and tried for 2 months, and improved my control pause. And then a shit cycle kicked in and my scores went back down and I lost the motivation to dedicate 1.5 hours a day to it. CP is around 20.
>
> MDMA - After a dose I have noticed a decrease in the solar plexus pain, more focus and more stability in general, which has led to the belief that I may well be producing too much serotonin. However due to MDMA’s neurotoxicity this isn’t a long term solution. Other non selective agonists I have tried don’t have the same long lasting effect. Although I am yet to try micro-dosing of LSD25.
>
> Cannot tolerate high fat meals especially. Lamb fat.
>
> Frozen (3 week) raw organic calf liver smoothies. These always had a positive effect on me until this year.
>
> Hair and nails grow very quickly compared to others in the family.
>
> Methylene Blue makes me anxious and edgy.
>
> Aspirin & Coffee - I know this isn’t a long term solution due to a-MSH down regulation, but it combined with microdosing of coffee (cannot handle more than half a cup of Arabica, due to it being highly stimulating for me) can give me some temporary relief.
>
> Supplements - I use various minerals but not daily. I have tried most things with varying degrees of success, I don’t feel anything exogenous should be used for long periods.
>
> Pharmacuticals - I haven't used them in years.
>
>
> **Proposed next course of action**
>
> A. Look into VDR Fok1 (+/+, GG) and test 1,25-hydroxy D. Could this be why as a rule I'm worse in summer?
>
> B. High dose creatine. I have found a few anecdotal cases of people who struggled to put on weight, and had wild energy variance that have used creatine with some success.
>
> C. Moving to the Spanish coast. Although a higher pop density, this would give me the ability to live at sea level and spend most of my time at or nr the beach.
>
> D. Moving to low density equatorial zone to allow things to heal, before returning back to Spain. How long is enough? Where is best?

So, I got a full Building Biology test from a great assessor based out of Barcelona.
Biggest learningthanks [@Saichi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23447/) & Antony for the heads up that the Cornet TF2 is a DANGER by putting people in a sense of false security. It was accurate as a Gauss meter but not for anything else. ELF's were missed completely, RF's were under measured.
**High level results (using BuildBioGuidelines-2015-englisch ratings)**
ELF = severe anomaly in some rooms
RF = severe anomaly in some rooms
BV = extreme anomaly
Magnetic fields = Safe
Ground Potential of the electrical system = 74ohms. Should be below 4.
Glyphosate and Cypermethrin = extreme anomaly
\----
So all in all, environmental pollutants cause disease for me once again! Now to try and find somewhere safe to move!
Wish me luck.

### Woodstock — 2024-10-23T18:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-341436>

> [dantothep said:](/goto/post?id=338490)
>
> “This means all diseases that are tied to the loss of electrons are COMPLETELY REVERSIBLE.” - Jack Redox RX
>
> I thought I would write as much detail as possible in the hope that this may trigger some ideas in this learned community. I firmly believe the quote from Jack above & I know I can reverse this rather than simply manage it.
>
> I haven’t been here for a while. I started following the protocols in 2015, they really got me out of a nightmarish hole that I found myself in whilst living and working in London for 15 years.
>
> In case anyone views this post as negative; it is in no way. Without finding Jacks work there is no doubt in my mind that I would either be very seriously ill, sectioned or dead had I tried to work through fixing my issues (severe amnesia, energy collapses, mental health issues) in the same environment that made me sick.
>
> I moved to southern Spain in 2016 after Jack’s advice, and my life significantly improved as a result. I moved for the 300+ days of clear sky and sun per year, the access to the beaches, the nature, the change of pace of life. I currently live about 400m above sea level, in a very low EMF house (tested regularly) surrounded by a few neighbours and nature mostly. I have had the least financial and work stress in the past 2 years than ever before.
>
> **Symptomatology**
>
> This is a quagmire of cycles with no consistent pattern \[apart from the seasonal one - more on that later\]. I have periods of good health, characterised by high energy, high libido, clear focused thoughts and ideation, great memory recall, easy breaths strong connection to source through meditation etc. I know what it is like to feel VITAL. I still get some symptoms in this state, and these can come on very quickly and sometimes leave just as quickly.
>
> My issue is a lack of consistency with energy, inflammation & mood. The severity depends on where my redox is at that point in time. This can shift hourly, daily, or weekly. I have had some years with very few flare cycles. Currently this has been the worst year since 2014 when I was living in London. The “shit” cycle started in grass pollen season (late April), and I have only had 1 or 2 weeks of normal cycle since then. Its very hard for me to see a high level pattern that I can use to come up with a treatment plan.
>
> I am here as I feel I may have missed something in Jacks teachings or maybe someone can see something that I do not.
>
> **_General symptoms:_**
>
> A. Energy control. I can switch between being able to perform fairily athletic abilities (vaulting, climbing, martial arts high kicks), to being out of breath when walking up stairs. This switch can be intra-daily. Although I must caveat I have never had any stamina for med-high intensity aerobic activities since I was younger. However my ability to tolerate exercise has dimished massively in the past 10 years.
>
> B. Solar Plexus pain, distention & Mood swings & Brain Fog. It feels like a tension, that when palpitated is most sore in the midway between my back and front. This pain affects my ability to breathe from the dan tien \[belly\] as it feels like energy is unable to flow up or down past this point, as if something is swollen and prevents my diaphragm from moving fully. When this hits above a 4/10 in severity it starts to affect mental processing & my mood quite severely. My thought process becomes like wading through mud and anger and frustration present themselves. At an 8/10 I will start exhibiting severe mental health issues. Sometimes this is accompanied by blaoting and gas. Stress is the trigger here
>
> C. Pain / Inflammation / tightness of hips, legs and feet or thoracic spine and shoulders. I do have congenital scoliosis and had a severe head trauma at the age of 16 months (falling from 2nd story window onto my skull and fracturing it). Sometimes the leg / foot pain is crippling. General tightness in the front myofascial line i.e. when arching spine.
>
> D. Continuously swollen glands. I cannot remember a time when they weren’t swollen.
>
> E. Stool variance. Colour and consistency vary without pattern. More vegtable fibre generally improves consistency. Wiping is more yellow than brown (every time). Sometimes greasy.
>
> F. Muscular weakness. Comes and goes very quickly.
>
> G. Athletes foot / Tinea Scalp. I am barefoot most of the time, I would concur that when I wear shoes from more than a day it gets worse. I have thick hair so maybe the
>
> H. Inability to gain weight.
>
> I. Lack of consistently deep sleep. A lot of movement in the night. But I consistently dream most nights.
>
> J. Libido very inconsistent and not tied to energy levels. E.g. Can have low energy and high libido and have large ejaculate size.
>
> K. Loss of urine control when urinating (intermittent).
>
> **_Symptoms when in a longer term shit cycle:_**
>
>
> -   Severe loss of weight. From 62 to 55kg in a week or two. Max weight ever was 65KG. Height is 173Cm.
> -   Suicidal tendencies.
> -   Side of head pressure headaches.
> -   Tinnitus
> -   Swollen hands and face with reynauds (used to be only winter, but now any time).
> -   Hands break out in blisters (which taste of iron), then are itchy and crack and bleed.
> -   Extreme exhaustion, needing to sleep up 18 hours per day.
> -   Amnesia
> -   Dizziness
> -   Short of breath (Hypoxic)
> -   Tremors
> -   Piles
> -   Mild Jaundice (bilirubin levels always within ref).
> -   Pins and Needles
> -   Tremors
> -   Heart palpitations
> -   Jaw pain
>
> **Background Context
>
> _Home Environment:_**
>
> 0.0010 mw/m2 or lower \[RF\]
> 0.06 uT or lower \[Mag\]
> 3.75 \[Elec\]
> 400 PPM CO2
> 30 to 50 RH
> 0.006 or lower TVOC
> 9 PM2.5
> 11 PM10
>
> **_AM Routine \[when working\]_**
>
> -   Up at sunrise or just before.
> -   Meditate 10-30 mins
> -   Qi Gong / general movement / 40 min walk
> -   Breakfast
> -   Work outside on laptop \[I run a semi-automated UK business from Spain\]
> -   Strength training if energy allows.
>
> **_RX’s that I implement regularly (at least 80% of the time):_**
>
> 1.  Sungazing at sunrise and sunset.
> 2.  Sunbathing through the year, with 90% of clothing removed. No skin burn.
> 3.  Water delivered from the best local spring (approx 100km away).
> 4.  No Phones near body without being on airplane mode.
> 5.  Eating food seasonally and as locally as possible. Try and make the last meal of the day the smallest
> 6.  In winter regular Redlight sessions (x2 per week), sauna x 1 per week
>
>
> Whilst all these protocols helped, I still had issues that would appear cyclically. So I continued to search, I’ve seen many, FM docs, natropaths, healers of varying types. Some worked for a while, but none of the treatments held for long. Things that have helped:
>
> A. Regular meditation. Various techniques learned. From vipassana to dynamic meditation (living in every moment with more awareness).
>
> B. Fighting Monkey (western style Qi Gong), has been very helpful in regaining child like mobility. [https://fightingmonkey.net/](https://fightingmonkey.net/)
>
> C. Plant medicines & therapy (helped to heal past traumas and some unconscious behaviours)
>
> D. Tuning forks.
>
>
> **_Allopathic Tests (over the past 4 years) with some clinical significance._**
>
> +Using SNPs to find that I have \[very mild\] Hemachromatosis. Initial ferritin was over 200, after 2 sessions (approx 400ml of blood letting)
>
> It was back to around 50, and maintenance is needed once a year (sometimes longer). I am of the belief that environment trumps SNPS, so have put less focus on this. See attached for the profiles. Clearly challenged in serotonin and dopamine focused SNPS however.
>
> +Very slowly declining pattern of insulin and peptide C and cyclically high Amylase and lipase. Although HB1AC is stabilised at 5.6 (pre pre-diabetic ref levels). Having never tested this at young age could I have always had low insulin? I did mange to get this down in winter in 2021 with Red Light Therapy 30cm/w2 for max 3 mins.
>
> +MRI’s for Iron accumulation = clear.
>
> +MRI’s liver, pancreas, and ducts for any sinister growth = clear
>
> +Ultrasound of renal and gallbladder = clear
>
> +Endoscopy & Colonoscopy = clear
>
> +H Pylori = Clear
>
> **_Past History_**
>
> -   Jaundice at birth
> -   Fractured skull at 18 months
> -   Undiagnosed stomach ulcer from 12 to 23 years old. Fix was 3 days of antibiotics (such relief post treatment)
> -   Asthma and Eczema as child.
> -   Hernia (left inguinal)
>
> **Triggers**
>
> \+ Grass pollen season. In Spain it’s earlier than the UK. This always leaves me a state of exhaustion and puts me in the “shit” cycle.
> \+ High levels of acute stress. Although I am MUCH better equipped to deal with this now.
> \+ High food intake with little movement.
> \+ Too much alcohol. TBH I am find alcohol less and less appealing as I age.
>
> **Other Observations**
>
> Vit D OH25 - is around 60-70 in summer and 30-40 in winter.
>
> 120 Min insulin curve was fine even with low insulin and pep C.
>
> Butyeko course - I took on a Butyeko tutor and tried for 2 months, and improved my control pause. And then a shit cycle kicked in and my scores went back down and I lost the motivation to dedicate 1.5 hours a day to it. CP is around 20.
>
> MDMA - After a dose I have noticed a decrease in the solar plexus pain, more focus and more stability in general, which has led to the belief that I may well be producing too much serotonin. However due to MDMA’s neurotoxicity this isn’t a long term solution. Other non selective agonists I have tried don’t have the same long lasting effect. Although I am yet to try micro-dosing of LSD25.
>
> Cannot tolerate high fat meals especially. Lamb fat.
>
> Frozen (3 week) raw organic calf liver smoothies. These always had a positive effect on me until this year.
>
> Hair and nails grow very quickly compared to others in the family.
>
> Methylene Blue makes me anxious and edgy.
>
> Aspirin & Coffee - I know this isn’t a long term solution due to a-MSH down regulation, but it combined with microdosing of coffee (cannot handle more than half a cup of Arabica, due to it being highly stimulating for me) can give me some temporary relief.
>
> Supplements - I use various minerals but not daily. I have tried most things with varying degrees of success, I don’t feel anything exogenous should be used for long periods.
>
> Pharmacuticals - I haven't used them in years.
>
>
> **Proposed next course of action**
>
> A. Look into VDR Fok1 (+/+, GG) and test 1,25-hydroxy D. Could this be why as a rule I'm worse in summer?
>
> B. High dose creatine. I have found a few anecdotal cases of people who struggled to put on weight, and had wild energy variance that have used creatine with some success.
>
> C. Moving to the Spanish coast. Although a higher pop density, this would give me the ability to live at sea level and spend most of my time at or nr the beach.
>
> D. Moving to low density equatorial zone to allow things to heal, before returning back to Spain. How long is enough? Where is best?

Reading through this I was noticing a lot of similarities. When I got to the part about MDMA and LSD I just wanted to let you know that I don’t think this will help and might mess up your brain even further. Let’s just say I was a guinea pig for a “guru” who said it would heal the synapses in my brain along with other substances used for “healing”. Let’s just say it was a learning experience. With zero help and a shit ton of gaslighting from centralized medicine, I was desperate at the time. It helped temporarily with pain etc but the rebound was hell and my overall health took a serious nose dive. Possibly microdose would be ok but with a history of head injury, I think you are better off following Dr Jack’s advice.

### andrea39 — 2024-10-23T19:01:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-rx%E2%80%99s-don%E2%80%99t-reverse-your-condition-next-steps-you-missed-the-nnemf-story.29315/post-341439>

I'm wondering if you are having difficulty digesting fats. Your stool color variance is one indicator. Yellowish or pale colored stools might indicate low levels of bile being released from your gall bladder. Adequate amounts of bile are necessary for full fat digestion. Greasy stools is another sign that much of the ingested fat is passing through (and out of) your digestive system instead of being digested and absorbed. Have you had any stool analyses done that evaluate your ability to digest fats, and all your foods? An inability to gain weight is another indicator that you are not properly digesting and absorbing your food - proteins and carbohydrate digestion might also be incomplete. Of course, bloating and gas would be common with this scenario.

I also wonder if you have an imbalance of your gut microflora - I wouldn't be surprised if you have an overgrowth of yeast or fungus, given your other symptoms of fungal overgrowth on skin and scalp.

TBI's make one more susceptible to harmful EMF's, as your blood-brain barrier has already been compromised. I met a lady at Environmental Health Center-Dallas (EHC-D) who's disabled from EHS, and she told me to buy the book "EMF Freedom," which goes into detail about all the symptoms of EHS. I stay fatigued all the time, which she said is because of EMF exposure. I'm taking a cruise next week to Mexico in order to get my redox up, but I'm afraid it won't help. I've been reading forum entries of other people who have altogether moved or taken month-long sabbaticals to places closer to the equator, but unfortunately they're still suffering. Is improved redox the only thing that's gonna help me? I'm just so tired and desperate to get better. The Center has recommended chelation IV and hyperbaric oxygen therapy. They also had me do skin testing so they can formulate neurotransmitter antigens I'll have to self-administer intravenously. Microwave poisoning is insidious; these bad frequencies are all around us. I'm not religious, but I'm calling it "spiritual warfare" because you're being attacked by something you can't see. I hope you get better. I'm not giving up, so don't you, either.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-08-16T21:21:42+0300

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T21:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-good-podcast-by-someone-telling-the-same-story-about-the-world-covid-and-human-behavior-as-jack.29317/post-338592>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=338515)
>
> I have listened to Dr Bret Weinstein over the years. Always interesting.

I think in this forum many of us have the experience of listening to people and knowing intuitively after 1 podcast whether that person is seeking the truth or driving an agenda. The truth seeker can be wrong, in fact very wrong, but it does not matter because the truth seeker will keep renovating, revising and unlearning and relearning until he/she gets it right. Listening to Dr. Weinstein it was clear to me that he is a truth seeker, and that means we will always learn something from truth seekers. He did not quite get to the sunshine yet, but mentioned sun and outdoor enough, but his thinking is glass clear. I want to surround myself with people who are willing to turn over every stone, are willing to pause and think, and say "wait a moment" and are willing to loose. It is so much fun and liberating to follow that compass.

But the irony is not lost on me that I am now suddenly more aligned with folks and groups that are considered conspiracy theorists, mindless antivaccers, presumably racists or anti-feminists (of course this is not true, just a dumb narrative), and pseudo scientists!

The CIA came up with the term “Conspiracy Theorist“ as a put down, dismissive, belittling against anyone speaking out against the established view.
With COVID the “conspiracy theorists” have turned out to be the truth tellers. The term is now a badge on honor for free thinking, anti establishment, truth seekers/tellers.

There are others who also do not belong to the "intellectual dark web" that are also worth listening...

For example, George Galloway and the Mother of all Talk Shows... , more focused on politics/geopolitics.. The Duran or Scott Ritter and more..

All truth seekers **got biases** but we need to listen to all the sides to form an opinion that will probably lead us to other people with different opinions, more rabbit holes.. They also do not care about what is talked about in this forum, but there is also life outside what is talked about the subjects in here.

And of course follow the money.. Mostly everyone is getting paid by someone or have vested interests on specific outcomes.. People are getting paid in shekels, dollars, rubbles, yuan, bitcoin (no pun intended)… This is the most difficult task to check... Mostly we can infer it from the message they convey.

So the bottom line is that the theatre of operation has always been our minds.. There are a lot of really smart people out there that got amazing education, eloquence, erudition and that more importantly FREE TIME that can weave amazing stories that can put us in awe. So it is really really hard for us to separate the wheat from the chaff, so as i said above we need to look deep into the message and follow the money as a rule of thumb and god or whatever is out there help us...

### drsongihan — 2024-08-18T23:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-good-podcast-by-someone-telling-the-same-story-about-the-world-covid-and-human-behavior-as-jack.29317/post-338606>

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=338599)
>
> There are others who also do not belong to the "intellectual dark web" that are also worth listening...
>
> For example, George Galloway and the Mother of all Talk Shows... , more focused on politics/geopolitics.. The Duran or Scott Ritter and more..
>
> All truth seekers **got biases** but we need to listen to all the sides to form an opinion that will probably lead us to other people with different opinions, more rabbit holes.. They also do not care about what is talked about in this forum, but there is also life outside what is talked about the subjects in here.
>
> And of course follow the money.. Mostly everyone is getting paid by someone or have vested interests on specific outcomes.. People are getting paid in shekels, dollars, rubbles, yuan, bitcoin (no pun intended)… This is the most difficult task to check... Mostly we can infer it from the message they convey.
>
> So the bottom line is that the theatre of operation has always been our minds.. There are a lot of really smart people out there that got amazing education, eloquence, erudition and that more importantly FREE TIME that can weave amazing stories that can put us in awe. So it is really really hard for us to separate the wheat from the chaff, so as i said above we need to look deep into the message and follow the money as a rule of thumb and god or whatever is out there help us...

Agreed in essence but I need to contest for a reason. Let me know whether it makes sense after reading my post. I purposefully said "truth seeker" and not intellectual dark web.

I found truth seekers everywhere. Deep within the establishment and outside the establishment, some ready to jump out, some operating in the mids of the establishment but raising questions and some of course charting a new movement. Some are just "thinking" and some are acting, and I found both contributions add value, even if somebody is not acting yet. I myself am half thinking and half acting, and certainly have not jumped out yet in full action. But critically, I found astonishingly many more people (maybe 80%?) who are clouded or even brainwashed by a centralized propaganda. I too was. But at the moment one is faced with a good question, we have a duty to pause and think. Many are unwilling to pause and even look into information in front of their nose. I find that astonishing. Yes, we should listen to all sort of people, but I am not differentiating between factions. I mentioned the names by which some of these people are called just to point out the hypocrisy. There are as many corrupt and brainwashed thinking in any faction, for sure.

Rather, I find truth seeking to be more of a mindset and a lifestyle that has to fulfill certain conditions, and is more of an individual compass that each of us have to follow. Certainly, follow the money is one way to differentiate. But there are many other factors that cloud people's mind. It could be your ego, fear of being wrong, desire of wanting to have clout, or simply fear to have to change one's comfortable life because seeing and saying the truth can mean loosing people, loved ones, being ridiculed etc.

What I am actually contesting is the idea that "truth seeking" (which is a very high bar and everyone aspiring to it does not mean is actually achieving it) is a relative term, and I am contesting that bias is a criteria in truth seeking. Bias implies there are many opinions that we we have to consider, like in democracy. But that is not how Nature works. There is one truth, and once we found the truth, it applies to everything and it works. That also means somebody will be right and somebody will be wrong. Some theories are correct and some theories are flat out wrong. The truth seeker is NOT necessarily right (yet) but his/her core purpose is to find the truth. And once any of us think we found "the truth" we need to test it to see whether it can withstand the storm or will fall apart, abandon it when it falls apart and keep searching until we find something closer to the truth. Btw, our understanding of the universe will not be complete in any of our lifetime for sure. In that journey we know when we are directionally correct or not. But it should not be hard to immediately admit that one is wrong at the moment we realize that we are wrong or at least unsure, cost it what it may. We have to say it when we realize we are wrong. If somebody cannot do that or hides knowledge, that is a red flag and the same as lying or deceiving.

At the end of all of it, there is one truth. That truth does not need to consider all opinions or viewpoints, as long as it's the truth.

So far, I found the understanding of the world and biology to be most comprehensively understood and portrayed by Jack Kruse. He is not always right, and his understanding is incomplete. His use of terminology is sometimes rough and imprecise, but he is shining the light broadly in the right direction, and I have to say in my 20 years of career among the so called Intellectuals I have not found a better and more comprehensive truth seeking mission than this one. Dr Weinstein does not have anywhere close to a comprehensive understanding of human biology, but I could tell that he is seeking and it was refreshing. And I would be looking for the names you have listed for sure.

P.S. No worries. What you are saying is probably an important criteria in judging and looking for what is the truth. Potentially we are saying the same thing, or maybe not, I was not sure. But the reason I am responding to this is because a frequent encounter in Academia is: "why can't we all be right?". "can we not consider all viewpoints?" And my answer to that is No. Either one is right, the other is right or both are wrong. And if both parties that had differing viewpoints are "right" then this means both were wrong because the entire logical framework must have been a false one to begin with.
