# Thread: FORGING A NATION
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-18T15:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/post-338580>

### The 12 Jungian Archetypes​

1.  Ruler
2.  Creator/Artist
3.  Sage
4.  Innocent
5.  Explorer
6.  Rebel
7.  Hero
8.  Wizard
9.  Jester
10.  Everyman
11.  Lover
12.  Caregiver

A nation trying to rebuild itself needs archetypes.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My belief is that the key figure in the transition is the THE REBEL who becomes THE HERO.

### The Rebel​

-   Motto: Rules are made to be broken
-   Core desire: revenge or revolution
-   Goal: to overturn what isn’t working
-   Greatest fear: to be powerless or ineffectual
-   Strategy: disrupt, destroy, or shock
-   Weakness: crossing over to the dark side, crime
-   Talent: outrageousness, radical freedom
-   The Outlaw is also known as: The rebel, revolutionary, wild man, the misfit, or iconoclast.

### The Hero​

-   Motto: Where there’s a will, there’s a way
-   Core desire: to prove one’s worth through courageous acts
-   Goal: expert mastery in a way that improves the world
-   Greatest fear: weakness, vulnerability, being a “chicken”
-   Strategy: to be as strong and competent as possible
-   Weakness: arrogance, always needing another battle to fight
-   Talent: competence and courage
-   The Hero is also known as: The warrior, crusader, rescuer, superhero, the soldier, dragon slayer, the winner and the team player.

**What do I think is my role in forging a country?
I want to focus my energy, guard my time, constantly train and retrain the mind & body, think for myself, curate my friends and the light environment I live under, keep my promises the best I can, & stay optimistic and constructive to my tribe while upgrading the world.
**

### Michalis — 2024-08-18T16:43:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forging-a-nation.29324/post-338582>

Having looked into Jung at some point and obsessed by the notion of the shadow while I was trying to look into my dark side I can say that there might be actually be value in the whole archetype thing and particularly in the Jungian psychologist Robert Johnson.

However after some research into Jung I have started taking him with a bit of salt. Really good salesman and a bit of a charlatan too.

### [The mysterious Jung: his cult, the lies he told, and the occult](https://www.hgi.org.uk/resources/delve-our-extensive-library/interviews/mysterious-jung-his-cult-lies-he-told-and-occult)

Ivan Tyrrell asks Professor Richard Noll, author of ‘The Jung Cult’, to unravel the lies Carl G Jung told to aggrandise himself and his charismatic psychoanalytic movement.

 www.hgi.org.uk

I am.not convinced that the solution is to try to be hero's it is just unattainable for most. There are a lot of rebels out there also that they are just plain idiots.

Collective work is needed in my opinion with the brightest to lead through a democratic process. And for those that their allergies break out hearing this I don't mean collectivism. Just saying

I am not that familiar with Jung, so I looked up the 12 archetypes.
The first thing I noticed was half the charts say “Rebel” the other halve say “Outlaw”.
In my mind an “Outlaw” is not the same as a “Rebel”.
All these Archetypes may have been more accurate in 1919 but I’m not so sure they work in the same way today.

That being said, with respect, I do not see you so much as the “Rebel” becoming the “Hero” but the “Sage” - (all be it a rebellious one) .

In the “Hero’s Journey” analogy you are not the “hero” . . . You are the “Mentor” . . .You are Yoda . . . You are Gandalf - YOU invite, challenge, motivate, inspire all the other archetypes to become ”Hero's“ . . . And as such you are our hero.

# Thread: Poor Posture - a Biomechanics or a ROS problem?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SanderC — 2024-08-18T17:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338584>

When I learned from Dr. Kruse that Sleep Apnea is PROTECTIVE, this got me thinking about forward head posture.

If I understood correctly, sleep apnea is a bodily response to consume less oxygen at night because it can't handle the ROS.

What if a 'tech neck' or forward head posture follows the same process, and is not just about slouching or looking down?

Typically people who are chronically on the phone or laptop are exposed to lots of blue light and nnEMF --> Hypoxia --> Melanopsin destruction --> Melanin degradation and mitochondria dysfunction --> Body incapable to handle excess ROS --> Postural changes to consume less oxygen (neck breathing instead of diaphragmatic breathing).

Could it be?? Looking forward to hearing your thoughts!

### digitald3 — 2024-08-18T19:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338587>

I’ll wait for Dr Jacks response as well but will chime in that the pathway you described is what makes sense to me as well. And add that some will have tissue growth/expansion, tongue, posterior pharynx etc as mitochondrial power reduces and gives the appearance of a closed off airway. Which is why sleep physicians, ents and dentists work to “open” the airway without considering the underlying quantum biology.

It’s also a reason that most of these approaches will make “AHI” better they dont necessarily lead to a better or longer life.

And this is going to be a long slog to get through to the centralized docs. I got in a bit of trouble this weekend even suggesting that apnea and mitochondrial function are connected

### SanderC — 2024-08-19T14:11:36+0300

Thank you Doug! From a Quantum Biology perspective, it would make sense that poor posture is a symptom and not a cause persé.

Let's see if [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can illuminate us!

P.S. Really enjoyed your podcast with uncle Jack

### digitald3 — 2024-08-19T15:13:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338625>

In the centralized world forward head posture is a response to maintain an airway. Ie if the airway is small or the tissues are enlarged a human adjusts their head forward to open the airway to breathe. As a centralized approach is always how do we more air to the lungs

I actually agree that this is a symptom of too much blue light and ALAN and nnEMF (and this is where I’m a bit fuzzy in the science)which leads to the mitochondria using the ROS/RNS system for energy. I know I butchered that. Regardless of the mechanics there is too much ROS in the cells and the body is doing what it needs to do to squash the extra ROS and O2 by not allowing more in to maintain normal cell processes.

So yeah the blue light devices are increasing the amount of ROS in the system and then the body is working to limit extra o2 in to the system which leads to hypertrophy on the tissues and eventual forward head posture.

And we definitely need studies done with these ideas.

### digitald3 — 2024-08-19T15:40:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338630>

I’m not sure I’m making sense. Forward head posture is usually in place to create more air flow. Or that’s what we use to check for poor airways. So if the tech screens and nnEMF are creating too much cellular ROS then I’m not sure they are directly correlated. As the cells don’t want more o2. Which is what centralized apnea therapies try to do. Add more o2 phone and tech use do affect posture. So I can make a connection I just don’t think it’s as direct as forward posture reduces airflow.

And forgot to add -Thanks for listening and glad you enjoyed it!

### SanderC — 2024-08-19T17:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338639>

Thank you for spotting the flaw in my thinking!

What if it is actually both: 1) if you're sitting in front of a screen/phone your airways are compressed so your body compensates (centralized advice) but also 2) blue light and nnEMF causing hypoxia so your body tries to compensate with a first line of defense to get more oxygen in (before you have melanin degradation / mito dysfunction)

### digitald3 — 2024-08-19T18:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338644>

At this point I will say that all of the above is possible.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-20T19:22:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poor-posture-a-biomechanics-or-a-ros-problem.29326/post-338706>

Fundamentally a melanin and water problem

Will some of them be a genetic psychopath? Yes a few. Are most of them? I expect not.
Are they redeemable? Well according to that Scandinavian video above, recidivism rates are lower, so it is achieveable. Do they have comparable populations? Probably not, because of the gang affilitations and culture. But is the tabula rasa the same in that each human has an innocent template? I believe so.
How to start solving the problem? Well, split them apart from other gang members, so they're in a smaller groups that don't know each other. Move group members according to personality dynamics. Perhaps maximum 7/8. Turn off ALAN. Maximize sunlight.
Then the key? Decondition them through meditation over multiple years/decades. Get them to talk to the victims families; the non-psychos will eventually give-in an apoligize.
I just don't think penning them up in those cages with all this stuff is the right way to go about things. What's done is done, and it's terrible. Humans aren't going to get out of their own mess if they can't forgive each other, no matter how heinous the crimes.

Wow, I feel so grateful to have found this incredible community, thank you Jack for bringing together such amazing humans!
My name is Vivian (Viv) and I live in NYC and Connecticut, and have been struggling with Lyme, mold poisoning, and recurring SIBO and SIFO, chronic insomnia and gut issues and bad brain fog. I have become extremely EMF sensitive so a place like NYC with 5G density has become hard for me, I often get a fever after just a few days back for work. When I get to CT and ground on the beach and swim I start to feel better. It's been a vicious cycle. I'm planning a trip down to El Salvador with two dear friends who are also new members here, and would love any recommendations on homes to rent in walking distance to the beach. Or if you have rooms to rent please let me know too! Also, is Sept and Oct the rainy season and thus not enough sun and we should wait until Nov or Dec? Thank you in advance for any help! ### Strictlylauren — 2024-08-26T09:22:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-338910>

I developed a cataract in my right eye at 36 years old, out of the blue (last year Dec) while staying for a week in a friends mouldy/blue light ridden flat - the only option I’m given is the cataract surgery. I absolutely don’t want this but there are no other options! Do you know anything that could help guide me as to what to do besides doing the sun things and using blue light blocking glasses?? Most importantly, will the artificial lens they insert in the surgery affect my body with regards to how much light I can filter? Please help!!

### Espresso — 2024-08-26T16:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-338926>

I really don't think that the cataract developed suddenly.
The artificial lens that they fit will block UV as far as I know. Jack says that a cataract is a way that the eye tries to block blue light. I assume that this means that you must avoid all un-natural blue light and see as much natural sunlight as you can. I know that this will stop further damage, but I don't know if it will reverse it.
Unfortunately for me I discovered Jack too late and I have had the surgery. As to whether there are long term adverse effects, I will just have to wait and see.

### Espresso — 2024-08-27T12:27:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-338956>

NNEMF is non native emf.
This includes all man made lights, the suns rays altered by going through glass or plastic, radio signals (Wifi, Bluetooth, 1G, 2G, 3G, 4G, 5G), electric and magnetic fields generated by alternating current flowing in wires.
Pretty much everything that out modern society revolves around.
How much of this you need to avoid will depend on how sick you are, and how well you want to become.

> He hints that he may be working with lens manufacturers to try to make a lens that does allow unfiltered light through. I don't know if it is actually feasible? After watching this, I asked the eye surgeon who did my cataract surgery if my lenses blocked UV light. **Oh yes** she said very proudly!!!!
> You may be able to slightly mitigate the effects of not getting proper light in your eyes by being sure to get a lot of light on your skin, but I personally aren't sure of this.

I went to Dr. Jay a couple of months ago and asked a ton of questions including the idea of manufacturing an IOL which does not block UV light.

At this time, he suggests the Bausch+Lomb enVista Aspire IOL which allows “minimal light.”

### Rocky — 2024-09-14T20:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-339583>

[@Strictlylauren](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31861/)

There is a lot you can do to prevent a cataract, not much you can do to reverse one, if at all.

Although cataract surgery isn't optimal, it's not like you have much choice if you can't see. So I would avoid listening to those bringing fear about having surgery, i.e. 'surgery is like chopping off your hand etc'.

The lens in the eye has no mitochondria and without them, making changes to lens contents is not going to be easy.

Maria,

Maybe I'm not interpreting the results correctly, but this study doesn't seem to be all that exciting, per the conclusion for anything other than 'early stage'. If you are halfway or late stage you are kind of out of luck.

I wasn't told I had cataracts until last year at the age of 71, so there isn't much that can be done about them other than have surgery or go blind at some point.

Not sure why Dr. Jack would say: "Stop letting them take your patients cataracts out.", when there is no viable solution except maybe in early stage and most likely don't even know at that point.

**Conclusions:** Serum 25(OH)D levels were unrelated to nuclear opacities in this study sample. However, exploratory analyses suggest a protective association in women younger than 70 years. Further investigations of the relationship between vitamin D and nuclear lens opacities are warranted.

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-10-01T02:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-340208>

> [Rocky said:](/goto/post?id=339583)
>
> [@Strictlylauren](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31861/)
>
> There is a lot you can do to prevent a cataract, not much you can do to reverse one, if at all.
>
> Although cataract surgery isn't optimal, it's not like you have much choice if you can't see. So I would avoid listening to those bringing fear about having surgery, i.e. 'surgery is like chopping off your hand etc'.
>
> The lens in the eye has no mitochondria and without them, making changes to lens contents is not going to be easy.

The lens does have mitochondria in the epithelial tissue and mitochondrial dysfunction is linked to cataracts, as well as other eye diseases.

Super short version of events: mitochondrial breakdown => less oxygen consumed by ETC => lens is less hypoxic => damage and cataracts

Fundamental solution: address the causative agent of mitochondrial breakdown

Check out this informative study: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/)

"As described above, our previous study reported that the lens exists in a hypoxic environment which is important in maintaining lens transparency, and that oxygen levels are generally lower closer to the lens.[29](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/#R29), [30](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/#R30) Exposure to increased oxygen has been identified as a risk factor for nuclear cataract, the most common type of age-related cataract.[40](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4996752/#R40) Therefore, we hypothesized that decreased mitochondrial function in lens epithelial cells (LECs) plays a key role in cataractogenesis."

### maria@modernhuman — 2024-10-04T01:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-alternatives.29362/post-340389>

@Would swimming do this by breathing in and out so much?
answer: it's not the swimming that's the problem it's the mitochondria being impaired that they can't handle what's being done to them.

@why would increased oxygen exacerbate a cataract?
answer: did you read the study

@Why is oxygen not a good thing?
answer: oxygen is the terminal electron acceptor in the mitochondria. if something is impairing the mitochondria and they can't process the oxygen available, it turns into ROS and free radicals. it's like shoving gasoline into a donkey. poor donkey.

@Hyperbaric chamber
answer: any over-oxygenation whether from hyperbaric oxygen or high FiO2 in an intubated patient in the ICU can be damaging if it is counterproductive to mitochondrial equilibrium of electron processing

[@Just](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1383/) don’t get it....How does it actually happen
answer: knowledge of ETC mitochondrial function is needed. khan academy has some good foundational videos.

That DHA clearly signaled to the brain that the light coming in was not full-spectrum chronically, so the lens made a quantum decision and decided to change its phenotype to block the nnEMF and light you allowed your eye to sense.

DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per my paradox resolution. This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

Melanin as a Semiconductor in Human Photosynthesis: It is abundant in your eye's RPE. That Leads to Optical Computation Melanin’s Role: Melanin, absorbing UV-visible light (200-700 nm), acts as a wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor, per Kruse (Becker, Szent-Györgyi). It captures photons, generating electrons and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS)/RNS and heavy metals. Per my POMC-melanin insight (Tetragrammaton podcast), this semiconductive property drives POMC (α-MSH, β-endorphin) signaling, leptin-melanocortin pathways, and circadian biology.

Human Photosynthesis: Unlike plants’ chlorophyll (Mg-based, 400-700 nm, 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂), humans use melanin (Fe-like, 200-700 nm) for a reverse reaction: 2H₂O ← 2H₂ + O₂ + 4e⁻. Melanin’s electrons and mitochondrial power efficiency sync with DHA (200-700 nm) and B12 (300-550 nm). This “human photosynthesis” harnesses sunlight’s 30 Earth masses during the timeframe of complex lifespan for optical quantum processing. Connecting to My QUILT document

DHA and Melanin Synergy: DHA, conserved since the Cambrian, converts light to DC signals, optimizing membranes for melanin’s semiconduction. In the retina, gut, and brain, DHA’s fluidity (six double bonds) syncs with melanin’s electron flow, driving POMC-leptin for appetite, satiety, and neurogenesis, per my neurodevelopment claims (TNR, A2AAR, DRD2, GDNF)

Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks. Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per my quantum computing claim (Crawford DHA quantum paper).

Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per my symphony.

Connecting to my B12 Hypothesis B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy, which can lead to lens opacification. Cause - Melanin Dehydration > Loss of Light Absorption > Reduced Proton Flow > Loss of EZ Water > Protein Aggregation > Cataract Formation > Increased Oxidative Stress > Further Protein Damage > Calcium Accumulation > Impaired Mitochondrial Function > Accelerated Cataract Progression.

To reverse engineer - Rehydrate Melanin > Restore Light Absorption > Re-establish Proton Flow > Rebuild EZ Water > Prevent Protein Misfolding > Minimize Oxidative Stress > Regulate Calcium > Enhance Cellular Repair > Restore Lens Clarity.

Due to lens epithelial cell & fibre cycles, I assume the longer you have had cataracts the longer it will take to reverse e.g. could be months or several years to see any improvements depending on the rate of new fibre integration and how well ox damaged can be reversed in the older fibres.

**Warning do not try this** ... this is purely me thinking out loud... MB targeted dose to eye and paired with 670nm light only... It would have to be heavily diluted though likely 0.01% e.g. 1 drop of 1% to 10ml of sterile saline sol .. maybe 1 drop or 2 each eye followed by a few minutes of 670nm light therapy.. However, to avoid more damage you would likely have to avoid any artificial blue rich light for a few hours after application..

Where I got this from ... It is used during cataract surgery to stain lens capsules ~ 0.025 - 0.1% and to treat certain infections (e.g. fungal keratitis) <0.1% with no obvious negative issues..

nnEMF drives high blood glucose and insulin, which causes a hazy lens caused by the brain trying to protect itself. This is the same way tinnitus tries to protect us from brain damage. No one sees the targets I see.

The glyoxalase system is a set of enzymes detoxifying methylglyoxal and the other reactive aldehydes produced as a normal part of metabolism. This system has been studied in bacteria and eukaryotes and is linked to human cataract formation.
The glycation of lens proteins essentially drives cataracts in the eye. The glycation of lens proteins is primarily driven by methylglyoxal. In direct contradiction to much of the low-carbohydrate literature, glycation is not all driven by dietary carbohydrates. NnEMF can drive it because this enzyme operates with transition metals susceptible to nnEMF in the microwave range. This is why in Dr. Becker's book, "The Electric Body", Dr. Savitz found increased cataract formation in people who were exposed to RF/microwaves at their jobs. Methylglyoxal is quantitatively the most important source of advanced glycation end products in the body.⠀Few people seem to know that, too. The Decentralized Medicine #35 blog on Patreon lays it out. But you must read it to gain the wisdom of Nature.

With respect to the important UV (and IR) part of the light spectrum, check out the [Vermont 2017](http://youtube.com/wthatatch?v=d7qjh4BIGbc) video at 1hr47min - 1hr49min. There was a question about this and Dr. Kruse replied that there is UV light assimilation through the sclera! He mentioned that he gazes about 15 degrees of angle of the sun for about 15-20 minutes. I highly suggest (re-)listening to the whole thing... It's amazing how much better you can understand things after spending time with all the information we are taking in here and there.

See this paper: [Influence of Circadian Rhythm in the Eye: Significance of Melatonin in Glaucoma](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7996162/#B43-biomolecules-11-00340). In it, it states,

> Focusing on the eye, receptors have been localized in different structures. On the ocular surface, **melatonin MT1 receptor has been detected in corneal epithelial cells in different animals \[43,44\] and humans \[45\]**, while MT2 receptor has only been identified in animals \[43,46,47\]. They have also been seen in rabbits in the iris and ciliary body \[48\] and in human uveal melanocytes and human uveal melanoma cells \[49\]. Presence of melatonin in aqueous humour \[50\] has also been confirmed as its enzymes AANAT and HIOMT \[51\]. Furthermore, within the lens, **MT2 receptor has been identified \[43\] being considered as another source of melatonin \[52\]. In sclera, both receptors are expressed \[43,44\].**

In Reality #4, Dr. Kruse mentions,

> It turns out **aromatic amino acids also have a specific absorbance spectra in the UV range** and purple light oscillation can control those amino acids to make the bio-genic amines in the eye. Those two bio-genic amines are dopamine and **melatonin. They both contain aromatic amino acids that absorb UV light 260-289nm that leaks into the eye in small quantities.**

I hope my understanding of this is correct and that Mother Nature is sticking it to the miscreants that give us these IOLs.

### BabyBecca — 2024-08-27T16:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-338964>

My eyeglass prescription is -750. I stopped wearing contact lens 4 months ago. Anyone know of place to get prescription daylight blue blockers with my prescription? Looks like RA Optics glasses go up to -600. Thanks!

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-08-27T16:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-338965>

You can visit any optician. In Oslo, we have an independent optician that is not part of a chain, where I can request glasses designed to block specific wavelengths, such as everything below 465 nm or 550 nm, based on your preferences. For added assurance, I brought a spectroscope to verify my order. Opticians should carry a wide range of test lenses, and if they don’t have what you need in stock, they should be able to order the appropriate lenses from their suppliers. Your prescription will of course be tailored to the glasses you need.

### Daniel Renaud — 2024-08-27T17:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-338968>

I have worn glasses for more than 60 years. Myopia and astigmatism. Pretty strong prescription. I stopped wearing almost entirely a few years ago, and started getting more sun (basic Uncle Jack protocol) and the last time I went to the optometrist my prescription changed for the better significantly for the first time ever. The optometrist was shocked. Amusingly he had no interest in how I achieved that miracle.

> I have worn glasses for more than 60 years. Myopia and astigmatism. Pretty strong prescription. I stopped wearing almost entirely a few years ago, and started getting more sun (basic Uncle Jack protocol) and the last time I went to the optometrist my prescription changed for the better significantly for the first time ever. The optometrist was shocked. Amusingly he had no interest in how I achieved that miracle.

Sweeeeet!!! Daniel, is there anything else besides "more sun" that you did to improve your myopia? Thanks, mb

### Daniel Renaud — 2024-12-19T14:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-blueblockers-myopia.29369/post-344115>

> [LadyCroMag said:](/goto/post?id=343843)
>

No. I do wear blue blockers under indoor light. More sun less time in corrective lenses.

Have you all listened to it yet? It’s goooood. The guy made a BS intro where he makes it sound like Dr Jack was attacking him. But really it’s a history lesson and brief summary of Dr Mary’s monkey.
I read Dr Mary’s Monkey but this goes deeper into the history piece and I love real history.
Plus, the whole “people are just trying to raise their kids and be good people” line makes me go completely bonkers. If you can’t go the extra mile for your kids, it’s no good. Not for the world we live in. Everyone with children has the responsibility to do better, ask more questions, and try harder. Makes me mad when people use that excuse.
As always, thanks Uncle Jack for all your blogs, podcasts, and information you freely share.

This one is BETTER.

New pod alert.

If you are silent about your beliefs because you are worried about offending others or a paradigm in power youre doing life wrong. The message it sends out to the cosmos is that you have no conviction in your beliefs. It tells you that YOUR beliefs really are not that important to you and are superfluous. No one will listen to your ideas.

The other message this sends out to world is that you care more about what people think about you is more important than your ideas. That tells everyone youre doing life wrong.

No one will listen to you.

Here is the lesson of this podcast: When you stand up for what is right and true, you will receiver hate & love from the cosmos. One thing is certain, the entire cosmos woll know what you are really fighting for. And there will be no mistaking of your intentions.

Knowing a concept incorrectly is more dangerous than skipping a concept when you are searching for wisdom. The illusion of knowledge is just like drinking too much wine with your beliefs. I'd suggest you never get drunk on your own dogma. This includes the history that the "victors" wrote for you in history books.

This idea is simply stating that ignoring may not harm you as much as partial or incomplete knowledge may do. Today this idea is harming millions in the centralized world of healthcare and few realize it.

They are inebriated with many false beliefs. Some people out there truly believe think they can help others with their incomplete knowledge but the reality is they are only creating deeper problems than a firm solution. This is the deep problem with the illusion of knowledge.
This podcast will shake you to you core. That I am positive of.

Do you have a network around you that acts like Windex for you? Windex is used to help clean debris from your lenses. Windex is a catalyst for change. Confused? Instead of better glasses, our dopamine network of people around us can help give us better eyes to see things. This is especially true because of what the government has done to millions with MKULTRA. This is gang warfare at global scale to brains.

If we choose incorrectly, it can harm us. I love meeting people in real life, and I remain pretty ambivalent about virtual introductions on social media. That all being said, networking is NOT just how many people we meet or how many followers or likes we get. It is how many people we educate, help, recommend, and introduce to others to get them closer to their goals. This presentation will help you understand what they have done to our world.

# Thread: How to chose a career?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T02:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339024>

**HOW TO CHOOSE YOUR CAREER IN LIFE?**

Growing up as a human being, one of the most important choices you will make is the choice of what job to do.
That is because, it is your job that will put food on your table, take care of your family, give you the kind of life you want etc.
By job here, I mean either a job, or a skill, or a business.
It can be anyone.
But what is the thing that should determine the career you will choose?
You see, there are a few things to look at, before picking a career.
First of all, choose what career you want to be in. Something youre passionate about.
Yes, what do you want to be?
You may want to be a doctor, a lawyer, a singer, a footballer etc.
Go for what you want.
There is a reason for this.
It is easier for you to work hard when you are doing something you want to do, rather than something you don't want to do.
And hard work always brings success.
However, if you are in a situation where the thing you want to do, cannot give you the type of money you want to have, then you should look for the career that will give you money.
Yes, this is important because, sometimes, you may want to do something, but if the thing you want to do does not give you money, you will soon get frustrated in it.
So as you are choosing what you want to do, make sure it is something that will give you money.
There are some careers however, that will actually give you money, but not at the starting point.
These types of careers will only begin to bring you money much later as you go on through life.
If you are in such a career, then you must learn to combine that career with a job for the time being, until you get to that phase where your career gets you money.
So when choosing a career, it should first be about what you want to do.
And if what you want to do does not have the potential to give you the money you want to have, then leave it and choose what will give you that kind of money.
And if your career is the type that does not pay immediately, then combine it with a job, especially at the beginning.

My advice to anyone enter adulthood and into a any career:

**Focus your energy, guard your time, train your mind and body, think for yourself, curate your friends and environment, keep your promises, stay optimistic and constructive to your tribe while upgrading the world.**

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T02:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339026>

**IF YOU DON'T FIND YOUR PASSION, DON'T EXPECT YOUR LIFE TO WORK AS YOUR DREAMS**

You have been told by some teachers and speakers that if you want to succeed in life, you have to find your passion.
According to them, your passion is that thing you can do for free, without even getting paid.
But they ignore the fact that, to succeed, you may need back breaking hardwork.
You will get tired sometimes.
You may want to give up sometimes.
Your passion is not the way to success.
A lot of young men are very passionate about sports, but how many of them are succeeding through sports? Very few.
When you find your passion, and you try to sell your passion, you will most likely end up realizing that you are the only one passionate about your passion.
The people you are trying to sell to are real human beings, with their own needs and wants, who do not care about how passionate you are with what you do.
Rather, find your purpose instead.
It is your purpose that will sell.
What is a purpose in this case?
Your purpose is that thing you can do to positively impact the lives of others.
So while your passion focuses on you, your purpose focuses on others.
If the focus of your production is on you, then you are the only one who will love and buy what you produce.
A good case study of this fact is a car produced in the 1950s, called the Ford Edsel.
You see, the staff members of Ford corporation wanted to produce a supercar.
They loved the idea, the concept design and everything about the car.
They named the car, Edsel.
If you are intelligent, you will Yandex about the Ford Edsel after reading this.
So they were able to come up with a good design for it.
In fact, during the design period, they closed up the studio from the mass media so that nobody will be able to copy the Edsel design.
After everything was done, the Edsel was produced.
The result?
No sales.
Why was that?
Because even though the Ford members of staff and management were passionate about the car, the members of the public were not interested in the passion of the Ford designers.
That is because, when you get passionate about something, you are passionate because it serves your interest.
But when you find a purpose, that purpose serves people's interests. And people are going to buy into things that serve their interests far more than they will buy into things that serve your interest.
So pursue purpose rather than passion.

### Mat.I — 2024-08-29T03:16:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339032>

Thanks Jack.
My thoughts on this -
Your career could also be something you are really good at to earn dollars, and eventually fuel your life purpose. Your purpose could be something completely unrelated to your career that does not necessarily need to be the primary source of income. I struggled with this quite a bit in the past accepting the fact that just because you are good at your career and an "expert" in the field, does not necessarily mean you are fulfilling your life purpose. My life purpose is in a different field, hence, why I'm on here. I know I must play the long game to get there, build and get the necessary things in place to be able to step away.

### Justin Goldberg — 2024-09-18T06:45:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339683>

What do you say for people who are lonely? Not a regular lonely. Sometimes being the smartest person I know, I feel terribly alone. Hanging with bitcoiners in real life has certainly helped this.

For example, I never felt at ease at a bar because I can't strike up conversations with regular people.

My faith helps too with feeling loved and wanted, but the congregants aren't on my level of understanding.

Thanks again for the advice on clubhouse, I will never forget it and I have taken it to heart. I am making efforts in exiting the USA thunderdome.

### eaandkw — 2024-09-18T17:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339699>

I'll give my two cents. First, try to understand the lifestyle you want to live. From there look for jobs or careers that can provide that lifestyle and not make you want to off yourself. Second, understand what you are giving up or what you are missing out on while pursuing that job or career. And lastly, always have an exit strategy. Meaning try not to have all your eggs in one basket. This doesn't necessarily mean working multiple jobs or having multiple streams of income. But don't lock yourself into a situation you can't get out of by having lots of debt.

25-50 years ago having a stable job was ideal and the ticket to a good life. Today I think mobility is more important. Being able to move to where jobs are, to move careers when needed, or even to work for yourself I think is more important today.

Granted, most of us are probably on the older side of life. This advice is more effective the younger you are but it still applies to us. You just have to acknowledge reality. For example, I might have been really good and strong when I was younger but, at about 50 years old I'm not going to be a world-class powerlifter. I can, however, pivot my experience or knowledge into something that involves the gym or powerlifting competitions. I could be a coach or personal trainer.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-18T20:21:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339707>

Love what you do (do what you love) . . . keep your expenses lower than your income . . . spend more time with positive people than negative ones.

### Brogan — 2024-09-19T01:12:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-339713>

Recognize that it's part and parcel of being a free-thinking human; the farther you develop yourself from the herd, the less you'll be able to relate to most people, and vice versa. Find tribe by forcing yourself to do different things around different people. Get as comfortable with your mind and heart as possible, and have compassion for your all-too-human need for social interaction.

### Mat.I — 2024-09-19T02:14:15+0300

Hi Dr Kruse,

Wise words – you truly are an inspiration and have offered so much to so many people. I was listening to one of your interviews on YouTube the other night and heard you talking about how much death you deal with as a neurosurgeon. I also heard you talking about the surgery you performed for the teenage girl who had been attacked and had many shards of bone lodged in her brain and how you were able to retrieve them successfully even without the assistance of imagery. What an awe-inspiring story! It was apparent how connected you are still to that experience.

Hearing you talk about the work you do and recount that story also really puts your personality into perspective. Some people may find you harsh and abrasive (which I know isn’t news to you) but if those people understood the way you are in the context of the kind of things you deal with, I think your no-bullshit approach to things would make much more sense to them.

I’ve had many interests and passions thus far in my life, like reading, writing, making art, learning languages, photography, acting, philosophy, activism and much more. I’ve also been inspired by many talented, wise, and impressive individuals. But I have to say that you are my biggest inspiration so far. For the last year and a half, I have been quite hell-bent on the idea of studying medicine. And whilst it remains to be seen whether or not that comes to fruition, I feel very called to bridge the gap between what modern medicine currently is and what it should be. The crap people are fed versus the truth that they need.

Thank you for doing the work that you do. I truly believe that it has a ripple effect and that if enough people choose to wear the truth, rather than hide from it, then the truth will shine its light over great distances.

Thanks again,

Kayla

### Sandy Abram — 2024-10-10T03:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-chose-a-career.29375/post-340728>

As a Mum of two teenagers the journey of helping to guide my children as they explore their future careers, studies and dreams has become so much more difficult.

Once upon a time, parents would be in tears "what do you mean you're not going to University?!". Now I see myself in a few years saying, "why would you want to do a crazy thing like go to University?!".

Universities are not what they once appeared to be. Once a placed students aspired to go (and parents pushed them to go) it's now become very apparent that Uni's are a key part of the centralised corrupt system. Young adults become debt slaves before they walk out the door, they undertake studies sponsored by corrupt corporations and in regards to the University itself, they are subjected to woke agendas and dangerous ideologies.

So now what? What do you do if your child's passion and purpose intersects with an evil education system that is broken? Or worse, a profession where unless you comply, all your hard work and experience is gone in an instant.

As we try and build a more decentralised world, hopefully one day soon, we see decentralised education platforms a part of that picture.

# Thread: WHAT AN EDUCATION DOES NOT TEACH YOU?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-an-education-does-not-teach-you.29376/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-29T02:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-an-education-does-not-teach-you.29376/post-339025>

**LECTURERS IN SCHOLL WILL NEVER TELL YOU THIS IN THE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE.**

If you are invited for a job interview, at the entrance of the gate or within the Organization's premises:

1.Politely greet everybody. I have seen MDs and Managers disguise and stand at the gate to observe interviewees for a deep reason.
2\. Do not go inside and start charging your phone or begin to look for where to charge your phone.
3\. Do not go inside and begin to chat with your phone or make unnecessary calls. Put the phone inside your bag and turn it OFF. They could be monitoring you through the camera.
4\. Avoid too much talk with fellow interviewees. Don't go there and begin to argue about the government of the day, escalated food prices and even lack of employment opportunities. Just be calm and positive.
5\. Don't start praying and speaking in tongues to demonstrate your religiosity and closeness to God. Pray at home before going.
6\. If they offer you Tea or food, Please reject it politely and thank them. You can take after the interview. If you take that Tea or food, you are out.
7\. Don't chew gum, biscuits or anything. Go with a book and be reading it. Have a notebook and pen ready to write during the interview.
8\. Never ever criticize your previous employer even if they deserve it.
9\. If you see somebody you know in the organization, either as a staff or a fellow interviewee, greet him officially. Avoid creating an impression that you know him.
10\. Do not appear too poor, helpless and inferior before the interviewer. Forget about problems we face in the USA, your personal and family problems. Focus on the job.
11\. After the interview, put up a smiling face, thank them and leave quickly.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In most cases, interviewers assess and score interviewees by the factors above and not necessarily by the answers they provide on paper. They'll train you eventually to do the job if they employ you.

This property allows proteins to selectively absorb or respond to specific polarizations, potentially enhancing processes like electron transfer in cytochrome c oxidase (COX) during mitochondrial respiration.

At night time, when incident sunlight is absent, the temperature drops and more light is emitted from cells while the surfaces remain opaque to light. Totally depends where you are.
The most widely used 5G frequencies (in the us) are below 4 GHz so travel further and penetrate more. 4G travels a good distance and goes through most things including YOU. If your phone works you have RF.

Grounding outside, especially at the beach has massive benefits but does not stop any RF from reaching you. Being protected by a cliff and/or in the water helps.

# Thread: If youre an artist here is where your journey should start
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-an-artist-here-is-where-your-journey-should-start.29393/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-02T18:47:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-an-artist-here-is-where-your-journey-should-start.29393/post-339165>

New blog out today that covers how our use of technology is REFLECTED in our art and creativity today. This was the topic of my recent talk at the event held in El Salvador in August of 2024. How does art link to physics? The story is ancient. Democrtitus, in the fifth century B.C. had declared that all the world was composed of only two elements: atomes and the void. This reduction of the myriad of forms to only two was the ultimate in dualistic reasoning. Christianity adopted dualism when it created the strict division between good and evil and heaven and hell. Art is a reflection of the state of the semiconductors that are located between the environment all five of your sense organs. A neurosurgeon is both an artist and a scientist…

I believe the decentralized ones rely more heavily on the artistic side of their brains than the left side. Centralized NS are TAUGHT to rely more heavily on their left sided hemispheres. You can see this in the advice they give. The advice they give is a reflection of the light emitted from the surfaces of their semiconducting brains. Neurosurgeons should learn to rely heavily on their intuitive visual-spatial right-hemispheric mode because of our medical school curriculums. They are polluted with BigHarma ideas. At the same time, our training is obviously scientific. Left-brained logic, reason, and abstract thinking are the stepping-stones leading to the vast scientific literature’s arcane tenets. The need in my profession to shuttle back and forth constantly between these two complementary functions of the human psyche.

Art and physics seem like a strange coupling to the centralized mind. Of the many human disciplines, could there be two that seem more divergent? The artist employs image and metaphor; the physicist uses number and equation. Art encompasses an imaginative realm of aesthetic qualities; physics exists in a world of crisply circumscribed mathematical relationships between quantifiable properties. Traditionally, art has created illusions meant to elicit emotion; physics has been an exact science that made sense Yet, despite what appear to be irreconcilable differences, there is one fundamental feature that solidly connects these disciplines. Revolutionary art and visionary physics are both investigations into the nature of reality of the present. They reflect the state of the environment when they were created. This talk/blog shows you this is not hyperbole, but reality. It shows you how it happened 65K when there was Neaderthals and no modern humans. [https://patreon.com/posts/110094014](https://t.co/1n0a37Sloi)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-04T17:14:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/centralized-pediatricians-are-running-disease-camps-for-centralized-medicine.29402/post-339248>

I ran the numbers for a typical pediatrics practice and can confirm a least 50% of their revenue comes from vaccines. What's not mentioned here is that their overhead is 50%, so without vaccines they can't even stay in business. A single vaccine administration fee for the pediatrician is only $35, but they do so many of them that it really adds up: An average pediatrics practice has 1500 patients per provider (some have twice as much or more).

Those 1500 patients get 60 shots over the course of birth to 18 years, which is 1,620,000 total shots administered in 18 years, or an average of 5,000 vaccines administered per year. That’s $175,000 in vaccine administration fees alone at $35 a pop. This is essentially the median salary of a pediatrician. Of course most practices bring in about double to account for their overhead expenses, which run 50-60%. But without the $35 payments there would be basically nothing left over. Pediatrics is not economic for the most part without vaccines.

And this doesn't even count the bonus payments they get for vaccinating 85%+ of their practice.

Edward Mandell House (originally “Huis” which became “House”) was born July 26, 1858 in Houston, Texas. He became active in Texas politics and served as an advisor to President Woodrow Wilson, particularly in the area of foreign affairs. House functioned as Wilson's chief negotiator in Europe during the negotiations for peace (1917-1919), and as chief deputy for Wilson at the Paris Peace Conference. He died on March 28, 1938 in New York City.

DATA on why I am right: What was the real purpose of retirement account creation under the Reagan administrations? It began in 1981when Reagan hatched a plan with his military contractors who got him elected as California's govenor and then as President. What was the first payback?

The **Iran–Contra affair** was a [political scandal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Political_scandal) in the United States that occurred during the first and second term of the [Reagan administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reagan_administration). Between 1981 and 1986, senior administration officials secretly facilitated the illegal sale of arms to [Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamic_Republic_of_Iran), which was subject to an [arms embargo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arms_embargo) at the time. Because it was successful, the Reagan legal team came up with the idea to launer money on a larger scale to fund military wars using the Public's retirements accounts in the future as a slush fund to fund covert operations

During 1981 the Reagan administration hoped to use the proceeds of the arms sale to fund the [Contras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Contras), an anti-[Sandinista](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandinista_National_Liberation_Front) rebel group in [Nicaragua](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nicaragua). Under the [Boland Amendment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boland_Amendment), further funding of the Contras by [legislative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Congress) appropriations was prohibited by [Congress](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Congress), but the Reagan administration figured out a [loophole](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loophole) by secretively using non-appropriated funds instead.

How did this scandal set the stage for COVID? The Iran Contral affair was investigated by Congress and by the three-person, Reagan-appointed [Tower Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Commission). Neither investigation found evidence that President Reagan himself knew of the extent of the multiple programs. Additionally, [US Deputy Attorney General](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/US_Deputy_Attorney_General) [Lawrence Walsh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lawrence_Walsh) was appointed [independent counsel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Office_of_the_Independent_Counsel) in December 1986 to investigate possible criminal actions by officials involved in the scheme. In the end, several dozen administration officials were indicted, including then–[Secretary of Defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Secretary_of_Defense) [Caspar Weinberger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caspar_Weinberger). Eleven convictions resulted, some of which were vacated on appeal. Weinberger's trial however opened the door to prosecute George W. Bush whow was caught red handed.

The rest of those indicted or convicted were all pardoned in the final days of the [presidency of George H. W. Bush](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidency_of_George_H._W._Bush), who had been vice president at the time of the affair. W Bush has deep ties to the industrial military complex via his family ties that date back to the OSS that became the CIA under General Groves during the FDR and Truman presidency's.

How bad was it for W. Bush? Former Independent Counsel Walsh noted that, in issuing the pardons, Bush appeared to have been preempting being implicated himself by evidence that came to light during the Weinberger trial and noted that there was a pattern of "deception and obstruction" by Bush, Weinberger, and other senior Reagan administration officials. Walsh submitted his final report on 4 August 1993 and later wrote an account of his experiences as counsel, _Firewall: The Iran-Contra Conspiracy and Cover-Up.

### Reagan's Tax Plan Offers IRAs for All : from WaPo​

June 11, 1981 at 1:00 a.m. EDT

Tucked away in President Reagan's latest tax cut plan is a provision to give a major new tax deduction to millions of working Americans by permitting them to establish individual retirement funds even if they are participating in company pension plans.

What was the REAL monetary effect of the IRA program?

Dallas Salisbury, at this time, was the executive director of the Employe Benefit Research Institute. He was critical in getting the backing of the banking and investment industries during the early Reagan administrations. Salisury told the Washington Post back then that the "new Reagan plan" should significantly increase individual savings. Salisbury sold this as a benefit to the public to have a new way to save, but the real goal was to create another slush fund that the military could use to launder money to fund their own covert agenda.

Salisbury said by creating a specific tax deduction for investments in retirement accounts it WOULD incentivize the funding of the project using public funds that fit the Iran Contra idea of using non appropriated funds for the industrial military complex.
**Salisbury predicted that with the new proposal, individual savings would increase by nearly $2 billion a year at a cost to the Treasury of $200 million to $300 million.

With compounding since the 45 years since the IRA programs were created and with the effect of money printing and inflation and re-hypothecation of money, the industrial military complex were able to create a huge pile of money to steal from the public.

This created a new problem. COVID was the solution to.**

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-15T14:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-339601>

### **In 1948, 4 Star General Eisenhower met with General Leslie Groves, who was director of the Manhattan Project, Eisenhower presented Groves with a long list of complaints, ending Groves' career. Groves was furious. Groves had a beef with the Executive branch when Eisenhower wins the Presidency in 4 years. Groves left the military and becomes part of the defense contractor industry that morphs into the industrial military complex that in 8 years becomes a serious threat to Eisenhower and the Executive branch.** ​

### **Post WW2, Eisenhower didn't view Groves in a positive light, given the success of the Manhattan Project.** ​

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-15T15:42:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-339602>

Relatively little, however, has been written on the history of key Army engineer who made the Manhattan project a revolutionary success. This was done by design. Leslie Richard “Dick” Groves, Jr., a career officer in the Army Corps of Engineers, was tasked with assembling the crucial links between _**government, industry, science,**_ **_and the military_** beginning in September 1942, due to his imposing personality, iron will, and remarkable administrative acumen. Groves also had a grasp pertinent scientific principles, as well as a unique ability to choose among technical alternatives to deliver quick results. Most importantly, Groves saw the Manhattan project and the atomic bomb as the means to end the war. Eisenhower believed that Stalin and the Russians were more important in winning in Europe than Groves bomb was in Japan.

Eisenhower knew how Truman was selected by Groves and Jimmy Burns in Dec 1944. Eisenhower did not like that Groves perpetrated a covert coup with Truman.

Groves made a _lot_ of enemies throughout his military career, and Eisenhower's gripe with him in 1948 was not about whether he did a good job in 1945, but whether he was doing a good job in 1948. By 1948 he was running the Armed Forces Special Weapons Project, a very different sort of organization than the Manhattan Project.

With the Manhattan Project, Groves had the ability to essentially be a minor dictator of his own industrial military covert agency — he was insulated from criticism and audit, and had the coveted AAA priority rating that allowed him to requisition whatever resources or people he wanted without being significantly challengeable. His style of operation was very well-suited to the conditions of the Manhattan Project, where he was able to bulldoze his way towards a very specific outcome. It was extremely ill-suited for the postwar, military of rebuilding the world he helped destroy. He personality is where all the modern Neo-Cons come from. Groves second gig building the modern deep state, however, does not go as well as the Manahattan Project, because the outcomes were more nebulous and ill defined, and his authority was no longer absolute, and his oversized ego had become resented by other military brass who considered him to be perhaps a bit too big for his britches.

To Eisenhower, he wasn't some kind of war hero, because many felt he was not in the theatre of battle. Eisenhower felt that Groves was "Toy Soldier" who babysat a bunch of Jewish scientists for FDR. Groves harnessed the scientists in silos of that Allen Dulles harvested in Operation Paperclip from Europe. Groves not not viewed as a war hero by the military in WW2 in the same sense that Eisenhower or MacArthur were — he was an production engineer for the army Corp of Engineers that managed an industrial production project.

Despite all this, Groves had some supporters in the Senate, and some in the military. But he had a huge list of enemies, including David Lilienthal, the head of the Atomic Energy Commission, and the scientists on the General Advisory Committee secretly regarded him as a nuisance in transitioning the atomic weapons business from its "crash" wartime footing into something more regular and systematic in nature. It became a huge business.

Groves and Eisenhower never got along, ever. Their styles didn't mesh, their personalities didn't mesh, and their experiences didn't mesh. Eisenhower's specific complaints were almost exclusively about these kinds of things:

Groves was rude and ruthless when it came to other officers who he felt were standing in his way; he was arrogant; he was single-minded in his attempt to accomplish his goals, no matter who got in the way; he did not abide by rules; he repeatedly worked to achieve advancement through tricks rather than by putting in the "dues" that were required of others.

And — perhaps ironically — he was too young to be the Chief of Engineers, and so there weren't a lot of positions actually open to him for advancement at that point in post war America, and it was an "up or out" situation regarding promotion. The people Eisenhower wanted to promote, in any event, were the commanders who had succeeded in Europe and the Pacific — not those who had stayed on the home front.

All of which to say, there is a classic truism in manufactured history that says that Churchill was a great Prime Minister for war, but a terrible Prime Minister for peace. One can debate that particular example, but Groves was a great project manager for war, and the Manhattan Project probably wouldn't have been a success without him. But those particular qualities that made him good for a "crash" program made him awful for the day-to-day of peacetime operation, and he showed himself totally unwilling and unable to adapt to the culture clash that existed between him and the combat commanders who were going to be running the show in the Army for a long time.

The very things that made Groves successful in 1942-1945 made him fail in 1947-1948, to put it very simply, because the context of the latter was very different than the former. It is of note that much of the Manhattan Project's operations underwent many transitions in this time as well; Norris Bradbury was a very different sort of manager and scientist than J. Robert Oppenheimer.

The best biography of Groves is Robert Standish Norris' _Racing for the Bomb_, and the specifics of Eisenhower's complaints that I've given come from chapter 25, which goes into great detail on the specific circumstances that lead to Groves' retirement and into the world of defense contractors just outside the military. Eisenhower's Farewell speech is linked directly to how powerful Groves became as a product engineer for the Oilmen and Howard Hughes businesses. He comes back to capture the Eisenhower's Executive branch.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-16T01:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-the-fed-and-the-link-of-the-royal-family-to-the-rise-of-hitler-and-formation-of-israel.29403/post-339612>

Albert Einstein and J. Robert Oppenheimer at Princeton, 1947.[](https://www.icp.org/browse/archive/objects/robert-oppenheimer-director-of-the-institute-of-advanced-study-discussing)

Almost all scientists could see three facts: that the Soviets would get the weapon; that there would eventually be another great power conflict. And that a resulting atomic war would mean the end of civilization. “The unleashed power of the atom,” wrote Einstein, “has changed everything save our modes of thinking and we thus drift toward unparalleled catastrophe.”

The United Nations had been formed just a few months before, but it had (and still has) limited power, thanks to a great power veto. Einstein saw the UN merely as “a transitional system toward the final goal... a supranational authority vested with sufficient legislative and executive powers to keep the peace.”

If supreme military power in the form of the atom bomb were held by a United Nations body, as Oppenheimer wanted, then it would almost be a world state. In fact, many scientists explicitly advocated a 'world federal government.' They published a book, _One World Or None_, with an accompanying film \[see film below\]. This view was not just held by scientists. One of the supporters was Gen 'Hap' Arnold, the head of the United States Air Force — the man in charge of dropping the bomb. The idea of international control even took root at the highest levels of government.

President Harry Truman had two advisers with diametrically opposed views. One was Henry Stimson, the elderly and aristocratic Secretary of War. The other was James Byrnes, Secretary of State. Stimson had famously prevented the Bomb's being dropped on Kyoto, a city he had once visited with his wife. Plus, he had an almost mystical view of how the world had changed. He thought the Bomb “should not be considered simply in terms of military weapons, but as a new relationship of man to the universe.” Stimson’s plan also included international control via the new UN agency: “It must be controlled if possible to make it an assurance of future peace rather than a menace to civilization.”

Daniel Kahneman was prominent in psychology, but do you know his history? Alongside Amos Tversky, in Isreal, he applied cognitive psychology to economic analysis, laying the foundation for a new field of research and earning the Nobel Prize in Economics in 2002.

This is why he was tapped by DARPA. They needed his skill set in the updated versions of MKULTA to get people to accept the coming economic reset. Much of his work was hindered in 2016-2020.

Kahneman was given the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 2013 from President Barack Obama. The use of propaganda use on America was prohibited before Obama's presidency. Obama weaponized Kahneman's work for DARPA's use. What is their goals? The use of algorithms is a huge red flag. This will be a psy-ops opportunity.

DARPA has named this program to develop an artificially intelligent quantitative metric Systematizing Confidence in Open Research and Evidence, or SCORE. As DARPA explains in its request for proposals in 2019:

These tools will assign explainable confidence scores with a reliability that is equal to, or better than, the best current human expert methods. If successful, SCORE will enable the Department of Defense personnel to quickly calibrate the level of confidence they should have in the reproducibility and replicability of a given SBS result or claim at borders, and thereby increase the effective use of SBS literature and research to address important human domain challenges, such as enhancing deterrence, enabling stability, and reducing extremism they deem problematic to the shadow government. It establishes a private right of action for Americans and financial institutions harmed by illicit government activity. Please like and share if you think more Americans should know about this grave threat to our freedom.

When Lansky gives the bad news to the Fed that their system is corrupted he begins to mobilize his funds to new project. Gaming the new Fed system he actually game them. Lansky always believed in gaming that the casino games should be honest and you should beat the house by being wise.

So what was his plan? He knew exactly what the Fed families were going to build so he was going to help build a parallel system that was an answer to this. Much of his own wealth was funneled from Ira Malnick in 1974 through 1983 to young men who were working on the mathematics of cryptography. Lansky was a master at numbers and he knew their had to be a way to beat the Rothchilds again.

Cryptopgraphy was a highly protected field in the US government because of Alan Turing and what he was able to do with the German. History tells a story that Turing broke the Enigma Code but I believe that Turing was sending information directly from the Royal family and their bankers to the Nazis. I believe this is why Turing was treated the way he was by Britain after the War. I also think it is why he was terminated by MI6.

His end made no sense to me for the longest time until I relaized he was the direct line of communication to the NAZIS and the Pope Pius XI and XII were the back channel to the Nazis during WW2.

In post World War 2 during the early days of the Cold War, the U.S. has developed an elaborate series of export control regulations designed to prevent a wide range of Western technology from falling into the hands of others, particularly the Eastern bloc. I believe Stalin knew about what Turing was doing and this was relayed to FDR at Yalta. Immediately in post war US cryptography became a tool of war. The encryption technology & techniques using any crypto software was included as a Category XIII item into the United States Munitions List. This was highly unusual place to catorgize a branch of mathematics that history books tell us that Turing used to break an Enigma code to help the UK break the German codes. It made no sense when you consider why Edward the VIII abdicated his throne and why the Popes were working with the bankers and NAZIS.

With that said, Lansky knew implicitly that being a cryptographer in post war USA meant being watched and pressured into signing secrecy orders and keeping silence about your work.
