Unlike other Department of Defense organizations, DARPA does not have its own laboratories or research facilities, and it maintains only a skeletal [bureaucracy](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/bureaucracy). Instead of hiring a large, permanent staff, DARPA awards short contracts (typically three to five years) to eminent scientists to direct [research](https://www.britannica.com/topic/research-and-development) as project managers.

These project managers, in turn, are given significant freedom to fund research that they believe will benefit the military. Typically, project managers use their expertise and research contacts to form a project team with members located at various American universities and corporations.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T00:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340033>

During the Civil War, Abraham Lincoln hired the private detective Allan Pinkerton to provide security for the Union side and to gather information
on the Confederacy. After the war, he earned headlines by going after the famous outlaws Frank and Jesse James. However, this adventure backfired when Pinkerton agents wounded the bandits’ mother and killed their young half-brother. This incident—combined with the Pinkerton agency’s involvement in the bloody labor battles of the late 1800s—contributed to Americans’ dim view of the spy profession even after the Union victory. The World War I era did see the US government enter further into the intelligence business than it ever had before. The State Department
created an elite, central intelligence unit called U-1. A colorful character named Herbert Yardley ran a code-breaking operation known as the
Black Chamber. And in 1924, a young detective by the name of J. Edgar Hoover became director of the Bureau of Investigation, later
the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

The government appeared to be gradually taking over the business of national security from private agencies like the Pinkertons.

The moment did not last, however. As Americans retreated into isolationism after World War I, the anti-spy tradition came roaring back. The government closed down U-1 and the Black Chamber. This was the background to the approach of World War II, and one of the greatest intelligence shocks in American history: Japan’s surprise attack on Pearl Harbor. Most historians agree that two factors were crucial to
the surprise. One was a failure by politicians to imagine that the Japanese were capable of such an attack. The other was a lack of specific intelligence pointing toward the precise timing and place of the raid.

Whatever its exact causes, Pearl Harbor would have huge consequences for the subsequent development of US intelligence. First, it created a strong argument for those advocating a permanent intelligence agency. Second, Pearl Harbor brought the United States into World War II. In doing so, it dealt a huge blow to the isolationist, anti-spy habits of mind that had previously slowed the growth of American intelligence.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T01:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340035>

The second phase of US intelligence history involves the Office of Strategic Services, or OSS. If any single individual could be called
the father of the CIA, it is the solider and diplomat William “Wild Bill” Donovan. In 1940, President Franklin D. Roosevelt dispatched
Donovan to London to assess Britain’s chances of surviving a Nazi invasion.

During his visit, Donovan had extraordinary access to British intelligence. He began to formulate a plan for an American secret service, modeled largely on MI6.

When Donovan returned to the United States with this plan, he encountered opposition from J. Edgar Hoover, who saw it as a threat to the FBI. The State Department and military intelligence units—such as the Office of Naval Intelligence, and the Army’s G-2—also opposed the idea. It did not matter. The president shared Donovan’s regard for the British and wanted to prepare the country for the war that was surely coming.

In the summer of 1941, Roosevelt appointed Donovan the coordinator of information, with historically unprecedented powers over the existing
civilian and military agencies. After the failures at Pearl Harbor, the function was renamed the Office of Strategic Services, or OSS.

The United States had its first central intelligence agency. Though it had some organizational chaos and ill-formed ideas, the OSS had a fine overall war record. Its special operations branch ran guerilla campaigns in occupied territory that inspired local resistance movements and distracted the enemy. The OSS was also important historically as the forerunner of the CIA.

First, many people who served in the OSS went on to serve in the CIA, including no fewer than four directors of Central Intelligence.
Second, the OSS had a distinctly Ivy League, Anglophile—even aristocratic—air to it. This social identity carried over into the CIA, at
least during the agency’s early years. This would give the new intelligence organization a strong sense of social cohesion and esprit de corps. But it would also earn the mistrust of those who were suspicious of elites and foreign influences. It did not help that the OSS and CIA were the creations of Democratic presidents. Many conservatives saw them as outgrowths of liberal big government, endangering American freedoms.

This was the zeitgeist of the Federal government at end of FDR's reign.

_IN THE LATE AFTERNOON OF April 12, 1945, a Secret Service agent on Vice President Harry S Truman’s protection detail informed him he was urgently needed at the White House. Truman was surprised by this request. Since their inauguration in January of 1945, the sixty-one-year-old Vice President had seen President Franklin Delano Roosevelt only twice, and had never been alone with him. Groves silo'd FDR from his Manchurian canidate by design. That idea was built into the Manhattan Project._

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T01:23:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340037>

Finally, the OSS prefigured the CIA in that it housed intelligence work and covert action under one roof. This was not necessarily a problem so
long as the two missions were given equal weight. However, Donovan’s natural inclination was toward covert action rather than intelligence
work. This preference would be inherited by the CIA, with occasionally unfortunate consequences.

After World War II ended in 1945, the familiar pattern of the US government pulling back from intelligence work during peacetime appeared again. Bill Donovan tried to fight the tide. He lobbied the White House with a plan for a permanent civilian intelligence agency, but opponents in Congress and the media attacked the proposal.

It is possible that Roosevelt might have supported Donovan, but the president died in April 1945 as mentioned above, and his successor Harry Truman turned out to share the concerns of Donovan’s critics. In September 1945, the president ordered the breakup of the OSS. This gave General Groves his opportunity to create an intelligence army that would be loyal to him and not Donovan.

However, Groves changed Truman’s mind. This is why Groves hand selected Truman to replace Henry Wallace in December of 1944 at the DNC conference in Chicago. Groves heard Henry Wallace's speech called "The Common Man." In this speech Wallace laid out a view of post War america he envisioned. That vision was not shared by Groves at all. Wallace was embracing Stalin and FDR vision laid out at Yalta. Groves sent Truman to Potsdam and told him to reverse everything FDR promised Stalin at Yalta.

Groves new this would make the postwar world was a confusing, threatening place, especially for a new president with little experience in foreign
affairs. This is how Groves got Truman to agree to morphing the OSS into the CIA in 1947. Groves ramped up State Deptartment briefings with Truman because he knew he hated them and he was setting up an environment where he could solve this problem for Truman with a new CIA. History reports that Truman hated the lengthy briefings he was getting from career foreign service officers in the State Department.

In January 1946, Truman established a new body, the Central Intelligence Group, to provide him with daily foreign intelligence digests. To run this body, Truman listened to Groves and turned to a trusted friend of them both, Rear Admiral Sidney Souers. The Central Intelligence

Group still had no intelligence-gathering capability of its own, and no powers of covert action. Truman’s timing proved just right because
the international environment began to deteriorate again in 1946. This leads to the main reason why the CIA was created: the start of the Cold War with Russia at Genral Groves covert pressures.

The two postwar superpowers—the United States and Soviet Union—failed to reach an agreement on how to deal with various countries in
Eastern Europe after the War. The Soviets wanted them firmly within Moscow’s sphere of influence, to act as a buffer zone against possible future
foreign aggressors. The Americans wanted them independent, part of a new international order that implicitly favored US interests.

From this initial disagreement, superpower tensions escalated at alarming speed because of what General Groves did to Stalin at Potsdam. Truman reverse every promise FDR made and Groves knew how Stalin and the USSR would react. This preconditioned Truman to change his mind on creation of the CIA from the OSS. Donovan had no sphere of influence on Truman at all. Each move by one side seemed to increase the other’s feelings ofinsecurity. Gradually, a new pattern of conflict emerged, in which the two nations used any means short of direct military conflict to check on others.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T01:53:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340038>

_After Roosevelt died the story went around in Washington DC that Truman was closer to FDR’s wife, Eleanor, than he was to the ailing sixty-three-year-old FDR. Groves was the reason for that. When Truman got to the White House, he in fact was greeted by Mrs. Roosevelt.
“Harry,” she said. “The President is dead.” Roosevelt had died hours before in Warm Springs, Georgia, in the company of his mistress, Lucy Mercer.

It has been reliably reported that immediately after Truman was sworn in by Chief Justice Harlan Stone as the thirty-third President of the United States, his first order was: “Get Justice Jackson and Captain Souers over here right now.” Souers was being heavily coached at this time by General Groves and by Senator Jimmy Byrnes of South Carolina who was very close with Truman. Byrnes was responsible for getting Truman to replace Wallace in December 1944 DNC conference. General Groves orchestrated the entire event._
Byrnes was born in Charleston, South Carolina to an industrious mother who was widowed shortly before his birth. He left school after the seventh grade to work in a law office where he received excellent tutelage from several judges. Byrnes was admitted to the South Carolina bar in 1903 and practiced in Aiken. In 1908 he was elected to his first public office as district prosecuting attorney. Two years later he was elected to the U.S. Congress, where he served from 1911 to 1925.

Byrnes returned briefly to private practice before his election to the U.S. Senate in 1931. During his ten years in the Senate, he championed President Franklin D. Roosevelt’s New Deal. The President appointed Byrnes to the Supreme Court in 1941. It was a short-lived appointment for the restless judge who missed the political action of the legislative branch. He resigned the following year from the SCOTUS.

Following U.S. entry into World War II, Byrnes was chosen as Director of the Office of Economic Stabilization, and in 1943, Director of Economic Mobilization. Here is became a huge ally of General Gorves during the Manahattan Project. He was told by FDR about the project and General Groves allowed it. Byrnes was so powerful to FDR he accompanied President Roosevelt to the [Yalta Conference](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://history.state.gov/milestones/1937-1945/yalta-conf) in 1945 to hear what FDR told Stalin. Byrnes was the person who informed Groves of that conversation. Following President Roosevelt’s death in April, Byrnes returned to South Carolina.

_In April of 1945 Truman neither liked nor trusted the people close to President Roosevelt, and knew he needed the advice of friends whom he could trust without reservation. Truman never knew how tight Groves was with Senator Burns. The next day, April 13th, there was proof that Roosevelt and the people around him didn’t like or trust Truman either.

U.S. Army Major General Leslie Groves—whom Truman had never met—was shown into the Oval Office by a visibly nervous Secretary of State Edward Stettinis Jr. Then Stettinis left the President alone with General Groves. Groves phoned up Senator Jimmy Byrnes and had the Senator break the news of the Manhattan Project to Truman.

Byrnes and Groves told Truman America’s greatest secret: The United States had developed the most powerful weapon the world had ever known: the atomic bomb. Truman was initially furious that he had been kept in the dark by Roosevelt, almost certainly at the advice of those around him.
But Byrnes and Groves reassured him that this was part of Groves plan to keep the lid tight on espionage around the project. Many details of Soviet espionage was given to Truman during this meeting. This meeting was pre-conditioning Truman for Groves coming plan._
You have to know who Byrnes was and why Groves selected him to handle Truma. Jimmy Byrnes was a slick talking politicians & skilled policymaker, Byrnes spent much of his time outside of Washington meeting with foreign leaders. President Truman was uncertain of his own aptitude in matters of foreign policy. This is why Groves selected him to replace Wallace at the DNC in 1944. As a result, Truman came to rely heavily on the confidence of Secretary Byrnes. Groves political acumen was brilliant. Truman went on to name Byrnes his Secretary of State as a result.

_A month after General Alfred Jodl, the chief of staff of the German Armed Forces High Command, had signed on May 7th the unconditional surrender documents ending the war in Europe, the first of Roosevelt’s cronies to go was Stettinius. Truman fired him on June 27, 1945, replacing him on July 3rd—some said at the recommendation of General Groves and Justice Jackson—with James F. Byrnes._ President Truman asked Byrnes to return to Washington on july 3rd as his Secretary of State. He was unanimously confirmed by the Senate without a Congressional hearing.
Within days of his appointment, Byrnes accompanied President Truman to the [Potsdam Conference](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://history.state.gov/milestones/1937-1945/potsdam-conf), a critical turning point in U.S.-Soviet relations. He continued to negotiate the rapidly fraying post-war alliance at conferences in London and Moscow. What happened at Potsdam?
Truman was instructed by Groves and Byrnes to increase tensions between the U.S. and the Soviet Union. Groves instilled a deep distrust of Stalin into Truman and Byrnes made sure it remained so. Stalin had a great trust relationship with FDR and Groves knew that had to change. Because of Truman and Stalin's distrust of one another; Stalin's determination to retain control of Eastern Europe; and Truman's informing Stalin of the development of the atomic bomb. This changed the tone for the USSR.

Byrnes increasingly wielded less control over U.S. foreign policy as disagreements arose with President Truman over how forceful the country should be against the increasingly uncooperative Soviet Union. Groves needed Stalin and the USSR to replace Hitler in post war America to run the forever war machine he helped create in the desert of New Mexico.

Because of Groves, Byrnes was a huge proponent of using the newly-developed atomic bomb against Japan, This was at odds with the other generals who were fighting in Europe and the Pacific at this time. Because of Groves insistance Byrnes gave consisten and persistent advice to Truman, that we needed to drop two [atomic bombs](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://history.state.gov/milestones/1945-1952/atomic) to end the War. As a result, two bombs were detonated over Japan in August of 1945 after the famous Trinty test that Groves completed in the desert of New Mexico in July of 1945. It was also during Byrnes’ tenure as Secretary of State that Groves instructed [George Kennan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://history.state.gov/departmenthistory/people/kennan-george-frost) to write and author his famous [1946 “Long Telegram,”](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://history.state.gov/departmenthistory/short-history/kennan) advocating a change in policy towards the Soviets. This telegram effetively was Groves beginning the Cold War and using Kennan, Byrnes, and Truman as his pawns in this game of War. This infuriated General Eisenhower. President Truman and Secretary Byrnes implemented these changes swiftly, laying the foundations for the early Cold War.

Superpower tensions came to a head in 1947. In March of that year, President Truman went before Congress to announce what became known
as the Truman Doctrine. This was a new foreign policy committing the United States to defending smaller nations threatened by Soviet takeover.
Truman did not explicitly mention the Soviet Union as requested by Groves, but it is clear that is what he meant.

That June, new Secretary of State George Marshall proposed US assistance to rebuild the war-shattered economies of Western Europe. The Marshall Plan was another escalation of the Cold War supported by Groves as part of the Yalta agreements. That plan excluded the USSR. Then, in July, Truman signed the National Security Act, creating the Central Intelligence Agency and much of the modern US national security apparatus.

That is how General Groves sculpted US policy at the end of WW2 to give us the birth of the Industrial military complex.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340039>

Groves taps Kennan to be his main contact in the CIA. Remeber Groves was not in Europe or Asia fighting. Eisenhower was. Groves knew that Kennan was Wild Bill Donovan man in the War time intelligence game. When groves setup Truman to changes US policy at Potsdam, Groves contacted Kennan about his plan.

As a result of Groves plans, George F. Kennan became a key figure in the conversion of the CIA into a covert-operations shop. Kennan was a State Department under Byrnes and an expert on post War USSR headed by Stalin. In May 1947, he acquired power with the help of Groves and Byrnes when he was installed to became chief of the State Department’s Policy Planning Staff, created at the request of the new Secretary of State George C. Marshall. Marshall took over for Byrnes.

This was a brand new, top-level government unit set up by Groves as he left the military responsible for devising US Cold War strategy.

In his new position, Kennan urged the US government to adopt a series of aggressive measures against the Soviet Union. After all, the Soviets
themselves were already waging a secret assault in Western Europe, he said. Europeans were still reeling from the effects of World War II. In 1947, they suffered the worst winter for decades.

Food riots were breaking out in French cities. Looters terrorized the streets of Rome. Moscow was taking advantage of the suffering to foment protests and strikes, hoping that local communist parties would seize power—just as they had in Eastern Europe. The Soviets were also propagandizing against the United States, portraying American efforts to help Europeans—in particular the Marshall Plan—as dollar imperialism.

George F. Kennan was a deeply emotional man like Groves. This is why the were friends. He had a deep affection for the Russian people but despised Soviet communism. General Groves was built the same way.

The Agency: A History of the CIA In October 1947, the Soviet leader Joseph Stalin created a Russian-led political body of Eastern Bloc
communist parties known as the Cominform party in order to coordinate these propaganda attacks.

Fortunately for Kennan, there were others who shared an enthusiasm for political warfare and the desire to take on the Soviets. Among them was
General Groves friends, Donovan’s former deputy in Europe, Allen W. Dulles. Allen Dulles worked with Groves to harvest the Jewish scientist who were brought to America by FDR to build the bomb. Dulles had worked with the Office of Strategic Services (OSS) and was now head of the Council on Foreign Relations.

Kennan’s efforts paid off in December 1947. Harry Truman signed National Security Council directive 4/A based on the urging or a retiring Groves, which authorized the CIA to carry out “covert psychological operations” to counteract Soviet propaganda. The CIA soon put its newfound powers to the test. In the spring of 1948, Italy faced a crucial general election. The communists were bidding for a majority in the country’s first republican parliament.

To counteract the communists, the CIA arranged for the printing of anti-communist posters. It bought up scarce newsprint for the use of pro-
Western newspapers. The agency did not yet have a budget of its own for covert operations, so the money came from an obscure US government currency exchange fund. This was the first time the US government laundered money covertly for the CIA. These funds found their way to Italy by various routes, including apparent gifts from wealthy Italian Americans, the mafia, and US labor officials (Jimmy Hoffa).

James Angleton, a young American intelligence officer who’d had wartime experience in Italy, led the operation on the ground. And the center-right Christian Democrats beat the Italian communists handily. Washington celebrated, and CIA boosters claimed credit, sowing the seeds of a notion that covert interventions could be a magic bullet in the Cold War. Italy was an ally of Hitler. This was a huge success for the newly minted CIA and Truman liked the early results.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T02:26:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340041>

In February 1948, communists took over in Prague. Czechoslovakia fell behind the Iron Curtain. In Germany’s occupied and divided city of
Berlin, the Soviets began to block the access of their western Allies—the United States, Britain, and France—to the sectors under Moscow’s control.
Early in May 1948, Kennan’s policy planning staff presented a plan that he hatched with a now retired General groves for “the inauguration of organized political warfare.” It envisioned the creation of a new “covert political warfare operations directorate within the government.” In other words, Kennan was proposing to take covert operations away from the CIA.

The agency, Kennan explained, was failing to carry out its covert-action mission and was leaving the field to freewheeling private operators. These operators where all working outside the military with General Groves. What was needed, Kennan went on to Truman, was a more determined approach and, at the same time, greater government control.

Kennan got his way. In June 1948, National Security Council directive 10/2 created a new unit to carry out covert operations. This was the Office of Policy Coordination, as it soon became known—a deliberately bland name designed to conceal the organization’s extraordinary range of secret powers. This memo is how General Groves was able to act covertly below Eisenhower's ability to see his actions. Kennan agreed to run the show from the government side and Groves would support Kennan from the industrial side with any support he needed. This is how the industrial military complex was amalgamated covertly as a shadow government.

**HOW IT WAS DONE: NO PRESIDENT WAS NEEDED. GROVES KNEW EXACTLY WHAT HE WAS DOING WITH KENNAN**

The Office of Policy Coordination—or OPC—was a strange beast because of the influence of Groves due to what he learned about the Executive branch under FDR and the his lead of the Manhattan Project. Per Kennan’s desire for control, it answered to the secretaries of state and defense
rather than the director of Central Intelligence. Groves saw to this because he installed Truman and Byrnes in power before he left. When Byrnes left Marshall was loyal to Truman and both thought they were immune from the War machine. Neither knew about the alliance of Groves with Kennan. However, to save the State Department and Defense Department from the potential taint of dirty tricks, the OPC was housed administratively within the CIA. Covert operations were now the responsibility of a unit that hovered somewhere in-between the State Department and CIA by design of Groves who was a process engineer and he knew how information flowed in the Executive branch.

This arrangement would last until 1952 under Eisenhower, when the OPC was folded into the CIA. This was Eisenhower's biggest error. He basically reinvited Groves back into the military as a covert operator due to Kennan's help.

The obvious pick to head the OPC was Dulles, but when Kennan offered

him the job, Dulles turned it down because he thought Truman would lose to Dewey in 1948 and he did not want to be replaced before he had control. Kennan turned to another veteran of the OSS: Frank G. Wisner. As assistant director for policy coordination, Wisner quickly got to work recruiting staff members who shared his love of covert action and hatred of communism.

Many were—like him—upper class and former operatives of the now-defunct OSS. All of these people were loyal to Groves as well and many were his friends from the Manhattan Project.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T02:30:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340042>

The Office of Policy Coordination opened for business in September 1948. At this stage, it had an improvised feel. However, it soon became clear that the new unit had bureaucratic clout. Field officers fanned out across the world, usually under the cover of a diplomatic posting
Genuine diplomats in the US foreign service now had to get used to spies appearing in their midst, often with mysterious access to foreign officials and better pay.

The OPC had money because it was granted secret access to the massive funds associated with the Marshall Plan, the post–World War II US
program for reconstructing the economies of Western Europe. Since the USSR was not part of the Marshall Plan the CIA had massive funding from the get go. The OPC was able to spend these funds on covert operations—or so-called covert projects—without having to give any account to Congress.

The following year, in June 1949, Congress enshrined the principle in law through legislation known as the Central Intelligence Agency Act. It exempted the agency from having to disclose any of its spending. In effect, Congress had handed Frank Wisner a blank check.

The OPC stepped up its covert funding of politicians and groups deemed friendly to the US cause. These included the European Movement—
a coalition of European leaders who were advocating for continental federation. This was an initiative that would eventually become the
European Union. This was essentially when europe became the 51st of the USA unofficially.

In the late 1940s, Washington was keen on European unity: trade, diplomacy, and security. All would benefit, the Americans felt, if they could
deal with a politically united Europe. Consequently, US officials sought to support the European Movement, but they wanted to be discreet because they did not want to embarrass their allies politically. The solution was along the lines of one that George Kennan himself
proposed: the creation of an apparently private group of US citizens who would accept funding and guidance from the government and act
as a secret channel of financial support to the European Movement.

The American Committee on United Europe came into existence in early 1949 and was soon channelling OPC subsidies to the European
Movement of nearly $1 million a year. The intelligence connections of the American Committee on United Europe were not even very well
disguised. The chairman was none other than Wild Bill Donovan and the vice chair was Allen Dulles.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T03:05:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340043>

The Cold War work of the National Committee for a Free Europe was limited to psychological warfare (or propaganda). But Kennan’s vision
of liberation also involved even more direct action. It was at this time that the FBI hired Alton Ochsner to work for them. Kennan was briefed on Dr. Ochsner role in the polio vaccine for the military, for his staunch anti-Communist stance in New Orleans, and for his aid he gave to many of the dictators in Central and South America who were helping Kennan during operations at this time.

Across the pond in Europe these changes led to the OPC’s effort to infiltrate Eastern Bloc émigrés behind the Iron Curtain, inhopes that they might inspire resistance movements against the communists, including the October 1949 operation against Albania’s Enver Hoxha, a communist leader.

This turned out to be a massive failure on the same order of scale as the the 1962 invasion of the Bay of Pigs and it showed Kennan and Truman that covert operations were not a be all end all. It was this failure that turned Truman against the CIA. He realized creating them was a huge error and he told this to General Eisenhower late in his second term.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-09-26T03:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-340044>

During the 1930s, German aggression built up, culminating in the invasion of Poland on Sept. 1, 1939. As a result, France and England declared war on Germany. The United States entered the war in 1941 after Japan attacked Pearl Harbor. During World War II, Eisenhower was sent to Europe to help develop the campaign against the Axis powers. Faced with juggling personal inputs from world leaders like President Franklin D. Roosevelt and Prime Minister Winston Churchill with those of Allied generals, Eisenhower found that Connor’s tutelage had given him the ability to combine and execute political and military objectives. Among the top Allied European field commanders were men from his past, including West Point classmate General Omar Bradley, and now-General Patton.

The British were fighting Italian and German forces in North Africa by the time Eisenhower was sent overseas. He worked with British commanders, including Field Marshal Bernard Law Montgomery, and navigated through the complicated loyalties of the French military leaders (some supported the Nazi regime and others supported the Allies). He planned Operation Torch, which called for an invasion of the French colonies in northern Africa in November 1942. The campaign was a success, and President Roosevelt wanted to award the Medal of Honor to Eisenhower for his planning efforts. Eisenhower refused, insisting that the medal was for heroism displayed during combat and that he was unworthy of such an honor. He was promoted to Supreme Commander, Allied Expeditionary Forces in December 1943.

Although the Allies began to show signs of winning the war on the Eastern European front by 1943, the German army still occupied all of western Europe. The Allies would need to invade and liberate western Eurpope and take pressure off of the Soviets fighting the Germans on the eastern front. Eisenhower worked closely with Bradley to orchestrate the air, land, and sea invasion of Normandy that took place on June 6, 1944. This invasion is also known as Operation Overlord. Eisenhower’s experience from the cross-country convoy in 1919 provided invaluable logistic insight as he moved men and equipment across North Africa and Europe. Additionally, the in-depth study of the World War I French landscape gave him the advantage of providing alternate routes of attack in driving the Germans out of occupied France.

Eisenhower’s reputation for connecting with the troops, along with ensuring the enlisted men were given the same morale amenities as officers, earned him widespread respect among Soldiers. When he learned that the Italian island of Capri had been designated only for officers to enjoy Rest & Recuperation (R&R), he demanded that equal opportunity be given to the enlisted men, stating, “This is supposed to be a rest center—for combat men—not a playground for the Brass!” He led by example in small ways, too. During the war, food and cigarettes were rationed out among the Soldiers. If a Soldier ran out of cigarettes, they would have to wait until the next supply of rations came in. Eisenhower held himself to the same standard and rolled his own cigarettes when he ran out. He also made a personal effort to connect with the troops. After giving the final approval of the D-Day invasion of Normandy, Eisenhower visited the paratroopers in Newbury, England, on June 5, 1944, while they geared up for the invasion. He asked them questions and shook their hands. He then stayed to watch the planes depart. In his pocket was a piece of paper that he wrote and signed, accepting complete responsibility if Operation Overlord failed.

By the time World War II ended, Eisenhower was popular throughout the United States. Three years after the war ended, he stepped back from the military spotlight to become president of Columbia University in New York. In 1950, the United States became involved in the Korean War. General Groves was involved in this war from outside the military. Groves loved war because it brought him profit & power. Groves and Eisenhower saw the world differently.

Many Americans believed Eisenhower could bring a swift end to the war and encouraged him to run for president of the United States. In 1952, he officially declared his intention to run on the Republican ticket. He won the election and was inaugurated as the 34th president of the United States.

He served as president for two terms, between 1953-61. The diplomacy he learned from dealing with world leaders during World War II, along with his military strategic mindset, guided his decisions as a president. The experiences he gained as Allied Supreme Commander prepared him to evaluate international conflicts. He recognized that the Korean War battles would continue to perpetuate a stalemate, and advised against investing more American troops and equipment to the conflict. Seven months after his inauguration, an armistice was signed between North and South Korea. In 1954, when France asked for extensive military support in their fight against Vietnam, Eisenhower viewed the situation with a military strategic mindset and declined their request. This became a huge problem for Groves, Dulles, the Industrial military complex and for JFK.

I just joined the forum and was drawn to this thread first. Thank you Dr. Kruse for exposing the dire impact on the arts. Thank you for ALL you do to expose the truth and wake us up. As a professional classical composer I wanted to add that the avant garde influence on classical music began in Vienna, Austria with the "Second Viennese School” in the early 1900's. Comprised of composer Arnold Schoenberg and his pupils, the Second Viennese School morphed into atonality and 12-tone technique, abandoning any tonal center, rhythm or harmony - the three pillars of music. It destroyed music.

In college I was taught by a Pulitzer Prize-winning composer who embraced the Second Viennese School but allowed me my freedom, for which I was grateful. My honors thesis for orchestra, soloists and chorus which I also conducted, resulted in five professional commissions which launched my international career. Nevertheless, academia was and STILL IS infested with this darkness (my daughter is currently a graduate student in Piano Performance at a prestigious music conservatory). Most professors are intellectuals rather than true artists who have no interest in natural geometry and natural form expressed through beauty, rhythm, melody and harmony - the essence of all the arts. Instead we are audibly assaulted by snippets of good ideas (at times) that are reduced to cacophonous rubble and utter ugliness with no coherent form. Or the dumbed down version of tonal music wrapped in a saccharine bow called “minimalism". This so-called music is devoid of spiritual sustenance. It does not speak to the Heart.

As a result of what I found in academia, I did not pursue higher degrees and instead made my own way as a musician and composer both in Europe and the U.S. Yet, even removed from academia, the general attitude of those in the modern music scene was not inspiring or appetizing to be around. Yes, audiences and critics loved my music, but the environment was stale and cold. Similar to what AI is doing to humanity now. Removing the Creative Spark.

So I slowly stepped away from classical composing in the 2000's and focused instead on creating a thriving online business. I am first and foremost a classical musician - music is my life's blood. I treasure those who appreciate the importance of great art as an instrument of transcendence, healing, joy and beauty, an appreciation that is utterly lacking in the U.S. - especially in our schools where arts are mostly absent. When you hear great music, no matter what the genre, magic happens. The same goes for the other arts. Thank you Dr. Kruse for exposing the history of this dark story.

As a side note, the last great classical composers who were not corrupted (though suffered greatly in the Soviet Union) were mostly Russian - including Rachmaninoff, Stravinsky, Shostakovich and Prokofiev.

>
> So I slowly stepped away from classical composing in the 2000's and focused instead on creating a thriving online business. I am first and foremost a classical musician - music is my life's blood. I treasure those who appreciate the importance of great art as an instrument of transcendence, healing, joy and beauty, an appreciation that is utterly lacking in the U.S. - especially in our schools where arts are mostly absent. When you hear great music, no matter what the genre, magic happens. The same goes for the other arts. Thank you Dr. Kruse for exposing the history of this dark story.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-03T00:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-groves-forced-out-of-the-military-in-1947.29488/post-353797>

Tito was the founder of the Non-Aligned Movement, positioning Yugoslavia as a neutral player during the Cold War, refusing to fully align with either the United States or the Soviet Union. This was extraordinary for a communist leader, as it defied the bipolar world order and gave smaller nations a voice.

His diplomatic skill enabled him to secure economic and political support from both the East and the West, a balancing act that few leaders have achieved.

In 1948, Tito broke with Stalin and the Soviet Union, refusing to subordinate Yugoslavia to Moscow’s control. This was a bold move, as few communist leaders dared to challenge Stalin. The Tito-Stalin split made Yugoslavia the first communist country to chart an independent path, earning Tito global attention. He survived Soviet attempts to undermine or assassinate him, showcasing his resilience and strategic acumen.

Tito led Yugoslavia, a country with diverse ethnic groups (Serbs, Croats, Slovenes, Bosnians, Macedonians, etc.), languages, and religions, through a period of relative stability. His ability to maintain unity in such a fragmented region was remarkable, especially given the ethnic tensions that resurfaced after his death. He implemented the policy of "Brotherhood and Unity," which promoted coexistence while suppressing nationalist movements that could destabilize the country. He was a communist in name only.

Tito was known for his charm, accessibility, and ability to connect with ordinary people. His lifestyle—luxurious yet relatable—made him a larger-than-life figure. For example, he enjoyed fine things (yachts, tailored suits) but was seen as a "man of the people." He was pragmatic, blending Marxist ideology with practical policies, such as market socialism (worker self-management), which gave Yugoslavia a unique economic model compared to other communist states.

Tito cultivated a strong cult of personality, with his image ubiquitous in Yugoslavia. This helped foster national pride and loyalty, though it also suppressed dissent. His ability to maintain this image without alienating the masses was a testament to his political skill.

Before the 1948 split with the USSR, Tito was deeply involved with the Comintern (Communist International) and trained in Moscow during the 1930s. Some speculate he worked with Soviet intelligence (NKVD) during this period, as many Comintern operatives did. However, his later defiance of Stalin suggests he prioritized Yugoslav interests over any earlier allegiances. Declassified documents (e.g., from Soviet archives) show Stalin’s frustration with Tito, including plans to assassinate him, which undermines claims of Tito being a Soviet puppet.

My research shows he was not a puppet to anyone. Tito controlled Yugoslavia’s intelligence service, the UDBA (State Security Administration), which was highly effective in suppressing internal dissent and monitoring external threats (e.g., Soviet agents after 1948). The UDBA was loyal to Tito and operated independently of foreign agencies. I found no concrete evidence that Tito was a direct asset of Western intelligence. His cooperation with the West was pragmatic, driven by the need to counter Soviet pressure following the 1948 assassination attempt. For example, Yugoslavia received U.S. economic aid and military equipment, but this was part of open diplomatic relations.

I understand why Slavs love the guy. Under Tito, Yugoslavia experienced relative economic growth, industrialization, and social mobility compared to other communist states. Citizens enjoyed freedoms (e.g., travel, cultural expression) uncommon in the Eastern Bloc.
Tito’s defiance of both superpowers and his role in WWII made him a symbol of Yugoslav strength and independence. His charisma, wartime heroism, and visible presence in public life endeared him to many. He was seen as a protector against external threats and internal division. He gave his country free healthcare, education, and workers’ rights. Under his regime, improved living standards fostered loyalty.

# Thread: Questioning the Ingredients in "Traditional" Vaccines Today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questioning-the-ingredients-in-traditional-vaccines-today.29489/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyhoffman — 2024-09-24T00:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questioning-the-ingredients-in-traditional-vaccines-today.29489/post-339918>

Hey everyone,

I've been reading up on the push to convert many of the "traditional" vaccines—like the flu vaccine, tetanus (Tdap), shingles, pneumococcal, Hep A/B, HPV, etc.—from the older egg/culture-based technology (with adjuvants like aluminum) to the mRNA "software" approach. Fauci and others have talked about how this shift would give vaccine manufacturers much greater flexibility and efficiency when it comes to dealing with infectious diseases.

This got me thinking: there's been a lot of speculation about how some vaccines, like the flu shot, may have been tampered with before the COVID-19 pandemic, potentially making people more susceptible to COVID or even leading to false positives with PCR tests.

So my question is: Are these "traditional" vaccines (like the flu or tetanus booster) being tainted in some way today? If someone were to go get a flu shot or a Tdap booster right now, can we trust that the ingredients listed on the vaccine insert are truly what's inside?

1.  **What kind of contamination would it be?** From what I’ve seen, they still need to reapply for approval and go through standard clinical trials if they want to make a new product. So, it seems unlikely they could just magically add RNA into a preexisting vaccine that’s been around for a decade or more, given the stringent regulations. Also, what could possible contaminants be doing in the body? I’m assuming it’s not something like charged lipid nanoparticles delivering cytotoxic spike protein fragments into every organ, or SV40 integrating into your genome, but if you have evidence of that, I'd love to see it.
2.  **What I’ve found so far is that the old vaccines are still toxic.** There is **definitely** a current push to convert them to another platform, but that seems to be the extent of it for now. I’ve also come across recommendations to start using **manganese instead of aluminum** as an adjuvant, which could be disastrous in its own right.
3.  **Has the manufacturing process changed?** From what I’ve researched, the manufacturing process might have already changed, or could change soon. For example, the tetanus vaccine used to be made by injecting horse serum-derived antitetanus antibodies into soldiers back in the early 1900s. Then in the 1920s, formaldehyde was used to inactivate the tetanus toxoid, which created the first toxoid vaccines. Today, tetani bacterium is grown in culture, then deactivated and mass-produced in bioreactors. But as far as I can tell, vaccine manufacturing hasn’t changed that much—it’s still based on very old, outdated science.

Has anyone come across any additional info about changes in the manufacturing process or potential contaminants in the "traditional" vaccines? I'd love to know what everyone here thinks.

### gccyclefit — 2024-09-25T02:12:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dermatomyositis.29492/post-339997>

Thanks ahead of time for any guidance.

My wife has a tentative dx of Dermatomyositis. It looks very daunting. At this point, we have a host of referrals to specialist and labs. Unfortunately, I dont have any data other than her symptoms.
Condition started 2 mos. ago with itching and rash along the lateral and posterior aspect of her arms. As the symptoms have progress, she has developed pain, muscle spasm and edema in the upper extremities. Her pain is exacerbated by active use of her arms such as drying her hair or typing. Ice helps with the symptoms temporarily. She has other sporatic areas of dermatitis on her lateral hips in medial knees that resembles contact dermatitis. In addition, to the UE symptoms, she has proximal quad fatigue and weakness.

Thanks for any success stories.

David

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-25T03:29:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/microchimerism.29494/post-356930>

Telegony is linked to this topic, so let’s clear up some misconceptions: A woman doesn’t have to get pregnant for “foreign DNA” to permanently persist in her system. Any act of insemination (even via ingestion) leads to spermatozoa-somatic cell fusion. These hybrid cells are irrevocably altered and replicate indefinitely. In animal models, the peritoneal injection of sperm with stained DNA (even if it’s from a different species) is found to not only persist indefinitely in the body, but also spread to every single tissue system within 48 hours. Sometimes, the presence of chimeric cells triggers autoimmune reactions that eventually lead to tumors and other diseases. All of this happens regardless of whether an egg is fertilized/conception occurs.

Sperm fuses with somatic cells to form chimeric cells (regardless of fertilization)
Chimeric cells with foreign DNA are highly susceptible to malignant tumor formation
Chimeric cells persist for 40-50 generations (bloodline curse)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-28T03:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/microchimerism.29494/post-357053>

I’ve posited in the viral marketing blog that 98% of human DNA that doesn’t code for proteins serves as a “long-term memory” library that encodes evolutionary environmental events (e.g., mass extinctions) through the vibrational potentials of water bonds. This aligns with my earlier view that memory is a frequency-based phenomenon stored in water, rather than DNA’s nucleotide sequence alone:

Water Bond Potentials: Non-coding DNA, interacting with cellular water (e.g., in the nucleus or CSF), forms a fractal network of hydrogen bonds whose vibrational frequencies reflect past environmental stresses (e.g., ice ages, volcanic eruptions). These frequencies act as an electromagnetic “barcode” of evolutionary memory. This was critical in haplotype formation.

Fractal Nature: The fractal aspect suggests self-similar patterns across scales, microscopic water bonds mirroring macroscopic evolutionary events—implying a recursive information system.

Microchimerism and Telegony: Memory Across Generations

Microchimerism and telegony introduce the idea that this frequency barcode might extend beyond an individual’s lifetime, influencing or retaining memory across generations:

-   Microchimerism: This refers to the presence of small populations of genetically distinct cells (e.g., from a mother or fetus) in an individual’s body. These cells could carry water bond frequencies from other individuals, potentially encoding memories or traits from those sources. For example, maternal cells in a child might retain vibrational signatures of the mother’s environmental experiences.
-   Telegony: Historically, telegony is the discredited idea that a female’s offspring can inherit traits from a previous mate. In your frequency-based model, this might be reinterpreted as the transfer of water bond vibrations (e.g., via seminal fluid or placental exchange) that imprint evolutionary memories onto future generations, preserved in non-coding DNA.

Biophysical Mechanism

Let’s hypothesize how this works, building on my framework:

Water as a Memory Medium: Cellular water, influenced by light and EMF, forms unique vibrational states that interact with non-coding DNA. These vibrations, altered by environmental frequencies during mass extinctions, are stored as bond potentials.

Fractal Transmission: Microchimeric cells or telegonic exchanges could transfer these water bond frequencies, creating a fractal resonance across generations. For instance, a mother’s exposure to UV light might imprint a frequency onto her water bonds, passed to her child via microchimeric cells, echoing an ancient extinction event.

Non-Coding DNA Role: The 98% non-coding DNA, with its repetitive sequences (e.g., satellite DNA), might act as a resonator, amplifying and stabilizing these frequencies. A change in water bond vibration (e.g., from modern EMF stress) could trigger a “memory” response, altering gene expression to mimic past conditions.

VDR on the IMM, modulated by sunlight, stabilizes water bond coherence via CCO, protecting mtDNA and influencing cellular water dynamics. This local memory system could sync with the non-coding DNA’s long-term barcode, ensuring evolutionary frequencies are preserved.

Evolutionary Timeline:

-   GOE (~2.4–2.1 Bya): Proto-VDR/CCO evolved to encode initial frequency barcodes in water, with non-coding DNA beginning to store fractal memories of oxygen/UV stress.
-   Neoproterozoic (~800–600 Mya): True VDR refined this concept, integrating microchimeric or telegonic frequency transfers in early metazoans, thereby enhancing evolutionary memory.
-   Cambrian Explosion (~541 Mya): Non-coding DNA amplified fractal memories, driving rapid diversification via water bond changes in boundary tissues.
-   Modern Era (~Present): EMF disrupts VDR/CCO and water coherence, potentially scrambling non-coding DNA frequencies and affecting microchimeric/telegenic memory transfer.

These actions are deeply unconstitutional, a violation of your 4th Amendment rights, and enable political persecution. That’s why Mike Lee just introduced the bank a new law. The Saving Privacy Act looks to Repeals the Bank Secrecy Act’s SAR and CTR reporting requirements while maintaining record-keeping provisions.

It repeals the Corporate Transparency Act.
It strengthens Fourth Amendment protections, bolstering warrant requirements in the Right to Financial Privacy Act of 1978.
It repeals the SEC’s Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) database.
It prohibits the creation of a Central Bank Digital Currency.
It requires congressional authorization for financial regulations deemed major rules. (weak point) Needs SCOTUS
It requires congressional approval for any new databases that collect personally identifiable information of U.S. citizens.
It institutes penalties for federal employees who illegally seek constitutionally protected financial information = penalty should be treason for the industrial miliatry complex thiefs.

It establishes a private right of action for Americans and financial institutions harmed by illicit government activity. Please like and share if you think more Americans should know about this grave threat to our freedom.

For the credit/debit card transactions that was one of the main reasons we went to chip/pin. There are three types of credit card transactions. 1,2,3. 1 only collected the merchants BDA, billing zipcode, and transaction amount. Level 2 collected Sales tax info, customer code, merchant zipcode, Merchant tax id, and merchant state. Type 3 basically collects everything. Ship from/to zip code, invoice number, order number, item product code, item description, item quantity, unit of measure, freight amount, duty amount, and some others.

Chip and pin was never for your privacy or safety. It was always to collect more data.

He was also a member of [Skull and Bones](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skull_and_Bones) with his classmate and friend, [Prescott Bush](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prescott_Bush).

Harriman joined the [Merchant Shipbuilding Corporation](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Merchant_Shipbuilding_Corporation), a firm in which his brother W. Averell Harriman was interested.

In 1922, Harriman joined W. A. Harriman Company, investment bankers in New York City, and in 1923, he became its vice president. In 1927, the two brothers formed the banking firm [Harriman Brothers and Company](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harriman_Brothers_and_Company). This was after the Treaty of Versailles and right before the Depression when the Bankers hatched their plan to cause a depression to lure the world into another War so the Crown could reclaim the allodial title of the USA and Russia back. Hitler's Nazi Germany rise was part of their plan.

King Edward VIII was a member of the Pilgrim Society, and so was Fritz Thyssen. Who is he? Harriman was one of the seven directors of the [Union Banking Corporation](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Union_Banking_Corporation)(along with Prescott Sheldon Bush, father of future U.S. president [George H. W. Bush](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_H._W._Bush)), which financed [Fritz Thyssen](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fritz_Thyssen), a donor to the [Nazi Party](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nazi_Party) and whose assets were seized by the United States government during [World War II](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_War_II) under the [Trading with the Enemy Act](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trading_with_the_Enemy_Act) and Executive Order No. 9095.

In 1931, the firm was merged with Brown Bros. & Co., with Roland as vice president. Headquartered on Wall Street, Brown Brothers Harriman started with nine partners and about 200 employees. It performed specialized banking services for customers, mainly medium-sized corporations; it was not a member of the [Federal Reserve](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Reserve) System or the [Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Deposit_Insurance_Corporation). They were able to operate outside the Fed system. This was by design for the Crown.

In 1968, Harriman and three other senior partners at Brown Brothers ([Robert A. Lovett](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_A._Lovett), secretary of defense under President [Harry Truman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harry_Truman); [Prescott Bush](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prescott_Bush), a former senator from [Connecticut](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Connecticut); and [Knight Woolley](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Knight_Woolley&action=edit&redlink=1) — ironically all Yale men) moved "upstairs," literally and figuratively, to make way for the younger partners, one of whom was [Robert Roosa](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Roosa), former undersecretary of the U.S. Treasury.

They also merged with members of the immediate Royal Family, United States secretaries of state, and United States ambassadors to the Court of St. James's, who are customarily admitted ex-officio to membership in the Society. In the crucible of adversity, where pressure mounts and challenges loom large, we uncover the raw essence of accountability and purpose. Today, March 29, 2025, the United States stands at a precipice—its political landscape fractured by unrelenting division, economic uncertainty, and a relentless tug-of-war between ideals. Our burdens, from the erosion of trust in institutions to the fierce debates over liberty and justice, are not mere weights to bear; they are the very forge in which our national character is tempered. When failure threatens—be it through legislative gridlock, cultural discord, or the specter of external threats—we are jolted from complacency, compelled to confront our fears rather than bow to them. Adversity, in its unyielding beauty, strips away illusion and reveals our resilience: a people capable of rising, as we always have, through the storms of democracy. In this moment, our strength lies not in surrender to the obstacles, but in the courage to wield our collective purpose against them.

In my work we study people like Mae Wan Ho and Erich Blechschmidt and today I was thinking about the fluid body of the embryo in it's early days. Blechschmidt theorizes that changes happening within the embryo came from forces outside of the cell informing the cell nucleus. So the cell is a responsive and reactive player not the leader of the actions or changes. In his book Biokinetics he talks about

forces causing movements such as pushing pulling stretching folding, must be generated within the embryonic growth field as organs and tissues start to form.

In my quest to understand where these forces are coming from, I have been reading your blogs and wanted to ask if Tensegrity #6: Hydrogen Bonding networks in water is explaining what's behind this? Is this blog explaining the forces that are shaping the embryo especially in the first 20 days post conception? Is it a memory in the water, or the hyrogen network?

This light has tremendous power to sculpt the matter in cells and water to drive development forward because small wavelength light carries huge momentum in how it interacts with water and all matter in the embryo at this stage.

There is a deep connection between thermodynamics in biology and the processing of energy and information. In the adult form this process begins in the skin & eyes and these biophysical process were explored in that talk.

In 2016 I came Nourish Vermont and explained how food can only be understood via photosynthesis. In 2017 I showed how obesity begins in the eye at the choroid layer of the eyes and not with foods of exercise. All of these things tie light, water, and magnetism the computations that mitochondria must make.

What stands between the sun and our mitochondria in the adult form?

The skin.

What stands between the sun and the mitochondria in the embryo?

The mother's skin and her PLACENTA

Do nature and biology add lipid droplets to our skin and placenta to create a laser from visiable light that is capable of changing sunlight into coherent light that activates programs in the human placenta? Yes, it does. Vermont 2018 was about the skin in the adult form uses light of the SQ fat mass to build a disipative state.

# Thread: Interacting with Melanin Renovation Rx blog

### BEW — 2024-09-27T16:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interacting-with-melanin-renovation-rx-blog.29499/post-340084>

On p 36 (pdf) uncle Jack stated “Vagal nerve stimulation increases melanin renovation. Anything that increases vagal nerve stimulation is beneficial.” I don’t know much about the vagus nerve much less how to stimulate it, so I asked Duckduckgo AI chat about it and here’s it’s answer:

Increasing stimulation of the vagus nerve can have various health benefits, including improved mood, reduced stress, and enhanced digestion. Here are several methods to stimulate the vagus nerve:

1\. \*\*Deep Breathing Exercises\*\*: Engaging in deep, slow breathing can activate the vagus nerve. Try diaphragmatic breathing, where you breathe deeply into your belly rather than your chest.

2\. \*\*Meditation and Mindfulness\*\*: Practices that promote relaxation and mindfulness can enhance vagal tone. Regular meditation can help reduce stress and improve overall well-being.

3\. \*\*Cold Exposure\*\*: Exposure to cold, such as taking cold showers or splashing cold water on your face, can stimulate the vagus nerve.

4\. \*\*Singing, Humming, or Chanting\*\*: Activities that involve vocalization can stimulate the vagus nerve due to its connection to the muscles in the throat.

5\. \*\*Yoga and Tai Chi\*\*: These practices combine movement, breath control, and meditation, which can enhance vagal tone.

6\. \*\*Massage\*\*: Certain types of massage, particularly around the neck and throat, may stimulate the vagus nerve.

7\. \*\*Probiotics\*\*: Some studies suggest that gut health can influence vagal tone. Consuming probiotics may help improve gut health and, in turn, stimulate the vagus nerve.

8\. \*\*Social Connections\*\*: Engaging in positive social interactions and maintaining strong relationships can enhance vagal tone.

9\. \*\*Laughter\*\*: Laughter can stimulate the vagus nerve and promote relaxation.

10\. \*\*Intermittent Fasting\*\*: Some research suggests that intermittent fasting may enhance vagal tone and improve overall health.

Before making significant changes to your lifestyle or health regimen, it's always a good idea to consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you have underlying health conditions

### rtd — 2024-09-27T19:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interacting-with-melanin-renovation-rx-blog.29499/post-340094>

> [BEW said:](/goto/post?id=340087)
>
> Increasing stimulation of the vagus nerve can have various health benefits

I disclose that I own and use the product NuCalm. Minus purchasing it and time, I have zero monetary investments in it; I am not a paid promoter. I was apprehensive about purchasing a subscription to the product. I did research and glad I did purchase it, it works for me. One can read their website on the applicable neuroscience.

My short take:
HRV is the signal measured in time between heartbeats. Convert the measurement from the time domain to the frequency domain and plot a power spectral density (PSD). Low Frequency (LF) band is the sympathetic nervous system. High frequency (HF) is the parasympathetic nervous system. Take a ratio. 1 = balanced. Not one, can see which system is dominant.

NuCalm uses non-linear oscillating binaural beats, with optional bio-disc (a whole different application of energy medicine), to affect the vagus nerve to be in parasympathetic dominant state.

The product works for my N=1. While using it and after, my heart rate is lower, I find myself taking deeper breathes, and my blood pressure readings are improved. During it I feel peaceful and my body feels heavy. There are zero drugs invovled.

Down side: finding the time to use it regularly. I now do the rescue 120 session around 3:30/4-6am, followed by a 30 min flow state.

I was apprehensive about purchasing a subscription to the product. I did research and glad I did purchase it, it works for me. One can read their website on the applicable neuroscience.
>
> My short take:
> HRV is the signal measured in time between heartbeats. Convert the measurement from the time domain to the frequency domain and plot a power spectral density (PSD). Low Frequency (LF) band is the sympathetic nervous system. High frequency (HF) is the parasympathetic nervous system. Take a ratio. 1 = balanced. Not one, can see which system is dominant.
>
> NuCalm uses non-linear oscillating binaural beats, with optional bio-disc (a whole different application of energy medicine), to affect the vagus nerve to be in parasympathetic dominant state.
>
> The product works for my N=1. While using it and after, my heart rate is lower, I find myself taking deeper breathes, and my blood pressure readings are improved. During it I feel peaceful and my body feels heavy. There are zero drugs invovled.
>
> Down side: finding the time to use it regularly. I now do the rescue 120 session around 3:30/4-6am, followed by a 30 min flow state.

Hello. Do you do anything to minimize your EMF exposure while using NuCalm? After listening to neuroscientist Dr. Trish Leigh describe "brain rewiring," I ordered a Muse 2--it sounds similar to NuCalm. I'm having second thoughts about using the device because I'm actually trying to AVOID turning on my phone. Does NuCalm require Bluetooth? I'm glad the device is working for you. In my case, maybe the benefits of using the device would outweigh any risks.

### rtd — 2025-01-08T18:29:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interacting-with-melanin-renovation-rx-blog.29499/post-344887>

> [La-Shawn Hill said:](/goto/post?id=344739)
>
> Hello. Do you do anything to minimize your EMF exposure while using NuCalm? After listening to neuroscientist Dr. Trish Leigh describe "brain rewiring," I ordered a Muse 2--it sounds similar to NuCalm. I'm having second thoughts about using the device because I'm actually trying to AVOID turning on my phone. Does NuCalm require Bluetooth? I'm glad the device is working for you. In my case, maybe the benefits of using the device would outweigh any risks.

1) NuCalm does require a phone app.
2) The tracks can be downloaded to play offline, e.g. when you phone is in Airplane mode.
3) The app needs a network connection at first to initialize, then downloaded tracks can be played offline. (I contacted NuCalm asking for full offline capability, even providing a technical explanation on how to do it and still track usage. My guess is deaf ears).
4) NuCalm's "DeepSleep" tracks do not require headphones. All the other products do.
5) I mostly download tracks and play them offline.
6) NuCalm app does not require blue tooth. I purchased a USB-C to 1/8" audio jack plus charge port dongle because many new phones have eliminated the head phone jack.

The endpoint of sound is an analog wave to the ear. Using a computer, the application Audacity, an A/D converter, it is possible to capture the analog signal from the phone audio jack and record a WAV file.

I was wondering what the best way for me to live and eat in this environment is.

Also is there value in doing delibrate cold exposure, either through cold showers or ice baths? If so when would be the best times of the day to do this?

Any other tips for living in this environment would be appreciated. In the process of fixing up and putting my house on the market to get out of this area because the light is just too poor for too much of the year. But in the mean time I'd like to make the time there less inflammatory overall.

"Through his studies involving dogs Richet demonstrated an opposite effect in 1902. After an initial low dose of a substance, a new dose some weeks later could produce a severe reaction." "In 1913, \[Richet\] was awarded the Nobel Prize for his researches on anaphylaxis. He invented this word to designate the sensitivity developed by an organism after it had been given a parenteral injection of a colloid or protein substance or a toxin (1902)." (Parenteral drug administration is drug administration by intravenous, intramuscular, or subcutaneous injection.)

Source: [https://nobelprize.org/prizes/medicine/1913/richet/biographical/#:~:text=In%201913%2C%20he%20was%20awarded,or%20a%20toxin%20(1902)](https://t.co/u1IZUdU6Zy)

Source: [https://nobelprize.org/prizes/medicine/1913/richet/facts/](https://t.co/eONFf1m6BO)

[](https://x.com/SenseReceptor/status/1840945995414138893/photo/1)
[](https://x.com/SenseReceptor/status/1840945995414138893/photo/2)
HE WAS A EUGENICIST. Just like Gates.

Richet believed in the inferiority of black people, was a proponent of eugenics, and presided over the French Eugenics Society towards the end of his life." "Richet was a proponent of eugenics, advocating sterilization and marriage prohibition for those with mental disabilities. He expressed his eugenist ideas in his 1919 book La Sélection Humaine. From 1920 to 1926 he presided over the French Eugenics Society.

His work shows why it's IMPOSSIBLE to "vaccinate" against anything.
THIS is why "vaccines" are the PERFECT POISONS that generate future reactions.
THIS is why eugenicists like Bill Gates love "vaccines."
1986 gave them cart blanche to clear the lower classes of being pests on the globalist system.

Retired pharma R&D executive Sasha Latypova [@sasha\_latypova](https://x.com/sasha_latypova) describes for James Delingpole [@JMCDelingpole](https://x.com/JMCDelingpole) how "vaccines," essentially, work in the exact opposite way as we're told they do—meaning it's literally impossible to make a vaccine that does anything but poison somebody.

### [Sasha Latypova](https://rumble.com/v5gnghp-sasha-latypova.html?e9s=src_v1_ucp)

Alexandra 'Sasha' Latypova is a former big pharma industry exec. Her research has exposed how all covid countermeasures, including the biological warfare agents marketed as ‘covid-19 vaccines’ were cr

 rumble.com

While we're told that "vaccines" work by giving us a small amount of a toxic substance—e.g. a bacteria or virus— in turn allowing us to create immunity against future infections, what the injections actually do is make us vulnerable to said toxins. Furthermore, "vaccines" prime our bodies to react badly to \*anything\* that's injected into us, including benign substances like milk or egg proteins. Hence the proliferation of allergies, such as allergies to milk, eggs, wheat, peanuts, etc. Incredibly, Latypova explains how this has been known since 1913, when Charles Richet—a French physiologist and self-proclaimed eugenicist—won a Nobel Prize for figuring out that injecting animals with toxins primes them for harmful or deadly reactions if they encounter the same toxins in the environment, even in small amounts. He called these reactions "anaphylactic" reactions, but said that these reactions also included allergies. After researching this topic and analyzing Richet's work, Latypova says she's come to the conclusion that "everybody who is...closely familiar with this history and work cannot think that it's possible to vaccinate." The pharma insider adds, "It's impossible to vaccinate for anything. And Richet has demonstrated it conclusively and was given Nobel Prize for it...because he figured out how to poison everyone by sensitizing them to the most commonly occurring things in their environment." "It's the most ingenious way of poisoning," Latypova says.

The eugenicists never went away. They all still think that way.

They all still think that they should poison us and limit our reproduction because, you know, we're polluting the earth now. We're causing climate change," Latypova adds. "They're brainwashing themselves and their followers into thinking that this is actually acceptable. It's acceptable to poison people. It's acceptable to sterilize people. It's acceptable to lie to people because it's for the greater good. So what started with Richet continues today." Partial transcription of clip: "One of the historical examples, Katherine \[Watt\] and I ran into, and and this became a huge, epiphany for me is, Charles Richet, who was a French researcher in that in that time. So he worked in early 1900s. In 1913, he was given Nobel Prize for this work, and he is credited with the work on anaphylaxis, although he wasn't the only one, but so he received the Nobel Prize.

And so that that to me, you know, opened so much kind of I was like, of course. You know, why didn't I see this before? But, basically, you know, when you look at this work and then when you look what preceded and what went after, you kind of understand a few things. "First of all, everybody who is, let's say, closely familiar with this history and work cannot think that it's possible to vaccinate. It's impossible to vaccinate for anything. And Richet has demonstrated it conclusively and was given Nobel Prize for it...because he figured out how to poison everyone by sensitizing them to the most commonly occurring things in their environment. It's the most ingenious way of poisoning. "He was a committed eugenicist. And at that time, you know, everyone should realize, eugenicism was a fashionable society attitude. So, you know, all the well-to-do classes were subscribing to it. The web the the good breeding was always, you know, promoted. And, at the time, the sentiment was, well, how can we help the poor being less dirty and less numerous?

Make them less humorous. And AKA kill them. Because this side of them is so offensive to us when we ride our horses through the park and, you know, and then there's like them. What can we do about it? And what can we do about it became into let's figure out how to control their, overbreeding because they tend to reproduce reproduce too much, and they tend to live in crowded conditions, and they tend to have poor hygiene and no sanitation. "So instead of working on those issues, they will decide, oh, let's vaccinate them. And, actually, the same same thing continues with Bill Gates in Africa and India for the same reason. And, you know, but but all of these thoughts extended now to us, to normal people. "Now all of the globalist and this, you know, elites, I don't call them elites, but they all think of us that way. That's, you know, that's what people need to understand. The eugenicists never went away. They all still think that way. They all still think that they should poison us and and limit our reproduction because, you know, we're polluting the earth now. We're causing the climate change. Whatever those ideas are, but they're brainwashing themselves and their followers into thinking that this is actually acceptable. It's acceptable to poison people. It's acceptable to sterilize people. It's acceptable to lie to people, because it's for the greater good. You know? So what what started with Richet continues today."

### eve001 — 2024-10-02T21:16:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-airplane-travel-easier-for-ehs-people.29523/post-340326>

Hello,

Can anyone share how they soften the load of airplane travel?
I feel very affected by nnEMFs w/ often immediate impact on belly/stomach and brain.

I read the Jet Lag RX from 2015. [https://jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/](https://jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/) and will re-read it.
I have travelled alone often in EU by car and plane. But not since I became aware of my EHS.
I'm worried bout my brain and gut/stomach (ulcers).

Anyone experience/ideas on how I could make flights less bad? Or any reference to info that may help me?
I heard of seafood, fasting, coQ1O, methylene blue, but no idea of these hacks are still valid nor how to do them at what dose and if there are counterindications for me. How about shielding covers or so? Blue block glasses? ...

Do all planes (from EU) have wif and bluetooth etc now? Are these RF allowed to be used by the travellers?
Last time I took a plane was 2017 or 2018

I'd love to start exploring places outside EU that I'd may wanna live.
I will probably first try a flight to a nearby place in Europe.

Thx.

### sheas — 2024-10-03T16:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-airplane-travel-easier-for-ehs-people.29523/post-340364>

When I went from TX to Romania the end of May, I roasted in the sun before as much as possible getting as tan as possible. On the flight I took a lot of coQ10. I think the blog said every hour? I didn't take it that much because I tried to sleep but still took quite a bit. Once in Romania we were barely around any nnEMF. I had zero jet lag and felt great the whole time there. Also all the food we had was garden grown or slaughtered too. Coming back did the same, took lots of coQ10 and drank lots of water. Although I did have wine on the plane both ways. Got home and felt totally fine and never was off with sleeping.

In June I had to go teach a class in Santa Cruz. Did the same routine and while in CA I was outside most of the time there (it was a horse thing). But felt terrible, jet lagged, couldn't get on the time zone, my low back which is a big problem for me was so bad there I could barely walk at times (In Romania it did not hurt at all which I thought was interesting). Took weeks to recover when I got back home. August went to Miami just for a night and same thing. Ended up getting sick prob covid since that was the only thing going around. So I came to the conclusion it was the destinations and the bad environments loaded with nnEMF. More so than the actual flight. But I don't know. Going back to Romania in November so will see how this goes. Spending as much time in the sun as possible. But I am in TX so it is warm and lots of sun still. Also have mitigated my home after hiring a building biologist so hoping this will make a difference too.
