I feel you...

Nothing is wrong with you but everything is wrong with the system in place. It’s an attack on Mother Nature and we are a part of her... they want us apart from her. They want to control collective consciousness and make a cheap dollar off of us at the same time. Your body will do what it needs to for survival in the given environment. Going crazy at an airport that is literally microwaving and X-raying you... should be a normal reaction. But they have made us seem like the crazy ones. Everyone just numbly walks through oblivious to what is really going on.

I say burn the bras! Especially Victoria Secret bras... they “secretly” have RFID chips woven in the fabric and the fabrics have toxic forever-type chemicals. Your body is communicating with you but we’ve been trained to ignore the body and listen only to specialists. Those chips and all the wearable electronics now are by design- designed to track, control, and ultimately weaken us.

I’d probably not waste another dime on specialist.

I am almost at seven years now since my rock bottom. First... redox before detox. This is a biggie. Move to a safe environment that works for you before you do any kind of detox. And this will take some dedication and time. I needed a “low tech” home blocked with a forest and low population density. Second... detox. I stopped almost everything over the first few years... prescriptions, supplements, doctors, dentists, X-rays, microwaves, toxic foods, toxic clothes, toxic lotions, perfumes, makeup, shampoos, and toothpaste etc. I live pretty simple with very few ingredients now. I let Mother Nature do her thing with my health. I listen to my body. Third... I try to remain very consistence with her rules. Sunrise, midday and sunset. We haven’t had Wi-Fi in our home in seven years, power off every night, outside connecting with Nature. It’s also extremely important to continue connecting with others. You need to find healthy people to hang out with and create a strong circle of six.

Sending you some...

Thank you so much for responding and for your support. For anyone else who can’t stand chest constriction, I’ve been wearing CAKES nipple covers. It took so long getting here since I thought I was just having a really hard time with perimenopause. ‘Breast tenderness and anxiety?’ Maybe I need iodine or my estrogen level is swinging. ‘Newfound sensory issues?’ Maybe I have undiagnosed autism in addition to ADHD, both of which worsen during perimenopause. It’s hard to say why we are so sensitive to the environment. It can be any number of reasons... targeted individuals or chosen by nature depending on how you want to look at it..as it is just a matter of perspective. I think for myself I was living completely detached from nature and I was breaking all her rules... completely dumbed down by the programming. I have always had very heightened senses and a strong intuition. But I was ignoring these gifts from nature to follow man’s rules...doctors taught me to ignore what my body was telling me. A pill for every ill.

The term "climate canary" is used to refer to a species (called an indicator species) that is affected by an environmental danger prior to other species, thus serving as an early warning system for the other species with regard to the danger.

As Dr. Kruse likes to say if not you then who? It’s up to us what we do as canaries. Die inside the mine or save ourselves in order to help save others.

Hi there, How are you?

While still in my former 5G environment I also started to wonder if I had ADHD. Be careful with applying such labels to oneself, I'd say. Were you able to focus well in the past? nnEMFS too give ADHD-like symptoms and are predictive some say of autism in the children of women surrounded w/ lots of nnEMFs. Dr. Kruse wrote on autism too. Dr. Klinghardt too refers autism and EMFs. A kid in the building I lived in (surrounded by 5G) was diagnosed with ADHD soon after installment of the antenna.

I now do no longer think I have ADHD. I finished many projects in the past, so I was confused as to why I shoudl be ADHD and a coaching program for ADHD people just did not fit me and I used my not satified money back guarantee..
Besides EMFs, lack/loss of focus can also be caused by emotional distress and other stress, by trauma, by being highly gifted and never having had to learn focus/planning.

I used to have tender breasts a lot b4 my period and now away from RF EMFs that has gone. I can't stand chest constriction either! I saw many women who are on low protein/low fat vegetarian diets getting grey hair early and early (peri)menopause. Check when your mom got menopause. It is said that is a good indication.
Kruse also wrote on breast tenderness and iodine in EMF Rx top 10 FAQ.
I have no idea of my Iodine levels. I sprinkle sea algae/weeds on foods several times per week. And try to up my seafood intake.

### [EMF Rx: The Top Ten EMF FAQs](https://jackkruse.com/emf-rx-the-top-ten-emf-faqs/)

Readers Summary What are the top ten FAQ's for EMF? Where are they found in the Quilt? How does paleo help or hurt this? What are the biggest factors in EMF Rx? The EMF Rx is a critical link to these FAQ's Soon the EMF Rx will go live in our March 2013 webinar so in advance of it, I

 jackkruse.com

If the ADHD/autism approach helps you to get better good.
Do not assume though you are innately flawed - ever. It is time for environmental medicine.
On autism: [https://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-8-autism/](https://jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-8-autism/) have not read it myself (yet).

People and Dr. Kruse talk of haplotypes and of heteroplasmy of the mitochondria, related to where your maternal lineage comes from. That impacts how your health and how mitochondria deal w/ which foods, weather, temperatures. It is related to distance between protein complexes in the mitochondria.

Take care

Good morning ☀️ and thank you so much for your reply. I really appreciate your concern, and everything you told me is true. Yesterday I was so distraught. I called a friend and “got out my ugly cry.” I woke up yesterday, thinking, ‘What if I really do have to move in order get better? I’m not rich! I can’t just leave. What about Mom and Dad? What about my job?’ I mean, it’s a blessing to know what’s wrong with you, but at the same time I also had to take a minute to just grieve all the time and money I’ve wasted from not being on the right track. Today I’m feeling more stable. I’ve got a million light bulbs going off inside my head. For starters, no, I do not have “ADHD.” ADHD is just the label centralized medicine uses to frame symptoms I’m experiencing in response to nnEMF’s and light pollution. I just got through telling my niece how in the past five years I’ve cried so many times, telling myself, ‘I haven’t always been this way.’ When I was in grade school I had my “attention problems,” but like most people with “ADHD,” I adopted coping skills and kept pushing. When I began my career after college and had attention issues, I worked longer hours to compensate for my executive dysfunction. When I started perimenopause, my mind and body could no longer carry the heavy lifting required for me to function as a seemingly neurotypical person. Now that I know what’s really going on with me, I’m not ashamed to admit that in the past six years I’ve developed a hoarding issue, just like my grandmother had. Just like Dr. Kruse has stated in podcasts, so many diseases and disorders started out as a novelty, seeming to have developed out of thin air, when the truth is every disease or disorder is the manifestation of mitochondrial dysfunction. I’ve been going hard on Dr. Kruse’s podcasts. I might’ve cried yesterday, but I know I’m a strong person. I’m trying to get myself well. When I succeed, it’s gonna be easier for others to believe that all this ‘light talk’ is true and that I can help them, too. Again, from the bottom of my heart, thank you so much for your reply.

I totally relate to so much of what you wrote here. I haven’t been able to wear bras for 6+ years either. I can’t even wear a snug tank top. I had the EHS symptoms before getting wifi out of my home 6 years ago. I have issues with executive function. I was born with issues though due to a traumatic birth and separation from my birth mother once the cord was cut and not adopted for a month after. Looking back I had signs of autism but no one noticed because I could read from age 3 or 4. I likely got Lyme around age 4 and at 5 moved to a town that had been half destroyed by a tornado so the water was likely toxic. I suspect heavy metals. I think I had some ocd behaviors back then. I used to spell words in my head before I would speak. I was labeled shy. I had “fibromyalgia“ symptoms from childhood and had severe POTS from around age 10 Though nothing was ever diagnosed. I think I would have been diagnosed with PANDAS because of chronic sore throat and swollen glands. They took my tonsils out after 2 years of Penicillin. I was chronically depressed since I was very young Though not depressed anymore. Figuring out the root cause of my health stuff has given me hope instead of depresssion because I always blamed myself for not being better or normal. I have never gotten over the chronic aches and pains, though that is doing better these days. The EHS was very debilitating though before getting rid of Wi-Fi and the tightness in my neck and shoulders got worse over the last 5 years despite that. I’ve been seeing a chiropractor that works on the nervous system and that has helped a lot to relieve the tension in neck and shoulders. Still can’t wear even a stretchy bra though. I wonder what that is all about? I am new here and wasn’t doing sunrise walks until a week ago and my circadian rhythm was way off. Fixing that has been GOLD. Working on cold thermogenesis. its a slow process but I have high hopes! I am not sure about the epi-paleo diet because I have issues digesting fats. But I am trying to make changes in that direction. I tried to clarify the importance of diet or not because I kept hearing Jack say food doesn’t matter but he said he can’t stand people like me when I asked the question. Gotta have a thick skin around here. I am spending as many hours a day as I can reading everything and honestly my brain has trouble when I see conflicting information and sometimes I just need clarification. Some recent tests showing neurotransmitters and Neurological markers show a lot of inflammation in my brain and leaking in the blood brain barrier. Also very active high Epstein Barr markers and Strep that apparently hasn’t gone away since the childhood PANDAS and getting tonsils out. This was long before blue light, EMFs and Wi-Fi were a thing. But maybe I didn’t get enough sunlight. Also maybe emfs were from TV and Radio back then? Not sure. I hope you have better luck than me asking questions. I hate to piss off the good doctor with my questions because I have learned so much in just a week. I just get confused with conflicting info sometimes. Always have. Have been driving people nuts with my questions since I could talk. Lol.

Hello, there. I'm sorry you got nipped by Dr. Kruse, but I hope you'll forgive him. We don't know the half of what he goes through to be a truthteller. The man's a friggin' genius, aeons ahead of our times, and so I'm sure dealing with all our rudimentary questions can be draining for him. At the end of the day, we're both here because the man has all the answers we're looking for. Yes, it sounds like we share a lot of the same experiences. It's amazing how much our lives have been filled with adversity, yet nobody really took notice of it because all we've done all our lives is adapt-adapt-adapt, bearing the weight of "normalcy." I can't believe they gave you penicillin for that length of time. Your gut was trashed from all that, so it's no wonder you were depressed and had all those other illnesses. Did your family home happen to have a swimming pool? I just started reading this book written by a woman with EHS called "Backyard Secrets Exposed." Her chlorinated backyard swimming pool, in concert with a nearby cell tower, was making her sick.

No swimming pool. And I am not upset with the doc, just disappointed at myself for asking too many questions. It’s been a long road and maybe I am a bit too anxious to find the answers. I’ve been looking in the wrong places the last 20 years because apparently I could have been following him and be well by now. I am ready to start my life at 55. I actually have come a very long way from how sick I was in my 20s. And I had so much denial about my sickness for decades because no doctor would recognize it or run any tests. A Chiropractor said I had Fibromyalgia in my late 20s but I was told it was incurable. I tried to fix myself with diet and yoga. Really intense Ashtanga yoga. And acupuncture. I felt pretty good for about a year but then my dad died and I got really depressed and lost the healing path and could never find my way back to almost feeling healed again. That was 25 years ago! For the past 10 years I have suffered various ailments: a sense of air hunger, a sharp chest pain, diffuse neuropathic pain on my left neck and I feel it in my right wrist, my right knee and right ankle. But focuing on light, magnetism and water, things have improved a lot but I'm still not optimal.

I have seen neurosurgery who recommended a fusion of my cervical spine due to evidence of herniations, however repeat MRIs do not show the herniation so I declined the surgery.

This past summer I worked on my solar callus and decreasing blue light exposure and I have felt a difference.

The thing that bothers me the most is this neuropathic pain.

I was hoping Dr. Jack Kruse could help me answer a question (short of moving to El Salvador, which I plan on doing in 12 months, I will be visiting next year):

### Hon Wu — 2025-09-04T05:22:50+0300

At this time a CT head showed a subdural hemorrhage with mass effect upon the left cerebral hemisphere, partial effacement of the left lateral ventricle, and 7mm midline shift. After being stabilized, he was then transferred to a neuro-rehabilitation unit. Upon arrival, he was noted to be A&O x 4. However, on rehabilitation day 14, he was noted to have worsening confusion and new onset focal neurological deficits. You were called as the on-call physician. STAT CT head is shown below. What would you do based on the story and scan?

# Thread: HOW DID POPE PIUS XI and POPE PIUS XII get into the fray?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-pope-pius-xi-and-pope-pius-xii-get-into-the-fray.29564/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-07T16:18:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-pope-pius-xi-and-pope-pius-xii-get-into-the-fray.29564/post-340538>

The Jesuits control most of the politics of the Royal Family. They are powered by the bankers.

The Bankers were powered by Napoleonic Wars gone bad at the Battle of Waterloo, where they gained control of the process.

### France Upsets the Financial Community of the Pope and Royal Family​

Usury has been the cause of most of society’s financial problems for many centuries. In previous posts, I’ve exposed the results of usury since [the Holy Roman Empire](https://worldcyclesinstitute.com/the-beginnings-of-financial-slavery/)[.](https://worldcyclesinstitute.com/the-problem-worldwide-in-one-word-usury/)

You should remember how the French were linked to the loss of America for the Crown. They helped us defeat the British in the Revolutionary War. The French Revolution in 1789 left France in bankruptcy, mostly due to over fifty years of war. This is the main reason why Napoleon sold Jefferson the Louisiana Purchase for 3 million dollars. This opened the door for one of the greatest commanders in history to rise to power.

When Napoleon took over the creation of France’s money, taxes on French citizens were dramatically reduced, and he made the French franc the most valuable currency in Europe. Under Napoleon (emperor 1804-15), in the absence of compounding interest from central bankers beyond France's borders, the country thrived.

Similarly, other countries (one of them Canada) have realized much more vibrant economies as a result of creating their money supply, not paying foreign bankers to do it for them (these foreign bankers then charge governments compounding interest for the privilege). In Canada, from 1938 to 1974 (through the Bank of Canada), the country paid for WWII, the Trans Canada Highway, the Trans Canada Railroad, the St. Lawrence Seaway, and the Canadian medical system by creating their own money interest-free. Since 1974, however, foreign bankers have been contracted to develop Canada’s money instead and charged the country compounded interest. As a result, infrastructure has suffered, the cost of living has risen, and Canadians have been squeezed financially, resulting in the highest household debt in the world. Australia has experienced a similar fate.

Under Napoleon, France upgraded its infrastructure, building over 30,000 kilometers of roads, over 1600 kilometers of canals and bridges, and expanding harbors. This was all financed with interest-free money from the Banque de France.

When Napoleon gained power and changed the financial system in Europe. In fact, France no longer participated in the London financial markets, which upset the Rothschild family, which was now entrenched within the Bank of England. They were the bankers of the Papacy for hundreds of years. How did the financial superpower emerge?

In 1803, England, under the direction of her international bankers (the Rothschilds), provided funds required to Austria, Prussia, Russia, Spain, and Sweden to wage war against Napoleon. Napoleon had refused to sign a trade treaty with England the year before. Europe was “upset.”

Napoleon, lacking the funds to fight this coalition, sold Louisiana to the United States and received $3 million in return. There was peace for a couple of years, but several years of wars followed the allodial title of MONEY.

[The Battle of Waterloo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo) was one of the most important in the history of the modern world and was the catalyst for the unsustainable financial situation we have today. It was the turning point for the Rothschild family and catapulted their fortunes into the stratosphere. This is why the BIS was built in 1930, close to the Battle of Waterloo's victory for the Rothschild Family.

In 1814, Napoleon returned to France and regained control. Without any money, he had no choice but to borrow $5 million from the City of London. The bankers had won.

At the same time, the English led an army put together by [“The Seventh Coalition.”](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hundred_Days) The commander in charge was the Duke of Wellington. The result was that Napoleon was defeated soundly at the Battle of Waterloo.

In the background moved the Rothschilds. [Nathan Rothschild](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nathan_Mayer_Rothschild) had developed a vast network of gold smugglers throughout Europe and had a courier system that was second to none. In London, Nathan was the first to hear of Napoleon’s defeat—a full twenty-four hours before anyone else.

England’s position in the bond market at this time was somewhat precarious, as they’d spent so much money trying to defeat Napoleon. Nathan’s visit to his usual chair in the London stock market the following day was watched by everyone in the room. Dejectedly, he began to sell bonds. It sparked a deluge of selling across the floor. Prices tanked.

A short time later, Nathan reversed his call and bought the dominant holding of England’s entire debt, but at a fraction of its actual value. The trickery became obvious when the news of Wellington’s success hit the streets, but it was too late. The Rothschilds had increased their fortune, and when he sold the bonds two years later, they were up 40%, amounting to a haul of some $600 million in today’s money.

At the time, in Eurasia, only France and Russia were not under the influence of the Rothschild family (the world’s banksters). Most other European countries were, having borrowed from them to fund the war with France.

As a result, Napoleon agreed with [Czar Alexander I of Russia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_I_of_Russia) to enforce a blockade against England. However, that agreement eventually broke down, and Napoleon went to war with Russia in an attempt to force it to enforce the blockade. That was a mistake. Russia retreated, and Napoleon, in chasing them into Russia in winter, lost 75% of his army due to the cold and a lack of food and supplies.

Napoleon was eventually defeated at the [Battle of Nations in 1813](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Leipzig) by the European coalition and bannished to the Island of Elba.

### [Giovanni "Johnny" Cirucci](https://odysee.com/$/embed/@Johnny_Cirucci:4/The-Truth-about-Zionism:9)

Author, investigator, speaker, soldier. Giovanni Augustino "Johnny" Cirucci is the leading source of real truth. This is his back-up channel.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-07T16:23:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-pope-pius-xi-and-pope-pius-xii-get-into-the-fray.29564/post-340539>

After the defeat at Waterloo, Napoleon was exiled to the island of St. Helena, where he was poisoned at the age of 51 in 1821. This was the work of the Royal family bankers, The Rothschilds, although it has never been proven because much of the data from that time were buried by their network of newsmakers. This network became copied by Meyer Lansky when he came to run the shadow bank of Murder Inc. Lansky, owning the media, could control the narratives of his underworld activities.

When Nathan Rothschild died in 1836, his fortune was equivalent to 0.62 percent of the British national income.

> “Between 1818 and 8152, the combined capital of the five Rothschild houses rose from £1.8 million to £9.5 million.” — _The Ascent of Money, Niall Ferguson_

One hundred two years after the Battle of Waterloo, the economy of Russia would become another victim of the greedy gaze of central bankers—through the [Russian Revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_Revolution) in 1917.

Napoleon was just the start. What followed was the export of the Rothschild’s influence to the United States and the Federal Reserve.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-07T16:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-pope-pius-xi-and-pope-pius-xii-get-into-the-fray.29564/post-340540>

The **Russian Revolution** was a period of [political](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Political_revolution_\(Trotskyism\)) and [social](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_revolution) change in [Russia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_Empire), starting in 1917. This period saw Russia [abolish its monarchy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dissolution_of_the_Russian_Empire) and adopt a socialist form of government following two successive revolutions and [a civil war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_Civil_War). It can also be seen as the precursor for [the other](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Revolutions_of_1917%E2%80%931923) revolutions that occurred in the [aftermath of World War I](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aftermath_of_World_War_I), such as the [German Revolution of 1918–1919](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/German_Revolution_of_1918%E2%80%931919). The Russian Revolution was one of the [key events of the 20th century](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Key_events_of_the_20th_century).

The Russian Revolution was inaugurated with the [February Revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/February_Revolution) in early 1917, in the midst of [World War I](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_War_I). With the [German Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/German_Empire) dealing major defeats on the war front, and increasing logistical problems in the rear causing shortages of bread and grain, the Russian Army was steadily losing morale, with large-scale mutiny looming.

Dylan Cooper on Churchill: [https://x.com/ComicDaveSmith/status/1949846037717303655](https://x.com/ComicDaveSmith/status/1949846037717303655)

High officials in the Communist Party were convinced that if [Tsar Nicholas II](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nicholas_II_of_Russia) abdicated, the unrest would subside. Those people were Trotsky and Stalin. The bankers of the Royal Family had a different agenda. They sought to gain control of the Communist Party's money supply, now in power. They hired Trotsky to cause foment in Russia, and what Trotsky did surprised them. Nicholas agreed and stepped down as Stalin and Lenin wanted to calm the nerves in Russia, ushering in a new [provisional government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russian_Provisional_Government) led by the [Russian Duma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_Duma_\(Russian_Empire\)) (the parliament). Trotsky had the entire Tsar family killed to continue his own leftist revolution. This action ultimately led to his death at the hands of Stalin in 1940 in Mexico City. Stalin retold this story to FDR at the Yalta Conference. Yalta was preceded by two key events for Groves.

One was Henry Wallace's speech in 1942, titled "The Common Man," and then the November 1943 conference in Tehran. Yalta was the second of three major wartime conferences among the [Big Three](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grand_Alliance_\(World_War_II\)). of Stalin, FDR, and Churchill. These two events were when Groves realized he had to replace Wallace with someone he could control in a post-war world, where the USA led the world. By late 1943, Groves knew that the scientists Dulles had collected from universities filled with Trotsky-loving academics had to replace the Vice President, because FDR was not going to be the leader of post-war America. That is when he hatched his plan with Senator Jimmy Byrnes and the DNC.

Upon hearing this story, which developed from Tehran to Yalta, that existed between Trotsky and Stalin, FDR informed Stalin that the USA would assist in rebuilding Russia and Europe. FDR had no idea that most of the scientists Dulles recruited to work on the bomb were supporters of the Trotsky movement. Most of them were from Ukraine and were of the same faith. The post-war reconstruction discussed in Tehran and Yalta was the idea that would eventually become the Marshall Plan. Yalta occurred in early February 1945. Groves's new FDR was close to death because his health was rapidly deteriorating.

General Groves became aware of the extent to which the discussion of rebuilding Russia had progressed at Yalta and was furious about the development. It is not clear to me that Groves knew about the conversation between Stalin and FDR about Trotsky's killing. I believe he was aware of it due to his connections in the industry, specifically with partners such as Rockefeller and Ford, as well as his ties to the Rothschilds and the Brown-Harriman bankers of the Bush family. Groves' idea of siloing scientists was brilliant towards his goals of building the Deep State. Since he was running the clandestine Manhattan Project, he was privy to all the high-level discussions at Tehran and Yalta. He also knew by this time that the War was ending in a kinetic sense, and the bomb was superfluous, but he was adamant that his work during the Manhattan Project would not be forgotten as a footnote in history. His actions with Wallace would assure that. Truman was his patsy. Byrnes helped him immensely pull off this plan, and both made sure to convince Truman covertly that Stalin was the real devil and not Trotsky. This was a huge problem for Churchill and the bankers. ### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-08T01:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-pope-pius-xi-and-pope-pius-xii-get-into-the-fray.29564/post-340564>

The Papacy bankers were concerned about Napoleon's views on usury during the French Revolution. The popes said they were against it publicly, but their bankers used it to enslave the Church's followers privately. The French Reformers did not like this duplicity. During the French Revolution, the Church was thrown out of France, and Napoleon imprisoned two popes in a row.

Pius VI condemned the [French Revolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/French_Revolution) and the suppression of the [Catholic Church in France](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_France) that resulted from it. French troops commanded by [Napoleon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Napoleon_Bonaparte) Bonaparte defeated the Papal army and occupied the [Papal States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Papal_States) in 1796. In 1798, upon his refusal to renounce his temporal power, Pius VI was taken prisoner and transported to France. He died eighteen months later in [Valence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valence,_Dr%C3%B4me). His reign of more than twenty-four years is the [fifth-longest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Longest-reigning_popes) in papal history.

The Decree on Usury, enacted during the early reign of Napoleon, had several significant effects on French society and the economy:
1\. Regulation of Interest Rates
Limits on Interest: The decree established legal limits on the maximum interest rates that could be charged, aiming to protect borrowers from exorbitant fees.
2\. Economic Stability
Reduced Exploitation: By curbing predatory lending practices, the decree helped stabilize the financial landscape, particularly for vulnerable populations who were often exploited by moneylenders.
3\. Increased Access to Credit
Encouraged Lending: With regulated interest rates, legitimate lenders could operate without fear of being undercut by usurious practices, potentially increasing access to credit for businesses and individuals.
4\. Social Impact
Protection for Borrowers: The decree provided protections for lower-income individuals, reducing the risk of falling into debt traps, which could lead to broader social unrest.
5\. Legal Framework
Judicial Enforcement: The establishment of legal norms around lending practices created a framework for resolving disputes related to loans and interest rates, enhancing the rule of law in financial transactions.
6\. Impact on Moneylenders
Shift in Business Practices: Many traditional moneylenders had to adapt their practices to comply with the decree, which could have led to a decline in informal lending networks.
7\. Long-term Financial Reforms
Foundation for Future Laws: The decree set a precedent for future financial regulations in France, influencing later laws regarding banking and credit.

The Decree on Usury had a multifaceted impact, promoting economic stability, protecting borrowers, and laying the groundwork for a more regulated financial system in France. This angered the bankers of the Vatican and Royal Families all over Europe at this time. After Pope Pius the VI died Pope Pius the VII was selected during the turn of the century. He ruled for a decade but ran into problems quickly with Napolean in France over banking regulations and land grabs.

On the night of 5 July 1809, French forces kidnapped Barnabà Chiaramonti, Pope Pius VII, from his private apartments in the Quirinal Palace in Rome. Napoleon arrested the pope for a reason. Do you know what that reason was? It was linked to the ALLODIAL title. The French army seized Pius VII and eventually took him to France as a prisoner for five years. Almost as soon as Pius returned in triumph to Rome in the spring of 1814, he acted to restore the Jesuits throughout the world.

### WRC777 — 2024-10-07T19:34:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-iols.29566/post-340547>

Hi everyone,

I’m 55 and had cataract surgery 2 years ago. I opted for the blue blocking IOLs to protect my eyes against further damage from screens, florescent lights, etc. Now I am thinking I’ve made a huge mistake. Is there anyway to get light into my eyes? Use red lights? Or maybe more light on my skin? Any suggestions would be welcome. Thanks so much!

### Rocky — 2024-12-04T05:10:27+0200

When there is no vision in my left eye due to cataract I'm considering having the lenses removed and not replacing with IOL, then use glasses to correct vision. The question is, will I will be able to correct enough to drive a car etc.

### Marshall_Sponder — 2024-12-04T07:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-iols.29566/post-343270>

> [Rocky said:](/goto/post?id=343267)
>
> When there is no vision in my left eye due to cataract I'm considering having the lenses removed and not replacing with IOL, then use glasses to correct vision. The question is, will I will be able to correct enough to drive a car etc.

Adding my own 2 cents here.

I have faced significant issues with both of my eyes in the past, including a detachment and subsequent cataract surgery on my left eye, as well as cataract surgery on my right eye. If I had known about the possibility of not replacing cataracts with an artificial lens, I might have considered other options. In fact, I did explore alternatives for my right eye but ultimately decided to proceed with the surgery.

Some of these issues may have been caused by the LASIK surgery I had on both eyes 24 years ago, which also led to serious complications—at the time and for a few years afterward. Even though I experienced a lot of ups and downs initially, including the use of steroids, I don't regret the LASIK procedure. I still enjoy fairly good vision today and feel that I gained more from it than what it cost me.

However, when you can't see clearly because your lens is cloudy, it doesn’t really matter why it became cloudy. Once the lens is clouded, restoring its clarity is nearly impossible. Even if it were feasible, the extensive effort required and the limited outcomes just aren’t worth it. Eventually, it becomes a matter of quality of life.

Regarding the loss of wavelengths entering the eye due to the new lens blocking certain types of light, we also absorb those wavelengths in the skin of other parts of our body.

Life isn't perfect, and there is no perfect solution. For instance, I didn't receive the COVID-19 vaccines, so I avoided that issue. However, I wasn't aware of the implications of cataract surgery or the wavelengths that would be missed before I learned about Jack. Still, we find ways to compensate for those losses in other ways.

I'm not sure if there's anyone in New York, where there are many optometrists, who would perform an IOL today. In a situation like this, if I can't see well, my quality of life suffers significantly. That likely matters more to me than the specific wavelengths in my eye. It's frustrating to have difficulty seeing clearly, as it leads to issues like tripping over things and managing everyday tasks.

New blog out on LASIK: [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-62-130349379](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-62-130349379)

Why is putting an excimer laser in the hands of a refractive eye surgeon like giving a serial killer a universal hotel room key?

Over 3.8 billion years, sunrise’s role in TCA/urea activation evolved with light, temperature and oxygen variations to optimize energy and repair, with thanatotranscriptomic genes adapting to these variables and these biological cycles. LASIK demoloshes this stiochiometry in nanoseconds.

Daytime TCA activity stores fat reserves, reducing sleep’s metabolic burden and enhancing repair (e.g., mitophagy).
Circadian Sync: Sunrise aligns melatonin suppression with TCA/urea cycle activation, ensuring deep sleep occurs at the end of a day that kills you slowly. It works this way so your colonies of mitochondria can recover every night to give you more time on Earth. If this fails you can die rapidly in ways you cannot fathom.

This blog tells you how the thanatotranscriptomic genes evolved and what their stiochiometry is. These genes were critical in evolving wakefulness from sleep. Enhancing AM light exposure (e.g., UV-mimicking therapies) amplifies TCA/urea benefits, complementing DDW production and ideal oxygen utilization to create precision in UPE output to extend life day after day. Temperature control is critically important to this thanatotranscriptomic method. This evidence is still seen in melatonin action today. It is maximally active in the brain after a 3-4 degree drop in temperature of the CSF. LASIK destroys this relationship.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-05T17:29:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-iols.29566/post-357610>

What Decentralized Medicine Insights on CATARACT formation are included in this new masterpiece blog for Savage consumption?

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-65-124070324](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-65-124070324)

Changes in light input via the lens predict epigenetic programming. Blocked sunlight disrupts histone modifications and DNA methylation, contributing to modern diseases MAHA could never solve because of their myopic focus on food.

The creeping blindness of cataract formation reflects a broader metaphor in modern life: modern comforts (e.g., technology abuse, centralized medicine) suppress the discomfort of poor thinking; poor thinking and poor outcomes should lead to fostering wisdom and identity. Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. This process opacifies the lens, causing cataracts.

Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

_Nanophthalmos_

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-08T17:53:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all-from-australia.29568/post-340617>

Microphthalmia is a rare ophthalmic disease and a severe form of diencephalic differentiation characterized by a small eye with a short axial length, severe hyperopia, an elevated lens/eye ratio, and a high incidence of angle-closure glaucoma. There are syndromic and nonsyndromic types of this disease. Syndromic is usually genetic and not epigenetic. The nonsyndromic is mtDNA and melanin-related.

Most non-syndromic cases of nanophthalmos are sporadic related to mom and dada germline problems. Li et al. 2008 excluded the three known eye development genes that map inside the NNO3 interval (Gli2, Inhibin beta B, and En-1 genes). Thus, a novel gene for that locus has yet to be identified. Interestingly, chromosome 2 appears to be the reason this disease happens. Since POMC was relocated to chromosome two, I think this points to a melanin problem and not a chromosomal gene as its key link. Centralized ophthalmology has its head up its ass about that.

Mutations in the _MFRP_ gene have been identified as a potential cause of classic non-syndromic recessive nanophthalmos in this group of individuals with autosomal recessive lineage. Functionally, MFRP seems to be essential for the eye to reach its full size at birth and also for correct emmetropization since it is associated with the regulation of ocular axial growth. Again, it is another melanin migration story to bad light in the germ line. I have a sense the melanin defect causes heteroplasmy in this tissue in the fetus, and voila, you get this disease.

Why is this a melanin embryology disease due to poor germline biophoton creation?

The MFRP protein is selectively expressed in the retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) and the ciliary body, with low expression in the brain substance itself. Therefore, it does not affect the rest of the diencephalon derivative, so it concentrates its effect in the eye. This makes it rare. MFRP concentrates towards the apical side of the RPE cells in the fetal eye, with virtually none in Bruch’s membrane.

Because of these unique morphological facts, patients who completely lack MFRP protein have no identifiable pathology outside the eye. In the fetal eye, at seven weeks gestation, no MFRP signal is detected in the RPE, based on papers I read long ago. A distinct signal is observed in the RPE at 20 weeks gestation, which indicates that MFRP expression begins relatively late during eye development.

Yamani _et al._ (1999) showed that all three scleral layers in eyes with nanophthalmos had abnormal collagen fibrils that were frayed and split. The degree of abnormality increased inwards, with less abnormal episclera and stroma and considerable changes in lamina fusca. Ultrastructural studies also demonstrated a more extensive range of collagen fibril diameters with normal fibril banding periodicities in eyes with nanophthalmos. The authors suggest that the frayed fibrils contribute to scleral inelasticity, which causes the sequestration of extracellular fluid and, consequently, choroidal congestion, choroidal detachment, and/or exudative retinal detachment.

Yamani A, Sugino I, Wanner, M. Abnormal collagen fibrils in nanophthalmos: a clinical and histologic study. _Am J Ophthalmol_. 1999; (973), 106–108.

That is the extent of my knowledge on this. I researched it long ago when I was looking at neural crest migration in humans and the eye. I've never seen a case of it myself.

I am not a fan of this for your disease.

My decentralized model predicts that nanophthalmos arise from nnEMF and blue light, which is likely in the germline of your Mom. It likely disrupted bioelectric gradients and is associated with mtDNA mutations in DDW productions, causing melanin dehydration in the affected eye, hypothalamic dysregulation, and mitochondrial function during eye development. This leads to arrested growth (small axial length, cornea, and anterior chamber), thickened sclera/choroid, and a predisposition to angle-closure glaucoma, driven by environmental stressors rather than genetics alone, as centralized medicine believes. This disease has a lot of features in common with my glaucoma and cataract models of disease. My decentralized perspective offers a new lens for understanding congenital eye disorders and suggests preventive strategies like minimizing nnEMF/ALAN exposure during pregnancy. The fact that you have experienced some positive changes since you're now using more sunlight lends credence to my work that there is no way this is just an nDNA disease.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-11T02:16:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-340811>

My new video today makes a case that most won't see.

**Silk Road 1.0** ---> [https://www.bitchute.com/video/iiyeHsOVAYbv/](https://www.bitchute.com/video/iiyeHsOVAYbv/)

**Silk Road 2.0-**\---> Charlie Shrem, co-founder and CEO of BitInstant and Vice Chairman of the Bitcoin Foundation, in 2013 (photo credit: Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, Cshrem)
The top executive of a New York City-based Bitcoin company and a Florida Bitcoin exchanger have been charged with conspiring to commit money laundering by selling more than $1 million in Bitcoins to users of the black market website Silk Road, authorities said Monday.

Charlie Shrem, 24, the chief executive officer of BitInstant and vice chairman of a foundation that promotes the Bitcoin currency system, was arrested Sunday at New York’s Kennedy Airport, and Robert Faiella was arrested Monday at his Cape Coral, Florida, residence, prosecutors said.

Faiella and Shrem conspired to sell more than $1 million in Bitcoins to criminals who wanted to sell narcotics on the Silk Road between December 2011 and October, US Attorney Preet Bharara said in a release.

“**Truly innovative business models don’t need to resort to old-fashioned law-breaking,** and when Bitcoins, like any traditional currency, are laundered and used to fuel criminal activity, law enforcement has no choice but to act,” Bharara said.

Shrem is Jewish and speaks English, Hebrew, and Arabic. He attended the Yeshivah of Flatbush Joel Braverman High School and graduated from Brooklyn College in 2011 with a Bachelor of Science in Economics and Finance.

Get The Times of Israel's Daily Edition by email and never miss our top stories.

**Silk Road 3.0** ---> Lansky's connections to Ross Ulbricht. Silk Road ran as an onion service on the Tor network, which implements data encryption and routes traffic through intermediary servers to anonymize the source and destination Internet Protocol addresses. By hosting his market as a Tor site, Ulbricht could conceal the server's IP address and, thus, its location. If you listened to the new video I did, this should raise an eyebrow.

More eyebrows? Look at his charges and Lansky's. On February 4, 2014, Ulbricht was charged with engaging in a continuing criminal enterprise, narcotics conspiracy, conspiracy to commit money laundering, and conspiracy to commit computer hacking. Federal prosecutors alleged that Ulbricht had paid $730,000 in murder-for-hire deals targeting at least five people, allegedly because they threatened to reveal the Silk Road enterprise. It's ironic but oddly similar to Meyer Lansky's MO.

Citibank started to operate its business activities in 1812 in New York under its original name, First National City Bank. Do you remember what I said happened in 1812? Citibank was also known formerly as the City Bank of New York. It was chartered by the [State of New York](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_of_New_York) on June 16, 1812, with $2 million (~$45 million in 2024) of capital. The bank merged with First National Bank of New York in 1955, becoming the First National City Bank of New York in 1955. The "New York" was dropped in 1962 on the 150th anniversary of the company's foundation. The company organically entered the leasing and credit card sectors, and its introduction of U.S. dollar-denominated certificates of deposit in [London](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/London), where the Rothschild controlled bank of England runs the game for the Royal Family marked the first new negotiable instrument in the market since 1888. The bank introduced its First National City Charge Service credit card—popularly known as the "[Everything card](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Everything_card)" and later to become [MasterCard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MasterCard)—in 1967 during LBJ's reign of terror that began with acute led toxicity in Dealey Plaza. Also in 1967, First National City Bank was reorganized as a one-bank holding company, First National City Corporation, or "Citicorp" for short. The bank had been nicknamed "Citibank" since the 1860s when it began using this as an eight-letter wire code address.

In 1974, under the leadership of CEO [Walter B. Wriston](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walter_B._Wriston), First National City Corporation changed its formal name to "Citicorp", with First National City Bank being formally renamed Citibank in 1976. Shortly afterward, the bank launched the Citicard, which gave depositors the use of 24-hour [ATMs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Automated_teller_machine) so they could charge you to take out YOUR OWN money. [John S. Reed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_S._Reed) was elected CEO in 1984, and Citi became a founding member of the [CHAPS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CHAPS) clearing house in London where the Royal Family and Papacy bankers control the monetary system. Under his leadership, the next 14 years would see Citibank become the largest bank in the United States and the largest issuer of credit cards and charge cards in the world, and expand its global reach to over 90 countries. ### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-11T20:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-340845>

There were several "loopholes" that regulators and financial firms that Weil and his partners used to exploit during the lifetime of Glass–Steagall restrictions. This is how we got the S&L crisis in the 1980s. Aside from the Section 21 prohibition on securities firms taking deposits, neither savings and loans nor state-chartered banks that did not belong to the Federal Reserve System were restricted by Glass–Steagall. Glass–Steagall also did not prevent securities firms from owning such institutions. [S&Ls](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Savings_and_loan_association) and securities firms took advantage of these loopholes starting in the 1960s to create products and affiliated companies that chipped away at commercial banks' deposit and lending businesses.

In 1913, the [Federal Reserve Act](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Act) established a [central bank](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_bank), the [Federal Reserve](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Reserve), to issue American currency. The OCC's role shifted to bank examination and regulation, though it retained "currency" as part of its name. In the 1960s, after the Industrial military complex killed JFK and LBJ was running the game for the Joint Chiefs, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency issued aggressive interpretations of Glass–Steagall to permit national banks to engage in certain securities activities. In other words, they allowed Sandy Weill and their partners to break the law with no consequences. Kind of like today with Harris and Mayorkas running the border and FEMA to suit their criminal friends.

Once Nixon and Ford were cleared as JFK conspirators, most of these interpretations were overturned by federal court decisions by JFK guys; by the late 1970s, when clueless Carter was in the White House, bank regulators began issuing Glass–Steagall interpretations that were upheld by courts and permitted banks and their affiliates to engage in an increasing variety of securities activities. Starting in the 1960s, banks and non-banks developed financial products that blurred the distinction between banking and securities products as they increasingly competed with each other.

After the GREAT [financial crisis of 2007–2008](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Financial_crisis_of_2007%E2%80%932008), some reasonable people argued that the repeal of Sections 20 and 32 had played an important role in leading to the housing bubble and financial crisis. [Economics Nobel Memorial](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobel_Memorial_Prize_in_Economic_Sciences) laureate [Joseph Stiglitz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Stiglitz), for instance, argued that "when repeal of Glass-Steagall Act brought investment and commercial banks together, the investment-bank culture came out on top", and banks which had previously been managed conservatively turned to riskier investments to increase their returns. It is always about cheap fiat money.

[S&Ls](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Savings_and_loan_association) and securities firms took advantage of these loopholes starting in the 1960s to create products and affiliated companies that chipped away at commercial banks' deposit and lending businesses.

In 1913, the [Federal Reserve Act](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Reserve_Act) established a [central bank](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_bank), the [Federal Reserve](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Reserve), to issue American currency. The OCC's role shifted to bank examination and regulation, though it retained "currency" as part of its name. In the 1960s, after the Industrial military complex killed JFK and LBJ was running the game for the Joint Chiefs, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency issued aggressive interpretations of Glass–Steagall to permit national banks to engage in certain securities activities. In other words, they allowed Sandy Weill and their partners to break the law with no consequences. Kind of like today with Harris and Mayorkas running the border and FEMA to suit their criminal friends.

Once Nixon and Ford were cleared as JFK conspirators, most of these interpretations were overturned by federal court decisions by JFK guys; by the late 1970s, when clueless Carter was in the White House, bank regulators began issuing Glass–Steagall interpretations that were upheld by courts and permitted banks and their affiliates to engage in an increasing variety of securities activities. Starting in the 1960s, banks and non-banks developed financial products that blurred the distinction between banking and securities products as they increasingly competed with each other.

After the GREAT [financial crisis of 2007–2008](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Financial_crisis_of_2007%E2%80%932008), some reasonable people argued that the repeal of Sections 20 and 32 had played an important role in leading to the housing bubble and financial crisis. [Economics Nobel Memorial](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobel_Memorial_Prize_in_Economic_Sciences) laureate [Joseph Stiglitz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joseph_Stiglitz), for instance, argued that "when repeal of Glass-Steagall Act brought investment and commercial banks together, the investment-bank culture came out on top", and banks which had previously been managed conservatively turned to riskier investments to increase their returns. It is always about cheap fiat money.

People say they go the extra mile, but almost no one actually does. Most people think, "Wait, no one else is here. Why am I doing this?"

The Black Swan is rare because their mindset is unique.

Be early. Stay late. Make the extra phone call. Send the extra email. Do the extra research. Know the history help human thrive.

Help a person solve a difficult problem. Help a customer unload or unpack a shipment. Don't wait to be asked for help; offer. Every time you do something, think of one extra thing you can do--especially if other people aren't doing that one thing. In this unpopulated wasteland, many will ask you why you persist. Tell them your using nature's light to sculpt your life by thinking better than you did before.

Sure, it's hard, to think anew and adapt. Being solo in the modern world is not something to fear........it is something to conquer. I been doing it for 20 years.

ANSWER: Nature had the foresight to make decisions in hindsight using sunlight. Why? Because light has more non linear properties than anything in the cosmos. The government found out how to make LIGHT A BIOWEAPON in the 1950s. It should be both fascinating and humbling to scientists that the massless, timeless, and phenomenal manifestation of light are the irrefutable requirement for all physical structure on Earth and in the cosmos. It turns out these abilities are why ME is mis-understood. The only constant in a universe of relentless flux because of how light works in us.

The problem with these "discoveries" is that they become systematized and programmed by paradigms. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to the sun and fully connected to Earth to work properly in nature to remain well.

Fibromyalgia is a loss of electrons in the muscles and collagen fascia of the entire body that dehydrates us (mitochondria) and lowers our piezoelectric current. This lowers the dielectric constant in cell water and it is become magnified when things like blue light and nnEMF dehydrate our cells. It usually starts in one cell type but it can spread fast if your base line redox is low. It also will alter proton recycling in the mitochondrial matrix. So if you are "young healthy 20 something" who works out in a blue lit gym, or a blue lit Harvard lab, you by definition, have a lowered redox potential because you DESTROYED POMC signaling via alpha-MSH. You also live at a high latitude and in a city with high population density when you were struck down. You likely have no idea how those two things dehydrate cells to set the stage for ME/FM/CFS. Dehydration of cells due to mitochondrial damage is the key initial starting point for ME. It can lead to a local loss in physiologic control over certain muscles too, just by a lack of red light from the skin above the muscles. The goal is not to avoid foods, molds, or toxins, with this issue, but to fix the structure of the tissues and gut by replacing the electrons lost to build the capacitor in cell water back. This turns your energy engine, the mitochondria, back on to protect your cells once again. Here is how I figured it all out.

There is a 1 trillion dollar hole in commercial real estate, and all the criminals in banking have told Warren Buffet and Ray Dalio what is coming. They have been selling their stocks in banks like mad. This is why we have a silver squeeze now.

The money center banks are in deep trouble. These details are public: Berkshire (BRK) sold 260 million shares of [$BAC](https://x.com/search?q=%24BAC&src=cashtag_click) at $41 for proceeds of $10.6B. But Berkshire still owns more than $30 billion worth of [$BAC](https://x.com/search?q=%24BAC&src=cashtag_click). But probably not for long: here's what's not public yet. Berkshire has also sold all of its commercial banks, except Citi, since early 2020. Sold 100% of its 346 million shares in [$WFC](https://x.com/search?q=%24WFC&src=cashtag_click); Sold 100% of its 150 million shares in [$USB](https://x.com/search?q=%24USB&src=cashtag_click); Sold 100% of its 60 million shares in [$JPM](https://x.com/search?q=%24JPM&src=cashtag_click); Sold 100% of its 12 million shares in [$GS](https://x.com/search?q=%24GS&src=cashtag_click).

Buffett isn't the only well-connected asset manager to dump every American bank. ### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-19T15:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-341232>

The reason: there's a $1 trillion+ hole in the commercial banks' balance sheets. No one currently at the Fed Reserve is willing to talk about these issues, but here's what Thomas Hoenig, former vice chairman of the Rothschild/Rockefeller Federal Reserve, said: "Gentleman, I only want to know one question, how long before you fail? Not how complicated you can make the formula to confuse me and certainly confuse the public."

What Hoenig is warning us about is that current regulations do not match economic reality.

"Risk-weighted capital flat-out misleads; the only thing a real bank investor wants to know is how much real equity capital is there. In layman's terms, where is all the missing money? That tells the investor how prepared the bank is to absorb a shock, no matter where it comes from on the balance sheet."

Buffett is a “real” bank investor. So is Dalio. But, as you can tell by [$BAC](https://x.com/search?q=%24BAC&src=cashtag_click)'s share price, which is trading at near all-time high multiples, most of the public is not. NOBODY in the mainstream media has bothered to tell the public anything about the genuinely enormous scope of these problems. I wonder why...

How bad is it? Quoting from the St. Louis Fed's Bank Capital Analysis report of June 30, 2022: Since 2019, banks increased securities holdings by $2.0 trillion, increasing the share of securities as a percentage of total assets to 33.7% in the second quarter of 2022 from 17.8%. Let me make sure you understand what that means. This means that one-third of the reserves of our biggest banks are deeply “underwater.” That's because they bought $2 trillion worth of long-term bonds (and mortgages) at interest rates of around 1%.

The real market value of these assets has plummeted because of rising interest rates. It was soaring losses on these assets, which led to the run on deposits in the spring of 2023 at Silicon Valley Bank, Signature Bank, and First Republic Bank.

These banks didn't fail because they made bad loans. They failed because they owned long-dated Treasury bonds. Total losses on those bank failures were $40 billion. How in the hell did this happen? Mr. Hoenig explained: "It's a political process. It's not a market process. The market no longer determines capital in the financial, especially in the banking industry. It's now criminal politicians, lobbyists, and the regulators who have to battle it out among themselves."

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-19T15:28:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-341233>

"Therefore, you get these non-market solutions like risk-weighted capital. And banks are incentivized to increasingly leverage their balance sheets. And, thanks to the ‘financial repression' of the COVID era, when the Fed's bond buying binges took long-term rates to below 1%, there was an enormous amount of interest rate risk in the U.S. bond market back in the summer of 2020." Money printing went nuts.

To stem the run on the banking system, which threatened to spread and could have easily de-stabilized the entire system, the Federal Reserve created a new lending program, The Bank Term Funding Program, which offered loans of up to one year, against the banks bond holdings.

Key to the program: the loans were made against the face value of the collateral, not the market value. This made sure that the banks could meet redemption demands from depositors. Loans under that program soared throughout 2023 and peaked in the spring of 2024, at over $160 billion. With the Feds handing out money, none of the big banks bothered to raise new equity to plug the holes in their balance sheets.

This was a "kick the can down the road" solution. Fingers crossed that interest rates will go back down and the banks balance sheets will be saved. But that didn't happen. In fact, when the Fed began to cut interest rates, long-term rates went higher! My take is they are continuing the slow destruction of money to create a financial emergency to get everyone to accept a CBDC.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-19T15:41:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-341234>

And then there's this: in March of this year, the Fed announced that no more loans would be made under the program. As these 1-year loans expire, they must be repaid. The outstanding balances on the program are plummeting, down to $66 billion now. And interest rates keep rising, pushing the value of those 2020 bonds down. Of all the banks that took on interest rate risk in the summer of 2020, nobody took more risk than Bank of America, which purchased more than $500 billion (!) of long-dated bonds in 2020. Today, Bank of America reports it has $86 billion in unrecognized “mark to market” losses on that bond portfolio. The bank has tangible equity (that is, real equity) of $200 billion. If rates go above 5%, I believe Bank of America's tangible equity would be wiped out. So if you have money there, you better pull it out and go somewhere else with it.

It' not just [$BAC](https://x.com/search?q=%24BAC&src=cashtag_click) either. The end of the Fed's BTFP will lead several major banks to raise more capital. But, if interest rates continue to rise, the bank runs we saw in the spring of 2023 will return – with Bank of America most at risk.

With $2 trillion in deposits, Bank of America's shareholders would most likely not survive a run on its deposits. Right now, the stock is trading at 15x earnings, which is at the top of its valuation range. And Buffett is dumping his share in a large way. The people who know the criminals at the Fed are being told the truth. Watch their behavior. This is going all those index funds that the public holds in pensions/IRA/SEPS go boom.

It is a guarantee that much higher interest rates are a virtual certainty now because of soaring Treasury issuance is ongoing because of this trillion dollar hole.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-21T06:55:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-341319>

I moved my life in 2018 in so many ways, but the thing that affected me most was this "spidey sense" I had that the replacement of the US Constitution by the UN Charter was close to fruition because of a SCOTUS case about the First Amendment. On June 27, 2018, the Court ruled in a 5–4 decision that the application of public sector union fees to non-members is a violation of the First Amendment, ruling against AFSCME. Justice Alito wrote for the Court, joined by Justices Roberts, Kennedy, Thomas, and Gorsuch.

Interestingly, it was not the majority opinion that caught my attention. It was the dissenting opinions that did. What Kagen wrote in her dissent told me what I should expect from the future of America. Today, six years later, I think my intuition was spot on.

In ancient Roman religion and myth, Janus is the god of beginnings, transitions, and endings. He is often depicted as having two faces, one looking to the future and one to the past. The Supreme Court’s Janus v AFSCME case from the last term of 2018 is fittingly named.

It was stunning that it disregarded the principles of stare decisis because Janus overruled the forty-year-old precedent in Abood v Detroit Board of Education. On May 23, 1977, the Supreme Court of the United States issued its ruling in Abood v. Detroit Board of Education in a 9-0 vote. It held that non-union public employees could be required to pay labor union fees for collective bargaining, contract administration, and grievance adjustment purposes as a condition of employment.

The Janus decision of 2017 marked the end of the post–New Deal compromise concerning public sector unions and the First Amendment.

This 5-4 2018 decision looks to the future because Janus lays the groundwork for further attacks on labor rights and a broader erosion of civil society and democracy at the expense of corporate power. This helps the industrial, military machine as it forms a global socialist government in Washington, DC.

In that way, Janus represented an unequivocal transition to what Justice Kagan termed a “weaponized” view of the First Amendment among the Court’s majority—indeed, far more so than her dissent elaborates. Today, we see this idea buried in the actions of the media and the ruling elite.

I think this case allows the future SCOTUS to be two-faced in its future actions with respect to the First Amendment.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-22T19:44:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-341394>

Dr. Kurt Plötner participated in a series of research tasks involving human experimentation at the Dachau concentration camp during the Second World War II. These included participation in the malaria experiments of Claus Schilling, in which prisoners were injected with drugs at lethal doses.
In 1944, he was given Dachau physician Sigmund Rascher's role as head of the "Department R" of the Ahnenerbe project for carrying out experimental work on living subjects. Plötner also administered the hallucinogen mescaline to Jews and Russian prisoners, watching them display schizophrenic and mental behavioral changes, as part of the Nazi search for a truth serum that could be employed as an aid in interrogations. It was also used initially for a mond control drug. It failed on both accounts. This information was captured by Lansky and given to Groves, MOSSAD, & Boris Pash. It is believed that Lansky was the famous NYC gangster that the CIA has admitted to below who actually participated early in MKUTLRA.

Plötner's work in the concentration camps came to the attention of Boris Pash, an American intelligence officer who would go on to work in the CIA at the time of Operation BLUEBIRD/Artichoke in the 1940s. This document below was formerly classified document from 1952 on the CIA’s Project BLUEBIRD, an offshoot of [Project MKULTRA](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_MKULTRA) which focused on hypnosis and behavior modification as a means of preventing Agency employees from providing intelligence to adversaries, was obtained from the [CIA Freedom of Information Act Reading Room](http://www.foia.cia.gov/).

### [CIA Special Research Project Bluebird 1952 | Public Intelligence](https://publicintelligence.net/cia-bluebird/)

 publicintelligence.net

Instead of treating him like a War Criminal, the United States Navy's intelligence officers recruited him in 1945, permitting him to continue his interrogation research for the new MKULTRA program in the USA. Plötner proceeded to live under the name of "Schmitt" in Schleswig-Holstein into the early 1950s.

Despite Plötner's actual residence in this western German zone, when the French government sought to have Plötner prosecuted in 1946 and appealed to the United States for assistance, the Americans replied that he could not be located, and was probably being shielded by the Soviet Union. He subsequently was able to quietly resume his real identity in 1952, at which time he was hired by the University of Freiburg in West Germany. He became an associate professor in 1954.

Plötner died on February 26, 1984.

[https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP81-00261R000300050005-3.pdf](https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP81-00261R000300050005-3.pdf)

Although the CIA insists that MK-ULTRA-type experiments have been abandoned, 14-year CIA veteran Victor Marchetti has stated in various interviews that the CIA routinely conducts disinformation campaigns and that CIA mind control research continued. In a 1977 interview, Marchetti specifically called the CIA's claim that MK-ULTRA was abandoned a "cover story" perpetuated by the Rockefeller Commission. On the Senate floor in 1977, Senator Ted Kennedy said: The Deputy Director of the CIA revealed that over thirty universities and institutions were involved in an "extensive testing and experimentation" program which included covert drug tests on unwitting citizens "at all social levels, high and low, native Americans and foreign."

Several of these tests involved the administration of LSD to "unwitting subjects in social situations." At least one death, that of Dr. Frank Olson, resulted from these activities. ### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-31T22:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-341819>

**Great speech by Bret Weinstein PhD:** "I believe we must zoom out if we are to understand the pattern that we are gathered here to explore, because the pattern is larger than federal health agencies and the COVID cartel. If we do zoom out and ask, what are they hiding? The answer becomes as obvious as it is disturbing. They are hiding everything. It will be jarring for many to hear a scientist speak with such certainty. It should be jarring. We are trained to present ideas with caution as hypotheses in need of a test. But in this case, I have tested the idea, and I am as certain of it as I am of anything. We are being systematically blinded. It is the only explanation I have encountered that will not only describe the present, but also, in my experience, predicts the future with all but perfect accuracy.

The pattern is a simple one. You can see it clearly and test it yourself.

Every single institution dedicated to public truthseeking is under simultaneous attack. They are all in a state of collapse. Every body of experts fails utterly. Individual experts who resist or worse, in an attempt to return their institutions to sanity, they find themselves coerced into submission. If they won't buckle, they are marginalized or forced out. Those outside of the institutions who either seek truth alone or who build new institutions with a truth-seeking mission face merciless attacks on both their integrity and expertise, often by the very institutions whose mission they refuse to abandon.

There is a saying in military circles, once is a mistake, twice is a coincidence, three times is enemy action.

I have no doubt that given an hour, the people on this panel could point to a hundred examples of the pattern I have just described, while finding even a handful of exceptions would pose a significant challenge. We are left in a fool's paradise. Our research universities spend huge sums of public money to reach preordained conclusions. Professors teach only lessons that are consistent with wisdom students have picked up on TikTok, even when those lessons contradict the foundational principles of their disciplines.

Once proud newspapers like the New York Times and Washington Post only report important stories after they have become common knowledge. Morticians must now raise the alarm over patterns missed by medical examiners. The CDC has become an excellent guide to protecting your health, but only for people who realize you should do the opposite of whatever it advises.

The courts, the last holdout in this ongoing inversion of reality, are now regularly used as a coercive weapon of elites against those who threaten them. We have literally witnessed the Department of Homeland Security attempt to set up a truth ministry and declare accurate critique of government as a kind of terrorism. To my fellow patriots in the West, the pattern is unmistakable. I cannot tell you with any certainty who they are or what they hope to accomplish, but I can tell you that we are being systematically denied the tools of enlightenment and the rights guaranteed in our Constitution. We, those who remain dedicated to the values of the West, must fight this battle courageously and we must win. For if we do not stem the tide, the result will be a dark age that differs from prior dark ages only in the power and sophistication of the coercive instruments wielded by those who will rule us."

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-12T16:44:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-342252>

The Trading with the Enemy Act of 1917 is still in force. This Act established the Office of Alien Property Custodian to manage the property of US enemies (Germany in WW1), such as patents filed in the US by German nationals. The US government office was intended to manage that intellectual property in the US interest during the WW1.

The Emergency Banking Act of 1933 was a bill passed in the midst of the Great Depression that took steps to stabilize and restore confidence in the U.S. banking system. It came in the wake of a series of bank runs following the stock market crash of 1929. Among its major KEY measures, the Act created the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), which began insuring bank accounts at no cost for up to $2,500.

THIS ONE IS REALLY KEY: HOW ONE ENDS THE FED IS BURIED IN THIS LAW: The president was given executive power to operate independently of the Federal Reserve during times of financial crisis. I have a deep sense this is a honey pot for Trump, Lutnick, and Fink in the next few years.
