### Finding K Factor — 2024-11-30T19:30:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clear-it-up-follow-up-to-the-danny-jones-podcast-%E2%80%93-dr-jack-kruse%E2%80%99s-webinar.29587/post-343092>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=341083)
>
> Now, the DoD has made it "legal" to shoot protesters if you disagree with your government. Are you paying attention yet? [https://x.com/JoshWalkos/status/1846378686158983577](https://x.com/JoshWalkos/status/1846378686158983577)

It is frustrating to me the focus social media all the sudden had on DoD Directives and selective focus only on specific parts of 5240.01 and omission of facts and law that Prevent DoD from busting down your door and start shooting. Section 3 starts right out by stating DoD is restricted by Posse Comitatus from engaging in civilian “Law Enforcement” unless the President writes an Exec Order declaring the Insurrection Act or by an act of Congress temporarily suspending Posse Comitatus Act. The loop hole here however in DoD Directive 5240.01 in Section 3.3 item (d) is the unmanned systems i.e. drones. DoD Directive 3000.09 is doctrine that covers unmanned systems in combination with DoD Law of War also known as Law of Armed Conflict which is where RoE Rules of Engagement are derived. So while HUMAN DoD personal have to follow very clear and defined US Law and DoD Doctrine to engage in CivilIan law enforcement activities unmanned systems do not have the same body of law governing them! I find it rather curious that the social media ”analysts“ missed this fact! Below are pertinent sections of DoD Directive 5240.01, DoD Directive 5210.56, DoD Directive 3000.09 and DoD Law of War Chapter 6 that manages Autonomous Weapons RoE.

SECTION 3: DEFENSE INTELLIGENCE COMPONENT ASSISTANCE TO
LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES AND OTHER CIVIL AUTHORITIES
3.1. GENERAL PRINCIPLES.
a. Defense Intelligence Components may provide intelligence and nonintelligence assistance
to other DoD Components, including DoD law enforcement, on the same terms under which all
other DoD Components assist each other. Defense Intelligence Components must consider
command relationships; **Section 1535 of Title 31, U.S.C. (also known and referred to in this
issuance as the “Economy Act”) support agreements; Section 1385 of Title 18, U.S.C. (also
known as the “Posse Comitatus Act”); and any other applicable law and policies.** When
performing missions or functions other than foreign intelligence or CI, Defense Intelligence
Components will comply with DoD policy applicable to DoD non-intelligence organizations and
any specific operational parameters specified by DoD policy for that mission or function.

DoD Directive 5210.56 stated at the end of Section 3.3 (c)

DOD DIRECTIVE 5210.56 ARMING AND THE USE OF FORCE

Deadly Force.

(1) The DoD Component heads may impose further restrictions on the use of deadly force if deemed necessary in their judgment and if such restrictions would not unduly compromise U.S. national security interests or unduly put DoD personnel at risk.

(2) Deadly force is justified only when there is a reasonable belief that the subject of such force poses an imminent threat of death or serious bodily harm to a person or under the circumstances described in Paragraph 3.4.e.(4).

(b) DoD recognizes and respects the paramount value of all human life. If less than deadly force could reasonably be expected to accomplish the same result without unreasonably increasing the danger to armed DoD personnel or to others, then it should be used.

[https://irp.fas.org/doddir/dod/d5210\_56.pdf](https://irp.fas.org/doddir/dod/d5210_56.pdf)

Part (d) in 5240.10 Section 3.3:
(d) Provision or use of DoD **unmanned systems** in the United States except as
delegated by the Secretary of Defense pursuant to the October 31, 2023 Secretary of Defense
Memorandum.

DoD Directive 3000.09 AUTONOMY IN WEAPON SYSTEMS covers “unmanned systems” for which there are limited controls for commander level control of targeting and fire.

[https://www.esd.whs.mil/portals/54/documents/dd/issuances/dodd/300009p.pdf](https://www.esd.whs.mil/portals/54/documents/dd/issuances/dodd/300009p.pdf)

DoD Law of War also referred to as Law of Armed Conflict, Chapter 6 Weapons

“6.5.9.3 Law of War Obligations of Distinction and Proportionality Apply to Persons Rather Than the Weapons Themselves. The law of war rules on conducting attacks (such as the rules relating to discrimination and proportionality) impose obligations on persons.

**The law of war does not require weapons to make legal determinations, even if the weapon (e.g., through computers, software, and sensors) may be characterized as capable of making factual determinations, such as whether to fire the weapon or to select and engage a target.**

Rudimentary autonomous weapons, such as mines, have been employed for many years, and there has never been a requirement that such weapons themselves determine that legal requirements are met. Rather, it is persons who must comply with the law of war. For example, persons may not use inherently indiscriminate weapons.105

[https://dod.defense.gov/Portals/1/Documents/pubs/DoD%20Law%20of%20War%20Manual%20-%20June%202015%20Updated%20Dec%202016.pdf?ver=2016-12-13-172036-190](https://dod.defense.gov/Portals/1/Documents/pubs/DoD%20Law%20of%20War%20Manual%20-%20June%202015%20Updated%20Dec%202016.pdf?ver=2016-12-13-172036-190)

People have only as much liberty as they have the intelligence to want and the courage to take. Liberty allows for disobedience, but that disobedience needs to be peaceful.

Liberty gives us the ability and the right to tell people what they do not want to hear. This is the basis of leadership.

Freedom is not something that anybody in government can give. "We The People" take freedom, and people are only as free as they choose to be.

But every morning I wake up is another day, hopeless and miserable, with nothing but a deafening silence, a wave of tears, memories, and your absence in my life. That will change in 2025.

I'm set to retire in 2035. It's been a well-known fact that if Congress didn't take action by 2035 that social security benefits would have to be cut by 25%. The shortfall stems, not from people living longer, but because birth rates have dropped--hence, we have fewer workers supporting the system. Well, people are naturally having fewer children because of the hard economic times we've faced for decades. I was already upset knowing that Uncle Sam planned to "shrink" the social security portion of my retirement package. After listening to Dr. Kruse on the Danny Jones Podcast talk about SV40 and the 401k's up for grabs, I'm completely undone. I'm a federal employee, and our retirement system is called the Thrift Savings Plan (TSP). As of December 31, 2023, the Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) had over $845.4 BILLION in assets under management by Blackrock. And by the way, this includes the retirement accounts of our very wealthy members of Congress. One thing I wonder is were "they" all made privy to this evil scheme. The beauty of it is we have so many elderly "career" members of Congress that they're eligible to move their retirement funds to safety.

> My new video today makes a case that most won't see.
>
> **Silk Road 1.0** ---> [https://www.bitchute.com/video/iiyeHsOVAYbv/](https://www.bitchute.com/video/iiyeHsOVAYbv/)
>
> **Silk Road 2.0-**\---> Charlie Shrem, co-founder and CEO of BitInstant and Vice Chairman of the Bitcoin Foundation, in 2013 (photo credit: Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0, Cshrem)
> The top executive of a New York City-based Bitcoin company and a Florida Bitcoin exchanger have been charged with conspiring to commit money laundering by selling more than $1 million in Bitcoins to users of the black market website Silk Road, authorities said Monday.
>
> Charlie Shrem, 24, the chief executive officer of BitInstant and vice chairman of a foundation that promotes the Bitcoin currency system, was arrested Sunday at New York’s Kennedy Airport, and Robert Faiella was arrested Monday at his Cape Coral, Florida, residence, prosecutors said.
>
> Faiella and Shrem conspired to sell more than $1 million in Bitcoins to criminals who wanted to sell narcotics on the Silk Road between December 2011 and October, US Attorney Preet Bharara said in a release.
>
> “**Truly innovative business models don’t need to resort to old-fashioned law-breaking,** and when Bitcoins, like any traditional currency, are laundered and used to fuel criminal activity, law enforcement has no choice but to act,” Bharara said.
>
> Shrem is Jewish and speaks English, Hebrew, and Arabic. He attended the Yeshivah of Flatbush Joel Braverman High School and graduated from Brooklyn College in 2011 with a Bachelor of Science in Economics and Finance.
>
> Get The Times of Israel's Daily Edition by email and never miss our top stories.
>
>
> **Silk Road 3.0** ---> Lansky's connections to Ross Ulbricht. Silk Road ran as an onion service on the Tor network, which implements data encryption and routes traffic through intermediary servers to anonymize the source and destination Internet Protocol addresses. By hosting his market as a Tor site, Ulbricht could conceal the server's IP address and, thus, its location. If you listened to the new video I did, this should raise an eyebrow.
>
> More eyebrows? Look at his charges and Lansky's. On February 4, 2014, Ulbricht was charged with engaging in a continuing criminal enterprise, narcotics conspiracy, conspiracy to commit money laundering, and conspiracy to commit computer hacking. Federal prosecutors alleged that Ulbricht had paid $730,000 in murder-for-hire deals targeting at least five people, allegedly because they threatened to reveal the Silk Road enterprise. It's ironic but oddly similar to Meyer Lansky's MO.

Here are some questions I wish people would ask CAF in podcasts:
**Solari Report quote**: "The initial Bitcoin design was set up to create 21MM Bitcoin, of which 19.8MM are officially reported to have been created as of December 2024. However that limit can be changed—it is by no means firm."
**Question**: What is your understanding of how that limit might be changed? What would it take to do that? How would it work, in your opinion?

**Solari Report quote:** "There is no way to audit or confirm the existence of the total number of Bitcoins that holders believe they own."
**Question:** Do you know what a Bitcoin node is? What is your understanding of the cost to run and the level of technical expertise required to run a Bitcoin node?

**Solari Report quote**: "Because the blockchain infrastructure is very energy and environmentally expensive, the infrastructure may not be sustainable."
**Question**: Have you heard about arbitrage in energy prices incentivizing companies to build energy infrastructure in remote places that otherwise have no such infrastructure, with bitcoin miners as the first customers of those energy installations?

# Thread: A THREAD ON ALKALOIDS & POMC & MKULTA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-on-alkaloids-pomc-mkulta.29592/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-11T15:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-on-alkaloids-pomc-mkulta.29592/post-340823>

**Morphine, Nicotine, and Cocaine -** Caffeine's chemical family. Why is the use of these things on a massive uptick? Might the world's ability to make dopamine is dropping in a blue-lit, RF/microwaved world? A mitochondriac gets that perspective, but do you? Do you see why the opiate crisis is massive or why a guy from the West Coast can convince many humans to drink a lot of coffee and add MCT oil or butter to it? Might this be a sign of a broken light environment? The chemicals that caffeine belongs to are known as alkaloids, the most addictive substances known to man. This is linked to altered POMC signaling due to altered beta-endorphin signaling due to a lack of SUNLIGHT.

At various points in history, Morphine (Opium) and Nicotine (Tobacco) were socially sanctioned for daily use. Still, this trend reversed when the terrible side effects of chronic usage emerged in the 20th century. The 20th century brought with it industrialization and brought men from outside to inside to work in factories to manufacture things. Might it have been that migration, which blocked man from the sun, been further worsened because technology and the electric power grid zapped more dopamine from mankind, and then we defaulted to other bio-molecules with hexagonal rings that absorb light to replace the lost dopamine? I think you know my answer. Caffeine is no less addictive for many people, and the long-term effects on health are becoming increasingly apparent as average daily usage soars. It is no panacea for a low heteroplasmy rate.

Key conditions that caffeine significantly aggravates in my clinical experience are stress of all kinds, all muscle pain, most digestive complaints, liver metabolism, high blood pressure, PMS, insomnia, fatigue, weight loss AND weight gain, arthritis, blood sugar imbalances, skin conditions, and many mood disorders. Alkaloids are found primarily in plants and are especially common in certain families of flowering plants. Flowers have net negative electric charges that draw bees to them for pollination. Bees and most insects are positively charged in nature. Flowers grow through sunlight and photosynthesis and normally have a -30V electric field. The function of alkaloids in plants is poorly understood. Still, I sense it is related to flowers having the ability to alter their charges for many biological reasons tied to how they sense their environments. In some plants, the concentration of alkaloids increases just before seed formation and then drops off when the seed is ripe, suggesting that alkaloids may play a role in this process. Seed production and germination are heavily tied to solar cycles. Alkaloids may also protect some plants from destruction by certain insect species. This suggests that they may affect the charge in flowers to lure or defend against insects and may interact in the physiochemical process of generating the DC electric current in plants. This may explain how they affect human neurologic function by altering the charges associated with the DC electric current in man. It is well known that morphine has major effects on wound healing when used chronically. Generally, an alkaloid contains at least one nitrogen atom in an amine-type structure—i.e., one derived from ammonia by replacing hydrogen atoms with hydrogen-carbon groups called hydrocarbons. This or another nitrogen atom can be active as a base in acid-base reactions. The pH has massive effects on the size of the EZ in water in cells. This is likely how these drugs affect the nervous system and act as chemical proxies for a lack of dopamine. This is also why many alkaloids possess local anesthetic properties (cocaine).

Local anesthetics work by lowering the pH of the environment around nerves to disrupt action potentials. Their molecular structures, however, are not as precise in their actions as dopamine, and we get variable results from them because of these chemical changes. Dopamine is amine. Amines that are isolated from plants are known as alkaloids. This is why these drugs are dopamine analogs in animals. Their ring structure absorbs UV light to varying degrees compared to dopamine. When you marry a microwaved/RF environment that is blue-lit chronically, It creates a lack of DHA in the short and long loops of the retina. With a chronic lack of DHA in cell membranes in the SCN, you create the perfect storm. Your Vitamin A cycle in the retina and brain is broken, leading to CNS inflammation and lowered melatonin levels. The lowered melatonin levels cause further mitochondrial breakdown because melatonin is a protector antioxidant for mtDNA. When melatonin is destroyed, so is dopamine because both are made from aromatic amino acids in the retina in AM sunlight when IR-A and UV-A are present diurnally. So when you reach for your morning smoke or coffee you have announced to the world and yourself your dopamine and melatonin levels are destroyed. If you do this chronically and think you're bulletproof, you start using nootropics, opiates, and THC. This is a sign that you are getting worse, not better, because you need larger dopamine hits as your sleep worsens. It leads to a complete uncoupling of ubiquitination from melatonin cycles in the brain, skin, gut, and lungs.

This means your SCN is no longer running quicker than the clocks below it, and the timing mechanism built into all circadian controllers is destroyed. What does that mean to a mitochondriac? Mitochondrial diseases are coming your way fast and furious. DHA loss destroys the optical relationship between light bending in our gravitational field to uncouple light cycles from the cell cycle and metabolism. This is why blue light destroys DHA and melatonin levels in humans quickly. Diet will not fix this clock speed mismatch between the SCN and the organ clocks, but solar light can because it makes dopamine and melatonin in your eye every morning if you allow it to happen. Most of you DON'T, so you rely on guys to sell you coffee, drugs, or stories of how you can be diseased proof with some supplement. My advice is simple. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-20T14:49:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-on-alkaloids-pomc-mkulta.29592/post-345458>

In 1953, the CIA began drugging unsuspecting Americans with LSD. No, this isn't a conspiracy theory. It was called "Operation Midnight Climax" - and it's one of the darkest chapters in CIA history. The Cold War wasn't just about nuclear weapons. It was also a battle for the mind. When the CIA feared the Soviets were advancing in psychological warfare, they launched MK-ULTRA - a top-secret program aimed at mastering mind control. Enter George Hunter White below: • Former journalist turned federal agent • Known for "aggressive methods" • Described as "deadly and dedicated" • Perfect for the CIA's most unethical experiment.

The plan was simple but sinister: Set up a fake brothel in San Francisco. Install two-way mirrors and recording equipment. Hire prostitutes to lure men inside. Then secretly dose them with LSD. The CIA called these locations "safe houses." But there was nothing safe about them. Unsuspecting men would have a drink, meet a woman, and suddenly find themselves part of an illegal government experiment. The prostitutes were told to: • Slip LSD into clients' drinks • Engage them in specific conversations • Report their behavior • Test how easily they could be manipulated All while CIA agents watched from behind the mirrors. White's own diary reveals the CIA's true goal: "Controlling an individual to the point where he will do our bidding against his will - even against such fundamental laws of nature as self-preservation." This was government-sanctioned mind control. The victims experienced: • Intense hallucinations • Severe paranoia • Psychological trauma • No medical support Many never knew why they had such a "strange night" in San Francisco.

The operation ran until 1966. But the most disturbing part? White had no regrets. Before his death, he wrote: "It was fun, fun, fun. Where else could a red-blooded American boy lie, kill, cheat, steal, deceive, rape and pillage with official blessing?" The truth emerged in 1974 through a New York Times exposé. Congressional hearings revealed that Operation Midnight Climax was just one of 150 sub-projects under MK-ULTRA. The CIA had crossed a line they could never uncross.

Right before the expose the head of the CIA Richard Helms destroyed all of the documents linked to MKULTRA. Richard Helms's actions also linked this program to the JFK assassination because in 1979 he finally admited that the only person ever put on trial for the murder of JFK, Clay Shaw, was in fact a CIA asset as Jim Garrison brought up at his trial in 1967.

The program's exposure led to the Church commission: • Public outrage • Congressional investigations • New oversight laws • CIA reforms But for the victims, the damage was already done. Today, Operation Midnight Climax serves as a warning: When government power goes unchecked, ordinary citizens become unwitting test subjects and human targets. And the line between national security and human rights disappears.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-23T01:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-on-alkaloids-pomc-mkulta.29592/post-353174>

**Manufactured Nicotine vs. Nicotine in Foods or Leaves**

Manufactured nicotine, often referred to as synthetic or tobacco-free nicotine, is chemically synthesized in a lab to replicate the structure of nicotine (C₁₀H₁₄N₂), a pyridine-pyrrolidine alkaloid. Nicotine in sun-grown tobacco (e.g., Nicotiana tabacum) or foods (e.g., nightshades like tomatoes, eggplants, and potatoes, which contain trace amounts) is biosynthesized by plants, existing alongside other alkaloids (e.g., nornicotine, mymosine, chlorogenic acid, quercetin) and impurities. Here’s the comparison:

-   Chemical Structure: Manufactured nicotine is designed to be chemically identical to natural nicotine—both are (S)-nicotine in most cases, with the same molecular formula and stereochemistry. However, synthetic nicotine lacks the trace impurities and co-occurring alkaloids present in plant-derived nicotine. For example, sun-grown tobacco contains minor alkaloids like nornicotine (0.2–3% of total nicotine) and beneficial compounds like mymosine (an aromatase inhibitor) and MAOIs, as noted in posts on X discussing synthetic nicotine (e.g., Zyn) versus pasteurized tobacco (e.g., Swedish snus).
-   Purity and Impurities: Synthetic nicotine is marketed as “cleaner” because it avoids tobacco-related impurities (e.g., tobacco-specific nitrosamines, TSNAs) and heavy metals often found in sun-grown tobacco. Studies on synthetic nicotine confirm its higher purity, noting the absence of plant-derived contaminants. However, nicotine in foods or leaves exists in a complex matrix with other bioactive compounds, which can influence its biological effects. For instance, chlorogenic acid in tobacco may have antioxidant properties, potentially mitigating some oxidative stress from nicotine metabolism.
-   Biological Context: In plants, nicotine is part of a defense mechanism, often concentrated in vacuoles with organic acids, existing in free-base or protonated forms depending on pH. In foods, nicotine levels are extremely low (e.g., 2–7 µg/kg in eggplants), making its impact negligible compared to tobacco (1–3% by weight). Manufactured nicotine in lozenges, like Nic Nac’s product, is typically a free-base or salt form (e.g., nicotine polacrilex), formulated for controlled release and absorption, often with buffering agents to enhance buccal uptake.

Conclusion: Manufactured nicotine is chemically the same as natural nicotine but lacks the co-occurring alkaloids and impurities found in sun-grown tobacco or foods. This “cleaner” profile may reduce exposure to harmful TSNAs but also eliminates potentially beneficial compounds like mymosine or MAOIs, which could modulate nicotine’s effects on dopamine signaling and mitochondrial function.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-23T01:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-on-alkaloids-pomc-mkulta.29592/post-353175>

Absorption and Emission Spectra: Manufactured vs. Natural Nicotine
Absorption and emission spectra depend on a molecule’s electronic structure, which dictates how it interacts with light. Let’s compare the two forms of nicotine:

-   Absorption Spectra:
    -   Nicotine’s Electronic Structure: Nicotine’s absorption arises from its aromatic pyridine ring and the pyrrolidine ring’s nitrogen lone pairs. The pyridine ring’s π→π\* transitions dominate, with a primary absorption peak around 260–280 nm (UV range), as confirmed by studies on nicotine and nornicotine in aqueous solution. The pyrrolidine ring contributes weaker n→π\* transitions around 240 nm.
    -   Manufactured vs. Natural: Since manufactured nicotine is chemically identical to natural nicotine (same molecular structure, stereochemistry), its absorption spectrum should be the same: a strong peak at ~260 nm with vibronic fine structure. However, natural nicotine from tobacco may show slight spectral shifts or broadening due to impurities or co-occurring alkaloids (e.g., nornicotine absorbs similarly but with less intensity). Acidification (e.g., in lozenges or tobacco smoke) enhances absorption by protonating nicotine, shifting the equilibrium toward the protonated form (NicH⁺), which absorbs slightly differently (e.g., reduced n→π\* intensity at 240 nm).
    -   Foods vs. Tobacco/Lozenge: In foods, nicotine concentrations are too low to measure absorption spectra directly, but the matrix (e.g., organic acids, pigments) might cause minor spectral interference if extracted. In contrast, both manufactured and tobacco-derived nicotine in lozenges or smoke are concentrated enough to show clear UV absorption, with no significant difference expected between the two forms in isolation.
-   Emission Spectra:
    -   Nicotine’s Fluorescence: Nicotine exhibits weak fluorescence, emitting in the UV-blue range (300–350 nm) when excited at its absorption peak (260 nm). This is due to radiative decay from excited π\* states in the pyridine ring. Studies note that nicotine’s emission is often quenched by non-radiative decay (e.g., molecular vibrations), making it faint compared to more fluorescent alkaloids like morphine.
    -   Manufactured vs. Natural: The emission spectra of manufactured and natural nicotine should be identical in isolation, peaking at ~300–350 nm. However, in tobacco, co-occurring compounds (e.g., nornicotine, flavonoids like quercetin) may contribute additional emission peaks or quench nicotine’s fluorescence through energy transfer. For example, nornicotine’s ECD signals are less intense than nicotine’s, suggesting weaker emission. In lozenges, buffering agents or excipients (e.g., aspartame, polacrilex) might slightly alter the local environment, affecting emission intensity but not the peak wavelength.
    -   Foods: Nicotine in foods is too dilute to contribute measurable emission, but the food matrix (e.g., chlorophyll, anthocyanins) could emit in the visible range (400–700 nm), overshadowing nicotine’s weak fluorescence.
    -   The absorption and emission spectra of manufactured nicotine and natural nicotine are expected to be nearly identical—absorption at ~260 nm, emission at ~300–350 nm, since their molecular structures are the same. However, natural nicotine in tobacco may show minor spectral variations due to impurities or co-occurring alkaloids, which could broaden or quench emission. In foods, nicotine’s spectral contribution is negligible due to low concentrations.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-23T04:26:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-on-alkaloids-pomc-mkulta.29592/post-353179>

UPES measures biological systems' ultraweak photon emission (UPE), primarily driven by mitochondrial ROS production and excited state relaxation. In a broken Krebs cycle scenario (e.g., due to thiamine deficiency), mitochondria are already dysfunctional, producing excess ROS and altered UPE. Let’s assess how manufactured versus natural nicotine affects UPES:

-   Manufactured Nicotine (Nic Nac Lozenge):
    -   Direct UPE Contribution: Manufactured nicotine’s weak fluorescence (300–350 nm) may contribute minimally to UPE if excited by mitochondrial photons. However, its primary effect is indirect, via its pharmacological action.
    -   Mitochondrial Effects: Nicotine stimulates nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, increasing metabolic demand. In a broken Krebs cycle, this demand can’t be met, leading to electron leakage in the ETC, higher ROS production, and a 2–5x increase in UPE intensity (red-shifted, 600–700 nm from singlet oxygen). The absence of beneficial alkaloids (e.g., mymosine, MAOIs) in manufactured nicotine means it lacks potential protective effects. For example, MAOIs in tobacco might reduce monoamine breakdown, preserve dopamine, and mitigate some mitochondrial stress, while chlorogenic acid’s antioxidant properties could lower ROS. Without these, manufactured nicotine may exacerbate ROS-driven UPE more than natural nicotine.
    -   Spectral Impact: The UPE spectrum will show a stronger red shift (600–700 nm) due to increased ROS, with minimal contribution from nicotine’s emission (300–350 nm) due to quenching by cellular components.
-   Natural Nicotine (Sun-Grown Tobacco):
    -   Direct UPE Contribution: Natural nicotine’s emission is similar to manufactured nicotine (~300–350 nm), but co-occurring alkaloids like nornicotine (weaker emission) or quercetin (fluorescent at ~450–550 nm) may add minor UPE peaks or quench nicotine’s emission through energy transfer.
    -   Mitochondrial Effects: Natural nicotine also increases ROS in a broken Krebs cycle, increasing UPE intensity (600–700 nm). However, beneficial alkaloids may mitigate some effects. For instance, mymosine (an aromatase inhibitor) could reduce estrogen-mediated mitochondrial stress, and chlorogenic acid might scavenge ROS, slightly lowering UPE intensity compared to manufactured nicotine. MAOIs could preserve dopamine, reducing the metabolic burden on mitochondria.
    -   Spectral Impact: The UPE spectrum will still be red-shifted (600–700 nm) due to ROS, but minor contributions from co-occurring alkaloids (e.g., quercetin’s emission at 450–550 nm) might broaden the spectrum slightly. The overall intensity may be 10–20% lower than with manufactured nicotine due to the protective effects of co-alkaloids.
-   Nicotine in Foods:
    -   Negligible Impact: Food nicotine levels are too low (µg/kg) to affect UPE directly or influence mitochondrial function significantly. Any UPE changes would come from the food matrix (e.g., chlorophyll emission at 680 nm), not nicotine.

Probability of UPES Effects:

-   Manufactured Nicotine: 85–90% probability of a stronger UPE intensity increase (2–5x baseline, red-shifted to 600–700 nm) due to higher ROS from the lack of protective co-alkaloids. The absence of these compounds exacerbates mitochondrial stress in a broken Krebs cycle.
-   Natural Nicotine: 75–80% probability of a UPE intensity increase (1.5–4x baseline, red-shifted), but slightly less pronounced due to potential ROS mitigation by co-alkaloids. Minor spectral broadening is possible from quercetin or other fluorophores.
-   Biphasic Pattern: Both forms will show a biphasic UPE pattern (spike then drop) as mitochondrial damage accumulates, but manufactured nicotine may lead to a faster drop due to greater oxidative stress.
-   Coherence: Both will reduce UPE coherence due to chaotic ROS production, but natural nicotine might retain slightly more coherence if co-alkaloids stabilize mitochondrial function.

Broader Context: Implications for Nic Nac’s Product
Nic Nac’s claim of a “cleaner” nicotine lozenge may reduce exposure to TSNAs and heavy metals, but it also strips away potentially beneficial alkaloids present in sun-grown tobacco. In a broken Krebs cycle scenario, this could be a net negative:

-   Increased Oxidative Stress: Without protective compounds like chlorogenic acid or MAOIs, manufactured nicotine may amplify ROS and UPE more than natural nicotine, accelerating mitochondrial damage and heteroplasmy.
-   Dopamine and Mitochondrial Burden: As you’ve noted, alkaloids like nicotine act as dopamine proxies in a blue-lit, RF/microwaved world where dopamine production is impaired. Manufactured nicotine provides the dopamine hit but lacks MAOIs, which could preserve endogenous dopamine, potentially increasing the metabolic burden on mitochondria.
-   Wound Healing and Inflammation: Natural nicotine’s co-alkaloids might support wound healing (e.g., via MAOIs’ effects on monoamine signaling), while manufactured nicotine, as a pure stimulant, may impair it, as seen with morphine’s effects on DC electric currents.

Southern California’s high nnEMF environment (e.g., dense cell tower networks and the Van Allen belt issue) all should be expected to contribute to human Vitamin D levels because the rules for Vitamin D synthesis are highly constrained by Nature's requirements. They are unusual. Think Shannon's theorem. The UV index (UVI) measures total UV intensity but doesn’t guarantee UVB quality or coherence for 7-DHC conversion. Factors like atmospheric scattering, pollution, dehydration, or ozone can alter the UVB spectrum, reducing the proportion of effective wavelengths (~295–300 nm). The Quantum coherence of UVB photons is critical for efficient photolysis, but environmental EMF by itself isn't likely to significantly disrupt this at the molecular level. But it is the combination of effects that occur in this location to destroy what Nature requires to make Vitamin D from cholesterol and this is the big deal that people in California refuse to see. They all can tan, but they cannot make Vitamin D well. This tells you their is a precision error that has compounded in this region. ### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-11T16:08:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trucker-uv-lesson.29593/post-340829>

**HOW DO WINDOWS ALTER THE SOLAR SPECTRUM? AND WHAT DOES IT MEAN FOR YOUR HEALTH?** High-energy, short-wavelength light such as UV, violet, and blue light can generate reactive oxygen species. These can be beneficial in small amounts, but in excess, they become toxic. In natural sunlight, these short wavelengths are balanced by the regenerative properties of red and near-infrared light (which make up over 50% of the sun).

**WINDOWS BLOCK NEAR-INFRARED LIGHT (700-1400nm)** This creates an environment dominated by blue light, leading to chronic oxidative stress and causing skin damage. WINDOWS BLOCK UV-B (290-320nm) This prevents the body from producing vitamin D and melanin while also eliminating the natural signal of excessive sun exposure—sunburn. However, UV-A light can still penetrate through windows, causing damage such as photoaging by breaking down collagen and elastin in the dermis, which leads to wrinkles, sagging skin, and fine lines. Since UV-B is blocked, we don’t experience the sunburn warning signal, even though UV-A damage occurs. In essence, glass distorts the natural spectrum of sunlight, creating a light environment that can cause long-term damage. Slide from Dr. Alexander Wunsch

Do not think this is unique to one trucker as TWITTER PROVES.

**COVID-19 Vaccines from AstraZeneca, CanSino, Moderna, Pfizer, Sinopharm and Sputnik V**

"Among the undeclared elements were all 11 of the heavy metals: chromium was found in 100% of the samples; arsenic 82%; nickel 59%; cobalt and copper 47%; tin 35%; cadmium, lead and manganese in 18%; and mercury in 6% ... In all brands, we found boron, calcium, titanium, aluminum, arsenic, nickel, chromium, copper, gallium, strontium, niobium, molybdenum, barium and hafnium." The undeclared chemical elements were detected by Scanning Electron Microscopy Coupled with Energy-Dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (SEM-EDX) and high-precision Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS).

Your cells are a semiconductor factory. Do you think AMD or Intel would allow these contaminants into their fab factories during the construction of their semiconductors?

Why do your government and doctors let it happen?

**The Fluidity of Life and the Power of Choices**

Mitochondriac philosophy is rooted in a simple yet profound truth: Your current situation is never permanent. It is what you make of it from your choices. To the mitochondriac, life is not a solid monolith but a fluid, ever-changing stream. As a mitochondriac, he was obsessed with the cellular powerhouses that fuel life; he saw parallels between the adaptability of mitochondria and the resilience of the human soul. "If you are a stranger in your forest," he warned, "you must change something immediately."
He lived this ethos daily. Ideation without execution leads to deleting all good ideas, which he scribbled in his journal one March 31st. For him, execution trumped debate; a debate is masturbation for procrastination, and wasting time was an affront to life’s fleeting essence. His mission to decentralize the world wasn’t just a cause; it was about people. "We must be about people, not causes," he insisted. "This starts with an idea and a conversation, but it must be realized through risk, sacrifice, and endurance." His actions were slowly building decentralized networks for healthcare, education, and energy, and he became the best advocate for his vision—my Ode to Dr. Robert O. Becker.

[https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1959277281202856212](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1959277281202856212)

This science issues a stark warning: our fixation on the distant specter of Artificial General Intelligence is a dangerous distraction today. The real, world-altering disruption is already here, moving at lightning speed while we await a singular event. The chess game is over. The new, infinitely more consequential game is Protein Folding. Right now, the DoD is using vaccines and fake food to get atoms inside of us to ruin signal fidelity. Soon, they will use light in your environment to do this with quantum precision. They will use their new AlphaFold 3 algorithm to do it for you. This is an AI model from Google DeepMind that predicts 3D structures of biomolecular complexes, including proteins, DNA, RNA, ligands, and ions. This is a next-gen weapon that will be used to control all those who complied with the jab. Why? All those people now have intercalated DNA missense mutations this program will target. Google already has the ability to alter the light in your environment without your consent. They do this with indoor lighting, tech screens, and your email color temperature. Their terms of service give them this power, and you complied with it. You have no idea what POWER you gave them.

And with AlphaFold 3, we’ve just been checkmated. Think of proteins not as biology, but as the universe's ultimate nano-machinery: copying machines, scissors, shears, engines, lights. All are built from a linear code of amino acids that crumples into a 3D object with a function. For decades, predicting that fold from the code was one of science's grandest challenges. Redox chemistry in cells controls this. Now Google has figured out how to control redox chemistry using light to refold the semiconductors that make up your life.

AlphaFold 3 has effectively solved it. This isn't a future threat; it's a present reality that most do not understand. What does this imply?

1.  Designer proteins that target specific cells with terrifying precision.
2.  The foundation for true nano-robots.
3.  A complete rewriting of medicine, biology, and materials science. The glow-in-the-dark rabbit isn't a curiosity; it’s a harbinger. We are now holding the code to build anything we can imagine at the molecular level.
4.  The power to create, or destroy, is already in our hands. We can now open the door to heaven or hell with the flip of a photonic switch. The question is no longer "Will artificial general intelligence be good or bad?" The question is: Why are you waiting for a future intelligence when a present, alien one is already unfolding in your brain? Your expectation of artificial general intelligence is itself a programmed illusion, a distraction from the profound and immediate revolution happening right now. AGI is a transhumanist wet dream. Their great game has begun, and most of you aren't even watching it unfold.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-10T15:32:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-the-jabbed-considerations.29616/post-343628>

A couple of years ago, a group of Mexican researchers tested Ivermectin against 28 different cancers. They had, you know, these different types of cancers in their lab, and they said, “Look, we’re gonna test Ivermectin against all of these 28 different cancers,” and they found every single cancer responded to Ivermectin. Now, some responded better, some responded not as well, but all 28 cancers responded to ivermectin.

**The one that responded the best was ovarian cancer. The second best was breast cancer, and then followed by prostate cancer, and then you’ve got colon cancer, pancreatic cancer, and so on. You know, the various leukemias, lymphomas, and so on.**

And so there’s this tremendous body of preclinical research. There’s research on mice, and they actually gave Ivermectin to children with leukemia and managed to put two of them into remission. These were children who had very aggressive leukemia. They took Ivermectin, and they were put into remission for a while, you know, even though chemo had failed.

Now, unfortunately, again, Ivermectin is off-patent. It went off-patent in 1996. Merck had a patent on it. Now, they’re no longer interested in it. During the pandemic, Merck came out and said, “Hey, listen, Ivermectin doesn’t work for COVID-19. Don’t use it!” and everybody wondered, “Why would they do that? Why would they bash their own drug?”

Well, I discovered the answer, earlier this year. It turns out that Merck has a 50-50 partnership with Moderna, and what they are developing is, they’re developing an mRNA cancer vaccine together. It’s now in Phase Three clinical trials. It’s a $500,000 treatment, once it comes out. That is the reason Ivermectin hit the editing floor.

You need to find Doctors like Dr. Marik. Dr. Marik is an accomplished physician with special knowledge in a diverse set of medical fields, with specific training in Internal Medicine, Critical Care, Neurocritical Care, Pharmacology, Anesthesia, Nutrition, and Tropical Medicine and Hygiene. He is a former tenured Professor of Medicine and Chief of the Division of Pulmonary and Critical Care Medicine at Eastern Virginia Medical School (EVMS) in Norfolk, Virginia. As part of his commitment to research and education, Dr. Marik has written over 500 peer-reviewed journal articles, 80 book chapters and authored four critical care books and the Cancer Care Monograph. His efforts have provided him with the distinction of the second most published critical care physician in the world. He has been cited over 54,500 times in peer-reviewed publications and has an H-index of 111. He has delivered over 350 lectures at international conferences and visiting professorships. As a result of his contributions, he has been the recipient of numerous teaching awards, including the National Teacher of the Year award by the American College of Physicians in 2017.

Do you model the behavior you desire or the behavior that nature requires of us? Today's new blog asks this question for the jabbed.

Beauty is the attractiveness that nature possesses, which is copied in all her creatures in subtle ways. Humans are different because they can alter nature. This implies that sometimes people are beautiful, not in looks, not in what they say, but just in what they are. Nature plays her music through people's actions for those of us who listen. I got to interact with some folks this AM I probably would have never come across if it wasn't for my job. Life has a way of washing new people and new tides on our shores when we need them or their messages. Deep in the tragedy of an emergency room with people I seemingly had no connection with, I realized nature had a lesson to teach me. Sometimes, it appears in life that we are like islands in the sea, separate on the surface but connected in the deep. Nature's lesson for me today was there is no perfection, only beautiful versions of brokenness.
Every day, in every way, we are leaving our mark somewhere or on someone. How do you imprint your mark on life and the people around you? All right, so there's at least five peer-reviewed studies that have come out looking at RNA sequencing of people who were vaccinated and vaccinated. And if you dig through that data that's in NCBI, you can take all those reads, map them against the plasmids from the vaccine manufacturers, and you can find that DNA in these patients. "There's a study from Ryan et al. that looked at like 75 people in Australia. And there's a great study from Chakraborty that went through that data and demonstrated both Ryan paper and the Odak paper have Moderna and Pfizer vaccine sequences in the patient's blood. "So this means that the blood supply is contaminated. That's a very serious issue if the blood banks aren't looking for it and re-injecting this into other people. This would have normally, I think, caused an enormous halt in any other scenario once you find components of the vaccine that were never declared, that have these links to oncogenic sequences, and they're now floating around the blood supply and no one's doing anything about it. All right. That should be a concern to everybody in the in the transfusion field. "There's three other papers that have recently confirmed this. Now there again these papers were not looking for it. The authors were studying other things. But their data has been peer reviewed and put into the NCBI short read archive, so anyone can comb through their data and find these sequences in there. But the Lee study looked at this, the Knabl study looked at this, and the Krawcyzk study looked at this. All of them, I have documented that there are residual sequences from the plasmids that are in those studies infecting their recipients. "Some of the studies are actually very useful. "So some of the RNA sequencing is actually quite revealing as to what the nature of the contaminant is because you can see certain gene expressions get turned on or off that are signatures of a cGAS-STING-like event that could be induced by the DNA that's there."

Might it be that they cannot sell you treatments for the sun? Is this why they refuse to let millions use repurposed drugs too? Yep. It harms the cancer franchise that drive the most profit in hospitals.

There is no passion to be found playing small. Never settle for the static status quo. When you do, you're rusting out. As children, we’re energetic and curious about the world. By the time we enter adulthood, most of this excitement washes off, giving way to realism and practicality. We eventually settle for whatever seems practical and normal.

Many of us become complacent early on. We decide to stop trying things, because where we are is “good enough”. We start to feel comfortable, to the point that anything new is immediately averted. Remember, when you settle for a B, C, or D when an A is available you are wasting your time and "rusting out" with a cancer.

**_Solar radiation is the radiation one needs TO AVOID human oncogenesis. Pass that on.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In this paper, which your dermatologist ignores, we see that.... "The findings of the current study confirm solar UV-B radiation is associated with reduced risk of cancer of the breast, colon, ovary, and prostate as well as non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Eight additional malignancies exhibited an inverse correlation between mortality rates and UV-B...." This means more sun = less cancer risk.

The paper goes on to say that.... "Conclusions: The results of the current study demonstrate that much of the geographic variation in cancer mortality rates in the U.S. can be attributed to variations in solar UV-B radiation exposure. Thus, many lives could be extended through increased careful exposure to solar UV-B radiation..." So, if you'd like to get cancer faster but have lighter to vampire-like white skin (depending on what skin color you are starting with), by all means, follow your dermatologist's advice and avoid the sun. But if you would like to extend your cancer-free time here on Earth, maybe do the opposite and follow the mitochondriacs advice that is blasted all over my website.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-11T18:58:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-the-jabbed-considerations.29616/post-365143>

What the current events are now for the jabbed based on best data.

Kevin McKernan's recent post has enormous consequences to the JABBED...;

Let’s unpack what Kevin McKernan is saying step by step in plain language so you understand why this is “damning” if it’s accurate.

1\. The context Kevin McKernan is a genomics expert who’s been independently examining the mRNA COVID-19 vaccine vials. He and other independent researchers have raised concerns about residual DNA contamination from the manufacturing process — specifically plasmid DNA (tiny circular DNA molecules used to produce the mRNA in bacteria before purification). When pharmaceutical mRNA is made industrially, it starts with bacterial plasmids that include: The spike protein gene(the RNA code that’s supposed to end up in the vaccine) A Kanamycin resistance gene (“Kan gene”) which is a marker used in bacterial cloning so that only bacteria containing the plasmid survive in a culture treated with kanamycin. That bacterial DNA is supposed to be completely removed from the final vaccine mRNA product. Regulators (FDA, EMA, etc.) require testing for any leftover DNA.

2\. What McKernan is saying happened According to him: “Pfizer gave the regulators a PCR assay to monitor the Kan gene as a proxy for DNA contamination.” That means Pfizer told regulators, “We’re testing for contamination by checking for the Kan gene.” Fair enough—if the Kan gene was indeed present in the plasmid backbone, that could work as a reasonable marker if done correctly. But then: “This region isn’t included in the spike PCR target! So they fed the regulators and assay to monitor nothing in P1!” Translation: Pfizer allegedly gave regulators a test that doesn’t actually detect the DNA sequences present in the final manufacturing version (called P1). In other words, the assay was targeting a DNA segment (the Kan gene) that wasn’t even part of the spike mRNA construct used in the actual product. So regulators were handed a meaningless test that would always show “no DNA contamination,” because it was searching for something that wasn’t there.

3\. Why this matters If true, it means: Regulators weren’t actually checking for the real DNA contamination that could remain from the bacterial process. Pfizer’s data to regulators could be technically misleading not outright fabrication, but effectively a decoy test. Any plasmid backbone DNA or fragments possibly containing SV40-promoter sequences or bacterial antibiotic resistance genes could have slipped through purification without detection. Residual DNA contamination isn’t a trivial problem. Even small amounts of double-stranded plasmid DNA (especially if encapsulated in lipid nanoparticles) can theoretically enter cells and integrate into the human genome or trigger immune dysregulation. Regulators set safety thresholds (typically 10 ng of DNA per dose), but independent assays have reportedly found higher amounts in some samples.

4\. The core accusation McKernan is essentially alleging that: Pfizer “fooled” regulators by proposing a DNA assay that could never detect the relevant contamination so the entire biologic license rested on a nonfunctional quality control test. That would be an enormous regulatory failure if validated. It fits the wider pattern of institutional capture where regulators accept data generated by the company itself without independent verification.

5\. The takeaway You don’t need to be a scientist to grasp the moral of the story: When corporations self-report safety data, especially genomic assays, “trust but verify” isn’t enough independent replication is essential. The entire biotechnology regulatory framework often relies on faith in the submitter’s integrity, rather than reproducible, external audits. If McKernan’s claims about assay mismatch are correct, this exposes a deep procedural rot in the way biological products are approved.

Multiple errors, Jeffrey Epstein was never married, although he had several high-profile relationships, including 10 years with Ghislaine Maxwell.

**Her father Robert Maxwell** co founded
**Pergamon Press** which was an [Oxford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxford)\-based (UK) publishing house, that published scientific and medical books and journals. Originally called **Butterworth-Springer**, it is now an [imprint](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imprint_\(trade_name\)) of [Elsevier](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elsevier).

Maxwell also bought the [British Printing Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Printing_Corporation), [Mirror Group Newspapers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirror_Group_Newspapers) and [Macmillan Inc.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macmillan_Inc.), among other publishing companies. I believe the British and American Macmillan companies were separate.

From 1989 to 1993 Macmillan had a 50/50 partnership with McGraw Hill, a separate company called Macmillan/McGraw Hill but Maxwell did not found nor own McGraw Hill.

If at all possible, do you have any idea / recollection as to where you got the information above from? That is, regarding some cancer cells being destroyed by certain frequencies? I would love to read up about it.

The idea comes from some of the articles I've looked at from using high intensity ultrasound coming out of the Institute for Cancer Research in London [https://www.icr.ac.uk/research-and-...und-waves-are-being-used-to-treat-cancer-pain](https://www.icr.ac.uk/research-and-discoveries/cancer-blogs/detail/science-talk/the-quiet-revolution-how-high-frequency-sound-waves-are-being-used-to-treat-cancer-pain)

Or using specific frequencies of RF [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3845545/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3845545/)
If you follow the discussions from the folks talking about Raymond Royal Rife's work in the 1920s that was looking into the specific resonant frequencies of varying pathogenic organisms to cause physical destruction of said pathogen. The idea is rather analogous to a singer shattering a wine glass, a frequency at the right amplitude will do the job. Every molecule, protein, or cell has it's own resonant frequency determined by it's shape. Tuning forks or a xylophone are the easiest to visualize physically.

There's a few things though that would affect resonance. For things like a protein I'm not sure which category would best define that in the body since you have electrical properties, it's in water for the most part and it has mechanical attributes. Jack would probably have the best idea about it I'm sure good or bad.

-   **For mechanical systems** (like a spring-mass system or a bridge), the resonant frequency is governed by the equation **f₀ = (1 / 2π) × √(k / m)**, where **k** is stiffness and **m** is mass. The medium (e.g., air or water) may affect damping or energy loss but does not change the fundamental resonant frequency unless it alters the system’s effective mass or stiffness.
-   **For wave-based systems** (like sound in a pipe or vibrations in a string), the medium affects wave speed (v), which influences wavelength (λ), but **frequency remains unchanged** if the source is fixed. Since **v = λf**, a change in medium alters λ, not f. However, if the medium changes the boundary conditions or the system’s effective properties (e.g., tension in a string submerged in fluid), resonance can shift.
-   **In electrical circuits**, the resonant frequency is **f₀ = 1 / (2π√(LC))**, dependent only on inductance (L) and capacitance (C). The medium (e.g., dielectric in a capacitor) can affect capacitance, thus changing the resonant frequency.

### e s — 2026-03-16T13:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-the-jabbed-considerations.29616/post-368935>

Does anyone know whether any of this I have found is true:

1.  Cytotoxic T Cells (CD8+): Recognize foreign peptides on MHC class I. If integrated DNA expresses a strong non-self antigen (e.g., spike), T cells bind, release perforin/granzymes → apoptosis. Effective for virally infected/tumor cells.
2.  NK Cells: Kill cells with low MHC I (e.g., via "missing self") or stress signals (MIC-A/B ligands upregulated by foreign DNA). No prior sensitization needed.
3.  Antibody-Dependent Cellular Cytotoxicity (ADCC): Anti-spike antibodies + NK cells trigger lysis if spike is surface-displayed (unlikely from genomic integration).
4.  Apoptosis from DNA Damage: Integration could disrupt genes → p53 activation → programmed death, cleared by macrophages.

# Thread: Tonsil/adenoid inflammation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tonsil-adenoid-inflammation.29618/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### frouyer — 2024-10-14T14:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tonsil-adenoid-inflammation.29618/post-340974>

Hello to all Black Swans,

I am writing from the 48th latitude in France near Les Vosges mountains.

I discovered Uncle Jack just over a month ago and started implementing as much as I could in my family’s life ever since. Reduced exposure to nnEMFs, reduced exposure to artificial light at night, seeing the sunrise every morning, drinking mountain water, grounding as much as possible are a few things that we implemented.

My husband and I are learning designers so we spend most of our workdays on the computer. This part still has to be sorted out. We turned off WiFi and wired the house. We never owned a TV and have a strict no screen policy for our 3,5yo daughter.

Our daughter has been mouth breathing and snoring at night for a good two years.
We went to the ENT specialist in August and it appeared that she had huge tonsils and huge adenoids.

Part of changing our environment included getting rid of her video babyphone at night/during naps a few days ago. I noticed it has been around 5 days she is breathing through her nose at night/during naps and I haven’t heard her snore once, when it was all night long before. My husband noticed it as well.

Am I crazy to believe getting rid of the nnEMF from the babyphone + implementing all those changes could have reduced inflammation in her tonsils and adenoids?

She is scheduled to have an adenoidectomy/tonsillectomy on Nov 29th.
I was not a big fan of the surgical solution, but until discovering Jack’s work, I didn’t see any other one.
Now that I know better, I kind of don’t want her to go through surgery at all anymore.

What are your thoughts? Is it me wanting to see a correlation when it is unlikely that this could have an effect in only a few days?

Many thanks on your take on this!
I am already forever grateful for this community and for what Jack has shared to the world and will keep watching and reading to educate myself.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-14T18:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tonsil-adenoid-inflammation.29618/post-340982>

nnEMF is devasting for this condition. Hypoxia is the gateway to many diseases. Just as the quantum engineering series of blogs says, that too many of you REFUSE TO READ.

Do some research as there is evidence based on lymphoid tissue development / growth phases/patterns ages 3 to 6 may be the worse period for some kids & then the adenoids/tonsils begin to shrink over time. This is obviously only applicable is nothing underlying is at play here. Decision to do something about it would be based on what other down stream effects this was causing especially sleep quality. We managed this with certain head position and posture at sleep time and use pillows on the side so no rolling can occur that way we know mouth is shut, no snoring and airway is good, with the hope it would become less of an issue with age (as the research literature supports this providing there are no other underlying issues at play)..

# Thread: To study melanin where did I go?  NEW MEXICO
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-study-melanin-where-did-i-go-new-mexico.29629/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-16T15:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-study-melanin-where-did-i-go-new-mexico.29629/post-341084>

Where did I travel to understand the KT event better when mammals took over and changed how they used POMC and melanin to make sense of the new world with reduced solar exposure? I went to New Mexico.

About 66 million years ago, a massive asteroid impact triggered the extinction of 75% of Earth's species, including the dinosaurs. The Bisti/De-Na-Zin Wilderness in San Juan County, New Mexico, spans 45,000 acres and preserves one of the most complete fossil records from this pivotal moment in Earth's history—the Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary. This area, with its striking rock formations and rich fossil beds, offers a unique glimpse into the transition from the Mesozoic Era to the Cenozoic Era, making it a geological treasure for understanding Earth's ancient past.

He further went on to say that if we, or any animal, were put in a super oxygen-rich atmosphere, we would grow to be giants—without any change in DNA, or the need to wait hundreds or thousands of years to evolve

### Martha Ray — 2024-10-18T06:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-all-drive.29634/post-341169>

### What do you all drive?​

No reply to my Tire questions of the past… now Oct 2024 and I am down on my rims in many areas of life... I have two cars to maintain 1_999 Honda CRV_ and _2010 Ford Fusion Hybrid_. Considering downsizing to one vehicle and I am having a hard time deciding which one to keep or selling or trading in both on a new car.

I am in love with both of my cars the Honda CRV now with about 195,000 miles but great visibility easy to get in and out of, rough ride and it needs new tires but I love it and the Ford Fusion Hybrid with with about 132,000 miles is very comfortable, very quiet, very smooth ride, new tires and gets great mileage. Not good visibility and hard to get in and out of pluse EMF wise I’m pretty sure the Hybrid is not so great.

Looking another _Honda CRV_ OR a _Ford Escape_ or _Subaru Forester_ or _Subaru Outback_.

I want to buy a used ( What years best built for each model ?), SAFE, silver exterior, Low Maintenance, Comfortable vehicle, good visibility, all wheel drive with good gas milage.

Still interested in tires as well for comfort and winter safety.

All factors with low EMF, safety and health considerations priorities.

**This is hard…. What do you all drive?**

### Woodstock — 2024-10-19T09:32:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-all-drive.29634/post-341226>

Do you have any issues with mold/Lyme/coinfections? If so, either have the car tested for mold before you buy or buy a new car. It’s not worth the risk! I actually have a 1971 Volkswagen but I let my husband drive it because I react a bit to it even though it has been well taken care of. Just has a musty smell to it. It doesn’t bother him and it makes people smile and happy to see it and they wave. No EMFs either! I gave my 2007 Jetta manual transmission which I loved to my 18 year old son and we got a new car a couple of years ago, a VW Atlas. I did react to the new car smell for a year but it doesn’t off gas anymore and I at least know it isn’t moldy because it’s new. I don’t drive that much or very far usually. So I don’t worry too much about the EMFs but that is definitely a big concern with any newer vehicle. I think when the radio is turned off it’s ok. I would not get an electric car as I’ve heard they give off horrible EMFs from the batteries.

I love CT, but it seems to predictably bring on a genital herpes (HSV-2) outbreak every time I do it.

I want the health benefits of CT, but triggering more outbreaks than I would have otherwise seems like I'm just harming myself. What should I do?
Has anyone else experienced this and learned something on the subject?
Is there anything I can do to mitigate?
Why is this happening and what could this be telling me about my health?

Researching this online I can only find a few things out there to corroborate my experience:

### [Stress Hormones Epinephrine and Corticosterone Selectively Modulate Herpes Simplex Virus 1 (HSV-1) and HSV-2 Productive Infections in Adult Sympathetic, but Not Sensory, Neurons](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5469259/)​

### [Stress Hormones Epinephrine and Corticosterone Selectively Modulate Herpes Simplex Virus 1 (HSV-1) and HSV-2 Productive Infections in Adult Sympathetic, but Not Sensory, Neurons - PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5469259/)

Herpes simplex viruses 1 and 2 (HSV-1 and HSV-2) infect and establish latency in peripheral neurons, from which they can reactivate to cause recurrent disease throughout the life of the host. Stress is associated with the exacerbation of clinical ...

 pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### ScottB — 2024-10-18T18:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peyronies-help-suggestions.29641/post-341186>

Hi all-New to the community, excited to be here. I'm looking for any advice/help with peyronies. I've had now for about 2 years. What protocols have you all tried/seen any reduction of plaque with?

### Allodial Life Jordan — 2024-10-19T18:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peyronies-help-suggestions.29641/post-341246>

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-19T20:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peyronies-help-suggestions.29641/post-341260>

Remove the sunglasses for starters.

### Allodial Life Jordan — 2024-10-19T23:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peyronies-help-suggestions.29641/post-341271>

Dr Kruse brings up an excellent point. I did not initially look at your pic and honestly assumed you were asking this after having already done his basic lifestyle changes to improve overall health. If you had already done everything and were still dealing with it or if it was a particularly severe case, then and only then would I recommend the Gainswave therapy.
So let me come correct with a more nuanced answer:
First thing you need to understand is the pathophysiology of Peyronie's. Some get it from injury to the penis, but it sounds like yours was a more insidious onset. In those cases, it is preceded by a gradual decrease in erection frequency and erectile quality, especially overnight erections, which are essential to penile health. The lack of regular and complete blood filling of the corpus cavernosum allows blood to pool in there and if you have metabolic issues and your blood is full of plaque building components, you can guess what happens next: penile plaque. Overtime the plaque continues to grow, at some point restricting the ability of blood to flow into the corpus cavernosum/allow them to completely fill and expand the tissues. The incomplete filling on one side is what causes the curvature.
At this point you need to ask yourself why are you accumulating plaque. If it's happening in the penis you can bet it's happening everywhere. Sun exposure according to Dr Kruse's protocols will allow more NO production which improves blood flow to all areas via vasodilation. Sun on the penis is non negotiable. You may consider a cardiac calcium score to see if you've already developed plaques in your heart vessels. Definitely read all of his blogs and posts and learn how to optimize your health. Peyronie's is your canary in the coal mine. Fix your life before it's too late.

### Caitlin (Metabolic Mastery) — 2024-10-30T06:26:05+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-31T23:02:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peyronies-help-suggestions.29641/post-341820>

Curved banana is a lack of junk maintence. As a neurosurgeon, the most important lesson I learned in residency is to think ahead to avoid disaster. We are taught from day one that prevention of thought saves mounds of complications. In brain and spine surgery, there are no do-overs. One of my mentors told me the best neurosurgeons think 6 steps ahead of the step he is currently completing. I have never forgotten that lesson. It has served me well for over 30 years in my profession. We need to think about our own health in the same fashion.

Planning and adapting means thinking carefully about your future health. Lunging at the latest podcast or blog idea may offer immediate gratification from your mind or your friends, but it won’t get you any closer to building decentralized optimal health. A goal that can be achieved in a single step is probably not very meaningful – or ambitious. There are no magic bullets in anything in life. There is not a pill for every ill, nor is there a food /supplement/exercise for every ill either. Nature is the library for health.

At the Harvard Business School, Clayton Christensen, a professor famously said, “If you study the root causes of business disasters, over and over you’ll find a predisposition toward endeavors that offer immediate gratification.” Simarily, your decentralized “health trust” must be long-term, consistent and persistent and should including testing your beliefs and hypothesis constantly. This way you will never get drunk on centralized dogma from any group or thoughts that permeate your consciousness. [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/decentralized-tbi-treatments-jack-kruse-eurye/?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/decentralized-tbi-treatments-jack-kruse-eurye/?published=t)

Mitochondriac philosophy is rooted in a simple yet profound truth: Your current situation is never permanent. It is what you make of it from your choices. As a mitochondriac, he was obsessed with the cellular powerhouses that fuel life; he saw parallels between the adaptability of mitochondria and the resilience of the human soul. "If you are a stranger in your forest," he warned, "you must change something immediately."
He lived this ethos daily. Ideation without execution leads to deleting all good ideas, which he scribbled in his journal one March 31st. For him, execution trumped debate; a debate is masturbation for procrastination, and wasting time was an affront to life’s fleeting essence. His mission to decentralize the world wasn’t just a cause; it was about people. "We must be about people, not causes," he insisted. "This starts with an idea and a conversation, but it must be realized through risk, sacrifice, and endurance." His actions were slowly building decentralized networks for healthcare, education, and energy, and he became the best advocate for his vision—my Ode to Dr. Robert O. Becker.

I went to a drugstore to see if I could buy some Ivermectin (IVM), but it was still two to three times more expensive than what I paid in Egypt. However, it is still cheaper compared to the prices in the U.S. when you can find it.

Since the pandemic began, there has been some discussion about resistance to IVM and hydroxychloroquine (HCQ). Several podcasts have addressed this issue, and I've heard that those who have been vaccinated might need to take these medications daily for lifelong protection - indefinitely. It's still unclear if the body develops resistance to IVM or if viruses mutate to adapt to these drugs, especially for those who take it regularly for long COVID-19, among other conditions.

> Hey, Eve. "Plasmids are small, circular DNA molecules that play a key role in the evolution of antibiotic-resistant bacteria, also known as superbugs." I think this is what worries me the most. I have access to Ivermectin AND Fenbendazole AND colostrum AND Toxaprevent AND et cetera....my question is will all this stuff eventually stop working on the gut as the bacteria continues to replicate, eventually achieving superbug status. What happens when your body can't fight off the most basic of infectious disease because of antibiotic-resistant superbugs?

I don't think this is the way to think of bacteria anymore - I mean this fear of microbes and the martial idea of killing them with antibiotic agents. Even though these may (have) save(d) lives.

We need microbes, we cannot live without them and we have to treat them well if we want them to treat us well and work with us instead of 'against' us. Whether one microbe will or will not produce biotoxins in our gut, and become pathogenic, depends on its environment. That includes light, food, EMFs, mood, ... .

Whatever we do to microbes we do to ourselves. They do not need airplanes, bird wings or bitcoin to fly and spread.

To me it seems that we might be the singularity that microbes have created as they started collaborating and building multicellurlar organisms. And who knows: some of them even came on meteorites from outer space. Maybe we and other animals are the spaceships they built to explore planet earth in ever new ways and configurations.

This begs for a whole new paradigm and understadning of life which these dangerously overconfident biotechnologists out there with their hubris and toys and love of control seem to ignore. It's very stupid of them, despite their tech know-how.

Life is very fragile and strong. I heard in a TV program of a woman of 83y who'd been on pantozol (PPI) for 30 years and she's asking advice for her dried-up mucous membranes. PPI dry up these mucosa. I guess this lady may not have been thriving but she made it to 83 in a state of mind fresh enough to ask this question.

The new insights on bacteria, on the web of life, on light, water etc. should take us away from this outdated paradigm of medicine as an art of warfare against pathogens, against disease, against symptoms.

Ivermectin is made with soil bacteria, I understand. Toxaprevent = zeolite = volcanic stone containing minerals that are common to earth and to its soil. Colostrum is supposed to contain pro/prebiotics. And - wikipedia - "Fenbendazole is a broad spectrum benzimidazole anthelmintic used against gastrointestinal parasites"

And "Benzimidazole is a heterocyclic aromatic organic compound. This bicyclic compound may be viewed as fused rings of the aromatic compounds benzene and imidazole. It is a white solid that appears in form of tabular crystals."

Aromatic: that brings us back to Dr. Kruse and the importance of light.

Like many others, I've been spending a lot of time trying to figure out what the heck is going on with this planet in the big scheme of things, and how to thrive in spite of the nightmarish stuff that's happening in many places.

Lately, I've been looking at the research by Dr. Ana Maria Mihalcea, Dr. David Nixon and Dr. Anita Baxas, who have been demonstrating strange, technology-like things in the blood of first the vaccinated, and now also the unvaccinated. They believe that this is nanotechnology that is being spread through vaccines, food, water and chemtrails in order to influence/control humans in general.

Fortunately, they also demonstrate that IV calcium EDTA and IV vitamin C is effective at cleaning up the blood (which often looked horrible before the Ca EDTA/vitC).

This is a very big claim, but unfortunately, it seems thorough and convincing to me. I'm also in the process of reading 2 books by Mihalcea, "Transhuman" I & II.

It's difficult and expensive for most people to get IV Ca EDTA treatments, but it seems that it might be possible to get the same results using liposomal Ca EDTA and liposomal vit C orally, using ultrasonic cleaners and phosphatidylcholine.

My background for assessing all of this is a master's degree in nanoscience, after which I got halfway through a PhD program in nanoscience before I got sick, and I've been unable to walk or work for 11 years now.

Mihalcea mentions that many of the independent tests that were performed on the C19 shots did not find enough or intact mRNA for that part to make sense, but that they all contained technology-materials such as graphene oxide and metals. One study found rare elements that are primarily used in opto-electronics.

What do you guys think? Do you believe they are wrong? Does someone else have a better explanation of what's going on?

Baxas and Nixon have free substacks, and Mihalcea has one that costs about 15 USD/month.

Hi Nicklas,

I'm sorry about your health. I hope you are getting some answers.

I searched the forum just now for this very information. I am aware of Mihalcea and Nixon. I look at the information randomly, enough to know that I don't know what I am seeing on the microscope images, but it doesn't look organic/normal either. I will look at the videos you shared in the next few days. I watched an interview with Dr. Mihalcea several months ago, where she interviewed a dental surgeon from Spain, who explained that she (the dental surgeon) looked at dental anesthetics that were many years old (on the shelf, I think she said around 10 years old) and it had similar findings to what Dr. Mihalcea was finding.

I would think this topic would be on the forum or amongst the topics. I am newer here. Have spent a bit of time searching for key words and so far haven't been too successful in finding this topic, but need to spend more time searching.

I've got to have dental work and have been putting it off for this very reason. I was able to have fillings switched to composites and the biological dentist was good enough to ask if I wanted local anesthesia and I could do it w/out if I wanted to, so I chose that (without a local) and it worked out fine. I have more dental work though and I don't think I can get away w/out having anesthesia.

I have very few people to discuss this topic with. Of the few that I attempt it, I actually just don't think they can handle it.

If you have come across any other information since you posted this in Jan, I would be interested.

I did come across what seemed to be a legit organization, where they also had been looking at products under the microsope. They were coming up with some potential solutions. I will go back to notes and see what info I had. I think this person was connected with Dr. Group as far as suppliments that were being suggested.

I'll check back and update when I find that source.
