### UlliJoanna — 2024-10-21T12:24:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peripheral-neuropathy.29657/post-341322>

I am new. I have done a lot of reading, listening and watching. I am also an expert on myself after 20plus years of seeking recovery from an MS diagnosis I have always questioned and after reading Jack Kruse info on Peripheral Neuropathy I believe this is what my body has.

The numbness and tingling began after a terrible bout of sickness and diarrhea that lasted 3-4 hours. When I got up from my the loo I couldn’t walk because my feet had gone numb. It has affected the motor portion of my nerves. It continues to this day. This is all that is affected.

I am going to follow the protocol given.
I want to do some sort of exercise that will alleviate the stiffness, repair my gait etc.
I swim and am doing Somatics and am looking at the ABM.

Getting pregnant 12 years ago helped me so so much.

Anyway I am so happy to have found this information.

Many thanks in advance
Joanne

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peripheral-neuropathy.29657/post-358164>

**HEALING PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY?**

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

HOW?

**When we subtract the sun we subtract the possibility of healing.

UVA-mTOR Interplay and Spectra:** UVA absorption via OPN5/SIRT1 boosts NAD+, activating AMPK to inhibit mTOR, favoring photo repair like mitochondrial biogenesis. Outputs include IR-A emissions (600–1000 nm) from ATP/AMP ratios and clock genes, promoting coherent UPE for DNA repair. Melanin (from tyrosine/phenylalanine) protects DNA by ROS quenching, while melatonin (95% mitochondrial, from tryptophan) switches metabolism seasonally. Interconnections involve quantum mechanisms like electron transfer. Sunscreen, sunglasses, nnEMF, bluelight, and shitty cities/states/countries block all of UV light = THOSE WHO STAY SICK WITH ANY DISEASE [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978)

Heme protein renovation is fundamentally tied to AM sunlight, with research confirming that **Ultraviolet A (UVA)** radiation in the **320–380 nm** range is a primary driver of this process. As highlighted in the slide above, this interaction is a critical component of a "photorepair" system that resyncs cellular metabolism.

**The Role of 380 nm UVA in Heme Renovation

Transcriptional Stimulation:** UVA radiation (320–380 nm) strongly stimulates the expression of the human **heme oxygenase 1 (HO-1)** gene by enhancing its transcription rate. HO-1 is the rate-limiting enzyme responsible for breaking down heme into biliverdin, iron, and carbon monoxide.

**Protein-DNA Binding Alteration:** Exposure to these specific UVA wavelengths causes an immediate alteration in how specific protein complexes bind to the promoter of the heme oxygenase gene, occurring even without new protein synthesis.

**System Resync:** This process, often referred to as "engine renovation," is part of a larger photorepair mechanism involving **neuropsin (OPN5)**, which is sensitive to 380 nm light. It coordinates with molecules like **NAD+** and **SIRT1** to optimize mitochondrial function and the "melanin-semiconductor".

**Melatonin and Photoprotection**
While UVA drives the renovation of heme, it also induces oxidative stress. **Melatonin, created inside the engine,** acts as a crucial "buffer" in this system:

**Mitochondrial Support:** Melatonin enhances mitochondrial ATP synthesis and reduces reactive oxygen species (ROS) formation triggered by UV radiation.

**Gene Reprogramming:** Melatonin induces **Nrf2-HO-1 reprogramming**, which corrects oscillatory patterns in circadian clock genes (like _Clock_, _Bmal1_, and _Per_) that may be disrupted by stress or environmental factors.

**Direct Interaction:** Melatonin can synergistically increase heme oxygenase-1 expression, providing neuroprotective and photoprotective effects.
In this decentralized framework, **sunrise** serves as the initial trigger, providing the necessary 380 nm UVA "instruction" to renovate heme proteins and resync the body's internal clocks before higher-energy UV rays arrive later in the day.

### DLO — 2024-10-21T17:06:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-medical-students.29659/post-341326>

### [10,000 future doctors to train on health impacts of 'climate change'](https://www.disclose.tv/id/0juq42d5mf/)

Breaking news from around the world.

 www.disclose.tv

Yes your health was damaged by the climate and not by the shots you've taken.
Twenty-five European universities have launched the European Network on Climate & Health Education (ENCHE), aiming to equip more than 10,000 medical students with the skills to address the health impacts of climate change and deliver sustainable healthcare. Assoc. Prof. Cecilia Sorensen of Columbia University says, future medics "will see the volume and nature of the illnesses they treat directly impacted by climate change in coming years."

This initiative is chaired by the University of Glasgow, with support from the World Health Organization (WHO) and the Sustainable Markets Initiative Health Systems Task Force, which includes major healthcare organizations like AstraZeneca, GSK, and Novartis.

The initiative responds to the growing recognition that climate change is driving an increase in both infectious and non-communicable diseases, such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, respiratory illnesses, and mental health conditions. As WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Ghebreyesus stated, “The health impacts of climate change are not hypothetical threats in the future; they are right here, and right now.”

The ENCHE will embed climate and health topics into medical school curricula across Europe, helping future doctors better understand and manage the rising health burden caused by "climate change." In its first three years, the ENCHE aims to train over 10,000 students with cutting-edge scientific knowledge and skills to combat these emerging health threats.

# Thread: Vitamin D at 53 Despite Lots of Sun and Supplementing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-at-53-despite-lots-of-sun-and-supplementing.29668/>

### TaylorJamesTJ — 2024-10-23T06:05:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-at-53-despite-lots-of-sun-and-supplementing.29668/post-341420>

I just got multiple markers tested (everything in Epi-Paleo RX book) and my Vitamin D.

My Vitamin D was at 53. I know Jack Kruse said that you want over 60.

Here's the thing - I spend a good amount of time outside in the sun. I do a 45 minute walk every morning (last 2.5 weeks all have been at sunrise, before that, many walks were later in the day).

Then I will sit outside directly in the sun in the afternoon for a good 20 minutes or so before getting burnt. Sometimes I have spent quite a bit of time outside.

I have gotten plenty of sun for a long time now, before even hearing about Jack Kruse.

I've also supplemented with 5,000 IU's of Vitamin D drops and 1,000 IU + 120mcg of K2 every day (6,000 IU of Vitamin D total).

Why do you think my Vitamin D would still not be over 60? Obviously I know redox is probably going to be the answer... and maybe my redox won't go up until I leave my location. But I have also spent a lot of time in nature, bare feet on the ground. Hell I even do most of my morning walks barefoot.

Any ideas on why a lot of sun and supplementing wouldn't get me over 60?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-at-53-despite-lots-of-sun-and-supplementing.29668/post-341424>

You live in Las Vegas.

1)It's a desert
2) there's no magnetism
3) the non native emf in Las Vegas is off the charts
4) it's never completely dark in Las Vegas for miles around ( not since 1988) (first time I visited) in 2023 it wasn't completely dark at night( second visit)
5). From what I've read in another situation( namely Chicago)Dr Kruse says that you need to be at least 75 miles away from that kind of population density/ nnEMF to get any kind of redox benefits
6) Latitude of Las Vegas is 36N obviously you need better sunshine.

7 ) you're not in the right location to make enough.
Edit to include 8+

8). Nothing grows in Las Vegas so you can't eat local food ( not that it would matter as light is the primary concern here)

9). How much DHA are you consuming ?
10) your cholesterol levels
11) 8-10 are relatively unimportant as Jack often says that if you stand on the equator and you can't make Vitamin D3 it shows a poor level of redox.

12) your redox is poor

13 ) if you want better Redox and Vit D3 levels you obviously need to relocate .

As an aside.

14) I have a friend in Henderson who cannot lose weight and whose redox remains poor.

### petrichor — 2024-10-27T03:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-at-53-despite-lots-of-sun-and-supplementing.29668/post-341571>

Just as a counter example to show that it is possible to maintain vitamin D levels in a less than optimal environment. I live in Portland, Oregon. Our last day with UVB present (theoretically) is 10/31. I get as much sun as possible in the spring and summer, I practice impeccable circadian habits daily, I spend a lot of time outside regardless of weather, and eat plenty of DHA rich seafood, do a little cold therapy but mostly just go outside and get cold. My house has decent EMF mitigation, though not perfect. The neighborhood is not great, but not terrible, and there is no 5G nearby.
I tested my D on Dec 21st last year and it was at at 63 ng/ml without supplementation. I see that you can only spend 20 minutes in the sun without getting burned. I can stay in the sun indefinitely, even when the UV is 9 or 10. I have never tested my D level at the end of summer, but it must be decent in order to still be at 63 on the winter solstice. Developing more sun tolerance seems like it would be a good starting point. Vegas has UVB year round (at least theoretically). Take advantage!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T04:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reductive-stress-causing-oxidative-stress-loop.29675/post-348630>

**My Decentralized Synthesis**
I bridge Lane’s work with a decentralized lens: Antioxidants in my system are superfluous as the research shows. There is very littel support in the literature to support the claims that antioxidants do what paid scientists say.

-   Sunlight/Grounding: Supply electrons, stabilize the IMM field, reduce ROS/RNS, and counter Warburg.
-   DDW: Insulates the IMM, preventing “electrocution” and heteroplasmy, reducing cancer risk = big for mRNA turbocancer patients.
-   GOE and Endosymbiosis: Reframe the Warburg effect as an evolutionary response to light/oxidative stress, with modern nnEMF mimicking ancient conditions forcing phenotype and metabolic changes.

Antioxidants in my system are superfluous as the research shows. There is very littel support in the literature to support the claims that antioxidants do what paid scientists say. The major antioxidant made in my framework is melatonin by photo-bio-electric signaling. The rest is markting bullshit.

1.  Antioxidants as Superfluous
    -   Claim: I argue that research shows little evidence supporting the efficacy of exogenous antioxidants (e.g., supplements) as claimed by mainstream science. This aligns with critiques in the literature where high-dose antioxidant supplementation (e.g., beta-carotene, vitamin E) has been linked to **_increased cancer risk_** or no benefit in certain studies (e.g., the Alpha-Tocopherol, Beta-Carotene Cancer Prevention Study, 1994).
    -   Implication: If antioxidants are ineffective or counterproductive, the body’s natural systems (e.g., melatonin via photo-bio-electric signaling) **should be prioritized over marketed solutions.**
2.  Melatonin as the Major Antioxidant
    -   Mechanism: I posit that melatonin, synthesized through photo-bio-electric signaling in mtDNA (e.g., via sunlight, melanopsin, and mitochondrial responses), serves as the primary endogenous antioxidant. Melatonin is known to neutralize ROS/RNS (e.g., •OH, ONOO⁻) directly and indirectly by upregulating antioxidant enzymes (e.g., superoxide dismutase, glutathione peroxidase) and supporting mitochondrial function.
    -   Connection to the Decentralized Framework: This ties into my sunlight/grounding model, where light exposure (AM solar EMF) and electron donation enhance melatonin production, countering nnEMF/ALAN-induced ROS/RNS and stabilizing the IMM’s 30 MV/m field (per Lane).
    -   Outcome: Increased melatonin reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and the Warburg shift, potentially lowering disease risk (cancer, neurodegeneration).
3.  Photo-Bio-Electric Signaling
    -   Mechanism: Sunlight drives melanin to split water, releasing electrons (Herrera’s work), and Pollack’s EZ water generates a negative charge, both enhancing melatonin synthesis. This photo-bio-electric process, amplified by grounding, replaces the need for exogenous antioxidants by directly neutralizing radicals and maintaining redox balance.

Closest cell tower is 3 miles away. I live on 3/4 of an acre vacant lot on one side closest house is 300 yards away. I have the power off at the breakers to most the house most of time. The sleeping areas measure between 30-40 millivolts on the body voltage the rest of the house is below 1on the body voltage. The only high spot is the kitchen anround 1.8. Only magnetic field is at the service entrance which is on the outside wall of my wife’s craft room that we don’t spend much time in.

My dha intake was really high when we lived in Ak until last year. Lots of fish and wild game. Since we moved to Texas I save most the salmon for the kids. My best sources would be grass finished beef, raw pastured milk and pastured eggs which is 95% of my diet

It sounds like you live similar to us, we home school the kids and are outside all day.. Just a FYI - body voltage measurement on their own are not accurate as it just measures the effectiveness of the capacitive coupling to your body (but not the actual intensity of the field you are in). I have explained why and given many examples in my EMF journal thread. You can have a low BV reading and still be in a high field, hence the importance to measure the "actual" field. Many people (in the professional space) do not use BV for this very reason, however, if you are using BV as a metric make sure you have a decent meter to measure the E field on X, Y, Z axis or alternatively use 3D meter. If doing 3 x single axis measurements make sure you get the sum (I have posted about this previously also). E.g. E total = square root of (X2 + Y2 + Z2) basic calculation. If I find these threads I will post the links for you.. Just remember that an electric field is a vector (meaning strength depends on direction).

There are some draw backs to the S&S pro (as the only meter) which I have posted about also with regard to calibration drift and accuracy ~ +/- 6dB and the possible implications...

What I'm highlighting here, if a-MSH has not normalised you are likely doing a lot correct by the sounds of it, but may not be storing that light too well (e.g. leaking charge)... So could be as simple as increasing the DHA intake, if you cannot get good fresh sources (due to budget) maybe look at some cheaper options like canned sardines in decent EVOO.. Good bang for buck. If that does not work, then the above "finer" details (that I laid out) would matter in your case, thus must be considered and investigated further..

Do not underestimate the importance DHA as you would struggle to get adequate amounts just eating land based. I would be aiming for no less than 2g per day. Without adequate DHA, photon to charge conversion would be weak (aka. catching the light but not storing the charge).. DHA also increases the dielectric constant and flexibility of the IMM so charge can be held, electron tunnelling efficiency, maintain coherent proton flow through the ATP synthase and so on..

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-24T15:52:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-modern-low-income-housing-operates-attack-allodial-title-with-zoning-changes.29679/post-341473>

Sept 2024 Asheville Affordable Housing Plan Proposal | Shortly before hurricane Helene devastated the city of Asheville, NC, Enterprise Advisors presented an affordable housing plan to the city of Asheville.

The recommendations, along with multiple grant funding mechanisms with private-public partnerships, strongly advocate that the City of Asheville use available resources to achieve equitable outcomes and identify additional opportunities to leverage City resources for advancing racial equity. Since 2010, Buncombe County’s population has grown due to in-migration, and this growth is projected to continue over the next few decades. The population is still projected to grow due to a continued increase in in-migration, gaining 45,000 new residents by 2050. Housing Racial Equity Quantitative Assessment The AHP team examined the City’s primary set of policies and programs (or “tools”) for promoting affordable housing through Land Use Incentive Grants, Housing Trust Fund, City Bonds, and city owned property to obtain the housing needs for the projected population growth. The AHP drew from Race Forward’s Racial Equity Impact Assessment as a model for its equity-related assessments.
[@Breeauna9](https://x.com/Breeauna9)
Land Use Incentive Grant (LUIG) Thrive Asheville, a local nonprofit that is deeply involved in affordable housing, conducted a racial equity assessment of Land Use Incentive Grant (LUIG) in 2023 that the AHP used as a baseline for its racial equity assessment. They determined, it is likely that the LUIG-supported rental homes disproportionately benefit white renters over BIPOC renters. "...mitigate some of LUIG’s potentially inequitable outcomes, but underscores the need for a revised application process, improved data tracking, and greater staff capacity." The Housing Trust Fund (HTF) The HTF should strive to keep long-time homeowners in place by expanding the program to fund home repair programs. The HTF could provide loans to affordable housing funders collaboratively to help leverage collective financial resources. Increase scoring for place-based priorities, such as areas benefiting from Capital Improvement Program investments or sites that would provide opportunities for transit-oriented development. Explore codifying dedicated revenue sources for the HTF.

It is one of the City’s most important tools for funding affordable housing that is accessible to low-income households, including households of color. Affordable Housing Bond (AHB) To be considered an eligible use of the bond, the funds must go towards capital costs. Generally, capital costs refer to the money needed to start or build something, such as a project, a business, or a big purchase. In the context of City-funded housing projects, capital costs are the cash required for the acquisition, rehabilitation, and construction of affordable housing. The bond’s granting authority can also help to build the capacity of mission-driven nonprofits that may be better positioned to advance certain equitable outcomes. Supporting the Asheville-Buncombe Community Land Trust and Haywood Street Community Development are examples of these kinds of partnerships. Fee Grant Program for Affordable Housing (FGP) Conceptually, one area of concern is the eligibility criteria to receive an upfront fee waiver as opposed to a rebate (some waivers are not allowed to be waived per statutes). Waivers can be granted to experienced developers who have formalized agreements with the City to provide affordable units.

As the City works to build the capacity of local nonprofit developers, including BIPOC-led companies, policymakers may consider a capped upfront waiver for eligible fees. Strategic Framework Environmental displacement occurs when housing is lost due to a natural disaster or climate-related issues. These strategies put the people of Asheville first by promoting opportunities for choice, affordability, and stability. These strategies specifically look at renters, homeowners, and systems-level changes that can help Asheville’s residents thrive today and into the future. While many housing affordability efforts should be citywide, there are some approaches that should be focused within specific parts of Asheville. These include changes to the zoning code, supporting transit-oriented development, targeting land acquisition strategies, and changing City development approval processes. What does it mean to center racial equity?

How will BIPOC community members be meaningfully engaged in the development of the policy/program/initiative? How will this policy/program/initiative positively impact BIPOC community members? How might this policy/program/initiative inadvertently negatively impact BIPOC community members? Does the proposal inadvertently exclude them? Are resources being allocated equitably across the city rather than reinforcing patterns of disinvestment and segregation? How can the proposal be altered to mitigate harm and improve outcomes for BIPOC community members? How will the outcomes of this policy/program/initiative be measured?

Advancing racial equity also requires understanding intersectionality. City and community leaders recognize that gender, sexual orientation, familial status, immigration status, ability, and age (amongst other identities), also impact how BIPOC residents access and experience housing in Asheville. Reparations-oriented homeownership and/or home repair assistance program Recognizing patterns of segregation, redlining, and disinvestment, some cities have designed homeownership initiatives for low- and moderate-income households in select neighborhoods. These programs can include community investments, such as supportive infrastructure or community facilities. Other reparations programs can come in the form of more direct payments, such as home repairs, down payment assistance, or mortgage subsidies. Specifically targeting these programs to address the racial homeownership gap can help rectify historical injustices and systemic inequities that have disproportionately affected BIPOC communities.

The 'Cease the Harm' study pointed out weaknesses and harms related to affordable housing programs, and the Community Reparations Commission finalized its housing recommendations in the Summer of 2024. This is a critical juncture to address those issues. The City of Asheville must continue to proactively build and integrate racial equity into all facets of city administration, policy design, and implementation to ensure lasting and meaningful change. Upzoning City Recommendation Unified Development Ordinance (UDO) Development regulations sometimes present barriers that are disproportionately challenging for nonprofits and small-scale local developers, limiting their ability to participate in the housing market and contribute to affordable housing solutions. The most impactful way to reduce regulatory barriers is through a comprehensive update of the city’s Unified Development Ordinance (UDO) through upzoning and revising the development process. Recommendations include collaborating with city planning & zoning communities on forthcoming UDO upzoning policies. Upzoning recommendations include streamlining permitting processes, reducing parking minimums, and reducing lot sizes, which can help to level the playing field and enable small developers to navigate the development process more efficiently and cost-effectively.

The city of Berkeley was the first municipality to implement the UCO upzoning concept. The upzoning changed local zoning ordinances of single-family homes to include ten houses allowable per acre in the former single-family zoning district, while density in other zoning districts is expected to change to allow 20 houses per acre. In other words, bring the poverty, crime, and new immigrants into the higher-end housing market municipalities and build government-funded density rich housing in the suburbs and mandate funding must include low-income building incentives. All developments that receive city funding are required to accept rental assistance. Requiring all developments that receive City funding (i.e., subsidy, tax abatements, land, etc.) to accept rental assistance will help prevent displacement and promote housing stability for low-income residents. The City has begun to do this already on a project-by-project basis, but this could be instituted rather than serving as a best practice. By implementing such a requirement, Asheville can ensure that affordable housing opportunities are integrated into a wider range of developments, including market-rate developments receiving public funding support. This policy not only expands access to affordable housing for voucher holders but also promotes economic diversity within higher-end developments across the city. Attached is the full proposal for what appears to be a plan to invest in high-density urban planning across all of Buncombe County.

Often, during disasters and emergencies, the progressives seize these opportunities to implement their progressive policies and vision for the future. Former President Obama was the first to propose forced UCO upzoning across America. Through numerous executive orders, President Biden has finished the mandate conceptualized by his old boss. It is important to note that the new affordable housing recommendations have not been approved to date. [https://publicinput.com/Customer/File/Full/92212b02-34cf-4257-bc60-](https://t.co/SaEv8QAhvK)

3) The facility is not a new structure and meets the criteria for a historic building (for public benefit);
4) The facility has not been rebuilt as the result of sustained structural damage in a previous Presidentially declared flooding disaster area or emergency; and
5) The facility is not a hospital, generating plant, emergency operations center, or a facility that contains dangerous materials). FEMA "intends" to provide assistance for the restoration of these facilities, roads, culverts, and bridges to their pre-disaster condition, but ONLY IF new climate-smart building codes are adhered too.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-24T16:14:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-modern-low-income-housing-operates-attack-allodial-title-with-zoning-changes.29679/post-341485>

FEMA's 50% rule explained: Will your flood-damaged home get bulldozed? My fear is for the many communities that have been completely flooded and/or completely destroyed in the hurricane(s) --FEMA will require/force residents and businesses to move their location to higher ground. The FEMA regulations require eligible FEMA assistance recipients to comply with the new regulations in order to receive federal disaster recovery funds. Please turn on my notification, share with your community, and comment below with your thoughts as we navigate through our new reality together post-hurricane Helene and Milton. New FEMA regulations, effective September 9, 2024, expanded floodplain zones, including new rules to mitigate future flood damage to areas situated in the revised floodplains. In addition, the floodplain calculations have been updated and more stringent when assessing construction, mitigation, and whether flood-damaged buildings will be rebuilt based on the new regulations. According to the FEMA fact sheet, Individuals, communities, businesses, and government agencies will have to follow the new FEMA Floodplain rule on how buildings damaged from a disaster are rebuilt and repaired. Rebuilding after a disaster provides an opportunity to make buildings stronger and safer. The primary goal is to reduce the risk of loss from future disasters. If local officials determine that a residential structure has been substantially damaged – meaning the cost of total repair to the home is equal to or greater than 50 percent of its market value before the disaster damage – the owner generally has three options to bring the structure into compliance.

Elevate the building to a height determined by local officials to Relocate the structure outside the floodplain. Demolish the structure. FEMA has the authority to require the application of the FFRMS as a condition of funding in its grant programs based on the grant programs' authorizing statutes. Congress granted FEMA the authority to provide Federal assistance through multiple grant programs under the Stafford Act, the NFIA, the Homeland Security Act of 2002, the Federal Fire Prevention and Control Act of 1974, the Earthquake Hazards Reduction Act of 1977, and various other appropriations acts. Under the FFRMS, a Federal agency may establish the floodplain for actions subject to the FFRMS using any of the following approaches: Approach 1: Climate-Informed Science Approach (CISA): Utilizing the best-available, actionable hydrologic and hydraulic data and methods that integrate current and future changes in flooding based on climate science; Approach 2: Freeboard Value Approach (FVA): Freeboard (1 percent annual chance flood elevation + X, where X is 3 feet for critical actions and 2 feet for other actions); \]Approach 3: 0.2-percent-annual-chance Flood Approach (0.2PFA): 0.2 percent annual chance flood (also known as the 500-year flood); or Approach 4: the elevation and flood hazard area that result from using any other method identified in an update to the FFRMS. Approach 4: the elevation and flood hazard area that result from using any other method identified in an update to the FFRMS. The Revised Guidelines state that for riverine flood hazard areas, agencies may select either the FVA or 0.2PFA (or a combination of approaches, as appropriate) when actionable science is not available, and an agency opts not to follow the CISA. The proposed FFRMS policy outlined the FFRMS approach FEMA would use for actions subject to the FFRMS. FEMA's final rule FFRMS policy will be applicable to actions in the FFRMS floodplain where FEMA funds are used for new construction, substantial improvement, or to address significant damage. Picture: The French Broad River rises above its banks in the River Arts District on Sept. 26 in Asheville, N.C.

Talk About Price Gouging!!! Force Placement 8 allows a lender to capitalize the cost of a Forced Placement by adding that cost onto the total outstanding principal balance and then obtain a policy that covers the total cost of the prior balance plus the cost of a policy to cover the full and total new balance. Example: say you owe 250k on your home, it gets remapped into a floodplain, and NO private insurance company will write the policy, BUT FEMA will insure the property for 100k per year. Your lender can now obtain a policy for 350k and bill you for it.

# Thread: Shielding AC Magnetic Field

### Garry Kirk — 2024-10-25T23:29:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-341534>

I am looking to shield my bedroom from an AC Magnetic Field that is penetrating my bedroom during the hours of sleep. I think this field is coming from an Electric Storage heater system since the reading shows between about midnight and 8am in the morning. These systems typically are run in the UK, during the night in an off peak fashion.

The reading is between 140nT and 170nT.

I can buy steel sheets, rivet and weld them and cover the neighboring wall without too much trouble, | just want to do it right first time. I am not sure if the kind of steel matters and how thick the steel needs to be. If anyone has any experience with this, I'm all ears.

G.

Hi Anthony,

That is not my experience at all, certainly in a domestic setting at least. If I boil a kettle in the kitchen, I get readings all through the house, both laterally, vertically and diagonally, which don't vary much, or drop much at all. I have a reading of between 140-180nT all through my house between these hours, which is what is making this difficult. I can't see through walls, so I can't locate the heaters. So in my bedroom (downstairs) I could cover the wall in steel, yet still be getting a reading coming though the ceiling above, diagonally. I can't use the document Mat provided because of this as well. I've had a look around, but there are no specialists in Scotland really either, so I don't think there is anyone at least with any better equipment than I am currently using, which leaves me thinking it's a shotgun approach, cover the wall and ceiling and that should do it. It's just a case of how thick the steel should be to work. I could start with the side wall with thicker steel first and see if that's enough, but I'm fumbling around in the dark really. I mean, floor and ceiling will work if I make it thick enough, but I don't want to spend a fortune on this unless I have to.

Cheers.

### Mat.I — 2025-03-06T00:27:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-347934>

### Mat.I — 2025-03-06T00:46:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-347937>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=347934)
>
> Hi Gary, I recall commenting on your other thread in the past.. How old is your townhouse? Have you ruled out a shared neutral?

Highly likely, in older building stack especially if the townhouse/sub-division conversion happened after construction. This likely explains "the readings throughout the house" due to return current taking alt paths & high net current (due to not being cancelled out). Have a electrician check or alternative get a clamp meter to check yourself & check current on metal pipes while you are at it.

E.g. - isolate your power and check/confirm any difference - if no difference - put the clamp meter on the main neutral line (if current flowing) - likely have a shared neutral.. Have your neighbour turn off the power - put clamp meter back on your main neutral- if current drops significantly - your neutral was carrying their return current... If it stays the same - could be utility N issue or other neighbour issue..

To check stray currents e.g. throughout the house via alt return paths etc.. - use the clamp meter on metallic service pipes & main earths - if current is flowing there is an issue with a N return path. With this scenario.. it can get quite messy and complicated subject to how things are setup. I'm just outlining basic things you could subtract quite quickly (i.e. without the finer details of diving into specifics)..

### Mat.I — 2025-03-07T23:56:45+0200

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=348068)

If that is the case then the issue is her side, as the readings are there when the power is isolated. I definitely have an issue with the ring main circuit in my house and it needs tested but with that fuse out, again, I still have the same readings at night.
>

There is a very high chance it has a shared neutral, but you have the metal conduit which like you say, is not good with this situation. It was originally storage heaters and the central heating system in there now is all plastic piping.

Hi Garry,
Yes it sounds like a shared neutral issue and likley stray return currents... re: legal issues - I had a very brief look at how this is governed in Scottland.. Landlords must provide a EICR every 5 years or in the case of an electrical issue report your best bet (if shared neutrals are the case) is to have it classified as no higher than a C2 issue so it has to be addressed asap for the landlord to fufill their duty of care to ensure a safe electrical installation has been provided to the tenant etc.. E.g. C2 classification - prevents safe isolation, potential overloading, no dedicated consumer installation shock risk and even potentially a C1 classification inclusive of fire risk... Another angle could be if work was carried out on the installation. Was there a trigger under the reg to bring the entire installation up to scratch with BS 7671 etc..

I would be getting an electrician (not the landlords one).to give an independent opinion on this that way you maintain the control and the onus will be on the landlord to address or counter the identified issues..

If you get stuck let me know. As I could have a look at the specific regs in your area if you are contemplating appointing a Lawyer it may save you some dollars. My day job is an expert witness for related fields..

### Mat.I — 2025-03-08T01:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-348080>

Noting if not shared N.. Faulty or high resistance N elsewhere in the network could also be causing the stray currents via the metallic pipes etc.. e.g. town main water supply.. This could be ruled out very quickly with a clamp meter as detailed above.. This scenario doesn't necessarily have to be your direct neighbour it could be anywhere in the network... Sometimes this is a pain to solve.. If network providers do not address the only way is to eliminate the stray path via non-conductive services break etc..

Had a spark out the other morning. He reckons it will be a shared neutral with the street. He changed the socket that was burnt through on the neutral, re terminated a couple of sockets, tested the sockets and reckons the neutrals are in a continuous loop and all sockets are ok (they tested ok on my socket tester too). I plugged in an appliance and the magnetic field was still huge and through the whole house, so the problem still exits on that circuit.

The issue with getting the neighbour to isolate for testing is she's an old bat basically and non co operative - hence the legal route. I've tried, so if it has to be painful for her, then so be it.

It's very strange and will take some better investigation. He was not keen at all to get into the fuse box and tbh, I expected this anyway. He reckons it all needs ripped out and a new consumer unit put in and the testing done then. Of course, I agree and that's exactly what I have thought since the beginning so I have the landlord is coming round tonight after work. He's a guy I used to work with in the fire service, there's no contract and I have very low rent, very low and he's the kind of guy, like you say that will want to find his own guy or normally DIY, although I think with this he will accept it's beyond him. The thing is, it's not like I'm through an agency or anything, but I do realise it is still the law we are talking about.

So I will put forward the proposition to go halves in on replacing it and getting it tested and hopefully he is receptive to this. I can deal with it all and he doesn't need to worry and just get it done. The thing is I want it done as these storage heaters will go off soon for spring, (even though it's still been frozen last few mornings here!) and then everything will be fine and I won't know if the work has resolved the issue or not until the end of the year again. I mean if it hasn't the plan will be to move, even if rent is 2x more expensive as I can't take another winter like this.

So a voltage pen is no use on the neutrals? I need to measure current? I might just get a clamp meter then for now and test myself.

Random question, can the water in a central heating system increase a field, even if run in plastic pipe?

### Mat.I — 2025-03-23T06:02:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349162>

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349036)
>
> He reckons it will be a shared neutral with the street.

If shared with one incoming N, then it splits downstream (assuming at point of metering) this would be quite easy to confirm. Any upstream N issues will cause alternative return path issues.. This could even be caused by a Distributor Network Operator (DNO) level issue to the street or further upstream.. I'm sure they could do a Neutral integrity check (if on their supply side, then they would be the responsible party).

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349036)
>
> He changed the socket that was burnt through on the neutral, re terminated a couple of sockets, tested the sockets and reckons the neutrals are in a continuous loop and all sockets are ok (they tested ok on my socket tester too). I plugged in an appliance and the magnetic field was still huge and through the whole house, so the problem still exits on that circuit.

Red flag here re: burnt socket as it is likely tell-tale sign of overloading (assuming there was no other obvious explanation)..

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349036)
>
> So a voltage pen is no use on the neutrals? I need to measure current? I might just get a clamp meter then for now and test myself.

Voltage pen is not useful. You need the clamp meter and use it as detailed above.. You could do a multimeter however a little bit more complicated (voltage diff infers current flow) and risky & not an absolute way to measure as you need to know what you are doing and need to contact exposed parts.. **Stick with the clamp meter.. Check any bonded piping also however, be very careful of stray currents. I'm surprised the electrician did not check this quickly with a clamp meter, even a quick thermal image at your main board to check hot spots under load..**

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349036)
>
> Random question, can the water in a central heating system increase a field, even if run in plastic pipe?

Very unlikely (on the assumption metal valves or radiator or other conductive parts are not bonded to the earth/neutral link). In the case of being bonded at certain parts, then yes (depending on the configuration and extent) water in a loop can also create a minor/weak bridge of continuity between the bonded points even via PVC piping between the points that are bonded (worst in a closed loop). You could rule this out very quickly with the clamp meter..

The clamp meter will give you the info that you need.

### Mat.I — 2025-03-23T06:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349163>

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349036)
>
> So I will put forward the proposition to go halves in on replacing it and getting it tested and hopefully he is receptive to this. I can deal with it all and he doesn't need to worry and just get it done.

Rule out any upstream issues first though - e.g. from point of attachment to street and street onwards etc..

You could rule this out very quickly with the clamp meter..

Quick Update Mat.

There is no current coming in at night with the main switch isolated, yet the high magnetic field persists. I would assume now that the issue is coming from her house. I suppose it makes sense as the readings are dropping at the other side of the my house, so from say 180nT to 140nt from one side of my house to the other.

When I plug a kettle into the ring circuit in my house, it's reading 500-600nT through the whole house!! so I am now thinking there is definitely an issue with that particular storage heater circuit in her house? Would that be a sensible conclusion. So in effect, there is an issue with my ring circuit and then a separate issue coming from her house.

I have also tried a little testing using chat GPT and the clamp meter my side on that ring circuit, but can't seem to find the issue. It definitely needs further investigation so a little more time to get to the bottom of it I think.

I have a response from local environmental health officer now as well, but landlord wants to get a consumer unit in first and the old fuses away before going ahead with them. I am hoping they will investigate, or at least force her to address the issue based on safety concerns.

So bottom line is if I can't get things sorted with the neighbour by the end of the summer (as we're now getting into spring) then I'll need to move I think, which means 2x on the rent at least, but definitely can't do another winter like this.

### Saichi — 2025-03-25T14:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349295>

All certified electricians know shared neutrals are against code but none of them know there's any serious biological effect. I dealt with multiple burned out sockets as well from having shared neutrals in the house I rent and after figuring out which circuits were sharing I just keep them off permanently and use long extension cords from non-shared circuits to supply power in the living room.

A $15 high pass filter on the internet cable line will also eliminate objectionable current on it.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-25T16:32:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349314>

Turn off main breaker, do you still have high mag field? If so, it comes from the outside.
If not then turn off each circuit in the panel one at a time and measure Mag field each time.
When mag field drops you have the (an) offering circuit.
As Saichi says maybe just leave it off or turn it on only when needed.

### Mat.I — 2025-03-26T06:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349364>

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349292)
>
> Quick Update Mat.
>
> There is no current coming in at night with the main switch isolated, yet the high magnetic field persists. I would assume now that the issue is coming from her house. I suppose it makes sense as the readings are dropping at the other side of the my house, so from say 180nT to 140nt from one side of my house to the other.
>
> When I plug a kettle into the ring circuit in my house, it's reading 500-600nT through the whole house!! so I am now thinking there is definitely an issue with that particular storage heater circuit in her house? Would that be a sensible conclusion. So in effect, there is an issue with my ring circuit and then a separate issue coming from her house.
>
> I have also tried a little testing using chat GPT and the clamp meter my side on that ring circuit, but can't seem to find the issue. It definitely needs further investigation so a little more time to get to the bottom of it I think.

If I was there I would be able to locate it very quickly for you. Some ideas that come to mind (whilst being blind to a sight unseen) are below..

You must have metal conduits or water pipes that are bonded somewhere in the house??

As when you are loading with a kettle ~10amps or so, there is an issue with the return (being the neutral). This can be from inside or outside.. Easy way to look at this as your in the fire service - think about the attack line as the N conductor and you have a kink, if the onsite fire pump has a PRV; during over pressurisation, the PRV will open based on the diaphragm spring tension set point discharging the water to the drain it is plumbed to an onsite water storage tank that feeds back to the suction of the pump.. Think about the drain as the earth and anything bonded to the Neutral as the pressure relief (alternative flow path back to neutral)..

If this is not a Neutral only issue the only reasonable explanation... is the use of split single's (e.g. separated runs of actives vs neutral wiring paths).. You would be able to determine this based on a visual inspection e.g. twins + earths or presence of singles, separate entries from different areas etc.. This creates very nasty Mag fields further the separation between A & N = more severe Mag field when circuit is in use (under load).. Amperes law leading to net Mag fields etc..

Have you checked any metal conduits &/or service pipes with the clamp meter (as current should not be flowing here under normal conditions)? As this will flush out a return path issue very quickly.. Check loaded and unloaded (with power off).. When you use your kettle did you check any bonded pipes or conduits for current flow or earth point etc? You can also check for net current with clamping both the A & N at the same time when loading the circuit. Should read near zero amps if all is good. Say more than ~ 0.05 to 0.1 usually will mean the return current is taking alt paths e.g. via bonded pipes etc.. Say ~0.5/1A & above will be indicative of a serious fault condition.. E.g. larger the imbalance = stronger mag field..

Note: If you have minimal net current, this does not rule out the potential single geometry issue mentioned above..

If you supply is isolated and you are still getting Mag fields... whilst the cause is external to your house you could be getting stray return currents from others (as I assume your breaker or pull out fuse will be for the active only), thus the N connection is still active.. Either stray return currents or just being in close proximity to the issue next door. Easy to rule out with your clamp meter check your N and your bonded pipework and/or conduits with your main switch off..

I'm sure a decent electrician would be able to get to the bottom of this issue.

### Garry Kirk — 2025-03-26T12:35:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349370>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=349364)

Think about the drain as the earth and anything bonded to the Neutral as the pressure relief (alternative flow path back to neutral)..

You would be able to determine this based on a visual inspection e.g. twins + earths or presence of singles, separate entries from different areas etc.. As this will flush out a return path issue very quickly.. Check loaded and unloaded (with power off).. When you use your kettle did you check any bonded pipes or conduits for current flow or earth point etc? You can also check for net current with clamping both the A & N at the same time when loading the circuit. Should read near zero amps if all is good. Say more than ~ 0.05 to 0.1 usually will mean the return current is taking alt paths e.g. via bonded pipes etc.. Say ~0.5/1A & above will be indicative of a serious fault condition.. E.g. larger the imbalance = stronger mag field..
>
> Note: If you have minimal net current, this does not rule out the potential single geometry issue mentioned above..
>
> If you supply is isolated and you are still getting Mag fields... whilst the cause is external to your house you could be getting stray return currents from others (as I assume your breaker or pull out fuse will be for the active only), thus the N connection is still active.. Either stray return currents or just being in close proximity to the issue next door. Easy to rule out with your clamp meter check your N and your bonded pipework and/or conduits with your main switch off..
>
> I'm sure a decent electrician would be able to get to the bottom of this issue.

OK, so this ....

> If this is not a Neutral only issue the only reasonable explanation... is the use of split single's (e.g. separated runs of actives vs neutral wiring paths).. You would be able to determine this based on a visual inspection e.g. twins + earths or presence of singles, separate entries from different areas etc.. This creates very nasty Mag fields further the separation between A & N = more severe Mag field when circuit is in use (under load).. Amperes law leading to net Mag fields etc..

This seems like it could be a plausible explanation. It might also explain next door, because her house is obv metal conduit too. This seems though like a much harder fix. My house has fuses, not RCD's or RCBO's. It is metal conduit in brick walls and concrete floors and ceilings, so the conduit is everywhere and it is indeed singles, although there is a separate consumer unit which has 2 sockets off a ring circuit which I am basically using now for most things. This is wired in twin and earth, and no high mag issues there. Makes so much sense. So there are two metal conduit running into the top of the fuse box with singles running into that ring circuit from both, which I already knew, I just didn't put 2+2 together. This seems like the most obvious answer now as there's continuity in all the cables. The likelihood is they are running in separate conduit and not next to each other, but even still, would it still not cancel out? I suppose it depends..hmm

> Have you checked any metal conduits &/or service pipes with the clamp meter (as current should not be flowing here under normal conditions)? As this will flush out a return path issue very quickly.. Check loaded and unloaded (with power off).. When you use your kettle did you check any bonded pipes or conduits for current flow or earth point etc? You can also check for net current with clamping both the A & N at the same time when loading the circuit. Should read near zero amps if all is good. Say more than ~ 0.05 to 0.1 usually will mean the return current is taking alt paths e.g. via bonded pipes etc.. Say ~0.5/1A & above will be indicative of a serious fault condition.. E.g. larger the imbalance = stronger mag field..

Yes checked pipes etc, but just checked L&N tails clamped with the kettle on and I got the readings below. I did check on the other ring circuit though and also got a reading, however the other ring circuit doesn't induce high mag readings through the house. That is the circuit in T&E cabling.

> If you supply is isolated and you are still getting Mag fields... whilst the cause is external to your house you could be getting stray return currents from others (as I assume your breaker or pull out fuse will be for the active only), thus the N connection is still active.. Either stray return currents or just being in close proximity to the issue next door. Easy to rule out with your clamp meter check your N and your bonded pipework and/or conduits with your main switch off..

With mains switch isolated there is no current in either the L, N or E tails. It's all coming from next door and also decreasing with distance, not by much but it is.

### Mat.I — 2025-03-27T03:00:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shielding-ac-magnetic-field.29684/post-349419>

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349370)
>
> So there are two metal conduit running into the top of the fuse box with singles running into that ring circuit from both, which I already knew, I just didn't put 2+2 together. This seems like the most obvious answer now as there's continuity in all the cables. The likelihood is they are running in separate conduit and not next to each other, but even still, would it still not cancel out? I suppose it depends..hmm

The further the distance between the A & N conductors the worst the Mag field gets based on amperes law etc.. As typically, if side by side they will cancel each other out. When there is segregation, this will result in net Mag fields.. That is each conductor will generate their own Mag field..

Also the mention of the "ring" circuit can be very problematic due to the current flow directions.. We use radial circuits here in Aus (with end of line, no loop).. Ring is good for load, however not good for Mag fields.. You have a bit of a messy one on your hands by the sounds of it... Couldn't get much worst in terms of potential separated singles + ring circuit config.. Plus if there are bonded steel conduits, they would carry stray currents in the event of any N issues..

> [Garry Kirk said:](/goto/post?id=349370)
>
> Yes checked pipes etc, but just checked L&N tails clamped with the kettle on and I got the readings below. I did check on the other ring circuit though and also got a reading, however the other ring circuit doesn't induce high mag readings through the house. That is the circuit in T&E cabling.

You have 0.49A of net current - you may have leakage through other paths.. Rule out any capacitive coupling of faulty appliances and try another high load appliance such as a toaster & compare with different circuits etc.. Can you clamp around any water pipes & steel conduits whilst it is in use?? You can also measure A & N separately whilst under load as lower N current when compared to A is indicative of return issues (aka missing current, taking alt paths)..

A bonus you have is the double throw isolating switch for A & N..

You are likely dealing with the separation issue & N issues..

Hi Mat,

Yeah, it's not pipes and the current is similar on both L & N tails, so no drop.

It does look like a separation issue and I'm wondering if that might also be the issue next door. So this leaves me with either shielding, which theoretically could work now I know I'm shielding from the neighbour, albeit it will be costly and also something of a suck it and see, or moving at the end of the summer, or as soon as I find something suitable.

I did get a call today from environmental health and the guy didn't seem to think there was much could be done, although he hasn't came across it before and told me he'd seek advice from others and get back to me. I mean technically 200nT whilst it is destroying me, legally, I just don't think they will move on it or do anything with it. It's just a case of move or shield.

OK, well thanks for your help. It has been an education anyway and is certainly something I will be looking out for going forward as well.

Will follow up if there's any developments or I get anywhere with it.

Cheers,

G.

### Mat.I — 2025-04-01T05:53:46+0300

I mean technically 200nT whilst it is destroying me, legally, I just don't think they will move on it or do anything with it. It's just a case of move or shield.

> G.

No worries.. Some food for thought - you could use BS 7671 as the benchmark for determining the legal obligation to ensure your home is electrically safe under the Housing Act? maybe? You would think the local Council could issue a type of rectification or investigative order to the property owner? There should be sections in the standard that prescribes certain requirements whether in a qualitative or the like manner it should be focused on minimising net Mag fields as result of minimizing conductor separation and taking the most direct path and so on etc..

What about the Health Protection Agency in the UK? Can you determine the trigger for nuisance levels e.g. - [https://www.gov.uk/government/publi...tric-and-magnetic-fields-sources-and-exposure](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/electric-and-magnetic-fields-sources-and-exposure/electric-and-magnetic-fields-sources-and-exposure)

Extract below -

## _What are the exposure levels in the home?_​

_Magnetic fields are measured in a unit called tesla. A tesla is a very big unit and the fields usually encountered from power supplies in the UK are measured in millionths of a tesla or microtesla (µT).

The background level measured away from appliances usually comes from the local electricity distribution cables which supply the house. The typical background magnetic field level in homes is usually in the range 0.01 to 0.2 µT. The highest magnetic fields in homes usually occur close to electrical appliances and the electrical wiring, even if it is inside the walls. Magnetic fields of up to a few tens of microteslas can occur very close to appliances. In homes within a few tens of metres of large power lines, magnetic field levels are typically a few microteslas but under the largest lines may sometimes reach up to a few tens of microteslas.

By way of contrast, the static magnetic field produced by planet Earth is roughly 50 µT in the UK._

Based on the above it could be reasonable to set the upper exposure limit at 0.2uT = 200nT - **This alone should give Council some grounds to take action..**

Also , there may be grounds under the Environmental Protection Act 1990 under section 79 as an Statutory Nuisance (electrical interference) as they can be interfering with sleep & well being etc.. Council will be required to investigate this issue if it is affecting the health or the use and enjoyment of property etc.. E.g. sleep disturbances, headaches, fatigue, discomfort could all be framed as impact on health and well being..

I deal with Councils on a regular basis.. Sometime you have to spoon feed them and wrap it up in a bow, then they must act on it.. Especially, if you have clean ties back to legislation and make a solid argument on this..

Good luck mate and all the best..

# Thread: PHOTOKERATITIS - Please Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photokeratitis-please-help.29692/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evan McDonald — 2024-10-27T02:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photokeratitis-please-help.29692/post-341570>

Hello fellow mitochondriacs,

After learning about the negative consequences of sunglasses I stopped wearing them for about 2 months, so I was suddenly exposing my eyes to lots more UV than normal. (Had been wearing dark sunglassss when outside in bright sun for past 3-4 years). Also at this time I started spending even more time outside during the day.

Then a couple times while driving with windows down I came around a corner and caught a glance directly into the sun with my left eye. (Also caught glances into the sun a couple other times over the span of about 3 weeks but this was the final and worse time)

Ever since then my left cornea has been hurting, and any exposure to UV or intense blue light makes it hurt worse, and causes damage to the those corneal cell layers I assume.

So in reaction I’ve been hiding in the darkness in my pitch-black bedroom to let the cornea heal.

And when I do have to venture out into the light I’m wearing Oakley snowboarding goggles that block 100% UV light and 95% of visible light, and keeping my eye closed as much as possible. Because if I don’t the left eye hurts worse.

So I’ve been spending as much time as possible in darkness, and have seen progress with healing. I would estimate it’s about 80-90% healed.

My questions for y’all:
\- Have any of you dealt with Photokeratitis and successfully healed from it?
\- Is there anything I can be doing to speed recovery?
\- Is my strategy of spending as much time in darkness as possible the right thing to do?

About me:
\- Ancestry: Scotch-Irish, French, & German.
\- Brown hair, blue eyes, pale skin.
\- Living the mitochondriac lifestyle: clean diet, watch every sunrise/sunset, get as much sunlight as possible, no artificial light after dark, limit nnEMFs.
\- Sleep grounded, also spend as much time barefoot as possible.
\- living in Oak Hill, Austin, Texas.
\- grew up on a farm in Fannin County, Northeast Texas.

Considering diet:
\- I’m eating zero-carb carnivore.
\- mainly organ blend ground beef, steak, eggs, liver, and salmon.
\- using tallow and olive oil for cooking & finishing oils respectively.
\- everything grass-fed/organic/wild caught.

I’m feeling a bit lonely and depressed from having to spend so much time alone in my dark room, so any advice to heal quickly and get out of this situation is greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

P.S.

If this is the wrong part of the forum for this question please let me know so I can move it

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photokeratitis-please-help.29692/post-341610>

Additional question:

Should I be exposing my cornea cells to a bit of UV light each day (sunrise/sunset), despite the pain, to help stimulate repair of those cells?

How to protect your [#Bitcoin](https://x.com/hashtag/Bitcoin?src=hashtag_click) profits WITHOUT ever selling your [Bitcoin](https://x.com/hashtag/Bitcoin?src=hashtag_click) ever again.

Firstly - bookmark this post, it'll will have major value around Q2 next year.

**Look at this example:**
1\. Buy [Bitcoin](https://x.com/hashtag/Bitcoin?src=hashtag_click) @ say $73,000.
2\. Let it appreciate to say $300,000.
3\. Buy Puts on IBIT (when they are launched) at $300,000 with following math: Profit: $227,000. 10% of profits allocated to Puts: $22,700.

4\. **Scenario 1** - Bitcoin collapses down to 200W moving average - say $80,000. Watch the $22,700 in Puts ride up to $150,000 - $200,000 in profit. SELL the Puts and collect the cash.

**Scenario 2** \- [Bitcoin](https://x.com/hashtag/Bitcoin?src=hashtag_click) hits S Curve and America announces Strategic National Reserve buying 1 Million Bitcoin and Bitcoin goes vertical to say $1m and stabilizes.

You lose $22,700 in the Puts you bought.

But your [#Bitcoin](https://x.com/hashtag/Bitcoin?src=hashtag_click) is now worth $930,000. You're hedged both ways. AND YOU NEVER EVER NEED TO SELL YOUR BITCOIN.

And it works in both ways if you hold ETFs or Cold Storage. You can always just buy Puts in a brokerage account while having the Bitcoin in your cold storage.

Bookmark this post.

# Thread: Long Covid Hell - Looking for opinions / support / ideas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-hell-looking-for-opinions-support-ideas.29718/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Longcovidhelp — 2024-10-30T20:06:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-hell-looking-for-opinions-support-ideas.29718/post-341759>

Hello Everyone - I am dealing with the fight of my life to help save my wife and wanted introduce myself and share my story - would love ideas or to hear any success stories if anyone is dealing with something similar or knows of someone who is.

I am the husband of a severely ill wife who has been essentially bedbound for about two years with what we consider "Long Covid / Long Vax". Prior to June '22, my wife was in particularly excellent shape physically with a clean sheet of paper for a medical history. She ran 6-8 miles about 3x / wk and also did weights/HIIT as this was her favorite hobby. In April 2022 she and my two sons (5 & 7 at the time) got a covid infection. The boys got through it quickly, and my wife felt bad for a couple days with a fever then it was gone. She gave it about 1.5-2 weeks before she resumed her normal fitness routine. During a run she came home and said he actually needed to stop during the run due to chest pain and overall tiredness. This was something she NEVER did during her runs as she was able to run a 1/2 marathon with no issue. That was a red flag. Fast forward to about a month later, and she contracted a vaginal infection which was treated with antibiotics (Metronidazole - topical i believe). She had a reaction to this. Everything seemed to decline from here. She began having a racing heart which lead us to the ER twice. Cardio Dr said they thought it was dysautonomia and "we are learning more about post covid syndrome." No help to speak of. Tests were all "normal." Through personal relationships we were able to see a cardio doctor at Vanderbilt who specializes in dysautonomia / autonomic dysfunction. They were no help - They said to "drink water with salt in it" and "wear compression socks." We researched a stellate ganglion block and ended up trying that. It did nothing and triggered tinnitus. At that point, we made the decision to relocate our family to Texas to be closer to my wife's side of the family. This has completely flipped our lives upside down. We cannot find any answers and are making no progress with my wife's symptoms - She cannot drive a car, can barely walk (we use a wheelchair for any distance longer than 100' or so), showering is very difficult for her, basic kitchen tasks are not impossible for her, standing longer than 1 minute is impossible, the muscle definition she built over several years is wasting away, she never feels rested in the morning, and any sort of activity with our boys is impossible for her so needless to say her mental state is in a bad place.

She was vaxed twice - First with J&J (single), and moderna as a booster. In retrospect, neither of us would have gotten the vax had we known then what we know now. Looking back, there were signs my wife was affected negatively from the vax that she didn't share with me but did after the fact. It appears the shots compromised her immune system (among other systems) and the infection itself took her down. She has a positive ANA Test from a rheumatologist which points to auto-immunity. We know her spike antibodies are over 9,000 on the last test we did which means her body is full of spike protein. She has body pain ever day, hot/cold hands and feet, muscle loss, very little energy. When she does "push it" it sets her back for many days or even weeks. This is not someone who never pushed herself physically - quite the opposite. So I know it's not an issue of her trying to stay active - Her body is broken. We know she has microclots from fluorescence microscope testing done. We have seen the following practices - Dr. Peter McCullough, Dr. Pierre Korey, Dr. Jordan Vaughn among other conventional doctors in large healthcare systems. We have spent tens of thousands of dollars, and hundreds of hours.

I am at a dead end and losing hope, as is my wife. Grateful for any feedback, ideas, guidance, opinions, etc. Sorry for the long post! Thank you all.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-30T20:51:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-hell-looking-for-opinions-support-ideas.29718/post-341761>

Blogs answered it. Read them.

Hey everyone, first post here. My name is Tom 37 years young and I’m from the Netherlands, been following Jack for a while now but since the Thetragrammothon podcast i have been diving into this some more. I have some questions. I have been reading a lot on this forum now, and I do have to admit I don’t know a lot yet about quantum biology but I am trying to learn. I also have to admit its very hard to understand the things Jack says on X but ill get there..

Here’s the deal, I’m a welder now for almost 20 years and I want to know how bad this is for my health. I mean I know it is bad but what are we talking about?
I get up at 05:15 every day during the week I go to work at 06:30, I get in there and we have full blown led lights in the factory (probably 6500K) so my best guess is that is noon to me all day long untill 16:30. Besides that the factory is filled with very heavy blue light (uvb) from welding all day long. Another thing is the magnetic field that gets created by the wires that I hold during the day wile welding. And the last thing besides breathing in a lot of toxins, I touch a lot of metal that gets electrified while welding.

Already got Type1 diabetes 12years ago probably because of to much blue light and not enough sunlight.
And right now I’m struggling a bit with fybromyalgia.

I know this is a lot and I do know it’s bad but can someone tell me how bad?
Think I’m ready for the answer, maybe it’s the last push to make me quit this bullshit...

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-31T02:26:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-financing-and-the-collateral-effects-of-the-fiat-standard-by-the-boe.29723/post-341777>

The **Balfour Declaration** was a public statement issued by the [British Government](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Government) in 1917 during the [First World War](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_World_War) announcing its support for the establishment of a "national home for the Jewish people" in [Palestine](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palestine_\(region\)), then an [Ottoman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ottoman_Empire) region with a small minority [Jewish](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jewish) population. The declaration was contained in a letter dated 2 November 1917 from the United Kingdom's [Foreign Secretary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary_of_State_for_Foreign_Affairs_\(UK\)) [Arthur Balfour](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arthur_Balfour) to [Lord Rothschild](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walter_Rothschild,_2nd_Baron_Rothschild), a leader of the [British Jewish community](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Jews), for transmission to the [Zionist Federation of Great Britain and Ireland](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zionist_Federation_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland). The text of the declaration was published in the press on 9 November 1917.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-31T02:35:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-financing-and-the-collateral-effects-of-the-fiat-standard-by-the-boe.29723/post-341778>

Immediately following Britain's declaration of war on the [Ottoman Empire](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ottoman_Empire) in November 1914, it began to consider the future of Palestine. Within two months [a memorandum](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Future_of_Palestine) was circulated to the [War Cabinet](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_War_Cabinet) by a Zionist member, [Herbert Samuel](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herbert_Samuel,_1st_Viscount_Samuel), proposing the support of Zionist ambitions in order to enlist the support of Jews in the wider war. [A committee](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/De_Bunsen_Committee) was established in April 1915 by [British Prime Minister](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_the_United_Kingdom) [H. H. Asquith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H._H._Asquith) to determine their policy towards the Ottoman Empire including Palestine. Asquith, who had favoured post-war reform of the Ottoman Empire, resigned in December 1916; his replacement [David Lloyd George](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Lloyd_George) favored partition of the Empire. The first negotiations between the British and the [Zionists](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zionism) took place at a conference on 7 February 1917 that included [Sir Mark Sykes](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Sykes) and the Zionist leadership. Subsequent discussions led to Balfour's request, on 19 June, that Rothschild and [Chaim Weizmann](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaim_Weizmann) submit a draft of a public declaration. Further drafts were discussed by the British Cabinet during September and October, with input from Zionist and [anti-Zionist](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anti-Zionism) Jews but with no representation from the local population in Palestine.

By late 1917, in the lead-up to the Balfour Declaration, the wider world war had reached a stalemate, with two of [Britain's allies](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Allies_of_World_War_I) not fully engaged: the United States had yet to suffer a casualty, and the Russians were in the midst of a [revolution](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/October_Revolution) with [Bolsheviks](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bolsheviks) taking over the government which ws fomented by the Zionists. Trotsky had the Tsar killed when we found out that the Rothschilds in the BoE were behind the request to have the Tsar imprisoned in Siberia. A [stalemate in southern Palestine](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stalemate_in_Southern_Palestine) was broken by the [Battle of Beersheba](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Beersheba_\(1917\)) on 31 October 1917. The release of the final declaration was authorised on 31 October; the preceding Cabinet discussion had referenced perceived propaganda benefits amongst the worldwide Jewish community for the Allied war effort.

The opening words of the declaration represented the first public expression of support for Zionism by a major political power. The term "national home" had no precedent in international law, and was intentionally vague as to whether a [Jewish state](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jewish_state) was contemplated. The intended boundaries of Palestine were not specified, and the British government later confirmed that the words "in Palestine" meant that the Jewish national home was not intended to cover all of Palestine. The second half of the declaration was added to satisfy opponents of the policy, who had claimed that it would otherwise prejudice the position of the local population of Palestine and encourage [antisemitism](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antisemitism) worldwide by "stamping the Jews as strangers in their native lands". The declaration called for safeguarding the civil and religious rights for the [Palestinian Arabs](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palestinian_Arabs), who composed the [vast majority of the local population](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographic_history_of_Palestine_\(region\)), and also the rights and political status of the Jewish communities in other countries outside of Palestine. The British government acknowledged in 1939 that the local population's wishes and interests should have been taken into account, and recognized in 2017 that the declaration should have called for the protection of the Palestinian Arabs' political rights.

The declaration had many long-lasting consequences. It greatly increased popular support for [Zionism](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zionism) within [Jewish communities worldwide](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jewish_diaspora), and became a core component of the [British Mandate for Palestine](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandate_for_Palestine), the founding document of [Mandatory Palestine](https://forum.jackkruse.com/safari-reader%3A//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandatory_Palestine).
