### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-12T16:49:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ww1-financing-and-the-collateral-effects-of-the-fiat-standard-by-the-boe.29723/post-342255>

The Trading with the Enemy Act of 1917 is still in force. This Act established the Office of Alien Property Custodian to manage the property of US enemies (Germany in WW1), such as patents filed in the US by German nationals. The US government office was intended to manage that intellectual property in the US interest during the WW1.

The Emergency Banking Act of 1933 was a bill passed in the midst of the Great Depression that took steps to stabilize and restore confidence in the U.S. banking system. It came in the wake of a series of bank runs following the stock market crash of 1929. Among its major KEY measures, the Act created the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), which began insuring bank accounts at no cost for up to $2,500.

THIS ONE IS REALLY KEY: HOW ONE ENDS THE FED IS BURIED IN THIS LAW: The president was given executive power to operate independently of the Federal Reserve during times of financial crisis. I have a deep sense this is a honey pot for Trump, Lutnick, and Fink in the next few years.

# Thread: My sleep is not good

### CommonWitness — 2024-11-01T23:30:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-341864>

Hey guys,

I began my Mitochondriac journey nearly three months ago.
I have been following the leptin prescription as a diet and had been going really well, healthy weight, looking and feeling good, etc

But I have run into a serious disruption to my capacity to stay on this track, which is my sleep.
I seem to be affected by a seasonal perturbation whereby I can't sleep beyond 4-5 hours a night and certainly not past 4am. I wake and have a high heart rate and my mind is going a million miles a minute. It's been like this for maybe 3 weeks.
\- I see the sunrise every morning
\- block as much blue light throughout the day and night as possible
\- don't eat after nightfall and rarely less than 3 hours before bed
I put these in to say, my circadian is pretty good. Plus I have been significantly less attuned and eating garbage and my sleep has been better.
It has affected me sleeping on a spare bed in someone else's house, in my van, on a blow up mattress on the floor or with my partner currently in our cottage.

I have been affected by this at various times in my life and seemingly happens around this time of year (in australia its early spring) and may also happen with the transition of other seasons.
It always blindsides me though, so I don't track or expect it. Potentially it is more than once a year that this hits me. When I am in it, I am reeling from the lack of sleep while also doing my best to stay healthy, work my jobs and maintain the relationships I have, so I can't clearly decipher timing, length of bout, how much or how little sleep I am actually getting, etc.

What is going on? Has anyone else experienced this kind of thing?

Thanks in advance, also apologies for the way it is written. I am not functioning amazingly well right now.

### CommonWitness — 2024-11-02T23:53:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-341892>

So this morning I woke up and was able to check the time eventually seeing that it was 3:18am.
I then was able to rest yet remained always floating on the surface of sleep, not going deep.
This means that today I am feeling less debilitated by it but my focus and energy are still somewhat below par.

I implemented having a cold tub for about 3 minutes and then a cold shower, ten or so minutes before bed. This seemed to help keep me calm when I woke up, I didn't have the racing heart rate like I usually do.

The thing that is confusing the shit out of me and also why I will continue to update with further details because I could really use someone willing to spend a bit of time on this topic with me; I spend a night or two a week sleeping in a tent, in a country town, on a property and near a road that don't have street lights, far enough away from the city that there is no light pollution. I keep my tent flap open so as to be as cold possible and to be as connected to the elements as possible.
I bring this up because my sleep is interrupted at more or less the same time even when I'm there.

I was having good sleep for the first two months with a new routine of seeing the sunrise, eating before nightfall and going to bed 4 to 5 hours after I ate. Then it just slipped away.

\- Yeast and fungal infections are common. (I swear if I could wash in my own pee I would, with how effective peeing on my feet became for treating tinea)

### eve001 — 2024-11-12T12:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-342244>

Hi there, How's it going?
Have you measured both LF and RF/HF nnEMF fields? (Low frequency and Radio fequency/High frequency)
Since I reduced my LF to a still subomtimal level my sleep did improve.
I went from 2000-4000mV body voltage to about 200mV.
Builing biologist recommend <10mV ideally but as it is so rare <100mV is to be really happy with.
I am still above 100mV and sleep improved a lot.
Myself only recently am also measuring LF magnetic fields with a cheap old meter somebody borrowed me.
It gives one an idea
LF magnetic are okay in my bedroom but may affect me at some computers.
However, I find they are hard to distinguish from EMI and LF electric sometimes for me so far.
Fields change over time as neighbours get new tech (solar panels, electric cars, smart meters, safety cameras etc.) so one has to re-measure regularly. Also indoors our own tech may sometimes switch wifi/bluetooth ON again with updates etc.
Some say they only improved from nnEMF overdose after also choosing their water more carefully.
Some do better on low copper/higher zinc food diet.
Magnesium salt baths seem to help me - I do not tolerate oral magnesium supplements well.
Reading some older blogs is helpful, I agree
Thumbs up!

### Jhack — 2024-11-12T19:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-342259>

> [CommonWitness said:](/goto/post?id=341985)
>
> When I am in very distinct environments and when this isn't my constant sleeping pattern but something that has arisen, seemingly seasonally (is that a thing? Can anyone tell me if that is a fucking thing?) - I just can't see what I am doing wrong, how my environment is dictating this.
>
> I'm now only getting 2-3 hours of sleep a night. Its getting worse and I need some help.
>
> I dream when I sleep, yeah. I remember them unless I had them at between 10pm and 12am and then tossed around hoping to sleep for the next 6 hours.
> When I sleep well, I am in the one position.

STRESS…whether it’s consciously or unconsciously. If you are doing all the right things for your body (sunrise, sunset, CT, etc.) there is something being overlooked. What I have found with myself is It’s usually stress. I went from being a type A, high functioning, everything has to be done a certain way, highly stressed (stuck in sympathetic nervous system overdrive) individual who had insomnia for years, all kinds of different symptoms that I would just take a pill for (doing what the doc’s said) to becoming incredibly sick with “C” that turned into “LC”. It’s been over 2 years and I have done so much work to get to where I’m at now (which is still not very healthy). But after tons and tons of practicing mindfulness and meditation every single day along with all of the things I have learned from following Jack Kruse, I can now fall asleep and stay asleep without having to medicate myself every night. And with all of that being said, the one thing I notice is if I get stressed even the slightest it disrupts my sleep. For me, it seems that’s always the first thing to be affected. So now if I have a problem sleeping, I know that’s my morning sign to figure out what has me stressed and to fix that. And like I said, sometimes I don’t even know that I’m stressed because I have been able to calm down my sympathetic nervous system to where it’s nothing like it was before. Meaning, I am way more relaxed, I don’t react and stress over little things, I am calm, I go with the flow, and realize everything doesn’t have to go my way, and I don’t have to be responsible for and fix everything. Just sit back, meditate and try to notice any stress that may be present in your life. I hope this helps. I know it helps me. Coming from someone who has dealt with insomnia for the better part of 15 years. Good luck.

### Jhack — 2024-11-13T00:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-342276>

> [CommonWitness said:](/goto/post?id=341985)
>
> When I am in very distinct environments and when this isn't my constant sleeping pattern but something that has arisen, seemingly seasonally (is that a thing? Can anyone tell me if that is a fucking thing?) - I just can't see what I am doing wrong, how my environment is dictating this.
>

> When I sleep well, I am in the one position.

> [CommonWitness said:](/goto/post?id=341985)
>
> When I am in very distinct environments and when this isn't my constant sleeping pattern but something that has arisen, seemingly seasonally (is that a thing? Can anyone tell me if that is a fucking thing?) - I just can't see what I am doing wrong, how my environment is dictating this.
>
> I'm now only getting 2-3 hours of sleep a night. Its getting worse and I need some help.
>
> I dream when I sleep, yeah. I remember them unless I had them at between 10pm and 12am and then tossed around hoping to sleep for the next 6 hours.
> When I sleep well, I am in the one position.

I am only speaking from my own experience here, but yes “seasonal patterns” are definitely “a thing”! It’s just like everything else with our bodies. I’m not even going to attempt to try to explain how our neuronal pathways are formed which creates our beliefs, or actions, the way we do things, but it’s all definitely based on pathways and patterns. Just like we form routines intentionally, we also form them without even realizing it.
For example, I lost my mom from a car accident 28 years ago in the month of April. I was 21, of course it had a horrible effect on me. And of course I remember that date. But regardless of if I am thinking about it or not, it never fails I always get depressed and in a funk during the month of April. It took me awhile to realize why, especially when I started noticing the depression sometimes before I even realized that it was the month of April around when I lost my mom. Our bodies store memories and I believe they become cyclical just like changing of seasons. Another example for instance is feeling a certain way such as wanting change in my life. From journaling, I have noticed a lot of feelings like this will happen around the same time each year.
So yes, even if you feel like everything is good in your life at this time and nothing has changed such as your environment, etc. it’s still can be something from your past that happened around this time/season from your past, that is causing your insomnia. Hence, uncle Jack’s comment about it being in your environment. This may be physically, consciously or subconsciously.

### digitald3 — 2024-11-13T23:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-342324>

To piggy back on the stress idea... to answer the question about nnEMF.. the answer is zero. but for some like my wife and your partner it doesn't seem to be causing a problem that they know of right now... If you have AC power in your sleeping quarters, that power is running at 50 or 60 hz depending on where you are located.. the DC electric current in humans runs at 100hz.. this means that the body is sensing a frequency non native to what it needs to run... this can create stress in the cells... you really have to get the tools to test to know! once you are able to test and remove the nnemf.. The body voltage meter from Livesafeemf.com is a great tool to find out!

### digitald3 — 2024-11-14T00:20:53+0200

I currently know of 5 people directly that have tried to solve this through surgical/centralized approaches... to normalize the skeleton to enable one to breathe... all 5 showed zero improvement after spending thousands of dollars and side effects of the therapies... I was one of them a few short years ago that thought the skeleton mattered more than environment and circadian biology... I know its super frustrating to try and solve.. there is no magic fix... something in your environment is not allowing you to sleep.. my recommendation is to go on vacation to a low latitude and spend every day on the beach from sun up to sun down with zero lights on at night...

### Novah — 2024-11-23T08:47:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-sleep-is-not-good.29735/post-342825>

Hey Ryan,

Another thing to consider is your pajama and bedding material - Is there any polyester or synthetic fabric in your pajamas or bedding? That stuff can be really disruptive to sleep.

Are you using 100% cotton or linen sheets? And for breathability, cotton sheets in the 200-400 thread count range are recommended. Closer to 200 is better if you sleep hot.

If you use a mattress cover, is it polyester or a polyester cross? If so, try replacing with a 100% cotton one. They're harder to find, and you have to carefully read the labels, but they're out there.

Getting rid of synthetic bedding materials can reduce or/and eliminate hot flashes and restless sleep in women (I know that doesn't apply to you, but it shows how important and impactful bedding material is).

And I'm assuming your bedroom is cool and dark with no light coming in while you sleep?

Not sure if any of this is applicable to you, but a few things to consider.

I've noticed in low UV times of the year I have a harder time sleeping. I cut any stimulants out completely, am outside for sunrise every day (and all day as much as possible) freezing my ass off no matter what, and also eat a small mostly protein meal 3-4 hours before bed (<200 cal). I might do a can or 2 of sardines and some bone broth. I think low blood sugar is what has been waking me up and that seems to reduce the frequency of waking up.

I live in a very low nnEMF environment currently fwiw.

# Thread: THE TRANSFER AGREEMENT WAS THE FINAL SOLUTION
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-transfer-agreement-was-the-final-solution.29742/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-03T21:58:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-transfer-agreement-was-the-final-solution.29742/post-341905>

Here is a detailed summary of "The Transfer Agreement" by Edwin Black:

\*\*Introduction and Context:\*\*
\- The book delves into an economic agreement made in the early 1930s between Nazi Germany and the Zionist Federation of Germany, which facilitated the transfer of German Jews and their capital to Palestine.

\*\*Key Points:\*\*

1\. \*\*Background:\*\*
\- \*\*Economic Boycott:\*\* Initially, in response to the increasing anti-Semitic measures in Germany, Jewish organizations worldwide called for an economic boycott of German goods. This was seen as a way to pressure Germany economically to stop its persecution of Jews.

\- \*\*Nazi Economic Strategy:\*\* However, Nazi Germany needed to find a way to stabilize its economy amidst international boycotts. The Transfer Agreement was part of this strategy, allowing Germany to export goods in exchange for Jewish capital leaving Germany, thus bypassing the boycott.

2\. \*\*The Agreement:\*\*
\- \*\*Negotiations:\*\* The agreement, officially known as the Haavara (Transfer) Agreement, was negotiated in 1933 between the Jewish Agency for Palestine, represented by Haim Arlosoroff, and the German authorities.

\- \*\*Mechanics:\*\* Under the agreement, Jewish emigrants could transfer some of their assets out of Germany in the form of German export goods. They would deposit money into a German bank, which would then be used to buy German-made agricultural tools, building materials, and other goods necessary for establishing life in Palestine.

\- \*\*Benefits for Jews:\*\* Jews could escape Nazi Germany with some of their wealth, albeit in kind rather than in cash. This allowed them to start anew in Palestine.

\- \*\*Benefits for Germany:\*\* Germany could continue trade and receive foreign currency through the sale of these goods abroad, thus alleviating some of the effects of the boycott.

3\. \*\*Controversy and Criticism:\*\*
\- \*\*Moral and Political Debate:\*\* The agreement was highly controversial. Critics argued that it indirectly supported the Nazi regime by providing an economic outlet at a time when economic pressure might have been used to alter Nazi policies.

\- \*\*Zionist Justification:\*\* Zionists argued that saving Jewish lives and building the Jewish homeland were paramount, even if it meant dealing with the Nazis. They believed that without this agreement, many Jews would have been left penniless in Palestine or stuck in Nazi Germany.

\- \*\*Impact on Boycott Efforts:\*\* The agreement weakened the international boycott against Nazi Germany, as some Jewish organizations and communities were torn between supporting the boycott and the economic benefits for emigrating Jews.

4\. \*\*Long-term Effects:\*\*
\- \*\*Economic Boost for Palestine:\*\* The influx of capital and goods helped to develop the Jewish economy in Palestine, setting a foundation for later state-building efforts.

\- \*\*Nazi Propaganda:\*\* The Nazis used the agreement as propaganda to suggest that Jews were supporting the German economy, thereby undermining anti-Nazi sentiment abroad.

\- \*\*Historical Debate:\*\* The Transfer Agreement remains a point of historical controversy, reflecting the complex moral decisions made during desperate times. It has been debated regarding its role in either facilitating Jewish emigration or potentially aiding the Nazi regime economically.

\*\*Conclusion:\*\*
\- Black's book explores not just the mechanics of the agreement but also the human stories, the political intrigue, and the moral dilemmas faced by all parties involved. It examines how economic strategies intertwined with political survival and the broader implications for Jewish history and the development of Israel.

"The Transfer Agreement" provides a nuanced look at a lesser-known aspect of pre-World War II Jewish history, highlighting the complexities of survival, economic strategy, and political maneuvering in a time of rising fascism.

# Thread: Incandescent vs flicker-free & warm LEDs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 150
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### UncensoringSoftwareDev — 2024-11-04T00:11:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-341907>

Hi,
I'm new here after listening to a few interviews with Dr. Jack Kruse. My current understanding is that fluorescent lights are terrible & that most LEDs are, too. However, I listened to an episode of the Wise Traditions podcast by the Weston A Price Foundation where the guest talked about flicker-free, warm light (low to no blue light) LEDs being a great, healthy option due to avoiding the issues of most LEDs while also being way more energy efficient than incandescents.
I also can't find any information online about Jack's opinions on those types of LEDs.
If I want healthy, indoor lighting, are the better LEDs a good option? Or are incandescents so much better that I should go with them since they also supply a small amount of UV light?
Thanks

### robtherhino — 2024-11-04T11:20:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-341921>

I'd start from optimal and work back from there.

The three things you want to manage for are:

1\. Spectrum of light (no blue, and block green as well)
2\. Intensity / brightness (under 10 LUX).
3\. Location / height (below eye level).

Optimal is no artificial light after sunset.
After that would be red LEDs (below eye level and sub 10 LUX)

I'm not sure how to rank incandescent / candles / firelight / blue free LEDs.

\- Candles and firelight still give off a small amount of blue and green. They also flicker which can be tiring for the brain and stimulating for the nervous system. But generally they are quite low in LUX.

\- Incandescents also have some blue and green, though way less than "warm" LEDS. You'd need to make sure that these are below eye level an positioned in a way to be under 10 LUX.

\- Blue-free and "warm LEDs" are not the same thing, so watch out there. And again, you'd need to make sure that these are below eye level an positioned in a way to be under 10 LUX.

The best thing to do is to assess what is the minimum lighting you need after sunset and make a plan from there.

My wife and I have the following:
\- A "blue free" amber bulb in the kitchen and a downward facing light from the extraction fan.
\- A red plug-in LED in the bedroom.
\- x1 portable red USB light
\- x2 portable amber USB lights.

We also used orange blue blockers after dark.

But if you manage for those three items I mentioned above, you'll be doing pretty good.

LEDs will always be worse.

Incandescent bulbs are

— 100 CRI
— Low Flicker
— Emits _INFRARED_
— Minimal Blue Light
— Continuous Spectrum
— Low EMF (Dirty Electricity)
— 2700K Color Temperature

> 3\. Location / height (below eye level).

> After that would be red LEDs (below eye level and sub 10 LUX)
>

>
> \- Incandescents also have some blue and green, though way less than "warm" LEDS. You'd need to make sure that these are below eye level an positioned in a way to be under 10 LUX.
>
> \- Blue-free and "warm LEDs" are not the same thing, so watch out there. And again, you'd need to make sure that these are below eye level an positioned in a way to be under 10 LUX.

The flicker of firelight is due to movement and isn't the same as when that word is used to describe what happens with artificial lighting and screens i don't think.
sunlight > firelight > incandescent > LEDs imo. I also remember Dr Kruse mentioning so far he's unable to find any negative to using UV at in the evening. In a perfectly natural environment we'd still get most frequencies of light at very low intensities so I go for a shaded incandescent and fire. I just default to what is most "natural"

### Daulatwant — 2024-11-04T18:30:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-341931>

I bought a dozen 40w incandescent from china. The decorative ones with the long filament. These appear to be as dim as standard 25w incandescent. I've had these bulbs for 4 years not a single one has burnt out, meanwhile standard incandescents burn out within a year or much sooner.

I think it has to do with how the filament is designed, standard incandescent have very short filament winding that runs very hot, 40w decorative incandescent have a very long filament that dissipates the heat much more effectively, I can grip and hold the 40w bulb after it has fully warmed up, while standard 25w incandescents will burn the skin almost instantly after warmed up.

### Daulatwant — 2024-11-04T18:38:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-341932>

I've failed to find any incandescents that are actually made with red or orange glass. All the colored bulbs are just a regular bulb with some colored paint on them, and they suck at actually filtering the light and at best only tint the light. And every bulb I've tried the color coating starts burning off the moment you turn it on.

I have not tried the red reptile bulbs, because those start at 75w and that is far too powerful.

### Daulatwant — 2024-11-18T14:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-342568>

Since first posting here I tinkered around a bit. Those are 5W 12V car bulbs. And I used a 6V power brick, so it is only drawing 1.5W of power. This is just enough to navigate my room at night, and probably even read straight under it.

Such low power bulb allowed me to use a plastic candle holder as a light filter.

Underpowering the 12V automotive bulbs seems to be a viable strategy to solve the problems I ran into initially. 21W is definitely far too much for a such a small format bulb, and for nighttime it's far too much light.

Trying the colored bulbs was a waste of time, and it set me back. These 5W bulbs solve the heat problem that was holding me back. I can now attempt to make an LCD backlight for a TV or a monitor using them(which is why I started playing around with these) and just add some plastic film filters instead of the crappy coating on the bulb.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-342701>

I already tried extra diffusion, didn't do much, pulling away the diffusion 2-3 inches does make it nice and even. But making that gap is a fabrication nightmare.

I played around with some foil and reflecting the light going to the sides back up to the diffuser produced the most promising effect so far. Gonna glue some tape on the back of aluminium foil and make some reflective cones to put around the bulbs.

With these 5W bulbs, they are small enough and heat is low enough, it opened many possibilities.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-11-28T16:24:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-343033>

> [Paul T008 said:](/goto/post?id=343032)
>
> I
>
> I found that a lamp Dimmer from amazon , i reduce the power , to extend the life of the filament bulb , Not sure how measure flicker though cant see any

Most dimmer lamps create large amounts of “DE”.
ALL 2 prong lamps put out a large 50/60Hz electric field 6 - 8 feet is all directions. They need to ground if close to you.
Flicker - Put your phone on slow motion video, take a 1 sec video and play back (it will play for 4 secs) this gives a good idea of flicker.

Incandescents can have very high flicker, every bulb regardless of type or marketing has to be measured.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-12-13T06:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-343815>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=343813)
>
> Incandescents can have very high flicker, every bulb regardless of type or marketing has to be measured.

No. Incandescent light bulbs do not flicker
Incandescent lamps have a lot of thermal inertia. Because they are hot metal, the filament can't heat up and cool down 120 times per second, so they don't flicker.

### [What invisible flicker do different types of light bulbs have?](https://physics.stackexchange.com/questions/13400/what-invisible-flicker-do-different-types-of-light-bulbs-have)

What waveform does the light coming off different types of artificial light sources have and (of course) what are the underlying physical reason for them? I was inspired to ask this question becaus...

 physics.stackexchange.com

The only time you'll see an incandescent light bulb flicker is when it's about to blow and it's time to replace it...

### Saichi — 2024-12-21T18:55:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-344215>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=343815)

I've purchased new incandescents and have measured flicker as high as 130% which is higher than any other bulb I've measured.

I've measured countless bulbs over the years including incandescents and the lowest flicker an incandescent can have is around 8% although a good one averages 10 to 12%. The LED bulbs I use are under 1%. LED bulbs also have to be measured for DE since low flicker ones can emit hundreds of mV into the circuit and have audible radius in the air well beyond three feet from the bulb.

### Chlo_e — 2024-12-23T10:58:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-344274>

> [Jingpaw said:](/goto/post?id=344251)
>
> Do you know if the candle- like setting blue and green light free? I sent him a message before asking about night time mode and didn’t hear back

I had a call with him last year and he said the night-light feature has no blue and either no green or very little green. Try reaching out to him on linked in or something if he's not picking up emails quickly

Edit (from his website) - "NIRA near-infrared enhanced LED lighting offers a simple solution to blue light overexposure for you and your family. The unique hybrid design of NIRA bulbs enables you to decrease the brightness and change the color continuously from bright daylight levels to the soft orange glow of an old fashioned incandescent bulb. The NIRA bulb emits near-infrared energy throughout the various levels of brightness"

This is in line with what he informed me- that the night-light feature is comparable to an incandescent without any flicker. No dimmer required if using the lamp

### boerekamp — 2024-12-31T06:56:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-344535>

The flicker of LED's is dependent on how they are operated. An LED is a Diode so in DC they only work in one direction. Most LED's have an AC to DC electronic circuit to operate the LED. So, depending on the quality of the electronic circuit Flicker can be zero.

But in some modern applications for price reduction reason the electronic circuit is omitted and a series of LED's is operated under AC conditions in that case only halve of the AC cycle is useful, this is improved by putting another series in the opposite DC direction so both AC current directions are used. However this means flicker since the LED series alternating shine halve of the AC cycle.

### boerekamp — 2024-12-31T07:03:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-344536>

To have the lowest blue signal in an LED go for a Warm White type. 2700K or even better 2400/2200K. The higher the color temperature the higher the Blue signal. For low color temperature more fluorescent material is used so more blue light is converted into Green/Yellow/Red. An LED for lighting application always consist of a blue monochromatic LED covered with a fluorescent material/combination { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. NIRA bulbs combines standard LED light sources (Visible) with low temperature filament sources (IR/NIR)
I guess these types of IR/NIR sources are used:
[https://www.boselec.com/product-category/infrared-thermal-sources/](https://www.boselec.com/product-category/infrared-thermal-sources/)

### [Steady State and Pulsable IR Thermal Sources](http://hawkeyetechnologies.com/source-selection/)

Hawkeye Technologies product list includes Thin Film/MEMS, Filament, Silicon Nitride/Carbide IR thermal emitters.

hawkeyetechnologies.com

I guess the spectrum of the NIRA bulb is comparable the old incandescent lamps, where the latter of course is much cheaper

As compared with daylight, the only part missing in the NIRA bulb is the UV part of the spectrum

### UlliJoanna — 2024-12-31T17:46:16+0200

### boerekamp — 2024-12-31T18:26:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-344562>

> [UlliJoanna said:](/goto/post?id=344560)
>
> Why are non of the bulbs bayonet?

The Edison E26 (=E27) base is the most common type of light bulb and the standard in most countries in the world.
Anyway you could consider buying an adapter E26/E27 --> Bayonet. Available on Amazon

Due to malocclusions in three of my young children, I hired a Building Biologist at the advice of Dr. Kruse. In 2024 we invested $650 in light bulbs from blockbluelight.com (mama mia them are some expensive light bulbs!). Per the assessment the Building Biologist performed of our home, the light bulbs from Blockbluelight.com were putting out a lot of dirty electricity. I have just emailed the company asking for a full refund. It is very disappointing because we thought that they were a great option for daytime use.

The Building Biologist took one of the bulbs back with him to perform additional testing, and below is a video he made.

I apologize if i over step by sharing my opinion as this is not my forum. I personally think there should be some sort of list or a main thread that is updated for everyone. It seems more difficult than necessary to find information on here. There are like 50 threads on light bulbs, then you find out the one everyone recommends isn't even good. There needs to be place where products that have been tested and or confirmed safe or these are quality recommended products by our internal experts for the whole tribe. Easy to find & easy to trust recommendations. The forum is only growing. Its overwhelming to start changing all the lights in your house or buy red light bulbs for evenings. To do it cost effectively & pick the correct options. I just wish the trusted, confirmed, & recommended options were hosted in one place.

I appreciate Jack & everyone who tries to help eachother out in this forum so much! We all gotta stick together & help eachother out

This is simple - There are NO good light bulbs, except the BGT (big glowy thing). I think Jack has been clear on this.
I believe the “short cut” to the info is the search function.
This is a big group with lots of opinions expressed, mostly good BUT some not so good. AND many of these have evolved over time.
Then - there is ALL that Jack has shared and expressed over the years.
“De-centralizing” is a process that requires people to engage in the process - not just be given the answers.
The power comes from ownership of the information which requires knowing WHY things are the way they are.

> “De-centralizing” is a process that requires people to engage in the process - not just be given the answers

I'm not looking for an easy answer or expecting that its black and white.

When i want to learn something i will find the most qualified individual and trust that they can lead me in the correct direction. That's how i found Jack & this forum

you also said This is a big group with lots of opinions expressed, mostly good BUT some not so good. AND many of these have evolved over time.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-10T19:48:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-345017>

IF you want/need artificial lighting, the less blue and flicker it has the better. “Warm white” 2700K bulbs are readily available everywhere. Before buying any bulb in a store plug it in and take a slo-mo video to check for flicker. Not perfect but helps you avoid the worst ones.

You can find new LED’s that may not flicker BUT they still have blue and no NIR. Quartz bulbs can be a good compromise. I have balanced my lighting with red and NIR lights but my lights are DC not AC. I think you can still find incandescent red “party” bulbs in most places. I also have some full spectrum grow/aquarium lights and use an Ott light at my desk when gray outside . All this said avoid ALL artificial light after sunset for optimal results.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-15T20:34:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-345253>

Sit by a window during the day. MAYBE add a full spectrum grow/aquarium light (with UV & NIR) IF needed.
I would NOT use 60w or 100w after sunset. Any light after sunset should be RED.
If you do a slo-mo video and do not see flicker then you have one of the good (better) ones.
Look for 2700k quartz/halogen bulbs IF you have to have traditional extra lighting.

### Gian — 2025-01-18T05:52:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-345356>

Just an update; Block Blue Light never responded nor refunded my money despite me contacting them on 1/8 and 1/15, so as I informed them would happen if they did not respond, I filed a complaint with the FTC for violations of U.S.C. §§ 45 & U.S.C. §§ 52. I am hoping I will find recourse and get a refund. If you are concerned about Dirty Electricity, I would encourage others to seek recourse. These bulbs are exorbantley expensive, so I don't think it is unreasonable that the consumer expects that the bulbs are as advertised.

I cannot speak to the color specturms they produce, but I also purchased some "Traffic Light" incandescent bulbs in clear that come with the common E26 base and are offered in A19 and A21 sizes, and thus far they have been working well and when the Building Biologist assesed my home, they did not get flagged for producing nnEMFs. They are a bit expensive, but they are advertised as having lifespans from 8.000 - 16,000 hours. Being a specialty bulb, I presume they will be available for the forseeable future as bulbs classified as vintage, and special application (like appliances and traffic signal lights) are exempt from the ban on incandescents.

### MatthewMcKay — 2025-10-25T18:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-361600>

Had good convos last night. Friends were excited to show us their new evening lights in their house, they had been over previously and enjoyed our red incandescents. They were inspired, bought color-changed LEDs and were very excited to have control of the color. I told them "the thought was there but the execution is a swing and miss..... the lighting is still shit because it's still LED" (not to mention the flicker & nnEMF output from them being "smart" and BT controlled).

Their debate: LEDs that are red in color are "okay alternatives". I argued that just because the color appears red does not mean the EMFs are "more clean". I remember Jack showed on his IG live a while back that they put red film over hanging lights in their kitchen. When it was time to replace the bulb, you could see the LEDs burnt through the film at the LED locations. The film looked like those strawberry candies your grandma had in the bottom of her purse lol. "You can put lipstick on a pig, but it's still a pig......" right Barry?

_**My Question to the Tribe: Has anyone measured the below lighting options with a light spectrometer? I am curious how they compare:**

1) warm incandescent
2) red bulb incandescent
3) LEDs programmed to "red setting"
4) LEDs with red film over them_

This would be great resources for all Mitochondriacs to help educate their friends & families into making BETTER/CORRECT CHOICES during a time of misinformation and marketing ploys. PS: I understand candles/fire would > than all of the above. SUN = TINA. But would be cool to have one in the chamber next time this conversation pops up (aka next time we go out to dinner & we are wearing red lenses).

Hey [@Rusty](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26197/) have you stumbled across this or done the testing yourself?

_Cheers..........MM_

### Anthony Smith — 2025-11-04T19:00:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-362146>

> [Espresso said:](/goto/post?id=362137)
>
> Pretty standard for ocean going yachts.

DC power and gas heat is standard for many boats, RV’s etc. For this reason they make great office locations. AND if you can find the right location great homes for EHS people.
IF you have solar with battery back up in a house consider removing the inverter to AC and putting in DC lighting and plug/power circuits. Big reduction / removal of 60 Hz (50Hz) AC electric & magnetic fields.

### Espresso — 2025-11-05T11:50:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-362193>

The problem with a lot of boats going totally offshore is that they are usually loaded with solar panels, MPPT solar controllers, inverters, huge alternators and induction cookers. Add radar, SSB radio, VHF radio.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-11-05T20:46:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-362218>

> [Espresso said:](/goto/post?id=362193)
>
> The problem with a lot of boats going totally offshore is that they are usually loaded with solar panels, MPPT solar controllers, inverters, huge alternators and induction cookers. Add radar, SSB radio, VHF radio.

ALL of which have on/off switches . . .
Just like cell phones do (which is the #1 offender for most people).
I am suggesting an all DC system, no inverter or AC and propane where appropriate - cooking, heat, even refrigerator.

There many different types of boats and RV’s which either already are low EMF or can easily be converted.
AND - if the EMF in a location changes they can be relocated.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-01-03T20:26:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-364537>

Several years back most LED bulbs flickered.
However, most of the new ones I have tested recently do not flicker.
IF you want/need artificial light I recommend Halogen (lots of IR) but IF you buy LED (little to no IR) - In the store plug in the bulb and take a slo-mo video.
Always get the warmest bulb available <2700º K (NOT 5000ºK)
Things to consider with ANY bulb - flicker, color spectrum, “DE” (dirty electricity).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-03T21:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-364547>

### riksa — 2026-02-12T15:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-366739>

> [Rudolf said:](/goto/post?id=366737)
>
> so for the time you have to be inside and need more light than what you get from candles, you prefer warm halogen lamps? any specific recommendations? and available Europe?

My take: As long its a thermal emitter, do NOT use LEDs no matter how flicker free or warm they are. Quickest fix possible: Oven lamps are incandescent, sold regularly still in EU(e14 commonly). Better still, longer filaments = closer to candle light(<=2000K) over Halogens.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-12T18:25:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-366748>

> [Rudolf said:](/goto/post?id=366737)
>
> so for the time you have to be inside and need more light than what you get from candles, you prefer warm halogen lamps? any specific recommendations? and available Europe?

During the day IF required, I use full spectrum aquarium or grow lights that have infra red and UV built into them. After sunset red light but IF necessary incandescent if available, halogen if not. Oven lights would be a good choice still available to most.

### Daulatwant — 2026-02-13T22:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-366859>

Go to your local recycling points and look for old light fixtures and stuff. Grab any halogen or old lamps/fixtures, also look online on local listing sites and FB marketplace nobody wants these anymore. New stuff is being made from plastic and often not suitable for incandescent. Halogen bulbs get super hot and will melt stuff.

### Dan Huber — 2026-02-20T19:34:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/incandescent-vs-flicker-free-warm-leds.29743/post-367238>

> [bwo said:](/goto/post?id=367230)
>
> I would NOT recommend Lucia Eyes. Their glasses are often not the size specified and their customer service is the absolute worst.

Dan Huber here. Owner of LE. Sizes are listed on the site, with a detailed explanation on how to figure out

> [bwo said:](/goto/post?id=367230)
>
> I would NOT recommend Lucia Eyes. Their glasses are often not the size specified and their customer service is the absolute worst.

BWO...

Howdy...Dan Huber here

That’s a strong claim — especially since we can’t locate an order tied to your username. So before accepting “absolute worst customer service” as fact, we’d genuinely like to know:

-   Were you actually a customer?
-   When did you order?
-   What specifically happened?

Details matter. Precision matters. Context matters.

For anyone reading along:

Every frame we sell includes published measurements, displayed in **big, bold letters** (SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE, even WIDE LARGE) and here is the third item in our top menu that literally says HOW TO SELECT THE RIGHT FRAME:
:
[https://luciaeyes.com/pages/frame-measurement](https://luciaeyes.com/pages/frame-measurement)

**During checkout, customers must confirm sizing twice** — including a pop-ups that explicitly asks them to verify frame size and prescription before completing the order.

Despite all of that, a small percentage of people still click through everything without reading a single word and then feel surprised afterward. That’s not a manufacturing issue — that’s a modern attention-span issue. Low dopamine

When genuine problems occur, we almost always refund or replace glasses without argument, even when the mistake wasn’t ours, because _taking care of people matters_ more than being technically right — **especially for the tribe of the man who quite literally helped save my life, Dr. Kruse.**

Also worth mentioning: most of our profits fund an anti-suicide nonprofit focused on helping kids struggling with anxiety and depression. This company exists for reasons bigger than selling eyewear or maximizing profit.

If you had a real experience, please contact me [DH@LUCIAEYE.COM](mailto:DH@LUCIAEYE.COM) or share your order info so we can investigate and make it right.

If not, we’d encourage criticism to come from firsthand experience rather than forum mythology.

One additional note for transparency: there are several newer “blue-blocking” eyewear companies attempting to siphon customers using lower-quality imports. One overseas brand, **“Lucier”** (they’re missing one letter…F), is actively bidding on our name in Google ads and causing customer confusion. So if someone believes they purchased from us, it’s worth double-checking who actually fulfilled the order.

We’re easy to reach, we respond as quickly as we can, and we stand behind what we build, how we treat our customers and do our very best to make everyone happy with their glasses. Unfortunately there are still some that _even if_ we gave them there glasses for free they would still talk shit.

Warmest Regards,
Dan

For drinking water, the key is that it is clean \[of flouride and any other crap\]. UV from sunlight will kill prokaryotes in the water.

I think you are confusing the concept of grounding. Grounding appliances serves to discharge static electric fields or stray currents. This is true for humans, who also can absorb electrons from the earth crust for health benefits, particularly through thr sweat glands of hands and feet.

There are devices that “structure” water out there.. e.g. vortexers, spiral tubes, crystals. If you wanna spend the money cuz u think it’s fun or improves the taste, go for it.

The question you ask is not the right one imo. The better one is “how is water used in my body? In what ways/applications? Where does EZ fit into the picture? Where does drinking water play a role? Where does it not? Does the structure of drinking water consequential to good health?”

You ask for product recommendations assuming the premise for them (that structuring drinking water is a high-leverage) is correct.

Keep up the curiosity and welcome.

Viktor Schauberger observed the following ......

**"Schauberger observed a close relationship between water and woodland. Not as we currently perceive it, with the trees simply consuming water. Instead, he theorised that water was produced in the forest.

Water is kept cool in the forest by overhanging vegetation. When a forest is felled, the groundwater is exposed to the rays of the sun, making water hot and aggressive. Schauberger said that this sort of disregard for water makes water lose its character. Explaining that widespread deforestation is one of the causes of the deterioration of water quality

Energy can be equated with life or life force. Water reaches its highest energy content when it’s [at it’s densest –](https://www.sciencelearn.org.nz/resources/1009-water-density) which is when it’s at a temperature of +4°C (+39.2°F).

It just so happens that a true water spring will normally have a temperature of +4°C (+39.2°F). When water is exposed to heat, it’s said to lose it’s vital life energies.

Water maintains its energy by keeping cool and continually moving, preventing the germination of harmful bacteria. Water can resist rapid changes in temperature, which helps us maintain a healthy body temperature.
“Comprehend and copy nature” Viktor Schauberger**
As Joseph touched on your body makes metabolic water **,**we just need to get outside.

If one wanted a Vessel for holding water this is your best bet 1000's of years of usage for water and wine storage .this is what I use
a **Amphora

### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-20T14:50:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-israel-and-the-mossad-were-linked-to-jfks-demise.29748/post-345459>

In 1953, the CIA began drugging unsuspecting Americans with LSD. No, this isn't a conspiracy theory. It was called "Operation Midnight Climax" - and it's one of the darkest chapters in CIA history. The Cold War wasn't just about nuclear weapons. It was also a battle for the mind. When the CIA feared the Soviets were advancing in psychological warfare, they launched MK-ULTRA - a top-secret program aimed at mastering mind control. Enter George Hunter White below: • Former journalist turned federal agent • Known for "aggressive methods" • Described as "deadly and dedicated" • Perfect for the CIA's most unethical experiment.

[View attachment 29859](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/29859/)
The plan was simple but sinister: Set up a fake brothel in San Francisco. Install two-way mirrors and recording equipment. Hire prostitutes to lure men inside. Then secretly dose them with LSD. The CIA called these locations "safe houses." But there was nothing safe about them. Unsuspecting men would have a drink, meet a woman, and suddenly find themselves part of an illegal government experiment. The prostitutes were told to: • Slip LSD into clients' drinks • Engage them in specific conversations • Report their behavior • Test how easily they could be manipulated All while CIA agents watched from behind the mirrors. White's own diary reveals the CIA's true goal: "Controlling an individual to the point where he will do our bidding against his will - even against such fundamental laws of nature as self-preservation." This was government-sanctioned mind control. The victims experienced: • Intense hallucinations • Severe paranoia • Psychological trauma • No medical support Many never knew why they had such a "strange night" in San Francisco.

The operation ran until 1966. But the most disturbing part? White had no regrets. Before his death, he wrote: "It was fun, fun, fun. \- In an unprecedented twist during the 2024 Paris Olympics, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was apprehended in Washington, D.C., under the shadow of an ICC arrest warrant. This covert operation came to light while the world's attention was split between the Olympic games, President Joe Biden's sudden decision to quit his reelection campaign against Donald Trump, Trump's rally that night, and the ceremonial swearing-in of 200 New York City police officers as Capitol Police.

The news broke when President Isaac Herzog was held on the tarmac at Charles de Gaulle Airport in Paris. French security officials, revealing more than just a security scare, informed Herzog of Netanyahu's secret detainment in the U.S. capital. This revelation was timed with the chaos of global events; the Olympics had already captured headlines, Biden's withdrawal from the presidential race added to the political drama, Trump's rally was a focal point for many, and the high-profile event in New York further distracted the public and media.

Netanyahu's apprehension in D.C. was executed with precision, leveraging the distraction to avoid immediate public outcry or diplomatic uproar. The ICC's move against Netanyahu, initially speculated upon in the spring, was reportedly expedited under the cover of these high-profile events, showcasing a strategic use of timing by international legal bodies.

President Herzog, caught off-guard in Paris, was briefed by French authorities to ensure his safety and to prepare for the diplomatic implications. The Israeli delegation quickly entered into emergency discussions, coordinating with diplomats and legal teams in both Paris and Washington to address this sudden geopolitical shift.

The French government has remained tight-lipped about the events in Paris, while U.S. officials have yet to confirm or comment on Netanyahu's detainment in D.C. The international community watches closely, as this incident could redefine Israel's position on the global stage, affect its relations with the U.S., and test the enforcement of international law against sitting leaders.

This development marks a significant moment in international politics, unfolding under the guise of celebration, political campaigning, and local law enforcement ceremonies, raising questions about the timing and motives behind such actions. They came in with an acute inability to walk.

This caused the neurologist to do an EMG of both limbs and the urinary bladder. They also ordered an MRI.

Magnetic resonance imaging of the lumbar spinal cord was unrevealing. Nerve conduction studies, however, were compatible with lumbosacral plexopathy.

Magnetic resonance neurography of lumbosacral plexuses revealed increased signal intensity (arrows) and contrast enhancement of lumbosacral plexuses and the branches thereof bilaterally (Figure 1 on left) and increased signal intensity in pelvic muscles (Figure 2 on right).

Workup for autoimmune, metabolic, and infectious causes turned out negative.1 CSF analysis and cytology were unrevealing. Plexopathy was attributed to technology radiation abuse.

Use of DDW and sunlight was begun. Patient was grounded to beach 6-8 hours a day The patient began to recover in 2-3 weeks. The patient can now walk and urinate.

# Thread: Journey back to Optimal Health and healing my Ulcerative Colitis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-back-to-optimal-health-and-healing-my-ulcerative-colitis.29764/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chibs — 2024-11-08T03:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-back-to-optimal-health-and-healing-my-ulcerative-colitis.29764/post-342063>

I've relapsed back into a mild Ulcerative Colitis flare over the past couple months after being exposed to the COVID virus, as to what I believe as it hit me pretty hard and stirred up some symptoms again --small amount of bleeding, no mucus, formed and solid stools still, and regular bowel movements. I've adjusted my lifestyle and dietary patterns in a variety of ways but still can't get over the hurdle and am curious if anyone has any experience or suggestions. Using Cruse's advice, I have really emphasized my exposure to sunlight and grounding each day ontop of my protocol listed below. Which has had great benefit, but it is beginning to be tough as winter is approaching in Canada and the ground is getting cold. I am still experiencing a little blood and will continue the protocol, but am getting impatient.

Current Protocol and Interventions:
\- Animal Based Diet --little higher fat intake while approaching winter months
\- High Quality Water
\- Lots of walking, and exercise atleast 5x/week
\- Sleep 8h/day
\- Supplements: Indigo Naturalis (currently), Methylated B-Complex, Vitamin D3K2, Sodium Butyrate, Magnesium Biglycinate, Taurine, Glutamine, Collostrum, Creatine
\- Redlight Therapy Mask at night -- 2x10min sessions
\- Herbal Teas, Bone Broth, Electrolytes
\- Mezavant -- 2g/day

I work indoors everyday with no lights on during the day. However, I do work behind a computer screen for 8+h/day, and use a redlight filter but should probably invest in some glasses.

Curious if others have had similar stories, experimented with similar things, and have suggestions on what to adjust to see further improvements.

Much Love,
Chris

# Thread: Antibiotics Research and Guidelines for Babies / Toddlers / Children
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-research-and-guidelines-for-babies-toddlers-children.29766/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### robtherhino — 2024-11-08T12:55:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-research-and-guidelines-for-babies-toddlers-children.29766/post-342069>

My wife and I are in a disagreement about antibiotics for our son.

My concern is that they are recommended too freely where we live (i.e. for a post-viral cough that hasn't cleared after 3 weeks).
Her concern is that our GP only recommends it when it is necessary.

There is lots of research on antibiotic resistance due to overprescription in childhood.
But none say what frequency is okay / not okay.

He has been given them twice now over 1.5 years (both winters)

The pattern is he gets a cold in winter and is quite snotty for a few days > then gets a cough about a week later > then three weeks later the cough hasn't cleared > Doc recommended short course of antibiotics to prevent it progressing to pneumonia > wife gets freaked out > do the short course > try to mitigate gut flora damage with circadian principles (AM sun, lots of time outside, limiting EMF exposure, grounding via me in winter) and raw milk kefir daily, and then lots of summer sun on abdomen when the time comes.

Can anyone help us out with practical information / research on frequency, necessity, risks, etc?
I'm also not clear for a circadian perspective if there is a different cause / approach to "regular" winter colds.
I've posted separately regarding CT for babies / toddlers.

Am I being paranoid?
Is my wife buying the centralised narrative?

**

[https://activities.nps.org.au/nps-order-form/Resources/NPS-MedicineWise-RTI-Action-Plan.pdf](https://activities.nps.org.au/nps-order-form/Resources/NPS-MedicineWise-RTI-Action-Plan.pdf)

Then you may like to read these: [https://www.choosingwisely.org.au/recommendations/asid3](https://www.choosingwisely.org.au/recommendations/asid3)

### [How to reduce antibiotic resistance](https://www.nps.org.au/professionals/reducing-antibiotic-resistance)

Antibiotic resistance is an urgent global health issue. This program aims to reduce the inappropriate use of antibiotics in the community.

 www.nps.org.au

[https://www1.health.gov.au/internet/main/publishing.nsf/Content/cda-pubs-cdi-2003-cdi27suppl-htm-cdi27supi.htm](https://www1.health.gov.au/internet/main/publishing.nsf/Content/cda-pubs-cdi-2003-cdi27suppl-htm-cdi27supi.htm)

**
As Jack says: watch and wait

### robtherhino — 2024-11-10T14:29:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-research-and-guidelines-for-babies-toddlers-children.29766/post-342158>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=342094)
>
> Why not see how his own immuns system responds before drugging him?

I agree. I think we are robbing him of the opportunity to develop his immune system.

The issue is subjective.
When and in what circumstances is it necessary to intervene.

I propose to leave him to fight it off himself, and intervene with antibiotics if he gets pneumonia.
My wife agrees to a point - usually 3 or 4 weeks, then stats to doubt.
The doctor says to intervene after 2 or 3 weeks.

What the issue is that no one is able to point to anything concrete about under what circumstances to intervene with antibiotics.

### Magpie — 2024-11-10T15:16:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-research-and-guidelines-for-babies-toddlers-children.29766/post-342162>

I can't make that type of decision about antibiotics without carrying out a clinical examination of the patient. And that type of decision may need an examination several times during the course of each respiratory tract infection. I do not prescribe antibiotics for a post infective cough. If your GP has really prescribed antibiotics for a post infective cough, maybe you need a new GP. Post viral coughs do not in themselves turn into pneumonia.

### Magpie — 2024-11-11T21:37:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-research-and-guidelines-for-babies-toddlers-children.29766/post-342215>

Whenever the parents of a baby or young child bring that child into clinic for care there are some things I frequently tell them. Sometimes you don't know whether they needed to see a doctor until you see the doctor. That is why we never mind seeing children if their parents are worried. And even if I think a child is ok with current conservative management, things can change. If you are worried, we are always happy to review and if it is out of office hours an ER visit might be in order.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-08T23:42:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-is-lawless-frontier-where-cybersecurity-will-destroy-sovereignty.29773/post-342087>

The link between Trump and Musk has always concerned me.

• In **2019**, President Trump [signed](https://www.space.com/president-trump-space-force-directive.html) Space Policy Directive-4, ordering the Pentagon to establish the Space Force as the sixth branch of the U.S. military. This is not a good thing for Americans.

• On **December 9, 2020** President Trump [issued](https://www.space.commerce.gov/policy/national-space-policy/) the 2020 National Space Policy, replacing the 2010 version, with a goal of working with commercial and international partners, generating new markets and entrepreneurship, and leaving a permanent human presence on the Moon, Mars, and beyond.

Satellites store and move data, centralized blockchain of the shitcoiners of a16z and Google is making its way into this data, the digital financial system is accelerating, space fence is surveilling, space-based solar and data systems are being constructed, and the laws (or lawlessness) of running this full operation in space is on their side, which coincides perfectly with the many [immunities and privileges](https://www.coreysdigs.com/u-s/laundering-with-immunity-the-control-framework-part-1/) that central banks and numerous organizations already enjoy.

Imagine running a control grid from space, complete with global surveillance, the ability to generate 24/7 power, to hold and store all data, to manipulate the weather and atmosphere, to have weapons that can pulse frequencies to alter behaviors or incinerate blocks instantly with directed energy weapons. Just imagine the power of what could be done in a place where the Constitution is not the law of the land.

It’s all out on the table and the competition is palpable. Certain countries are aligning, as allies are critical to maintaining control over their own country and its population. The financial sector is chomping at the bit. While the central banks set the pace and give marching orders, all of the digital wallet and crypto companies are striving to be on top. Everyone wants a piece of the pie, and their eyes are on space.

Elon Musk would like X to be the “[everything app](https://twitter.com/lindayaX/status/1704890916685611390)” where users can tap into “their entire financial world” on a platform he hopes will run “[half of the global financial system](https://www.coreysdigs.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Elon-half-worlds-finances.mp4).” Elon just got great press for getting Trump elected to give him good PR but I think this might be a psy-ops to get millions to believe he was the "free speech guy in 2024. I doubt he'll be the free speech guy in the future. Why? He wants to dominate space for control. Who better connected than to pull this off with the military while people continue to raise him up as a hero of “free speech” and give him their financial digits for ad revenue to make his dream a reality? Even if it means providing a government ID along with biometrics that will soon be linked to a QR code one day. And even though many of these individuals are aware that Linda Yaccarino serves as the chair of the WEF’s Future of Work task force, was brought on by Elon to be Twitter/X’s CEO, recently announced they will be censoring “lawful but awful” behavior, they continue to make money for Twitter/X to build the enslavement system.

Would it make a difference if they knew that the ads they are promoting is much of legacy media, Disney, transgender shows, and Amazon – all places that push false narratives for control over people, plus Disney’s rap sheet of pedophiles?

New Chief of Staff is linked to Means siblings.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-09T00:06:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-is-lawless-frontier-where-cybersecurity-will-destroy-sovereignty.29773/post-342102>

Since DJT is in, Bitcoin is in and it appears CBDCs are out.........is this true? Or is it a timing matter?

#### Deloitte’s _“_Financial Services Industry Predictions” are Revealing​

On July 31, 2023, Deloitte released their report on “[Financial Services Industry Predictions](https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/about-deloitte/articles/press-releases/deloitte-announces-2023-financial-services-industry-predictions.html).” Deloitte is a key globalist player who often collaborates with the WEF. For example, their white paper on “A Blueprint for Digital Identity: The Role of Financial Institutions in Building the Digital Identity,”released in August 2016, stated that financial institutions should drive the creation of digital identity systems, disrupt the credit bureau model, blur the lines between financial and non-financial advisory, and bring in biometrics. Corey’s Digs covered this in the [report](https://www.coreysdigs.com/technology/the-global-landscape-on-vaccine-id-passports-part-3-the-key-implementers-of-your-digital-identity-onto-the-blockchain/) on vaccine ID passports. In this current paper, they “predict” the following:

• Deloitte estimates “the revenue for alternative data providers, earned from all industries globally with the majority coming from investment management firms, to grow 29x between 2022 and 2030. The new data largely consist of novel types and forms of **data such as satellite images, social media posts, geolocation data, credit card transactions, and mobile application data** that are starkly different from the traditionally structured financial data in banks.”

• Deloitte states that banks will develop more advanced **biometric security systems (MEANS SIBLINGS)** due to an increase in cybercriminals, and because of the increasing importance for digital currencies such as CBDCs. They give an example of the European Union’s work and explain that by expanding these verification systems it will not only provide safeguards with banking, but will also confirm a user’s identity when sharing medical data, taking online exams, or making age-restricted purchases. Deloitte is so confident about the expansion of biometrics, they estimate that in just a few short years “**three billion global consumers are expected to make US$5.1 trillion of purchases using biometric payments.**” It should be noted that biometrics is one area Twitter/X is [moving toward](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/8/21/x-blue-users-will-need-to-send-selfie-data-to-israeli-software-company) for those who wish to have a blue checkmark as well as plug into the ad revenue stream.

• Deloitte [says](https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/financial-services/financial-services-industry-predictions.html#consumers-increase) that consumers will “increasingly use their wallets to help fight climate change with **carbon-offset purchases**.” They “predict” that carbon offsets will be embedded in many purchasing decisions such as through travel, ordering food, or “negotiating a new mortgage rate,” and that it will become so pervasive (BLACKROCK) it will reach a **US$100 billion market by 2030**. Just as in their earlier report about banks being instrumental in rolling out a digital ID, they want banks pushing carbon credits as well. “Banks could be instrumental in developing and supporting the back-end infrastructure that connect brands’ payment processes to the carbon credit market. And banks can play an instrumental role in developing and supporting the carbon credit market.”

• Deloitte also estimates “advancements in self-driving technology may **eliminate the need for around 380,000 long-haul truck drivers** in the next five years.”

Based on the bullet points under “Brush Strokes” above, in addition to the complete breakdown tying the financial movement together, further down in this report, it would seem that Deloitte is once again on target. It would also seem that they can build the control grid before CBDCs even come into play (LUTNICK).

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-09T00:09:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-is-lawless-frontier-where-cybersecurity-will-destroy-sovereignty.29773/post-342104>

Despite people’s belief that Elon Musk is trying to prevent WWIII in UKRAINE/RUSSIA and won’t allow the U.S. government to utilize his satellites for battle and nefarious actions, nothing could be further from the truth.

Over four years ago, in April 2020, SpaceX [teamed up](https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone/32346/the-air-force-and-spacex-are-teaming-up-for-a-massive-live-fire-exercise) with the Air Force to link Starlink satellites with multiple armed forces systems for a huge live fire exercise that included ground forces, submarines, ships, and other space-based assets, to shoot down a cruise missile and a drone. Prior to that, Starlink satellites connected with a Lockheed Martin AC-130 gunship. SpaceX has teamed up with the Department of Defense on many payloads and projects, and [other](https://www.investors.com/news/spacex-starlink-impressed-air-force-in-big-live-fire-exercise/) live fire exercises.

The ultimate goal was to build the new Advanced Battle Management System (ABMS) to allow for the coordination of U.S. forces and allies in real-time across land, sea, air, and space, with this new system being equipped with artificial intelligence. The Department of Defense has a new strategy to ensure control over space, which includes utilizing small satellites and space-based capabilities that are unknown to the public and competitors.

**If you get ABMS built right, you’ve just built the military’s ‘internet of things’. Not bueno.**

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-09T00:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-is-lawless-frontier-where-cybersecurity-will-destroy-sovereignty.29773/post-342106>

Ukraine was a great test market for Starlink, which is what the Pentagon was hoping for. Everything else was theater. SpaceX sent over 22,000 Starlink satellites to Ukraine and replenished any that were destroyed in combat. In December 2022, Congress passed the 2023 spending bill with a hefty sum of over $69 billion for the DoD, including $1.7 billion for Space Force programs.

By December 2022, SpaceX [unveiled](https://spacenews.com/with-starshield-spacex-readies-for-battle/) Starshield, a “secured satellite network for government entities.” This entails sensors, custom-built spacecraft, and secure communications via Starlink satellites. “Because of the rapidly improving commercial space capabilities, a comprehensive plan for using commercial space systems in the context of classified U.S. space capabilities is needed,” said Heidi Shyu, undersecretary of defense for research and engineering, in a memo on November 21, 2022. One of the goals is to build a low earth orbit tracking layer of missile-detection satellites, whereby a mesh network will move data.

Israel invasion of GAZA has been a great test case for this.

### Michelle Grandbois — 2024-11-10T00:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.29775/post-342142>

Hello,
I am starting a Journal to keep track of things, changes and observations
I joined about a months ago after seeing Jack's interview with Danny Jones - it put a lot into perspective, especially with the current medico-political mess we are in....on so many levels

Notes:
Same day- shifted my screens to "night time", most my lights are incandescent but decreased them to the bare minimum until I can find red lights
Next day started sunrise exposure daily, and increasing sun exposure
Started digging and reading on the website....

after 3 days:
\-started waking up naturally just before sunrise
\-feeling more rested in the morning
\-slight decrease in body pain (random body pain all over x few years)

after 1 week:
\-lost 1 pound without restricting/changing food (19 to go)
\-noticeable decreased appetite and sugar craving
\-70% decrease in body pain (took only 1/2 Tylenol once. Use to keep it at maximum 1 Tylenol daily)
\-No migraines (used to have heat/sun induced migraine 3 times/week)
\-better mood and concentration
\-No more sunburns, except face sometimes (\*I used to burn after only 10-15 minutes in the sun. Started tanning again and not burning 2-3 days after starting bioidentical estrogens. Not sure why yet, but very noticeable. Friends commented about it. I turned brown.)

week 3:
\- same, + general wellbeing improvement
\- lost 2 more pounds without restricting/changing food (17 to go)
Now often sleepy at sunset (6pm), going to bed between 8h30-9h30. Sleeping well through the night (used to wake up around 3-4am often)
**Read about contact lenses** (!) - Wearing night&day Aqua x 20 years+; switching monthly only (keeping them on at night)
although they do not specifically filter UV light (as per manufacturer), I'm sure they do on some levels + block Oxygen to the eye
\-now wearing them maximum x3-4 hours in the afternoon. Need proper glasses made. -4.5 prescription, not great without glasses/contacts but manageable lol
I felt a big difference in overall wellbeing after only 2 days of removing my contacts for most of the day
**Read site and listened to webinar about CT** - now I understand what it is about. Very helpful.
I am very heat adapted, hypothyroid, have heavy metal toxicity (this should improve with light exposure and melanin), adrenal fatigue (although much better). I actually slightly shiver in a pool at 80 if I am not moving lol. But I used to do cold plunges when I lived in Canada many years ago. We will see if it is indeed cumulative for me lol. Will start CT gradually.
**Read MTHFR thread** - very interesting. Will shift a few things around here too.

Grateful to have found this site; the information is complete, intelligent and very helpful
Lots to deconstruct and reconstruct - it keeps life dynamic and interesting

I am gradually spreading the information I am learning about to people around me

TBC ...

### Michelle Grandbois — 2025-07-02T01:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.29775/post-355588>

Almost no sun here in Costa Rica for the last 3 months - Very unusual; usually we get great sunny mornings and rain later during the day during this period as this is rainy season. Heavy chemtrails in the mornings/sky opaque. Still doing sunrise, but the lack of general sunlight for that long has had a big impact in my energy level and mood

still mostly not using my contact lenses (was using them once every 2 weeks, if absolutely needed); BUT I lost my glasses in the ocean, and at -4.25 I am quite blind and clumsy without them, so used my contacts 3 times per week during last 2 weeks. Waiting for new glasses, which I won't be using all the time. \*\* **my prescription went from -4.75 to -4.25 in a few months** \- I am very happy about this. I saw other people in the forum reporting sight improvement - That's great!

**Started CT about 2.5 weeks ago**\- all new information for me, so going through the blogs and forum
\- I am VERY warm adapted (usually feeling cold in a pool at 75-80F if I am not moving); outdoor temp during the day usually around 98... so I was surprised to see that cold plunge at 55F was not a big deal for my body, and I read somewhere on the website that basically the body remembers the cold adaptation. I used to go to spas weekly, to do "juice fast", saunas and cold plunge at 40F on a weekly basis when I lived in Ottawa, and I absolutely loved it. CT also seems to activate similar pathways as the ones activated during **free diving(?)**, which I trained for a did quite a bit a few years back.
\- Bloodwork from 1 1/2 year ago had shown I had pretty bad hypothyroid; haven't checked levels again, focused on lifestyle change. I read I should start slow with CT if I have hypothyroid (?); but I am not sure what to look for/ what would not be good because of hypothyroid, lack of thermoregulation, other.
So to start slow, I started at:
\- 5 min full body immersion (not head lol) at 55F x 3d; I woke up the first 3 days at 4am feeling cold, with small shivering feeling on arms and legs having a hard time warming up (although temp in the room was probably around 80F)... not a big deal, just noteworthy
\- then 7 min x 3d
\- 10 min x 2d
\-14 min x 4 days; I started having some nausea and loss of appetite after plunge after the second day. I thought it could be leptin sensitivity shifting? Day 4- I had pretty bad pain in stomach/duodenal area about 1h after cold plunge. Felt similar to a gastritis/small ulcer I had in the past, so I stopped CT x 1 day, then went back to 5 min x 3 days but this time after a bigger breakfast/meal- it seems to have settled. So, more food seems to be key. wondering if CT induce gall bladder dumping/body stress response
\- Now back to 7 min x 2 days
\- today 14 min after a full meal= seems fine. Still feeling a bit cold 1h after, with slight shivering sensation arms/legs.

Energy level is a bit all over the place since starting CT (but also from the lack of sunshine), so it has been interesting to watch. Maybe from the facilitated peripheral conversion of T4 to T3 (and clearing of the reverse T3), and maybe that's the reason someone with hypothyroid should start slow(?).
I also wonder if CT has an effect on HRT; I would make sense since some hormones are stored in fat. Possibly the release of stored estrogen with fat cells apoptosis. Not sure yet.
Sleep is good; 9pm to 5:00-5:30am. Seems like I have more dreams
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Edit CT July 4th:
\- Now doing daily 17 min CT @54-56F, full body immersion x 2 days; slight abdominal pain second day.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
